gmw_00006853.name: J0015 + 1842
gmw_00006853.overview: J0015 + 1842 is a type-I quasar at z=2.63 [arXiv:2012.02487, arXiv:2103.09542] that is likely in a merger-driven evolutionary phase [arXiv:2103.09542]. The system hosts a large molecular gas mass [arXiv:2103.09542] and exhibits multiphase outflows on galactic scales [arXiv:2012.02487, arXiv:2103.09542].

sources.summary: The paper observes SDSS J001514 + 184212 as a high-redshift quasar with broad and narrow emission components, kpc-scale ionized gas, molecular absorption, and a multiphase outflow.

quotes.quote: We present ultraviolet to near infrared spectroscopic observations of the quasar SDSS J001514 + 184212 and its proximate molecular absorber at z=2.631 . The [ ] emission line of the quasar is composed of a broad (FWHM 1600 ), spatially unresolved component, blueshifted by about 600 \ from a narrow, spatially-resolved component (FWHM 650 ). The wide, blueshifted, unresolved component is consistent with the presence of outflowing gas in the nuclear region. The narrow component can be further decomposed into a blue and a red blob with a velocity width of several hundred \ each, seen 5 pkpc on opposite spatial locations from the nuclear continuum emission, indicating outflows on galactic scales. The presence of ionised gas on kpc scales is also seen from a weak \ emission component, detected in the trough of a saturated \ absorption that removes the strong nuclear emission from the quasar.

Our VLT/X-shooter observations of the proximate molecular absorber towards \ suggest that spatially resolved emission of ionised gas and kinematically wide absorption in various phases towards the nucleus are different manifestations of a single outflowing process. From detailed investigation of the physical conditions in the cold phase of the absorbing gas, we derived a distance to the central engine of the order of 10 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents CO(3-2) observations of the z=2.63 quasar, finding large molecular gas mass and velocity dispersion, moderate dust reddening, and a likely merger-driven quasar phase.

quotes.quote: We present 3-mm observations of the quasar \ at z=2.63 with the NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA). Our data reveals molecular gas, traced via a Gaussian CO(3-2) line, with a remarkably large velocity dispersion ( FWHM=1010 120 ) and corresponding to a total molecular mass M_ H_2 (3.4 - 17) 10^ 10 M_ , depending on the adopted CO-to-H _2 conversion factor _ CO =(0.8 - 4.0) M_ ( pc ^2)^ 1 . Assuming the 3-mm continuum emission is thermal, we derive a dust mass of the order of M_ dust 5 10^8 M_ .

While the large velocity dispersion of the CO line suggests a merging system , \ is observed to be a regular, only very moderately dust-reddened ( A_V 0.3 - 0.4 ) type-I quasar from its UV-optical spectrum, from which we infer a mass of the super-massive black hole be around M_ BH 6 10^8 M_ . We suggest that \ is observed at a galaxy evolutionary stage where a massive merger has brought significant amounts of gas towards an actively accreting super-massive black hole (quasar). While the host still contains a large amount of dust and molecular gas with high velocity dispersion, the quasar has already cleared the way towards the observer , likely through powerful outflows as recently revealed by optical observations of the same object.

gmw_00006854.name: SDSS J122558.34+570930.3
gmw_00006854.overview: SDSS J122558.34+570930.3 is a galaxy-like source identified in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey and Pan-STARRS1 images [arXiv:2305.12974]. It is classified as a galaxy with a photometric redshift of z = 0.215 [arXiv:2305.12974].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the source underlying ZTF18achdhvu as galaxy-like, classifies it as a photometric-redshift galaxy, and uses it to infer an extragalactic transient distance.

quotes.quote: There is an underlying source, SDSS J122558.34+570930.3, in Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; 2000AJ....120.1579Y and PS1 images, 0.4 \ from the position of ZTF18achdhvu with g = 21.89 0.07 mag, sgscore = 0.04 (i.e., a galaxy-like source), and E(B-V)_ MW = 0.011 mag 2011ApJ...737..103S . It is classified as a galaxy with a photometric redshift of z_ ph = 0.215 0.072 2015ApJS..219...12A by SDSS. Considering the high Galactic latitude ( b= 59.6^ ) and the galaxy-like morphology of the underlying source, it is reasonable to assume ZTF18achdhvu is an extragalactic transient. Taking the photometric redshift, we derive a distance modulus of 40.2^ +0.7 _ 1.0 mag, corresponding to Galactic-extinction-corrected absolute peak magnitudes of M_g 21.0^ +1.0 _ 0.7 mag and M_r 20.8^ +1.0 _ 0.7 mag.

gmw_00006855.name: Q0933-D16
gmw_00006855.overview: Q0933-D16 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2104.02081]. Although it was initially identified as an apparent Lyman continuum emitter, its emission is contaminated by lower-redshift interlopers [arXiv:2104.02081].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0933-D16 as a apparent LyC detection affected by foreground or low-redshift contamination and removes it from the cleaned KLCS sample used for LyC measurements.

quotes.quote: We found that two apparent LyC detections (Q0933-M23 and Q0933-D16) contained components that had a higher likelihood of being drawn from the contaminant sample than the sample. We also found one LyC non-detection (Q1422-md145) that was similarly consistent with being contaminated.
For Q0933-D16, Q1422-md145, and Q1422-d53, the subcomponents were either aligned along the slit or narrowly separated, making spectral deblending impossible.

With the removal of two apparent LyC detections, Q0933-D16 and Q0933-M23, whose emission is actually contaminated by lower-redshift interlopers, global properties of the sample will be unbiased by line-of-sight contamination and will present a more accurate picture of the global ionizing properties of galaxies in the Universe. We also remove the LyC non-detections Q1422-md145 and Q1422-d53 due to evidence of low-redshift subcomponents within their HST morphology.

gmw_00006856.name: Q0933-M23
gmw_00006856.overview: Q0933-M23 is a massive Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 3.2 [arXiv:1209.1484, arXiv:1003.5773] that lacks active galactic nucleus signatures [arXiv:1209.1484]. It is a normal main-sequence galaxy with a high star formation rate [arXiv:1309.5448], though its apparent Lyman continuum detection is likely affected by foreground contamination [arXiv:2104.02081].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M23 as a massive non-lensed z=3.216 LBG with a plausible CO detection and main-sequence star-formation properties.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies of this study (M23 and M18) were originally optically selected ( U_ n , G, R with R < 25.5 ) by Steidel et al. (2003) in the GOODS-N field. Rest-frame ultraviolet (UV) spectroscopy - which was also used to ascertain the absence of AGN signatures (i.e. strong high-ionization emission lines) - provided redshifts, z_ spec = 3.216 and z_ spec = 2.929 , respectively, determined from Ly- (among other) absorption lines.

We searched for CO emission at the position of the LBG targets (M23, RA: 189.2612457, DEC: 62.2405968 and M18, RA: 189.1837769, DEC: 62.2196693) and detected possible emission lines at S/N = 4.0 and 3.3 for M23 and M18, respectively. The first is exactly at phase center while the latter reaches 3.6 if we allow for a small offset of 1.4 '' (only 20 of the synthesized beam). In both cases this peak emission is within the velocity range allowed by the 1 optical redshift uncertainties of 600 km s ^ 1 and 1000 km s ^ 1 respectively, for M23 and M18.

Based on i) the fairly small chance of the signal being spurious, ii) the fact that the observed signal is spatially coincident with the position of the source (Fig. 2 left) and iii) that the emission is centred at the expected frequency within 1 of the optical spectroscopic uncertainty (Fig. 2 middle and right), we consider M23 to be a plausible detection, and derive a CO [ 3-2 ] luminosity of (1.60 0.4) 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper revises M23's tentative CO detection downward, estimates its SFR and stellar mass, and describes it as a normal z~3 galaxy rather than a starburst despite high SFR.

quotes.quote: M23 magdis12a reported CO(3-2) line emission with S/N=4 toward M23. We achieved no significant line-detection in the new observations, such that, in the combined dataset (threefold integration time increase relative to magdis12a ), the overall significance decreases to \ 3.5 (Figure  fig1 ), suggesting that the flux density reported in was boosted by noise. Based on the deeper data, we still consider the line feature in M23 as tentative and revise our estimate of its CO(3-2) luminosity to (9.5 2.7) 10^9 , though this may still represent an upper limit.

For M23, magdis12a estimate SFR = (310 110) M _ yr ^ 1 , based on radio and 250 m detections. After accounting for flux from a nearby (likely unrelated, compact, red) companion source, we revise the stellar mass estimate for M23 to M_ = (1.0 0.5) 10^ 11 M _ , implying a sSFR 3.1 Gyr ^ 1 . This value is close to the average sSFR of equally massive galaxies on the SFR- M_ main-sequence at z 3 (sSFR/sSFR _ MS 1.4), suggesting that M23 is a normal galaxy rather than a starburst, notwithstanding its high SFR.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0933-M23 as a apparent LyC detection affected by foreground or low-redshift contamination and removes it from the cleaned KLCS sample used for LyC measurements.

quotes.quote: We found that two apparent LyC detections (Q0933-M23 and Q0933-D16) contained components that had a higher likelihood of being drawn from the contaminant sample than the sample. We also found one LyC non-detection (Q1422-md145) that was similarly consistent with being contaminated.

In Q0933-M23, we found evidence for foreground contamination in the LRIS spectrum shown in Figure fig:M23_2D . Here, the Ly feature used for spectroscopic redshift measurement is clearly offset from the continuum towards the component predicted to lie at by our color-color method. There also exists an additional spectral feature offset toward the predicted low-redshift component, which was classified as Ly at z=3.380 by Steidel2018 . Based on the high-resolution photometric information offered by HST and the lack of other offset spectral features, it is more likely this emission line is [ O ii ] 3727 at z=0.43 .

With the removal of two apparent LyC detections, Q0933-D16 and Q0933-M23, whose emission is actually contaminated by lower-redshift interlopers, global properties of the sample will be unbiased by line-of-sight contamination and will present a more accurate picture of the global ionizing properties of galaxies in the Universe. We also remove the LyC non-detections Q1422-md145 and Q1422-d53 due to evidence of low-redshift subcomponents within their HST morphology.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HDFN-M23 as a z=3.21 MIPS-LBG detected at 1.4 GHz, with UV/radio SFRs near 808/870 solar masses per year and L_IR about 5 x 10^12 L_sun.

quotes.quote: We matched our LBG sample to the 5 radio catalog (Morrison et al. \ 2010) and identified one MIPS-LBG, HDFN-M23 with a flux S _ 1.4GHz =21.19 4.2 Jy ( z =3.21).

In Figure 3, we plot the SFR estimates based on the radio fluxes and the UV corrected for dust extinction for three samples: HDFN-M23 which is individually detected in the radio map, the stacked flux of radio-undetected MIPS-LBGs, and the stacked flux of radio-undetected IRAC-LBGs. We see that there is an excellent agreement between the radio and UV estimates, testifying to the validity of UV as a SFR indicator for UV-selected galaxies at z 3. The corresponding SFRs derived from UV (and radio) are 90 ^ +20 _ 40 M _ yr ^ 1 ,(96 32 M _ yr ^ 1 ) for the IRAC-LBGs, 250 ^ +35 _ 80 M _ yr ^ 1 ,(280 85 M _ yr ^ 1 ) for the MIPS-LBGS and 808 M _ yr ^ 1 ,(870 200 M _ yr ^ 1 ) for HDFN-M23.

On the other hand, MIPS-LBGs have higher luminosities, indicating that ULIRGs are present among the UV-selected galaxies at z 3. For instance, HDFN-M23 which was individually detected in the radio, has L _ IR 5( 2) 10 ^ 12 L _ .

sources.summary: The paper reports an H I satellite near NGC 973 with no optical counterpart, detected in nine channels and having an H I mass of 3.39 x 10^8 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We further report the detection of a small satellite galaxy slightly above the NE edge of the disk, at a distance of 36 kpc from the center of NGC 973 (as determined from our models). We estimate its central position as RA 02 h 34 m 23.9 s , Dec 32 32 5.0 and find no optical counterpart. The satellite appears in 9 consecutive (independent) channels in the data cube, between 4517 and 4572 km s ^ 1 , although often only at the 3 level, and has a total H i flux of 0.36 Jy km s ^ 1 . This translates to a H i mass of 3.39 10^ 8 M _ .

gmw_00006857.name: Q1422-md145
gmw_00006857.overview: Q1422-md145 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2104.02081]. It is classified as a Lyman continuum non-detection that was removed from the cleaned KLCS sample due to foreground or low-redshift contamination identified in its Hubble Space Telescope morphology [arXiv:2104.02081].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1422-md145 as a LyC non-detection affected by foreground or low-redshift contamination and removes it from the cleaned KLCS sample used for LyC measurements.

quotes.quote: We found that two apparent LyC detections (Q0933-M23 and Q0933-D16) contained components that had a higher likelihood of being drawn from the contaminant sample than the sample. We also found one LyC non-detection (Q1422-md145) that was similarly consistent with being contaminated.
For Q0933-D16, Q1422-md145, and Q1422-d53, the subcomponents were either aligned along the slit or narrowly separated, making spectral deblending impossible.

With the removal of two apparent LyC detections, Q0933-D16 and Q0933-M23, whose emission is actually contaminated by lower-redshift interlopers, global properties of the sample will be unbiased by line-of-sight contamination and will present a more accurate picture of the global ionizing properties of galaxies in the Universe. We also remove the LyC non-detections Q1422-md145 and Q1422-d53 due to evidence of low-redshift subcomponents within their HST morphology.

gmw_00006858.name: Q1422-d53
gmw_00006858.overview: Q1422-d53 is a high-redshift galaxy that was identified as a Lyman-continuum non-detection [arXiv:2104.02081]. It was removed from the cleaned Keck Lyman Continuum Spectroscopic Survey sample due to evidence of low-redshift contamination within its Hubble Space Telescope morphology [arXiv:2104.02081].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1422-d53 as a LyC non-detection affected by foreground or low-redshift contamination and removes it from the cleaned KLCS sample used for LyC measurements.

quotes.quote: We found that the only object to be classified differently by this method was the non-detection Q1422-d53, now predicted to contain low-redshift components. We display the 3D-HST and low-redshift PDFs after Q1422-d53 brightness and size cuts in Figure fig:VJH_d53 . Using these modified PDFs, components d , e , and f were predicted to be low-redshift. We considered this method to be more physically-motivated and remove Q1422-d53 from the non-detection sample.
For Q0933-D16, Q1422-md145, and Q1422-d53, the subcomponents were either aligned along the slit or narrowly separated, making spectral deblending impossible.

With the removal of two apparent LyC detections, Q0933-D16 and Q0933-M23, whose emission is actually contaminated by lower-redshift interlopers, global properties of the sample will be unbiased by line-of-sight contamination and will present a more accurate picture of the global ionizing properties of galaxies in the Universe. We also remove the LyC non-detections Q1422-md145 and Q1422-d53 due to evidence of low-redshift subcomponents within their HST morphology.

gmw_00006859.name: Lockman850.1
gmw_00006859.overview: Lockman850.1 is an extremely red, diffuse submillimetre galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.38 [arXiv:1004.0009, arXiv:astro-ph/0108131]. The source is characterized as an extended, clumpy, dusty star-forming galaxy that may represent a massive elliptical galaxy in formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0108131].

sources.summary: SMG01 is described as the brightest faint-subsample Lockman Hole SMG, with redshift 3.38, a single MERLIN component, weak EVN emission, and only an SMBH mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: We now give brief descriptions of each source: SMG01: The first source of the faint subsample is actually the brightest submm source in the Lockman Hole East region (850.01). Recent Spitzer mid-IR spectroscopy has determined a reliable redshift, 3.38 0.02 coppin2010 . SMG01 is detected as a single component by MERLIN with a 1.4-GHz flux of 90 Jy, a morphology that is consistent with its flat spectrum, =+0.21 . This would have corresponded to a 9- detection were it unresolved at the EVN resolution, but only weak (3- i.e. \ 30 Jy beam ^ 1 peak) emission is seen near the centre of the map.

At the same time, for the non-detections (SMG10, SMG01, SMG02, and SMG04), we can utilise the observed correlation to derive upper limits on M_ BH for these systems. This again requires we assume that no radio-quiet AGN are present, and that emission from large-scale jets is both negligible in these systems and in driving the observed M_ BH L_ 5GHz correlation, and therefore represents a fairly rough estimate. However, keeping these assumptions in mind we find the 3-- detections for SMG10, SMG01, SMG02 and SMG04 imply upper limits of log (M_ BH / M _ ) 8.1, 8.2, 7.5 and 7.6, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes LE 850.1 as an extremely faint, diffuse submillimetre galaxy requiring careful acquisition; its H-band flux is estimated, but only marginal emission lines are found and no redshift is assigned.

quotes.quote: LE 850.1 Due to the extremely faint and diffuse nature of this source, it took a substantial amount of imaging on the night of UT 2002 May 21 to make a firm imaging detection. Although by the time we had done so, the hour angle of the source was no longer favourable, we confirmed the offsets from a nearby star as being 11.6 arcsec west and 5.2 arcsec south. We returned to LE 850.1 two nights later, and used our previous imaging to hasten the acquisition. Since the distance between the target and offset star is larger than the OHS field of view, we first located the bright star near the end of the field of view, and made a small (2 arcsec) movement of the telescope by altering the location of the centroiding box on the autoguider camera.

The success of this technique depends on the accuracy to which the plate scales of CISCO and the AG camera are known, and we estimate the error to be no more than 0.2 arcsec. Since we were unable to obtain reliable photometry from our acquisition image, we applied the same flux scaling factor for LE 850.1 as was applied for LE 850.3; from the flux in the `clean' part of our H band spectrum (1.60--1.70 m), we estimate H=23.1 0.4 in our extraction aperture. Three emission lines are suggested by our line-finding algorithm, although all are extremely marginal.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Lockman850.1 as an extended, clumpy, extremely red dusty star-forming source near redshift 3, consistent with a massive elliptical galaxy in formation.

quotes.quote: The near infrared counterpart is an extended (20--30 kpc), clumpy, and extremely red object. The spectral energy distribution suggests it to be a dusty star forming object at a redshift of about 3 (2--4). Its star formation rate and near-infrared properties are consistent with Lockman850.1 being a massive elliptical in formation.

A first conclusion is that the SED of Lockman850.1 is well described by an ultraluminous (L _ IR 8 10^ 12 L _ for H _0 =75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , q _0 =0.5) infrared galaxy at at redshift of around 3. The redshift of the source can be estimated on the basis of its mm-to-radio spectral index. Using the `mean' relation for the 350GHz/1.4GHz spectral index derived by Carilli Yun ( , see also http://www.aoc.noao.edu/ ccarilli/alphaz.shtml), the index of 0.90 observed for Lockman850.1 corresponds to a most probable z=2.7 .

gmw_00006860.name: Q1517+0055
gmw_00006860.overview: Q1517+0055 is a type-II quasar located at a redshift of z=2.65 [arXiv:2307.14224]. It is surrounded by a diffuse circumgalactic medium with extended Lyman-alpha, He II, and C IV emission, as well as associated absorption features that suggest metal-enriched gas recycling [arXiv:2307.14224].

sources.summary: The paper studies the type-II quasar Q1517+0055 with KCWI, detecting extended CGM emission and absorption whose kinematics and metallicity favor metal-enriched gas recycling or inflow.

quotes.quote: This paper presents Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI) integral field unit spectroscopic observations on a type-II quasar, Q1517+0055 at z=2.65 , a pilot study of our Ly nebulae sample at z 2 . We revealed diffuse emission of the Ly 1216 , \ 1640 , and \ 1549 on the projected physical scale of 122 kpc, 45 kpc, and 79 kpc, respectively. The total Ly luminosity is L_ Ly = 3.04 0.02 10^ 44 \ erg \ s^ 1 . The line ratio diagnostics shows that HeII/Ly 0.08 and CIV/Ly 0.28 , consistent with the photoionization including recombination and photon punmping. We also identify the associated and \ absorption from the spectra.

We find that the velocity profile from both the absorption and the \ emission exhibit increasing trends. Moreover, both the line ratio diagnostic from the emission and the column density ratio from the absorption confirm that the cool gas metallicity is Z_ . From detailed modeling and estimation, gas recycling might be a more plausible interpretation compared with the scenario of a powerful outflow.

gmw_00006861.name: PG 0052 + 251
gmw_00006861.overview: PG 0052 + 251 is a disc-dominated galaxy that hosts a quasar [arXiv:1210.0566]. The galaxy features extended low-surface-brightness structures of [O III] emission, with gas velocity fields that indicate large-scale rotational motion [arXiv:1210.0566].

sources.summary: The paper finds deeper PMAS [O III] emission around PG 0052 + 251, notes missed low-surface-brightness structure, and interprets its gas as partly rotational disc motion.

quotes.quote: The HST images of PG 0052 + 251 and Mrk 1014 obtained with the WFC are deeper so that high surface brightness structures of extended [ O iii ] \ emission could be detected. Nevertheless, our ground-bases PMAS narrow-band images reach a lot deeper, in particular in the case of Mrk 1014 where diffuse emission is present between the high surface brightness structures. Similar IFU observation of Mrk 1014 obtained by Fu:2008 display the same [ O iii ] \ light distribution as our PMAS observation suggesting that the structures are real. The low surface brightness regions south-east of PG 0052 + 251 was missed by Bennert:2002 and became visible only after smoothing the HST data. The maximum [ O iii ] \ size was thus underestimated by a factor of 2 in these two cases.

EELRs dominated by rotational motion We find signatures of large scale rotational motion around I Zw 1, PG 0052 + 251, PKS 1545 + 21, PG 1612 + 261, HE 2158 0107 and HE 2353 0420. The corresponding gas velocity fields are shown in Fig. fig_pmas:EELR_kin_rot . Because the turn-over point in the velocity curve is not reached in most cases, we modelled the synthetic longslit curves with a straight line ( v_r = v_r +v_0 ) to search for the major kinematic axis with the steepest velocity gradient v_r among all 90 < PA <90 in steps of 3 . The velocity curves along this kinematic major axis are also shown in Fig. fig_pmas:EELR_kin_rot and the corresponding parameters are given in Table tab_pmas:kin_axis_rot . The majority of these systems (4/6) are morphological disc-dominated galaxies for which the kinematic major axis is well aligned with the photometric major axis within 14 (rms).

gmw_00006862.name: SDSS J1131+2632
gmw_00006862.overview: SDSS J1131+2632 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy undergoing an ongoing major merger [arXiv:1210.0566]. The system features an elongated extended emission-line region and a unique, offset ionized-gas shell or arc located southeast of the nucleus [arXiv:1210.0566].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1131+2632 as an ongoing major merger with an elongated EELR and a distinctive offset ionized-gas shell or arc.

quotes.quote: The EELR of SDSS J1131+2632 is very elongated, and we can only study the kinematics in a part of the galaxy similar to Mrk 1014. The most striking feature is a giant shell or arc of ionised gas, 4 \ South-East of the nucleus, offset by 352 33 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity in the rest-frame. This is a unique feature among all the QSO in our sample and does not correspond to any continuum substructure in the broad-band image of the complex merging system.

gmw_00006863.name: DESI 39627939041510701
gmw_00006863.overview: DESI 39627939041510701 is a previously unknown Bright Galaxy Survey outlier [arXiv:2307.07664]. It is identified as a dusty broad-absorption-line quasar at a redshift of z=1.535, displaying complex and broad absorption features [arXiv:2307.07664].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DESI 39627939041510701 as a previously unknown BGS outlier likely to be a dusty broad-absorption-line quasar at z=1.535.

quotes.quote: Also previously unknown, outlier 115 DESI 39627939041510701 (last row in fig:outliers ) has complex and broad absorption features. With the Redrock redshift estimate of z=0.0698 , the H region would be highly unusual. However, we believe that this source is in fact a Broad Absorption Line BAL; Hall2002 quasar at z=1.535 , showing MgII 2800 , CIII ] 1909 , and CIV 1549 absorption that removes almost all flux directly blueward of the nominal line centers. If true, it demonstrates the ability to search for these highly unusual quasars in DESI BGS at fainter luminosities than were accessible in SDSS.

gmw_00006864.name: CDG-1
gmw_00006864.overview: CDG-1 is a candidate dark galaxy located in the Perseus Cluster [arXiv:2204.05487]. It consists of four densely clumped globular cluster candidates but lacks any detectable diffuse stellar light [arXiv:2204.05487].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CDG-1 as a possible dark galaxy: four tightly clumped GC candidates without diffuse stellar light, unlikely to be random or tied to nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: Detection of CDG-1 In addition to detecting known UDGs, the most interesting object we have detected is CDG-1 -- Candidate Dark Galaxy-1 . CDG-1 consists of four densely clumped observable GC candidates but has no detectable diffuse stellar content at that location. Since we are not yet certain of the nature of CDG-1 , we will not classify it as a potential UDG. CDG-1 is in the V8-ACS pointing, and it is the only over-density that stands out.

Using our method, we have detected a potential ``dark" galaxy in the Perseus cluster --- CDG-1 in our catalog --- which is not visible with the traditional detection method through diffuse light. Examination of the spatial features of its constituent GCs showed it is unlikely a random alignment of intracluster GCs in Perseus.

gmw_00006865.name: WUDG-89
gmw_00006865.overview: WUDG-89 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Perseus Cluster [arXiv:2204.05487]. It is situated extremely close to the giant early-type elliptical galaxy UGC 02673, lying at a projected distance of approximately 40 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2204.05487].

sources.summary: The paper detects WUDG-89 despite contamination, noting its closeness to UGC 02673, weak detection signal relative to similar UDGs, and larger prediction error.

quotes.quote: This is especially clear with WUDG-89, which is extremely close ( 40 kpc) to an elliptical galaxy (UGC 02673). This proximity causes the LGCP model to be uncertain about whether the GC over-density belongs to the elliptical or is a system of its own. Moreover, there is a larger UDG (WUDG-88) with a stronger signal in the same pointing, whose presence also negatively affects the detection probability of WUDG-89: its signal is the weakest amongst UDGs with similar N_ GC and R_ GC . Nevertheless, we are still able to pick out the signal from WUDG-89.

The larger prediction error for this UDG is somewhat expected since as mentioned, V11-ACS is a severely contaminated pointing and WUDG-89 is extremely close to the boundary of the giant ETG UGC 02673.

gmw_00006866.name: IC 312
gmw_00006866.overview: IC 312 is a lenticular galaxy located in the Perseus Cluster [arXiv:2204.05487]. It has an absolute magnitude of M_B = -20.32 and a half-light radius of 2 kpc [arXiv:2204.05487].

sources.summary: The paper models IC 312 as a nearby lenticular contaminant and argues its globular-cluster system is statistically unlikely to produce the CDG-1 clump.

quotes.quote: A similar but lower-amplitude issue is that a smaller ETG, the lenticular IC 312, lies just off the lower left corner of V8-ACS and generates a contaminating signal of its own. Due to its closeness to CDG-1 , IC 312 is included as a covariate effect in our model.

For IC 312, CDG-1 has a projected distance of 26 kpc to its center (assuming distance to IC 312 is 75 Mpc) while IC 312 has a half-light radius of 2 kpc. If we assume the ``five times" scale ratio between the half-number radius of the GC system and the half-light radius of IC 312, the half-number radius of its GC system is then 10 kpc. From the Hyperleda catalog, the total absolute magnitude of IC 312 is M_B = -20.32 or M_V 21.22 . For a specific frequency S_N = 2 typical for galaxies in that range Harris+2013 , the estimated total GC population of IC 312 would be N_ GC 600 .

gmw_00006867.name: QSO B1312+7837
gmw_00006867.overview: QSO B1312+7837 is a quasar at a redshift of z = 2.0 that displays typical broad emission lines [arXiv:2109.13817]. Long-term monitoring reveals luminosity changes with a period of approximately 6.13 years superimposed on a gradual dimming trend, which may be driven by the orbital motion of two supermassive black holes [arXiv:2109.13817].

sources.summary: The paper reports 15-year variability of QSO B1312+7837, finding periodic luminosity changes and considering orbital motion of two SMBHs as a possible explanation.

quotes.quote: We report here the first results from a 15-yr long variability monitoring of the z=2.0 quasar QSO B1312+7837. It shows luminosity changes with a period P 6.13 yr (P 2.04 yr at rest frame) and an amplitude of 0.2 mag, superimposed on a gradual dimming at a rate of 0.55 mag per 100 yrs. Two false periods associated with power peaks in the data windowing function were discarded. The measured period is confirmed with a bootstrapping Monte-Carlo simulation.

A damped random walk model yields a better fit to the data than a sine-function model, but at the cost of employing some high frequency variations which are typically not seen in quasars. We consider the possible mechanisms driving this variability, and conclude that orbital motion of two supermassive black holes -- result from a recent galaxy merger -- is a possible explanation.

Here we report the results of a long-term variability monitoring of the quasar QSO B1312+7837 [ VV2006 ] J131321.4+782153 , WISEA J131321.33+782153.8, Gaia DR2 1716672593984035072; 2006A&A...455..773V at z =2.0 1999A&AS..134..483H . It shows the typical broad emission lines 2004AJ....128.1058T .

gmw_00006868.name: F415-3
gmw_00006868.overview: F415-3 is a nearby low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1506.03395, arXiv:1811.10579]. It hosts twenty-three stellar associations linked to distinct HII regions [arXiv:1506.03395], but displays a deficit of asymptotic giant branch stars due to its low past star formation rate [arXiv:1811.10579].

sources.summary: The paper resolves F415-3 into many stellar sources and associations, finding LSB-dwarf CMD features, active clustered star formation, and a large central HII complex.

quotes.quote: Overall 1869, 465 and 501 stellar sources are identified in the three LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1. The spatial distribution of young blue stars matches the H maps from ground-based imaging, indicating that star formation in LSB galaxies follows the same style as in other irregular galaxies. Several star complexes are identified, matching regions of higher surface brightness as seen from ground-based imaging. The color-magnitude diagrams for each LSB galaxy has the similar morphology to Local Volume (LV) dwarf galaxies, i.e. a blue main sequence, blue and red He burning branches and asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars.

F415-3 is our largest galaxy in sample with the most number of detected stellar sources. Twenty-three associations were identified by visual inspection, all associated with a distinct HII region. The irregular morphology of LSB galaxies is often driven by the presence of one or two knots, now identified with a single cluster or group of clusters.

sources.summary: The paper cites HST CMDs of F415-3 as a nearby LSB dwarf with few AGB stars, tying the deficit to low past star formation and suppressed AGB contributions.

quotes.quote: Not due to changes in SED models, but rather the recent HST observations on three, nearby LSB dwarfs (Schombert McGaugh 2015). The F555W-F814W CMD's for LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1 display a dramatic lack of AGB stars (only 5 to 10 compared to 20 for other metal-poor, high SFR dwarfs). A lack of AGB stars was doubly surprising as the metallicities of these LSB dwarfs ( [ Fe/H 0.8 ) was in the realm where AGB stars have a stronger contribution than for higher metallicity dwarfs. It appears that the extremely low past SFR for LSB galaxies (log SFR < -3 ) combined with the short lifetimes for the AGB population under produces, by a significant fraction, the expected numbers from simple SFH scenarios.

gmw_00006869.name: F608-1
gmw_00006869.overview: F608-1 is a nearby low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that contains several star complexes, most of which are associated with hydrogen emission [arXiv:1506.03395, arXiv:1811.10579]. The galaxy displays a lack of asymptotic giant branch stars, which is attributed to its extremely low past star formation rate [arXiv:1811.10579].

sources.summary: The paper treats F608-1 as a nearby small LSB dwarf with low baryon mass, resolved stellar sources, multiple associations, and CMD morphology like Local Volume dwarfs.

quotes.quote: Overall 1869, 465 and 501 stellar sources are identified in the three LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1. The spatial distribution of young blue stars matches the H maps from ground-based imaging, indicating that star formation in LSB galaxies follows the same style as in other irregular galaxies. Several star complexes are identified, matching regions of higher surface brightness as seen from ground-based imaging. The color-magnitude diagrams for each LSB galaxy has the similar morphology to Local Volume (LV) dwarf galaxies, i.e. a blue main sequence, blue and red He burning branches and asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars.
F608-1 is also UGC159, where the original coordinates were misprinted on POSS SAO overlays resulting in separate designations for several years.

F608-1 also displays a number of distinct associations (see Figure F608-1_clusters , ranging from 10^4 to a few times 10^5 M_ . Of the thirteen identified groupings, only two are not associated with H emission.

sources.summary: The paper cites HST CMDs of F608-1 as a nearby LSB dwarf with few AGB stars, tying the deficit to low past star formation and suppressed AGB contributions.

quotes.quote: Not due to changes in SED models, but rather the recent HST observations on three, nearby LSB dwarfs (Schombert McGaugh 2015). The F555W-F814W CMD's for LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1 display a dramatic lack of AGB stars (only 5 to 10 compared to 20 for other metal-poor, high SFR dwarfs). A lack of AGB stars was doubly surprising as the metallicities of these LSB dwarfs ( [ Fe/H 0.8 ) was in the realm where AGB stars have a stronger contribution than for higher metallicity dwarfs. It appears that the extremely low past SFR for LSB galaxies (log SFR < -3 ) combined with the short lifetimes for the AGB population under produces, by a significant fraction, the expected numbers from simple SFH scenarios.

gmw_00006870.name: F750-V1
gmw_00006870.overview: F750-V1 is a small, nearby low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1306.4278, arXiv:1506.03395]. It features a few faint H II regions alongside diffuse H-alpha emission [arXiv:1306.4278, arXiv:1506.03395], and its stellar population displays a deficit of asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1811.10579].

sources.summary: The paper describes F750-V1 as the smallest studied LSB dwarf, with low baryon mass, resolved stellar sources, five faint HII regions, and Local-Volume-like CMD morphology.

quotes.quote: Overall 1869, 465 and 501 stellar sources are identified in the three LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1. The spatial distribution of young blue stars matches the H maps from ground-based imaging, indicating that star formation in LSB galaxies follows the same style as in other irregular galaxies. Several star complexes are identified, matching regions of higher surface brightness as seen from ground-based imaging. The color-magnitude diagrams for each LSB galaxy has the similar morphology to Local Volume (LV) dwarf galaxies, i.e. a blue main sequence, blue and red He burning branches and asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars.

Our smallest galaxy, F750-V1 (see Figure F750_clusters ), has five distinct HII regions but with H luminosities near log L_ H = 36.0, i.e. the flux expected from one OB star. Cluster E is a distinct cluster of a handful of blue stars, but the HII regions to the north are a widely dispersed, over an area of 500 pc, with only a few OB stars. Clusters A and B are powered by a single O star.

sources.summary: The paper describes F750-V1 as a small nearby LSB galaxy where seven HII regions were selected, most H emission is diffuse, and blue colors are enhanced in diffuse regions.

quotes.quote: Galaxy F750-V1 (lower right) is a smaller, nearby LSB galaxy. While seven HII regions were selected, most of its H emission is diffuse. It is a subjective determination to select any knot in the core region. There is no signature from the HII regions in the continuum images; however, there is enhanced blue stellar colors in the diffuse regions.

sources.summary: The paper cites HST CMDs of F750-V1 as a nearby LSB dwarf with few AGB stars, tying the deficit to low past star formation and suppressed AGB contributions.

quotes.quote: Not due to changes in SED models, but rather the recent HST observations on three, nearby LSB dwarfs (Schombert McGaugh 2015). The F555W-F814W CMD's for LSB galaxies F415-3, F608-1 and F750-V1 display a dramatic lack of AGB stars (only 5 to 10 compared to 20 for other metal-poor, high SFR dwarfs). A lack of AGB stars was doubly surprising as the metallicities of these LSB dwarfs ( [ Fe/H 0.8 ) was in the realm where AGB stars have a stronger contribution than for higher metallicity dwarfs. It appears that the extremely low past SFR for LSB galaxies (log SFR < -3 ) combined with the short lifetimes for the AGB population under produces, by a significant fraction, the expected numbers from simple SFH scenarios.

gmw_00006871.name: LGS3
gmw_00006871.overview: LGS3 is a very faint member of the Local Group [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489]. It is suspected of being a transition-type galaxy intermediate between a dwarf irregular and a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489].

sources.summary: The paper treats LGS3 as a faint Local Group transition-type galaxy with a metal-poor RGB and a measured TRGB distance.

quotes.quote: LGS3 (Pisces Dwarf) LGS3 ( 1 h  3 m  52.9 s , +21^  53'  05'' ) is a very faint member of the Local Group, originally discovered by (see also kowal1978,thuan1979 ). conducted a study of the stellar populations; an RGB is evident and its colour implies a metallicity of Fe/H = -2.10 0.22 . A small number of bright AGB stars and several blue stars are also observed. Along with the Phoenix dwarf galaxy, LGS3 is suspected of being a transition-type galaxy, somewhere between a dwarf irregular and a dwarf spheroidal galaxy ( cook1989,vanderydt1991 ).

gmw_00006872.name: Andromeda VII
gmw_00006872.overview: Andromeda VII, also known as the Cassiopeia dwarf spheroidal, is a satellite galaxy belonging to the M31 subgroup [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489]. It resembles a typical dwarf spheroidal galaxy, showing little to no evidence of young or intermediate stellar populations [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Andromeda VII as a recent M31 subgroup dwarf spheroidal, notes its metallicity, and measures its TRGB distance.

quotes.quote: Andromeda V, Andromeda VI (Pegasus dSph) and Andromeda VII (Cassiopeia dSph) Andromeda V ( 1 h  10 m  17.1 s , +47^  37'  41'' ), VI ( 23 h  51 m  46.3 s , +24^  34'  57'' ), VII ( 23 h  26 m  31 s , +50^  41'  31'' ) were, prior to the discovery of And IX, the three newest additions to the M31 subgroup. And V and VI were discovered by after they had performed an analysis on the disgitised version of the second Palomar Sky Survey (POSS-II; reid1991,reid1993,lasker1993 ). At the same time, independently discovered And V (which they named the Pegasus dSph) as well as yet another companion, the Cassiopeia dSph (also named And  VII) from their analysis of the POSS-II plates. All of these satellites appear to resemble typical dwarf spheroidals, with little or no evidence for young or intermediate stellar populations ( armandroff1998,armandroff1999,grebel1999a,hopp1999,davidge2002 ).

gmw_00006873.name: Andromeda IX
gmw_00006873.overview: Andromeda IX is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that is a companion to M31 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489]. Discovered during an inspection of Sloan Digital Sky Survey data, it is a faint system with a very low surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0410489].

sources.summary: The paper describes Andromeda IX as a newly discovered, very faint M31 dwarf spheroidal companion and derives its TRGB distance.

quotes.quote: Andromeda IX Andromeda IX ( 0 h  52 m  52.8 s , +43^  12'  0'' ) was only very recently discovered by during inspection of an SDSS scan of the environs of M31. It is the faintest galaxy so far discovered, with a surface brightness of _V 26.8 mags arcsec ^ 2 . and appears to be very similar in morphological properties to the other dwarf spheroidal companions of M31. Due to its recent discovery, detailed information about its stellar content has not yet been obtained.

gmw_00006874.name: NGC 0036
gmw_00006874.overview: NGC 0036 is a barred disk galaxy [arXiv:1710.10320, arXiv:2006.02307] that features a central bulge [arXiv:2006.02307] and a two-armed logarithmic spiral structure [arXiv:1710.10320]. The galaxy contains an ultrafast bar [arXiv:1501.05498] and exhibits a small enhancement of oxygen abundance within its spiral arms [arXiv:1710.10320].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 36's ionized-gas velocity, oxygen abundance, and extinction with CALIFA data, derives spiral-arm geometry, finds a small arm abundance enhancement, and measures kinematic parameters.

quotes.quote: We derive maps of the observed velocity of ionized gas, the oxygen abundance, and the extinction (Balmer decrement) across the area of the four spiral galaxies NGC 36, NGC 180, NGC 6063, and NGC 7653 from integral field spectroscopy obtained by the Calar Alto Legacy Integral Field Area (CALIFA) survey. We searched for spiral arms through Fourier analysis of the spatial distribution of three tracers (non-circular motion, enhancement of the oxygen abundance, and of the extinction) in the discs of our target galaxies. The spiral arms (two-armed logarithmic spirals in the deprojected map) are shown in each target galaxy for each tracer considered. The pitch angles of the spiral arms in a given galaxy obtained with the three different tracers are close to each other. The enhancement of the oxygen abundance in the spiral arms as compared to the abundance in the interarm regions at a given galactocentric distance is small; within a few per cent.

We identified a metallicity gradient in our target galaxies. Both barred galaxies in our sample show flatter gradients than the two galaxies without bars. Galactic inclination, position angle of the major axis, and the rotation curve were also obtained for each target galaxy using the Fourier analysis of the two-dimensional velocity map.

sources.summary: NGC 0036 is used to demonstrate the bulge-disk spectral extraction, with separate spaxel selections, bulge-region spectrum, normalized disk spectrum, and plausible residual bulge profiles.

quotes.quote: The l.h.s of Fig.  maps displays the spaxels that were considered in constructing the average spectrum of the disk for NGC 0036 .

We document values for the S ersic index within 0.3 2.3 (not reaching, in any circumstance, unfeasible values such as 0.2 > > 8) and an average absolute difference of 0.5 between the best-fitting for expD and flatD and 0.2 for expD and decrD (see Fig. disks for a visual representation of the three possible bulge luminosity profiles for NGC 0036 ). Regarding the obtained estimates for the B/T, we report values within 3 B/T 51 and an average increase of 15 between the B/T estimated after subtraction of expD and the same after subtraction of flatD and 25 when comparing expD with decrD . Visual inspection of the remaining excess and respective models suggests that all the obtained bulge luminosity profiles are physically reasonable, demonstrating that experiments involving surface photometry do not yield strong discriminators of deviations of the disk from the exponentiality.

The l.h.s of Fig.  maps illustrates the spaxels that were used to construct the two spectra in the case of NGC 0036 . The total spectrum within the bulge region , (i.e., bulge plus a possible disk contribution, displayed in black at the .h.s of Fig.  maps , with its best-fitting stellar SED , overplotted in red) was created by summing up the spaxels residing within the red contour, i.e., all the spaxels that pertain to the zones within . The normalized disk spectrum , (plotted in the .h.s of the same Fig. in black, with its best-fitting stellar SED , overplotted in dark-blue) was constructed by summing up the spaxels that occupy the locus between the two blue contours (i.e., between one zone after the last one pertaining in the bulge and zone 14 We decided to exclude the last 4 zones from the analysis due to the decreasing surface brightness , and consequently decreasing signal-to-noise ratio, of the outermost spaxels.

sources.summary: The CALIFA study flags NGC0036 as an ultrafast-bar case; using its H I rotation velocity would raise corotation estimates and make only two measurements ultrafast.

quotes.quote: We have found the maximum circular velocity measured from HI data for one of the ultrafast bars NGC0036 theureau1998 . For this galaxy, we have V_ ,HI /V_ ,flat =1.14 . This means that if assuming V_ ,HI the corotation radius and R would be 14 greater than the values given in Tables tb3 and tb4 . Thus, if using V_ ,HI we would have R _1=1.0 , R _ 2 =0.7 , R _ 3 =0.7 , and R _ 4 =0.7 .

Assuming the same errors as given in Table tb4 , this galaxy would only be ultrafast for two measurements. This means that the reason that this galaxy is ultrafast could be related to uncertainties associated to the corotation radius determination.

gmw_00006875.name: IC 2604
gmw_00006875.overview: IC 2604 is a late-type galaxy [arXiv:2006.02307]. Photometric modeling of its central region indicates that the galaxy's disk profile must flatten or exhibit a central depression rather than continuing as a pure exponential or flat profile inward to the galactic center [arXiv:2006.02307].

sources.summary: For IC 2604, exponential or flat inward disk extrapolations produce negative blue flux and unphysical stellar fits, supporting a flattened or centrally depressed disk profile.

quotes.quote: Figure  disk_sub_im displays the results obtained for the LTG IC 2604 . Extrapolation of a pure exponential or flat profile for the disk to the galactic center yields negative flux for 4000  , implying that the disk profile has to flatten or show a central depression within . While assuming exponential and flat disk models, SLZ4's stellar age and metallicity estimates reached the maximum allowed value, which is per se an indication of an unphysical fit. Additionally, comparison of the resulting mass estimates for the bulge after subtraction of the exponential and flat models, ( M _ , eD ) and ( M _ , fD ) , respectively, with the same prior of subtraction ( M _ , B ) , indicates an increase of the stellar mass after disk removal, once more pointing to the invalidity of the assumed profiles for the disk (see next Sect.). For this galaxy, most of these criteria (used to classify the resulting net-bulge SED as unphysical) were met for all the four spectral modeling runs.

gmw_00006876.name: MMJ 045421.55
gmw_00006876.overview: MMJ 045421.55 is a galaxy identified through its radio and infrared emission [arXiv:0910.1595]. The system is located near two optical galaxies that are potential cluster members and may be in the early stages of a merger [arXiv:0910.1595].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MMJ 045421.55 through radio and IRAC emission, discusses possible interacting optical counterparts, and considers the system a possible cluster-member merger.

quotes.quote: MMJ 045421.55 is identified through radio emission 2.2 arcsec from the AzTEC centroid, which lies between two optical galaxies (2.1 and 1.6 arcsec from the northern (C1) and southern (C2) galaxies respectively). There is also an IRAC source at the location of the radio emission, which appears slightly extended towards C2 (Fig. fig:cutouts ). The three possible explanations for this system are: C1 and C2 are interacting and both millimetre bright; C2 is the millimetre counterpart; or neither C1 nor C2 are responsible for the millimetre emission. Each of these possibilities is discussed below. Individual photometric analysis of C1 and C2 (excluding the IRAC information) suggests that they are both potential cluster members, and with a separation of 2.6 arcsec - corresponding to 17 kpc at z=0.54 - they could be in the early stages of a merger.

gmw_00006877.name: MMJ 045431.35
gmw_00006877.overview: MMJ 045431.35 is a system of merging galaxies with a separation of 1.6 arcseconds and visible tidal tails [arXiv:0910.1595]. This interaction is suggested to have triggered a dusty starburst that drives the system's millimetre emission [arXiv:0910.1595].

sources.summary: The paper securely identifies MMJ 045431.35 with a radio counterpart, resolves it into merging galaxies with tidal tails, and interprets its millimetre emission as an interaction-triggered dusty starburst.

quotes.quote: The counterpart to MMJ 045431.35 is securely identified 5.4 arcsec from the AzTEC centroid through its radio emission. The source is detected in all our \ and ground-based imaging and resolved in the \ F814W image into merging galaxies with centroids separated by 1.6 arcsec ( 10 kpc at z=0.54 ) and tidal tails between them (Fig. fig:cutouts ). We suggest that a dusty starburst triggered by the interaction between the two galaxies is causing the millimetre emission from this system. The photometric redshift is calculated as z=0.60 0.11 , making MMJ 045431.35 a possible cluster member. If MMJ 045421.55 and MMJ 045431.35 are cluster members at z=0.54 SED-fitting suggests they each have L_ FIR 5 10^ 11 L and thus SFR 50 .

gmw_00006878.name: UGC 6284
gmw_00006878.overview: UGC 6284 is classified as a low-surface-brightness galaxy with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1510.07743]. It exhibits ionized gas outflows, which are indicated by blue-shifted components in both its hydrogen and forbidden oxygen emission lines [arXiv:1510.07743].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 6284 as a LINER in the low-surface-brightness AGN sample and reports blue-shifted H and [O iii] outflow components.

quotes.quote: The three galaxies in our sample, UGC 6614 (identification no 24), UGC 6284 (identification no 28) and UGC 5035 (identification no 30), which are classified to be LINERs show blue-shifted component in H emission lines. For UGC 6614, along with the blue shifted outflow component, there is a broad H component which is associated with the BH accretion. The blue shifted H component in these galaxies suggest the presence of an ionised gas outflow. The forbidden [ O iii ] emission lines are ideal tracers of extended ionized outflowing gas which cannot be produced by the high density broad line regions cresci2014 . Thus the asymmetric extended [ O iii ] line profiles are considered as powerful signature of the presence of outflowing gas.
UGC 6614 and UGC 6284 show the presence of outflow components in [ O iii ] emission line region along with H region. This suggests that outflow signatures in these galaxies are real.

gmw_00006879.name: HAE229
gmw_00006879.overview: HAE229 is a star-forming galaxy located within the Spiderweb protocluster [arXiv:2310.05206]. It features an extended rotating molecular gas disk and a super-galactic cold molecular gas reservoir spanning tens of kiloparsecs [arXiv:2310.05206].

sources.summary: The paper treats HAE229 as a star-forming Spiderweb-protocluster galaxy with an extended rotating molecular gas disk and a super-galactic gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: Through observations with the Australian Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) of the ground transition of carbon monoxide (CO(1-0)) as tracer, large cold molecular reservoirs have been revealed being physically related to the starbursting (proto-)BCG (Spiderweb Galaxy) and the star-forming galaxy HAE229 Emonts_2016Sci...354.1128E, Dannerbauer_2017AA...608A..48D . Within the same protocluster, the ATCA detection of an extended ( 40 kpc) rotating molecular gas disk from a normal star-forming galaxy, HAE229, was reported Dannerbauer_2017AA...608A..48D . However, the large reservoirs of molecular gas found in the normal star-forming galaxy HAE229 Dannerbauer_2017AA...608A..48D indicate that it might not be restricted only to the central galaxy.

In this paper, the ``extended/large molecular gas reservoirs'' refers to the gas reservoirs with super-galactic scale of tens of kpc ( 40 kpc; 40 kpc corresponds to the typical beam-size of the ATCA mosaic data we used in this paper) just like those in the Spiderweb galaxy and HAE229. Following two extended molecular gas reservoirs reported in the literature, the Spiderweb galaxy (COALAS-SW.02 in Jin_2021AA...652A..11J ) in Emonts_2013MNRAS.430.3465E, Emonts_2016Sci...354.1128E, Emonts_2018MNRAS.477L..60E and HAE229 (COALAS-SW.01 in Jin_2021AA...652A..11J ) in Dannerbauer_2017AA...608A..48D , COALAS-SW.03/DKB03 is the third large molecular gas reservoir confirmed with high-resolution observations within the Spiderweb protocluster (see Section sec:DKB03 ).

gmw_00006880.name: COALAS-SW.03
gmw_00006880.overview: COALAS-SW.03 is a galaxy located within the Spiderweb protocluster [arXiv:2310.05206]. High-resolution observations have confirmed that it hosts a large, extended molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2310.05206].

sources.summary: The paper presents COALAS-SW.03/DKB03 as a Spiderweb-protocluster CO detection with a confirmed extended molecular reservoir mapped using high-resolution ATCA observations.

quotes.quote: Section sec:DKB03 presents an analysis of the characteristics of a third confirmed large gas reservoir in the Spiderweb protocluster, COALAS-SW.03, utilising higher resolution ATCA observations with an angular resolution of approximately 2-3 arcseconds, equivalent to a physical scale of approximately 17-25 kpc. We compare the morphology and kinematics of COALAS-SW.03 in both the high-resolution and mosaic datasets, where the latter has an angular resolution of approximately 4-5 arcseconds at the location of COALAS-SW.03, equivalent to a physical scale of 34-42 kpc. COALAS-SW.03 (dubbed DKB03 in Dannerbauer_2014AA...570A..55D ) is one of the CO detections found in Jin_2021AA...652A..11J .

Following two extended molecular gas reservoirs reported in the literature, the Spiderweb galaxy (COALAS-SW.02 in Jin_2021AA...652A..11J ) in Emonts_2013MNRAS.430.3465E, Emonts_2016Sci...354.1128E, Emonts_2018MNRAS.477L..60E and HAE229 (COALAS-SW.01 in Jin_2021AA...652A..11J ) in Dannerbauer_2017AA...608A..48D , COALAS-SW.03/DKB03 is the third large molecular gas reservoir confirmed with high-resolution observations within the Spiderweb protocluster (see Section sec:DKB03 ). Our high-resolution CO(1-0) observations of COALAS-SW.03 (ID: 2017APRS/C3003, PI: B. Emonts) were performed with the ATCA during June 2nd to 6th 2017 in the 750m configuration (57.5 h in total). The pointing centre of the observations is on DKB03 (RA: 11:40:57.8; DEC: -26:30:48).

gmw_00006881.name: RG J123711
gmw_00006881.overview: RG J123711 is a hot-dust ultraluminous infrared galaxy containing both active galactic nucleus and star-forming components [arXiv:0906.5346]. The system is a merger where an extended star-forming region to the northeast is interacting with a compact, AGN-dominated galaxy to the southwest [arXiv:0906.5346].

sources.summary: The paper describes RG J123711 as a hot-dust ULIRG with AGN and star-forming components, separated radio/CO morphology, and adjusted star-forming luminosity estimates.

quotes.quote: The two hot-dust ULIRGs RG J123711 and RG J123649 which have the highest X-ray luminosities and mid-IR luminosities of the sample might contain powerful AGN despite a lack of rest-UV AGN spectral features. The MERLIN radio emission in RG J123711 (bottom left panel in Fig.  fig:MERLIN ) is separated in two components: an extended region to the NE (63 percent of the integrated flux), and a bright compact region to the SW (37 percent). The X-ray source coincides with this SW component. In contrast, a large 10 ^ 10 M _ molecular gas reservoir (a strong case that substantial star formation may be present) has been detected in CO and is morphologically separated evenly across the two NE and SW components Casey et al. 2009, in prep chapman08a . For these reasons, we believe the NE region is likely a star forming ULIRG merging with the SW region, an AGN-dominated galaxy.

We adjust the radio luminosity and inferred SFRs of both RG J123711 and RG J123649 to solely represent the estimated star forming components (see Table 1, ``RG J... SF component:''). We have also labeled both galaxies as AGN in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 to clarify that they differ from the other six.

gmw_00006882.name: RG J123649
gmw_00006882.overview: RG J123649 is a hot-dust ultraluminous infrared galaxy that likely hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0906.5346]. While its optical spectrum shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity, its active galactic nucleus emission is estimated to contribute approximately 40 percent of its radio flux [arXiv:0906.5346].

sources.summary: The paper treats RG J123649 as a hot-dust ULIRG likely hosting an AGN, estimates its AGN/star-forming radio fractions, and excludes it from star-formation-dominated aggregate analysis.

quotes.quote: While the existence of an AGN in RG J123649 is supported by a bright X-ray luminosity and a compact core in optical imaging, there is no sign of AGN activity in the optical spectrum or in its 8 kpc extended radio morphology. We estimate that AGN emission is 40 30 percent and the star forming component is 60 30 percent of the radio flux by deconvolving an unresolved 0.3 \ PSF from its radio morphology. We adjust the radio luminosity and inferred SFRs of both RG J123711 and RG J123649 to solely represent the estimated star forming components (see Table 1, ``RG J... SF component:''). We have also labeled both galaxies as AGN in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 to clarify that they differ from the other six.

It is possible that these two galaxies' 70 \ flux densities are dominated by emission from torus dust surrounding the AGN (which can have very hot dust temperatures, T_ D 1000 K). However, disentangling the relative contributions of AGN and star formation to 70 \ flux density is beyond the scope of this study since it requires detailed knowledge of the FIR SEDs. For this reason, we exclude RG J123711 and RG J123649 from further analysis of the hot dust ULIRG aggregate properties, since we wish to characterize the properties of star formation dominated ULIRGs exclusively.

gmw_00006883.name: Mrk 108
gmw_00006883.overview: Mrk 108 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located within the Holmberg 124 group [arXiv:1012.4540]. It has experienced tidal interactions with its neighboring spiral galaxies [arXiv:1012.4540, arXiv:astro-ph/0502036], which likely triggered nearby star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0502036].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 108 as a Holmberg 124 group BCD with distinct gas kinematics, emission blended toward NGC 2820, and signs of tidal interaction from neighboring spirals.

quotes.quote: Mrk 108 : This galaxy is in a group, namely Holmberg 124 and group interaction is clearly noticeable (refer Figure fig:mrk108_r (a)).

The velocity field of Mrk 108 is distinct from its large neighbour NGC 2820 (Figure fig:mrk108_r ()). The line profile of Mrk 108 is best fit with two components centered at 1406 and 1446 \ with widths 52 and 117 . The component with centre velocity at 1446 \ is contributed by NGC 2820. The emission from Mrk 108 is continuous to that from NGC 2820; hence we note that the mass from single dish observation by is overestimated. The M( )/L_K ratio is 0.41 and M (dyn) /L_K is 2.86 adopting ( V^2R/G ) and the width of line is around 52 .
Mrk 108 has experienced tidal interaction due to the large neighbouring spirals.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 108 as a Ho 124 triplet member involved in tidal interaction, with distinct HI emission and nearby star formation likely triggered by the interaction.

quotes.quote: We report radio continuum and HI observations using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) of the group Holmberg 124 (Ho 124) comprising four late-type galaxies, namely NGC 2820, Mrk 108, NGC 2814 and NGC 2805. The three galaxies, NGC 2820, Mrk 108 and NGC 2814 which are closely located in the sky plane have clearly undergone tidal interactions as seen from the various morphological tidal signatures and debris.
HI condensation is also seen near Mrk 108 and it is likely associated with a star forming region seen in the H image (see Fig fig7 ()), possibly triggered by the interaction.
The HI emission associated with Mrk 108 is clearly detected at 1417 km s ^ 1 and it encloses a HI mass of 6.1 10^7 M _ .

gmw_00006884.name: Mrk 1039
gmw_00006884.overview: Mrk 1039 is a galaxy featuring a warped gas disk that is approximately 1.1 times the optical size of its stellar disk [arXiv:1012.4540]. Its rotating velocity field exhibits distortions in the outer regions, while its velocity dispersion increases from 15 km/s at the edges to 45 km/s in the center [arXiv:1012.4540].

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 1039 has a warped gas disk, a rotating velocity field with outer distortions, high central velocity dispersion, and an H I disk slightly larger than its optical size.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1039 : The intensity and kinematics are shown in Figure fig:mrk1039_r (a),(b). A warped gas disk is visible in Figure fig:mrk1039_r (a) whereas the stellar disk does not show any signature of the warp. The velocity field (Figure fig:mrk1039_r (b)) shows the rotating gas disk with small distortions in the outer parts especially in the western half.

The velocity dispersion of Mrk 1039 varies from 15 \ at the edges of the galaxy to 45 \ in the central regions. The mass is estimated to be 1.25 10^9 M_ (Table tab:obs_hi ) which is in good agreement with the mass estimated from single dish measurements. The disk is about 1.1 times the ( D_ 25 ) optical size of the galaxy.

gmw_00006885.name: Mrk 1069
gmw_00006885.overview: Mrk 1069 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy classified as an Sa-type galaxy [arXiv:1012.4540]. It features a large, rotating neutral hydrogen disk that is warped in the north and abruptly truncated in the south, with radio continuum emission concentrated in its central and northern regions [arXiv:1012.4540].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1069 as a BCD with a large warped H I disk, rotation with center offsets, and radio continuum emission concentrated in the central/northern galaxy.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1069 : The moment maps are shown in Figure fig:mrk1069_r (a),(b). This galaxy, classified as a Sa type by Hyperleda http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr pat03 shows a large disk (see Figure fig:mrk1069_r (a)) about 6 times (Table tab:obs_hi ) the optical size of the galaxy. The disk shows a slight warp in the northern parts. The southern part of the disk appears to be abruptly truncated. The galaxy shows rotation (Figure fig:mrk1069_r (b)).

The position-velocity curve (refer Figure fig:mrk1069_r (e)) along the major axis of the galaxy shows rotation. Radio continuum emission from Mrk 1069 is detected at 325 MHz, 610 MHz and 1420 MHz (Figure fig:mrk1069_r f--h respectively). The radio continuum emission arises in the central parts of the galaxy and extends to the north.

gmw_00006886.name: GHZ4
gmw_00006886.overview: GHZ4 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy identified in the GLASS-JWST field [arXiv:2212.06666]. It is compatible with a redshift range of z ~ 9 to 11 and is subject to moderate gravitational lensing magnification [arXiv:2212.06666].

sources.summary: The paper keeps GHZ4 as a GLASS-JWST z 9-11 candidate, finding new SEDs consistent with earlier data, reduced low-redshift ambiguity, moderate magnification, and possible z 9-11 placement.

quotes.quote: The two objects GHZ1 and GHZ4 from C22a fall in the region recently reobserved in parallel to NIRSpec. Reassuringly, their SEDs and photometric redshifts obtained from the new data are in excellent agreement with those measured in the first GLASS-JWST NIRCam observations, with the deeper data analyzed here reducing the likelihood of the secondary photometric redshift solution at z 2 for GHZ4. Candidate GHZ1 also falls in the area that we have removed from the UNCOVER field to avoid duplications. The availability of the lensing model extending to the GLASS-JWST region allows us to estimate that they are moderately magnified, such that the intrinsic M_ UV are 0.5 -0.6 mag fainter than the estimates reported in our previous paper.
In turn, GHZ4 and UHZ1 are compatible with both the z 9-10 and the z 10-11 redshift ranges.

gmw_00006887.name: GHZ7
gmw_00006887.overview: GHZ7 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a likely redshift between 10 and 11.5 [arXiv:2212.06666]. It is moderately magnified by gravitational lensing and is likely physically associated in a close pair with the nearby galaxy GHZ8 [arXiv:2212.06666].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GHZ7 as a new GLASS-JWST z 9-11 candidate, likely at z 10-11.5, moderately magnified, and probably physically associated with nearby GHZ8.

quotes.quote: The three newly found candidates in the newly observed GLASS-JWST area have observed m_ F444W 27 -27.3 mag and similar SEDs. Two of them (GHZ7 and GHZ8, see Fig.  fig:field ) have very similar P(z) and are separated by only 4'' in the sky, corresponding to a projected separation of 16 kpc at z 10 -- i.e. they are likely to be physically associated in a close pair.

The seven galaxies at z 10 listed in Table  tab:candidates have low-to-moderate magnifications, ranging from 1.2 to 4 (see Table  tab:candidates ), and are not multiply imaged (i.e., they lie outside the main caustics on the source plane at z 10 ).

In this respect, we note that GHZ9 has best-fit photometric redshift solutions at z 9.3-9.5 , compatible with the spectroscopic value measured for DHZ1, while objects GHZ1, GHZ7 and GHZ8 are more likely placed at 10 z 11.5 .

gmw_00006888.name: GHZ8
gmw_00006888.overview: GHZ8 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate likely located at a redshift between 10 and 11.5 [arXiv:2212.06666]. It is moderately magnified by gravitational lensing and is likely physically associated in a close pair with the nearby galaxy GHZ7 [arXiv:2212.06666].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GHZ8 as a new GLASS-JWST z 9-11 candidate, likely at z 10-11.5, moderately magnified, and probably physically associated with nearby GHZ7.

quotes.quote: The three newly found candidates in the newly observed GLASS-JWST area have observed m_ F444W 27 -27.3 mag and similar SEDs. Two of them (GHZ7 and GHZ8, see Fig.  fig:field ) have very similar P(z) and are separated by only 4'' in the sky, corresponding to a projected separation of 16 kpc at z 10 -- i.e. they are likely to be physically associated in a close pair.

The seven galaxies at z 10 listed in Table  tab:candidates have low-to-moderate magnifications, ranging from 1.2 to 4 (see Table  tab:candidates ), and are not multiply imaged (i.e., they lie outside the main caustics on the source plane at z 10 ).

In this respect, we note that GHZ9 has best-fit photometric redshift solutions at z 9.3-9.5 , compatible with the spectroscopic value measured for DHZ1, while objects GHZ1, GHZ7 and GHZ8 are more likely placed at 10 z 11.5 .

gmw_00006889.name: GHZ9
gmw_00006889.overview: GHZ9 is a gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy with a photometric redshift estimated between 9.3 and 10.37 [arXiv:2403.14745, arXiv:2212.06666]. It has low-to-moderate magnification [arXiv:2212.06666] and is co-spatial with a soft-band X-ray source that may indicate an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2403.14745].

sources.summary: The paper describes GHZ 9 as a lensed z~10 Lyman-break galaxy with uncertain SED-derived properties and a co-spatial soft-band X-ray source interpreted as a candidate AGN.

quotes.quote: GHZ 9 (also known as galaxy ID 52008 in Atek et al. 2023) is a Lyman-break galaxy with a dropout in the F115W NIRCam bandpass (Figure fig:ghz9_jwst ), which suggests its high-redshift nature. Its photometric redshift, stellar mass and star formation rate are obtained by spectral energy distribution (SED) modeling. The templates used in SED modeling do not include the contribution from AGN or broad emission lines; the former is highly uncertain even at low redshift and line emission can be very different at the high ionization rates inferred in these highly star-forming galaxies. With these caveats in mind, we note that the redshift of GHZ 9 ranges from 10.37^ +0.32 _ 1.09 ( atek23 , eazy code) through 9.47^ +0.36 _ 0.35 ( atek23 , beagle code) to as low as 9.35^ +0.77 _ 0.35 ( castellano22b , eazy code). Note that these redshifts are in agreement within 1 uncertainty.

An X-ray source co-spatial with GHZ 9 is detected in the 0.5-3 keV band but remains undetected in the 3-7 keV band. The centroid of the X-ray source is 0.24 from the center of GHZ 9, which offset is consistent with the accuracy of the astrometry (Section sec:data ). Panel () of Figure fig:ghz9_jwst also shows the presence of a bright star Gaia ID 2320218780150294272 2013yCat.1324....0S located 4.4 toward the north from GHZ 9.

sources.summary: The paper treats GHZ9 as a GLASS-JWST z 9-11 candidate, observed with HST, having moderate magnification and photometric redshift solutions around z 9.3-9.5.

quotes.quote: Objects GHZ1, GHZ4, and GHZ9 (from GLASS-JWST), and the UNCOVER and DDT candidates, have also been observed with HST under the HFF and BUFFALO Steinhardt2020 programs.

The seven galaxies at z 10 listed in Table  tab:candidates have low-to-moderate magnifications, ranging from 1.2 to 4 (see Table  tab:candidates ), and are not multiply imaged (i.e., they lie outside the main caustics on the source plane at z 10 ).

In this respect, we note that GHZ9 has best-fit photometric redshift solutions at z 9.3-9.5 , compatible with the spectroscopic value measured for DHZ1, while objects GHZ1, GHZ7 and GHZ8 are more likely placed at 10 z 11.5 .

gmw_00006890.name: UHZ1
gmw_00006890.overview: UHZ1 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate [arXiv:2212.06666]. It has an F444W magnitude of 26.54 and is compatible with a redshift range of approximately 9 to 11 [arXiv:2212.06666].

sources.summary: The paper selects UHZ1 as a robust UNCOVER high-redshift candidate with F444W magnitude 26.54, excluding JD1B from LF analysis and placing UHZ1 in possible z 9-11 ranges.

quotes.quote: In UNCOVER we select a new robust candidate UHZ1 with m_ F444W =26.54 0.09 mag, outside of the area previously observed by the HFF. We note that object JD1B from Zitrin2014 , recently confirmed to be at z=9.76 with NIRSpec RobertsBorsani2022d , is also formally selected by our criteria. However, we will not further discuss object JD1B, one of the multiple images of a strongly magnified ultrafaint galaxy, and it will not be used to estimate the ultraviolet (UV) LF in the present analysis which is limited to moderate-magnification regions (see Sect. 4).
In turn, GHZ4 and UHZ1 are compatible with both the z 9-10 and the z 10-11 redshift ranges.

gmw_00006891.name: PMN J1033--4335
gmw_00006891.overview: PMN J1033--4335 is a bright, extended narrow-angle tail radio galaxy located near a northern relic [arXiv:2304.11784]. Its radio lobes trail southward from the core, suggesting that the galaxy is moving away from the cluster center [arXiv:2304.11784].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PMN J1033--4335 as a bright extended NAT radio galaxy near the northern relic, measuring radio fluxes, spectral index, morphology, and possible X-ray/IC emission.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of the nearby NAT radio galaxy, PMN J1033-4335, located 7 \ north-east of the PSZ2 cluster position, is WISEA J103302.46--433508.5 (2MASX J10330244--4335085). Two photometric redshifts are reported, \ 0.1513 Bilicki2014 and \ 0.1561 Bilicki2016 , which gives an average value of \ 0.153, adopted here. Two galaxies in the vicinity, listed in Table  tab:tab3 have similar spectroscopic redshifts.

The most prominent radio galaxy in our field is PMN J1033--4335, situated just east of the northern relic (see Fig.  fig:fig1 - fig:relic-RGB ). It is very bright and extended, well-resolved by both ASKAP and MeerKAT. We find its radio morphology that of a head-tail or narrow-angle tail (NAT) radio galaxy (see Fig.  fig:nat ). Radio lobes trailing south-wards from the core (head) suggest the galaxy is moving away from the cluster centre.

gmw_00006892.name: SDSS J133222.62+034739.9
gmw_00006892.overview: SDSS J133222.62+034739.9 is a gravitationally lensed quasar system at a redshift of 1.445 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609695]. The lensing agent is a redder elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.191 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609695].

sources.summary: Component G is identified as the redder elliptical lens galaxy at redshift 0.191, located between the quasar images and matching the luminosity and shape expected from the lens model.

quotes.quote: The stellar components have similar colors of i-H 1.6 and 1.8 mag for A and B, respectively, while the galaxy G has a redder color of i-H 2.4 mag. The photometry of the components is also summarized in Table tab:1332 . These astrometric and photometric properties are typical of known lensed quasar systems e.g., inada06 , and imply that both components A and B are lensed images of a quasar and component G is the lens galaxy.

While the spectrum is noisy, especially around the C 3 ] emission line and towards the blue wavelength region, the spectral energy distribution (SED) is consistent with a template spectrum of an elliptical galaxy kinney96 at the redshift of the Ca 2 absorption feature, z=0.191 . Given the strength of the absorption, we conclude that the SEDs of components A and B are identical and that component G is an elliptical galaxy at z=0.191 .

We note that the best-fit values for the ellipticity and the position angle, e=0.53 0.07 and _e=25^ 2^ , are in good agreement with those observed for the galaxy G (derived using GALFIT in i band image; see Figure fig:images ), of e=0.69 and _e=21^ , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J133222.62+034739.9 as a two-image gravitationally lensed quasar at redshift 1.445, selected from SDSS and confirmed with Subaru and UH88 follow-up.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the discovery of a gravitationally lensed quasar, SDSS J133222.62+034739.9 (hereafter SDSS J1332+0347). The quasar was confirmed to be doubly imaged by a low-redshift early-type galaxy, from observations using the Subaru 8.2-meter telescope and the University of Hawaii 2.2-meter (UH88) telescope.

SDSS J1332+0347 was selected as a lensed quasar candidate from the SDSS quasar sample, using the same selection algorithm as that described in inada03a . In this algorithm, the morphological parameters of each quasar are used to select lensed quasar candidates. SDSS J1332+0347 was also selected by an improved algorithm oguri06 whose selection function has been extensively tested, and it is in the well-defined source quasar sample of the SQLS for lensed quasar statistics.

All these results lead us to the conclusion that SDSS J1332+0347 is indeed a lensed quasar system, where a source quasar at z_s=1.445 is lensed by a bright lens galaxy at z_l=0.191 resulting in an image separation of =1 14 .

gmw_00006893.name: NGC 1079
gmw_00006893.overview: NGC 1079 is an isolated, grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:0710.4036] featuring a weak, ansae-type bar [arXiv:2304.00515]. Its star formation is concentrated in a resolved nuclear ring [arXiv:0710.4036], while its outer stellar pseudo-ring coincides with a neutral hydrogen ring [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1079 as an isolated grand-design spiral whose star formation is concentrated in a resolved nuclear ring with asymmetric emission-line hot spots.

quotes.quote: NGC 1079 NGC 1079 is an isolated, grand-design spiral in the Eridanus supergroup bro06 marked by a bright bulge and very faint outer disk. It represents a good example for a galaxy in which nearly the entire star formation activity takes place in the ring, as can be seen by the \ image of which shows an extremely quiescent galaxy disk The nuclear ring of NGC 1079 has first been identified by from HST ultraviolet imaging and ground-based spectroscopy which confirmed that the gas rotation curve is consistent with a rotating ring. Continuum emission Within the rather small SINFONI field of view, the K-band map of NGC 1079 appears smooth and featureless, in agreement with the H-band map of .

Emission lines Despite its small angular size (radius 1.5 ), the star formation ring in NGC 1079 is easily resolved in our SINFONI maps. The line maps of , , and \ all show strong emission from the southern half of the ring, while the northern half is either quiescent or deeply obscured. The \ map shows two hot spots of roughly equal flux at P.A. 150 and 240 . Both are also seen in , but in this line the eastern hot spot is substantially stronger. We detect little or no emission from \ at the sensitivity limit of our data (note that the data set for NGC 1079 only consists of two OBs, i.e. only one hour on-source integration time).
