Spectroscopic observations of 63 H ii regions in six spiral galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678) were carried out with the 6-meter telescope (BTA) of Russian Special Astrophysical Observatory with the Spectral Camera attached to the focal reducer SCORPIO in the multislit mode with a dispersion of 2.1 /pixel and a spectral resolution of 10 . These observations were used to estimate the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and the electron temperatures in H ii regions through the recent variant of the strong line method (NS calibration). The parameters of the radial distribution (the extrapolated central intercept value and the gradient) of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances in the disks of spiral galaxies NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678 have been determined. The abundances in the NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, and NGC 7678 are measured for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6217 as a barred ring galaxy with nuclear-origin spiral arms, many star-forming regions, young arms and ring, and an anomalous bulge position.

quotes.quote: NGC 6217. This galaxy has a powerful bar and symmetric ring. In contrast to classical barred galaxies, the spiral arms in NGC 6217 begin not from the ends of the bar, but from the nucleus, independent of the bar. Numerous star-forming regions are observed in the galaxy. The nucleus is blue.

The colors of the spiral arms and ring correspond to a stellar population with an age of 10--100 Myr, while the colors of the disk testify to active, ongoing star formation ( _1 10 Gyr). The bulge of NGC 6217 is shifted to the right of the NCS on the (U-B) (B-V) diagram, into the region of models with an ongoing secondary burst of star formation. The bulge of NGC 6217 is very small (radius 4 5 ), and is located between the blue nucleus and the beginning of the bar and spiral arms with active star formation.

sources.summary: NGC6217 is presented as a nearby barred spiral with a nuclear starburst and low-luminosity AGN; XMM confirms ROSAT jet knots and models the core as AGN plus starburst emission.

quotes.quote: NGC6217 is a nearby spiral galaxy with a starburst region near its center. Evidence for a low luminosity Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) in its core has also been found in optical spectra. Intriguingly, X-ray observations by ROSAT revealed three knots aligned with the galaxy center, resembling a jet structure. This paper presents a study of XMM-Newton observations made to assess the hypothesis of a jet emitted from the center of NGC6217.

The XMM data confirm the knots found with ROSAT and our spectral analysis shows that they have similar spectral properties with a hard photon index 1.7 . The core of NGC6217 is well fitted by a model with an AGN and a starburst component, where the AGN contributes at most 46 of the total flux. The candidate jet has an apparent length 15 kpc and a luminosity of 5 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies spatial regularity in NGC 6217, describing its barred, ringed structure and finding regular young-stellar group spacing in its inner ring near corotation.

quotes.quote: The purpose of our study is to verify the possible regularity in the distribution of star formation regions along the ring of NGC 6217. NGC 6217 is a galaxy with a prominent bar, two strong spiral arms, and two rings. One peculiarity of the galaxy is that its spiral arms begin from the nuclear region and, upon crossing the ends of the bar, they form a (pseudo-)ring with a major projected radius of 40.2 arcsec comeron2014 (Fig. fig:map ).

Spatial regularity in the ring of NGC 6217 indicates the constancy of parameters of the gas medium along the ring. The obtained characteristic separation, , between adjacent young stellar groupings in the ring of NGC 6217 is equal to 670 pc, that is twice as much as the characteristic separation obtained for young stellar groups in the spiral arms of M100 and M74. However, we suspect a characteristic separation with a scale of 12 along the ring of NGC 6217, the same as was found earlier in spiral arms of M100 and M74.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6217 as the barred spiral host of SN 2018gj, reports its redshift and distance, and uses EPM agreement to support the association.

quotes.quote: The discovery of SN 2018gj, in the outskirts of the barred spiral galaxy NGC 6217 (about 122 or 11 kpc away from the centre of the host galaxy) (Figure fig:SN2018gjfield ) was reported on 2018 January 14 (2458132.91 JD) at (J2000), RA, =16^ 32 02 .40 and Dec, =+78^ 12 41 .13 2018TNS2018gj .

Host Properties The preferred redshift (z) and distance (D) of NGC 6217 are 0.00454 0.00001 and 19.61 1.37 Mpc , respectively, and are referenced from NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . Other distance estimates exist with a great scatter ranging from 15 Mpc to 35 Mpc 1984A&AS...56..381B, 1997A&A...326..915T . The SN was located in the outskirts of the probable host NGC 6217 ( 9 13 .8 \ ``W" \ and \ 47 .4 \ ``N" \ implying \ 2 separation from the host's centre). To check the veracity of its association with NGC 6217, an independent estimate of the distance to the SN was made using the Expanding Photosphere Method (EPM) Kirshner1974_EPM, Schimdt1992_EPM, hamuy2001_ .
The distance of the SN estimated using EPM is in agreement with the distances given in NED for NGC 6217 and establishes the association of the SN with NGC 6217.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6217 as a barred ring galaxy whose young stellar population shows regular spacing, including modes near 700 pc and 1 kpc.

quotes.quote: The characteristic distance between neighboring objects 410 pc, which aligns with the previously obtained value for the star-forming regions in NGC 628. studied the distribution of star-forming regions in the ring of the barred galaxy NGC 6217 (type SBb; Fig. figure:fig1 ), for which the regularity of distances between neighboring regions ( 1.3 kpc) was suspected in . Using observational data in NUV, U , and H , the authors confirmed the regularity in the distribution of the young stellar population in the ring with a characteristic distance 700 pc. The presence of the 3 /2 1.0 kpc mode (Figs. figure:fig2 , figure:fig3 ), also observed in the ring of NGC 6217, may indicate the presence of the characteristic distance /2 , which is close to the value of 400 pc obtained for NGC 628 gusev2013 and NGC 4321 elmegreen2018 . It should be noted that NGC 6217 is located three times farther from us ( d=20.6 Mpc) than NGC 628, which poses a challenge in analyzing the spatial distribution in NGC 6217 at scales on the order of 500 pc or less gusev2020 . It should be noted that the galaxy ring is located near the corotation radius, where the presence of shock waves is not expected gusev2020 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6217 as a barred galaxy with a southeastern star-forming region, a central ring component, and an inner ring visible in the monochromatic image.

quotes.quote: NGC 6217: This galaxy presents a prominent bar, with an important region of stellar formation on it located at 10 \ in the southeastern direction from the galactic centre ( 1999ARep...43..377A 1999ARep...43..377A ). The galactic centre reveals the presence of a different structural component, a ring. The size of the ring in observations is approximately of 43 , which agrees with previous works in \ on this galaxy ( 1991A&A...245....7V 1991A&A...245....7V ). The inner ring structure in the centre of the galaxy can be easily seen in the monochromatic image.

sources.summary: ROSAT observations describe 6217 as a barred starburst spiral with a possible one-sided X-ray jet indicated by a southwest X-ray filament and matching radio extension.

quotes.quote: Pietsch (wnp@mpe.mpg.de) Received; accepted Deep ROSAT PSPC and HRI observations of the nuclear starburst, UV flat spectrum spiral 6217 reveal a jet-like X-ray filament extending out 2 7 (18.8 kpc) to the SW of the galaxy.
Radio images of 6217 show an extent in the same direction, giving further evidence for a one-sided X-ray jet interpretation of this feature.

Galaxies: individual: 6217 - Galaxies: jets - quasars: general - Galaxies: Seyfert - Radio continuum: galaxies - X-rays: galaxies 6217 is a B _ T = 11.86 mag barred spiral, RSBbc(s)II, as classified in the Revised-Shapley Ames Catalog of Bright Galaxies 1981rsac.book.....S .

gmw_00006939.name: NGC 7678
gmw_00006939.overview: NGC 7678 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1205.3910, arXiv:1509.02356]. It hosts active star formation within one of its spiral arms, as well as a recent burst of star formation in its nucleus [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: The paper reports BTA spectroscopy of H II regions in NGC 7678, deriving oxygen and nitrogen abundances, electron temperatures, extinction, and radial abundance information across the galaxy disk.

quotes.quote: Here we report spectra of 63 H ii regions in a sample of six spiral galaxies: NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678. A first motivation of the spectral observations is the determination of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and electron temperatures in individual H ii regions using the recent NS- and ON calibration pilyugin2010,pilyuginmattsson2011 . The abundances will be used in the computation of the grid of evolutionary models of star clusters embedded in studied H ii regions.

Spectroscopic observations of 63 H ii regions in six spiral galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678) were carried out with the 6-meter telescope (BTA) of Russian Special Astrophysical Observatory with the Spectral Camera attached to the focal reducer SCORPIO in the multislit mode with a dispersion of 2.1 /pixel and a spectral resolution of 10 . These observations were used to estimate the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and the electron temperatures in H ii regions through the recent variant of the strong line method (NS calibration). The parameters of the radial distribution (the extrapolated central intercept value and the gradient) of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances in the disks of spiral galaxies NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678 have been determined. The abundances in the NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, and NGC 7678 are measured for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7678 as a late-type barred galaxy with active star formation in a spiral arm and recent burst-like star formation in its nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7678. This is a late-type galaxy with a bar and active star formation ongoing in one of its spiral arms. The components are located around the NCS in the two-color diagrams, in the region corresponding to stellar systems with an exponentially decaying SFR with the characteristic time scale _1 5 Gyr. The nucleus of NGC 7678 deviates upward and to the right of the NCS on the (U-B) (B-V) , and to the right of the NCS on the (B-V) (V-R) diagram. The color characteristics suggest a burst of star formation has been occurring in the nucleus over the past 10--30 Myr.

gmw_00006940.name: NGC 448
gmw_00006940.overview: NGC 448 is an early-type galaxy that hosts a kinematically distinct, counter-rotating stellar disk in its innermost region [arXiv:1809.07758]. This embedded disk has a higher metallicity and lower alpha/Fe overabundance than the main stellar body, suggesting it formed from external gas acquisition or direct accretion [arXiv:1809.07758].

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 448 into kinematically distinct stellar components and finds a genuinely decoupled, compact counter-rotating disk with different chemistry from the main body.

quotes.quote: We study the kinematically distinct components in two early-type galaxies NGC 448 and NGC 4365 aided by integral-field observations with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) on the Very Large Telescope. NGC 448 has previously been shown to host a counter-rotating stellar disk.

The two kinematically decoupled stellar components in NGC 448 have similar ages, but different chemical compositions. The distinct kinematic feature in NGC 448 has a nearly exponential surface-brightness light profile, dominates in the innermost 10 , is smaller in size, and is very likely an embedded counter-rotating disk as also indicated by its kinematics. It has higher metallicity than the main galaxy stellar body and lower /Fe overabundance.

Our findings indicate that the kinematically decoupled component in NGC 448 is truly decoupled, has external origin, and was formed through either the acquisition of gas and a subsequent star-formation episode or it formed from the direct accretion of stars from a companion.

gmw_00006941.name: SDSS J173646.73+503252.3
gmw_00006941.overview: SDSS J173646.73+503252.3 is a compact host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0213 [arXiv:2005.12922]. It has an estimated star formation rate of 0.012 solar masses per year and features a northwest surface brightness peak that may trace a star-forming region [arXiv:2005.12922].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J173646.73+503252.3 as SN2019dge’s compact host, measuring its redshift, star-forming region offset, star formation rate, and nebular emission-line fluxes.

quotes.quote: \ resides in a compact galaxy SDSS J173646.73+503252.3. From our follow up spectra (see Section subsec:spec_properties ), we measure a host redshift of z=0.0213 , corresponding to a luminosity distance of D_L = 93 Mpc. Figure fig:offset shows that there is a surface brightness peak to the northwest of \ ( 0.2 kpc away), which might trace a star-forming region.

Host Galaxy Properties We measure properties of the host galaxy using the spectrum obtained at phase +314.4 d, assuming that the most prominent nebular line emissions of H and [ N II ] are from the host. The Galactic extinction corrected emission line fluxes of H and [ N II ] 6584 are (24.15 0.54) 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and (1.92 0.10 ) 10^ 16 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , respectively. The fluxes were measured by fitting a Gaussian profile to the emission line profiles, measuring the integrated flux under the profile. Using the Kennicutt1998 relation converted to a Chabrier initial mass function Chabrier2003, Madau2014 , we infer a star-formation rate of 0.012 M_ yr^ 1 from the H emission line.

gmw_00006942.name: CGCG 291-028
gmw_00006942.overview: CGCG 291-028 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.0471 to 0.0497 [arXiv:1308.3828]. It hosts a hard X-ray active galactic nucleus and is a compelling candidate source for the Ursa Major ultra-high-energy cosmic ray cluster [arXiv:1308.3828].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 291-028 as the low-redshift hard-X-ray AGN host aligned with the Ursa Major UHECR cluster and argues it is the compelling source candidate.

quotes.quote: The author checked for an alternative host candidate at lower redshift and found that the galaxy CGCG-291-028, at z=0.0497 , is within the error radius of \ J 1105.7+5854 . An inquiry determined that the 70 month BAT AGN catalog (subsequently made public ) resolves two sources, one of which was near but possibly not inside CGCG 291-028. A recent \ observation obtained by GRF and W. Baumgartner has shown that the X-ray souce is unambiguously within CGCG 291-028, and very close to or coincident with its nucleus. Thus CGCG 291-028 is the unique and compelling candidate for being the source of the UMC events.

We have shown that the Ursa Major cluster of 4 events in the combined AGASA and HiRes datasets, when backtracked in the Galactic magnetic field under the assumption that they are protons, become even more tightly clustered and have an rms separation of 0.8 ^ with respect to the galaxy CGCG 291-028 at redshift 0.0471 (distance of 200 Mpc), which is the host of a hard X-ray AGN.

gmw_00006943.name: 1H 1914-194
gmw_00006943.overview: 1H 1914-194 is an X-ray-selected BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z = 0.137 [arXiv:astro-ph/0306617, arXiv:1501.07115]. Its resolved host galaxy exhibits both nuclear emission lines and stellar absorption lines, while radio observations reveal a bright core with an extended jetlike feature [arXiv:astro-ph/0306617].

sources.summary: The paper identifies H-1914-194 as an X-ray-selected BL Lac with radio detections, a bright core and jetlike halo feature, resolved host galaxy, and redshift z=0.137.

quotes.quote: H-1914-194 is an X-ray-selected BL Lac (XBL) detected during the HEAO-1 Survey (Schwartz et al. 1989; Laurent-Muehleisen et al. 1993). This source was also detected in the radio during the Parkes-MIT-NRAO Survey (Griffith et al. 1994) and the Texas radio Survey (Douglas et al. 1996). Kollgaard et al. (1996) present a deep radio image for H-1914-194, which shows a bright core with a jetlike feature extended to the northwest toward a local brightening in a diffuse halo (see their figure 5a). The optical counterpart is determined within 3 '' of the X-ray position: Kollgaard et al. (1996) give the optical position with an error of 0.3

HST imaging at high resolution (Scarpa et al. 2000, Urry et al. 2000) clearly resolved this source. The average radial profile of the host galaxy is consistent with both a de Vaucouleurs (R _ host =16.9) and a disk model (R _ host =17.9). In our spectrum we clearly detect both nuclear emission lines and absorption lines from the host galaxy (see figure 1 and Table 2). Identification of these features with [ OII ] , [ OIII ] N1 and N2, CaII H K and G band yields a redshift of <z> =0.137 0.001.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1H 1914-194 as a poorly covered potential TeV BL Lac and derives an optimistic, upper-limit neutrino flux from an indicative SED fit.

quotes.quote: 1H 1914-194 at z=0.137 Carangelo2003_1H1914z . This is the first BL Lac of the sample that has not yet been detected in TeV rays, and the least well covered in different energy bands among the sources of this sample. In order to apply our model we constructed a SED using available data from the ASDC, which is comprised mainly of NED archival data. Given the quality of the available dataset, our main focus was to describe as well as possible the Fermi/LAT data, while roughly describing the observations in other energy bands.

1H 1914-194 Blazar 1H 1914-194 is one of the two sources that are potential TeV emitters and has the poorest MW coverage among the BL Lacs of the sample (for details, see Sect. case ). Because of the inhomogeneity (in time domain) of the available MW observations, we present only an indicative fit, where we attempted to describe at best the Fermi/LAT data. Being the least constrained case we chose parameters that ensure high efficiency in photopion production, i.e. we present the most optimistic, for neutrino emission, scenario.

gmw_00006944.name: Ion1
gmw_00006944.overview: Ion1 is a compact star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.795 [arXiv:1502.04708]. It is identified as a Lyman continuum emitter candidate with a marginally resolved effective radius of approximately 250 parsecs [arXiv:1502.04708].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Ion1 as a compact z=3.795 LyC-emitter candidate whose colors, UV slope, morphology, and low P(fesc=0) favor escaping ionizing radiation.

quotes.quote: The procedure described above recovers Ion1 as a LyC emitter candidate (CANDELS ID 23836, guo13 ), even excluding the direct information we have about the LyC detection (i.e., the U band detection Here the U band refers to the VIMOS U band, nonino09 , which is an intermediate-band filter, FWHM=350 (see also vanz10b ). at < 830 rest-frame, Fig. ion1 ). We recall that, currently, the global spectral energy distribution of this source is compatible with a star-forming galaxy: there is no X-ray detection in the 4Ms Chandra observations, and no emission lines (e.g., Ly , NV, CIV) have been measured in the spectrum ( vanz10b , 2012). It is a compact source, but is marginally resolved in the ACS images ( 1500 rest-frame) with a R_ eff 250 pc (see Sect. 4.6).

At the redshift of Ion1 more than half of the B_ 435 band probes the ionizing part of the spectrum ( <912 ) (see top-left panel of Fig. ion1 ). The UV slope measured with the five-band fitting procedure gives _ obs =-2.01 0.10 . The observed color is (B_ 435 V_ 606 )=1.72 with an error _ (B-V) =0.18, the z_ 850 band magnitude is z_ 850 =24.83 0.03 . We derived the probability P(fesc=0) by calculating the expected (B_ 435 V_ 606 )_ simul color including the I14 IGM prescription and adopting the galaxy (BC) model anchored to the observed UV slope, as discussed in the previous section. The distribution of the expected color (B_ 435 V_ 606 )_ simul for the Ion1 source is shown in Fig. ion1 and is compared to the observed color. Interestingly, although this source was selected as a B_ 435 band dropout (indicated with a star in Fig. colors , bottom-left panel), the observed (B_ 435 V_ 606 ) color is reproduced only marginally if fesc =0, that is, P(fesc=0) 4.7 (shaded region of the fesc =0 histogram shown in Fig. ion1 , upper right panel). With such a low probability it is selected as a LyC emitter candidate.

gmw_00006945.name: HSCJ100129+024427
gmw_00006945.overview: HSCJ100129+024427 is a candidate lensed galaxy identified through its lens-like appearance [arXiv:2009.07854]. High-resolution Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals three possible lensed images associated with this system [arXiv:2009.07854].

sources.summary: The paper keeps HSCJ100129+024427 for follow-up despite zero final effective-region area, because Chitah and HST imaging indicate probable lensed-galaxy features.

quotes.quote: Our time-variability-based search method picked out HSCJ100129+024427 in an earlier version of our code by mistake (and luck). HSCJ100129+024427 has zero effective region area across all the 13 epochs, and the final variability selection actually does not pick it out. Given its lens-like appearance, we keep this system for further investigation: Chitah identifies the lensing feature of HSCJ100129+024427, followed by an average score of probable lens from the visual inspection. Therefore, HSCJ100129+024427 is more likely a candidate of lensed galaxy, instead of a candidate of lensed quasar.

From the HST images, we can clearly see the four multiple images in HSCJ095921+020638 (top-left panel in Fig. fig:hst_imgs ), and three possible lensed images in HSCJ100129+024427 (top-right panel in Fig. fig:hst_imgs ).

gmw_00006946.name: MCG 5-23-16
gmw_00006946.overview: MCG 5-23-16 is a nearby, X-ray-bright S0 lenticular galaxy classified optically as a Seyfert 1.9 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1610.06243]. The galaxy exhibits near-infrared variability [arXiv:1105.2368] and likely hosts shocks in its narrow-line region [arXiv:2311.13085].

sources.summary: The paper finds MCG 5-23-16 has constrained [P II] upper limits, absorption near [P II], [S III] structure, and a high ratio implying NLR shocks.

quotes.quote: MCG 5-23-16 The [ S 9 ] 12520 line is also present in addition to the standard lines. Three absorption lines are observed in the [ P 2 ] band: 1400 km s ^ 1 , 200 km s ^ 1 , and +200 km s ^ 1 . Their line widths are broader than those in the telluric lines. In particular, the one at 1400 km s ^ 1 (11830 ) is the broadest among them. The upper limit of the [ P 2 ] is well constrained. The [ S 3 ] line shows a slight absorption at the center, or it may be due to two narrow emission lines constituting the central peak. We fit it with a single positive Gaussian.

We found that four sources (Ark 120, ESO 323-G77, MCG 5-23-16, and NGC 4507) have the [ Fe 2 ] / [ P 2 ] ratios larger than 10; notably, for NGC 4507, [ P 2 ] was detected and the ratio exceeded 10, which is the first report. They are likely to have shocks in the NLR.

sources.summary: The paper presents MCG 05 23 016 as an X-ray-bright S0 Seyfert 1.9 with polarized broad H and clear optical/NIR broad-line region components.

quotes.quote: MCG 05 23 016 is a nearby X-ray bright S0 galaxy, optically classified as a Seyfert 1.9 veron80 . Moreover, clear evidence was found for a broad H in the polarized flux lumsden04 . Its X-ray spectrum resembles a classical Compton-thin Seyfert 2 galaxy and it shows both narrow and broad components in the iron K line reeves07 . The best fit of the most relevant emission lines of MCG 05 23 016 is shown in Figure fig:MCG052316 . In the X-shooter optical spectrum, beside the narrow component, a clear evidence was found for a H BLR component with FWHM = 2232 , while no evidence for a BLR component in H was found. Furthermore, both the ISAAC and X-shooter NIR spectra show clear evidence for a BLR component in the Pa and in the He I (FWHM = 2134 and FWHM = 2474 , respectively).

sources.summary: The paper says MCG 5-23-16 has a narrow Fe K line in Chandra HETG data, no detectable ASCA broad component, and line emission from larger radii.

quotes.quote: Observations of MCG 5-23-16 with ASCA suggested that the Fe K line has a narrow core and a broad component (Weaver et al. \ 1997). The Chandra HETG spectrum of MCG 5-23-16 is shown in Figure 4. The 6.4 keV line is narrow with FWHM < 3,000 km s ^ 1 and an equivalent width of 90 eV (about the ASCA EW). The ASCA broad component is not detectable in the Chandra spectrum. Modeling the line as emission from a geometrically thin accretion disk yields a lower limit to the line-emitting region of 200 r_ g (units of GM/ ^2 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes MCG 5-23-16 as a near-IR-variable Seyfert whose fits need no added AGN component and whose moderate silicate-feature torus parameters are well constrained.

quotes.quote: Nine out of the 13 galaxies in our sample have been reported to show variability in the near-IR: NGC 7674 (Quillen et al. 2000), NGC 1068, IC 4329A, NGC 2110, MCG 5-23-16, NGC 5506, and NGC 7469 (Glass 2004), NGC 3227 (Suganuma et al. 2006), and NGC 4151 (Koshida et al. 2009).
Figure 8 suggests that for NGC 5506, NGC 7582, NGC 7674, and MCG 5-23-16 we do not need to add the AGN component.

For the three galaxies with moderate silicate features (MCG 5-23-16, NGC 2110, and NGC 7674, see Table 4) we are able to reproduce reasonably well the photometric SED points and the mid-IR spectroscopy, and the torus model parameters are mostly well constrained.

gmw_00006947.overview: The quasar 0454 463 features asymmetric radio hotspots located to its southeast and northwest [arXiv:1101.5822]. A short southeast jet extends from the core before bending toward the southern hotspot, accompanied by extended X-ray emission along its path [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper describes asymmetric radio hotspots, a short southeast jet that bends toward the southern hotspot, extended X-ray emission along the jet direction, and an additional X-ray source beyond the hotspot.

quotes.quote: 0454 463 (PKS B0454-463) The ATCA data show hotspots placed somewhat asymmetrically 5.9 \ to the southeast and 4.5 \ to the northwest of the core. A 3 \ long jet extends to the southeast before bending to the south hotspot. There is no apparent VLBI structure 2004AJ....127.3609O . The X-ray source appears extended along the jet direction to the southeast, coincident with the jet before it bends south. An X-ray source is found 1 \ beyond the southeast hotspot.

gmw_00006948.overview: The active galaxy 0820 + 225 is surrounded by extended radio emission oriented along an east-west axis [arXiv:1101.5822]. Its S-shaped VLBI jet exhibits an extended transverse Faraday-rotation gradient [arXiv:0708.0316, arXiv:1101.5822], which shows evidence of evolution over time [arXiv:0708.0316].

sources.summary: The paper describes extended radio emission around the source, an S-shaped VLBI jet, an adopted westward jet orientation for analysis, and no detected associated extended X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 0820 + 225 (4C +22.21) Extended radio emission surrounds the source, but is oriented predominantly along an east-west axis. A VLBI observation by shows an S-shaped jet extending over 20 mas generally to the southwest. We consider the jet to be oriented due west for the purposes of this analysis because the radio emission is somewhat brighter in this direction on a scale of a few arcsec. We detect no associated extended X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports an extended, confirmed transverse Faraday-rotation gradient in the jet of 0820+225 and says the RM distribution evolved, possibly because the electron-density distribution changed.

quotes.quote: We observe an extended RM gradient in the jet of 0820+225 (Fig. 4); Gabuzda, Murray and Cronin (2004) also observed a gradient in the jet of this source, but somewhat closer to the core, possibly suggesting that the electron-density distribution has changed between our observations.

The presence of transverse RM gradients has been confirmed for the jets of 0820+225 (Gabuzda, Murray Cronin 2004) and 1749+096 (Zavala Taylor 2004), and new transverse RM gradients have been detected in the jets of seven other objects.
Furthermore, we find evidence for evolution in the RM distribution in 0820+225.

gmw_00006949.overview: The source 1040 + 123, also known as 3C 245, is a moderately beamed radio-loud quasar with a jet oriented less than 20 degrees to the line of sight [arXiv:2011.03130]. Its eastern radio lobe exhibits likely inverse-Compton X-ray emission, while the western kiloparsec-scale jet shows no detected extended X-ray emission [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 245 as a moderately beamed quasar with a near-line-of-sight 1067 kpc jet, high core contribution, FIR non-thermal radiation, and relatively unobscured torus geometry.

quotes.quote: -3C 245 is a moderately beamed quasar with a jet oriented at <20^ to the line of sight, Foley1990,Marin2016 with a 1067 kpc jet and 66 core contribution at 5 GHz which is significantly higher than other sources in our sample (see Table tab:radio ). The radio emission is best described as a parabola with an additional core component (the fourth model in Section sec:radio ). To constrain the radio contribution to the FIR we recently obtained the SMA data (see Table tab:submmdata ), which is dominated by the non-thermal radiation from the radio structure (Figure fig:sed ). The extreme variability expected in blazars may not be seen in 3C 245 since our recent SMA observation is consistent with older radio data 1981AJ.....86.1306G . Consistent with its classification, the best-fitted torus indicates a combination of clumps with negligible opacity and a homogeneous disk with small opacity.

sources.summary: The paper includes the source for completeness, finds no significant X-ray emission from the western kpc-scale jet direction, and notes likely lobe-related inverse-Compton emission on the eastern side.

quotes.quote: 1040 + 123 (3C 245) This source was in the sample and is included in our analysis for completeness. We detect no significant extended X-ray emission associated with the western radio emission, which is the direction of the kpc scale jet as well as optical knots sambruna04 . The eastern lobe appears to be detected, so our algorithm for detecting an X-ray excess on the jet side (by comparing the X-ray profile along the position angle of the jet to a region 180 \ from it) may be failing in this case because the existence of what appears to be inverse Compton emission from the brighter radio lobe see also sambruna04 .

gmw_00006950.overview: The object 1251 713 is a quasar with a radio knot located approximately 10 arcseconds to its south [arXiv:1101.5822]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations show no structure on the milliarcsecond scale, and there is no associated extended X-ray emission [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper reports a southern ATCA radio knot, no mas-scale VLBI structure, and no associated extended X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 1251 713 (PKS B1251 713) The ATCA image shows a knot about 10 \ to the south of the core. VLBI at 8.64 GHz shows no structure on the mas scale ojha05 . We detect no associated extended X-ray emission.

gmw_00006951.overview: 1421 490 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.662 [arXiv:1101.5822]. Its central component B is associated with the quasar core, while the northeastern component A is identified as a radio lobe containing a compact hotspot [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper identifies component B as the quasar core at z = 0.662 and interprets component A as a radio lobe with an exceptionally bright compact hotspot.

quotes.quote: 1421 490 (PKS B1421 490) This observation was published by and is included here for completeness. Magellan spectra show that component B (in the center of the image shown in Fig.  fig:images ), which is the brightest optically and in the X-ray band, is the core of the quasar with z = 0.662 . Because component A (to the northeast of component B and brighter in the radio band) has been resolved using VLBI godfrey08 , we now consider that component B is associated with the quasar core and that component A is a radio lobe with an exceptionally bright and compact hotspot.

gmw_00006952.overview: The quasar 2255 282 features an eight-arcsecond radio jet extending from its core [arXiv:1101.5822]. X-ray emission is detected along the first four arcseconds of this jet, while VLBI observations show jet emission without detectable superluminal motion [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper describes an eight-arcsecond radio jet, VLBI jet emission without detectable superluminal motion, and X-ray emission along the first four arcseconds close to the core.

quotes.quote: 2255 282 (PKS B2255 282) The 8 \ long radio jet extends along a PA of -70 \ from the core. The VLBI images show jet emission without detectable superluminal motion 2007AJ....133.2357P at a PA of -130 to -140 (MOJAVE web site, ). X-ray emission is detected along the first 4 \ of the jet and as close as 1 \ from the core (see Fig.  fig:xrayprofiles ).

gmw_00006953.overview: The quasar 2326 477 features a strong radio component located approximately 4.5 arcseconds from its core [arXiv:1101.5822]. However, space VLBI imaging reveals no radio jet at milliarcsecond scales, and there is no detected extended X-ray emission associated with this component [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper reports a strong radio component offset from the core, no mas-scale VSOP jet, and no associated extended X-ray emission along the radio position angle.

quotes.quote: 2326 477 (PKS B2326 477) A strong radio component is detected about 4.5 \ from the core at a PA of -110 . VSOP imaging shows no jet at mas scales 2004ApJS..155...33S . We detect no associated extended X-ray emission along this PA.

gmw_00006954.name: SDSS J144916
gmw_00006954.overview: SDSS J144916 is an edge-on, quenched massive disk galaxy that exhibits a clear disky morphology [arXiv:2005.09663]. It has a CO(1-0) line detection at a confidence level greater than 3 sigma, a circular velocity of 274.7 km/s, and an estimated halo mass of approximately 10^13.1 solar masses [arXiv:2005.09663].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J144916 as an edge-on quenched massive disk galaxy with significant CO(1-0) detection, circular velocity of 274.7 km/s, and inferred halo mass near 10^13.1 solar masses.

quotes.quote: The first galaxy, SDSS J144916, is an edge-on galaxy showing a clear disky morphology.
Only two galaxies, SDSS J144916 and SDSS J114614, show a significant CO(1-0) line at a confidence level greater than 3 .

For the two galaxies with CO detection, the circular velocity can be approximated by the 20 peak line width of the spectra and certain inclination angle, which are 274.7 km s ^ 1 and 283.2 km s ^ 1 for SDSS J144916 and SDSS J114614, respectively. Assuming a typical NFW profile Navarro1997 , and the halo mass-concentration relation Prada2012 , we can estimated halo mass by fitting the circular velocity at the radius within which the CO is detected. We obtain similar halo mass of 10^ 13.1   M_ for both two galaxies.

gmw_00006955.name: SDSS J114614
gmw_00006955.overview: SDSS J114614 is a quenched massive spiral galaxy [arXiv:2005.09663]. It has an eight percent molecular gas fraction, a circular velocity of 283.2 km/s, and an estimated halo mass of approximately 10^13.1 solar masses [arXiv:2005.09663].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J114614 as one of two CO-detected quenched massive spirals, with an 8 percent molecular gas fraction, circular velocity of 283.2 km/s, and inferred halo mass near 10^13.1 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Only two galaxies, SDSS J144916 and SDSS J114614, show a significant CO(1-0) line at a confidence level greater than 3 .
The molecular gas fraction ( M_ H_2 /M_* ) is 4 in three galaxies and 8 in the remaining galaxy (SDSS J114614).

For the two galaxies with CO detection, the circular velocity can be approximated by the 20 peak line width of the spectra and certain inclination angle, which are 274.7 km s ^ 1 and 283.2 km s ^ 1 for SDSS J144916 and SDSS J114614, respectively. Assuming a typical NFW profile Navarro1997 , and the halo mass-concentration relation Prada2012 , we can estimated halo mass by fitting the circular velocity at the radius within which the CO is detected. We obtain similar halo mass of 10^ 13.1   M_ for both two galaxies.

gmw_00006956.name: Canis Major galaxy
gmw_00006956.overview: The Canis Major galaxy is a dwarf galaxy located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 8.0 kpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0311119]. Its stellar population is dominated by stars with an estimated age of 4 to 10 Gyr, alongside a younger population and associated open clusters [arXiv:astro-ph/0311119].

sources.summary: The paper treats Canis Major as a dwarf galaxy, measuring an 8 kpc distance, constraining its stellar populations, associating clusters, and arguing the overdensity is not just Galactic warp structure.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection of Main Sequence stars belonging to the recently identified Canis Major galaxy in a field located at 4.2 from the center of the stellar system. With Main Sequence fitting we obtain a distance modulus (m-M)_0=14.5 0.3 to the dwarf, corresponding to a distance of D_ 8.0 1.2 kpc, in full agreement with previous estimates based on the photometric parallax of M-giants. From the comparison with theoretical isochrones we constrain the age of the main population of the Canis Major system in the range 4-10 Gyr. A blue plume of likely younger stars (age < 1-2 Gyr) is also identified.

The position, distance and stellar population of the old open clusters AM-2 and Tombaugh 2 strongly suggest that they are physically associated with the Canis Major galaxy. Using our own photometry and data from 2MASS and the GSC2.2 surveys we demonstrate that the claim by Momany et al. that the CMa overdensity is entirely due to the Galactic warp is not supported by the existing observations, once all the available pieces of information are taken into account. It is shown that the CMa overdensity clearly emerges at a heliocentric distance of 8 kpc above any overdensity possibly produced by the Galactic warp.

gmw_00006957.name: KUG 1249+278
gmw_00006957.overview: KUG 1249+278 is a relatively faint Sc-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0801.2278]. It has an estimated average metallicity of 12+log(O/H) = 8.53 and hosted the supernova SN 2005la [arXiv:0801.2278].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KUG 1249+278 as the faint Sc spiral host of SN 2005la, estimates its distance, luminosity and metallicity, and reports archival monitoring limits for pre-supernova outbursts.

quotes.quote: SN 2005la was discovered in the spiral galaxy KUG 1249+278 by R. Q. and P. M. on behalf of the Texas Supernova Search http://grad40.as.utexas.edu/ quimby/tss/index.html . The unfiltered discovery images were obtained on Nov. 30.51 and Dec. 1.49 UT with the 0.45-m ROTSE-IIIb telescope at the McDonald Observatory, in which the SN appeared to be at a magnitude of about 17.6 puc05 . The coordinates of the new object were R.A. = 12^ h 52^ m 15 68 , Decl. = +27 31 52 5 (equinox 2000.0; uncertainty +/- 0 8), 6 W and 6 S of the center of the host galaxy. The field of KUG 1249+278 with SN 2005la is shown in Fig. fig1 , where 5 reference stars are also labelled. SN 2005la is hosted in an Sc-type galaxy, with total apparent B band magnitude 16.67 0.50 (LEDA  database) . http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ The distance to SN 2005la can be determined from the recession velocity of the host galaxy, which is derived from the position of the narrow H emission line of a nearby H II region v _ rec = 5570 km s ^ 1 , fil05 .

Assuming a negligible Galactic extinction (see below), we obtain for KUG 1249+278 a total absolute magnitude M _B 17.8. The host galaxy of SN 2005la is therefore a relatively faint spiral, similar to the galaxies hosting SNe 2006jc and 2002ao LEDA, see also pri07b . An average metallicity estimate for KUG 1249+278 can be obtained via the best fit of the luminosity-metallicity relation of tre04 . This provides 12+log(O/H) = 8.53 0.16 (dex) for the above estimated galaxy absolute magnitude.

gmw_00006958.name: OIIB1
gmw_00006958.overview: OIIB1 is a giant high-redshift oxygen-line blob with a spatial extent exceeding 75 kpc at a redshift of z=1.18 [arXiv:1306.5246]. The object harbors an obscured type-2 active galactic nucleus, which may drive gas outflows through shock excitation [arXiv:1306.5246].

sources.summary: The paper presents OIIB1 as a giant high-redshift oxygen-line blob with extended emission, an obscured type-2 AGN, and tentative blueshifted lines favoring AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: We discover a giant , dubbed `` \ 1," with a spatial extent over 75 kpc at a spectroscopic redshift of z=1.18 , and also identify a total of 12 s with a size of >30 kpc. Our optical spectrum of \ 1 presents 3426 line at the 6 level, indicating that this object harbors an obscured type-2 active galactic nucleus (AGN). The presence of gas outflows in this object is suggested by two marginal detections of 2587 absorption and 2613 emission lines both of which are blueshifted at as large as 500-600 km s ^ 1 , indicating that the heating source of \ 1 is AGN or associated shock excitation rather than supernovae produced by starbursts.

The nucleus of \ 1 is found to be an obscured type-2 AGN. The prominent 4000 \ break seen in the observed SED (Figure sedfitting ) indicates that we are not seeing the AGN continuum directly and that stellar components dominate the observed SED.

gmw_00006959.name: central cD galaxy in Abell 3827
gmw_00006959.overview: The central cD galaxy in Abell 3827 is a super-giant elliptical galaxy and brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1004.5410]. It is undergoing active galaxy cannibalism, appearing to devour at least four other galaxies within its extended, asymmetric halo [arXiv:1004.5410].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Abell 3827 central cD/BCG as an extremely massive, multi-component galaxy undergoing cannibalism, with strong-lensing mass estimates implying one of the largest local-galaxy masses.

quotes.quote: We report here the discovery of a multi-component BCG that is, to our knowledge, the most massive galaxy ever seen in the local universe. This is located in the core of Abell 3827, a massive galaxy clusters in Abell's cluster catalog (richness class 2 and Bautz-Morgan type I), with an X-ray emission of L_ X (0.1--2.4 keV) = 2.1 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 . This BCG is perhaps the most extreme example of ongoing galaxy cannibalism known: a super-giant elliptical that appears to be in the throes of devouring at least four other galaxies. Evidence for a recent major merger is also supported by the appearance of an extended asymmetric halo at the center of the cluster.

We estimated the mass of the cD galaxy as being 30 50 of the total mass of the cluster within 20 lim07 . This implies a cD galaxy mass between 8.1 10^ 12 M _ and 1.3 10^ 13 M _ . Even assuming a conservative value of 30 , this would mean that the central cD galaxy in Abell 3827 is perhaps the most massive galaxy observed in the local universe.

gmw_00006960.name: NGC 3557
gmw_00006960.overview: NGC 3557 is a radio galaxy that hosts a central molecular gas disk with asymmetric rotation and disturbed kinematics [arXiv:1908.09229, arXiv:2010.04685]. The galaxy exhibits a secure jet-disk misalignment of 57 degrees, which may indicate a low-level jet-interstellar medium interaction [arXiv:2010.04685].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3557 CO kinematics, finding asymmetric rotation and a central velocity upturn consistent with a Keplerian SMBH signature, though residuals leave ambiguity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3557 The CO(2-1) emission of NGC 3557 shows a disc-like structure, but asymmetric features are evident both in the rotation pattern (Fig. fig:ngc3557_mom1 ) and the velocity curve (Fig. fig:ngc3557_PVD ). Distortions are clearly visible in the mean velocity map at an RA offset of 0.8'' (positive velocities; Fig. fig:ngc3557_mom1 ), coincident with an asymmetry in the PVD (Fig. fig:ngc3557_PVD ). Furthermore, an increase of the gas velocity around the centre is visible at positive velocities.

The majority of the observed disc structures are reproduced well by our model, and accounting for the presence of a SMBH in the centre reproduces the velocity increase visible at positive velocities around the centre of the galaxy. We find a best fit M_ SMBH =(7.10 0.02) 10^ 8 M _ ( (M_ SMBH )=8.85 0.02 M _ ), consistent with the expectation from the M_ SMBH - _* relation e.g. Tremaine02,Connell13 . Nevertheless, some significant residuals ( 40 km s ^ 1 ) are visible in Figure fig:ngc3557_mom1_res at the centre of the gas distribution. The model predicts a symmetrical velocity increase at both positive and negative velocities, while only the former appears in the data. Thus, while the SMBH mass determined above seems reasonable, we cannot definitively claim that a massive dark object is the cause of the observed central velocity structures in NGC 3557.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3557 has the largest secure jet-disc misalignment in the sample and disturbed CO kinematics, but only low-level jet-ISM perturbations in a smooth inner jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 3557 is the source for which we measure the largest secure jet-disc misalignment ( _ dj =57^ 2^ ). In Paper II, we found clear evidence of disturbed CO kinematics in this source, possibly to be ascribed to a jet-ISM interaction (such an interaction was also tentatively claimed by Vilaro19 , based on molecular line ratios). However, although deviations from circular rotation are clearly visible both in the gas rotation pattern and in the velocity curve at a position that (at least in projection) appears located along the direction of the jet axis (see Paper II), these are low-level perturbations, not consistent with the high velocity outflows predicted by simulations. Neither are significant perturbations observed in the structure of the inner jet flow, which appears straight and smooth (Figure fig:ngc3557_VLA ).

NGC 3557 may then be a case where we are observing the residual effects of an evolved interaction, although the lack of appropriate models and higher resolution molecular gas observations prevent us from drawing solid conclusions.

gmw_00006961.name: IC 1531
gmw_00006961.overview: IC 1531 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:2010.04685, arXiv:2305.10102] that has also been classified as an uncertain type of blazar [arXiv:2305.10102]. It hosts a barely resolved carbon monoxide disc with regular rotation, though it exhibits s-shaped distortions and asymmetric structures [arXiv:1908.09229].

sources.summary: The paper says IC 1531 has a barely resolved CO disc with mostly regular rotation but clear s-shaped distortions and asymmetries, plausibly from non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: IC 1531 The barely resolved CO disc of IC 1531 mainly exhibits regular rotation pattern and morphology. Therefore, a simple axisymmetric model reproduces the observed gas features reasonably well (Fig. fig:IC1531 ). However, an s-shaped distortion is visible in the rotation pattern at 0.5'' , along the zero-velocity iso-contour (Fig. fig:ic1531_mom1 ).

Asymmetric structures are also present at the same position in the velocity curve, between 0 and 100 km s ^ 1 (Fig. fig:ic1531_PVD ). Such distortions may trace the presence of a warp and/or non-circular motions (e.g. inflow, outflow, streaming). We attempt to include both position and inclination angle warps in our model, but these are not favoured by the fitting routine.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1531 as a CO-detected FR I LERG with unresolved inner jets, a blazar-like small jet inclination, and ambiguous near/far CO-disc orientation.

quotes.quote: In IC 1531, the inner jet structure is unresolved at the spatial resolution of our 10 GHz observations ( 200 pc; see Table tab:JVLA obs ), the flaring point is not clearly identifiable and emission from the counter-jet is not securely detected, qualitatively consistent with its blazar classification Bassi18 and a small value of _ jet . In this case, we placed the two boxes as close to the core as possible (Fig. fig:ic1531_boxes ). We then derived a 3 upper limit to the flux density of the counter-jet from the distribution for source-free regions of the image. The lower limit on R corresponds to an upper limit _ jet 19^ . Bassi18 presented a detailed multi-wavelength study of IC 1531, and estimated the jet inclination angle to be _ jet = 15^ 5^ , consistent with this limit.
For IC 1531 and NGC 7075, no such information is available and we report both possible values.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1531 as a Fermi LAT FR-I with radio-galaxy and uncertain-blazar classifications, moderate viewing angle, SSC-modeled core emission, and modest Doppler factor.

quotes.quote: IC 1531 is classified as a radio galaxy in abd20 , but as a blazar of uncertain type in the third source catalog (3FGL) ace15 . bas18 used the multiband data to investigate its nature and jet properties, and concluded that IC 1531 is seen at moderate angles ( =10 20 ) and presented a moderate Doppler beaming effect in the flux emission. They also assembled the multiband data to fit the SED of the core of IC 1531, in which the ray flux of IC 1531 can be explained by the SSC model with 4 . When a viewing angle of =15 is adopted bas18 , the SED-derived Doppler factor corresponds to 3.86 . For IC 1531, our Doppler factor estimation ( _ =1.63 ) is smaller than the SED-derived Doppler factor (3.86) from bas18 .

gmw_00006962.name: NGC 7075
gmw_00006962.overview: NGC 7075 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:2010.04685] that hosts a barely resolved carbon monoxide disc [arXiv:1908.09229, arXiv:2010.04685]. This disc exhibits morphological and kinematical disturbances [arXiv:1908.09229], while the galaxy's inner radio jets are well resolved [arXiv:2010.04685].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7075 has a barely resolved CO disc with model residuals, an unreproduced PVD asymmetry, a gas-distribution hole, and likely disturbance.

quotes.quote: NGC 7075 The CO disc in NGC 7075 is barely resolved by our ALMA observations (see Paper I). Our best-fitting basic model shows no significant departures from the observations, although some residuals are present (Fig. fig:NGC7075_mom1_res ). Interestingly, there is an asymmetry in the PVD that is not reproduced by the model (Fig. fig:NGC7075_PVD ).

Furthermore, a hole is visible in the gas distribution at positive velocities. These features indicate that some disturbance must be occurring in the gas disc (both morphologically and kinematically), but higher resolution observations would be needed to investigate the origin of the observed structures.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7075 as a CO-detected FR I LERG with well-resolved inner jets, marginal counter-jet emission, and an ambiguous near/far CO-disc orientation.

quotes.quote: Five of the six sources detected in ^ 12 CO(2-1) were found to satisfy these criteria: IC 1531, NGC 3100, NGC 3557, IC 4296 and NGC 7075 (NGC 612 was excluded as we found no evidence for small-scale jet structure). Four of these sources show straight FR I jet structures, allowing us to estimate jet inclinations and, in turn, intrinsic jet--CO disc orientations as described in Section sec:method .

The method outlined above was applied to NGC 3557 and NGC 7075 using our new 10-GHz images. In these cases, the inner FR I jet structure is well resolved, although in NGC 7075 emission from the counter-jet is only marginally visible at the flaring point (Figs. fig:ngc3557_boxes and fig:ngc7075_boxes ).
For IC 1531 and NGC 7075, no such information is available and we report both possible values.

gmw_00006963.name: And XIV
gmw_00006963.overview: And XIV is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M31 that exhibits extreme kinematics, with a high line-of-sight velocity near the escape velocity of its host [arXiv:1211.2638]. It hosts a balanced horizontal branch and is more metal-poor than expected, containing a substantial fraction of old, metal-poor stars [arXiv:2004.03425].

sources.summary: The paper describes And XIV as an extreme-velocity M31 satellite near escape speed, poorly fit by the smooth model, with multiple pericentric passages still possible.

quotes.quote: Two of the entourage, And XII martin2006, chapman2007, collins2010 and And XIV majewski2007, kalirai2010 , are unusual in that they have extreme kinematics with high line-of-sight velocities. And XII may have been formed outside of the virial radius of the Local Group chapman2007 and only recently been accreted. Similarly, majewski2007 found that, even at its projected radius, And XIV is nearly at the M31 escape velocity, which implies that And XIV may also be on its first infall into the Local Group.

The period of And XII is estimated at T 3.5 Gyr and the period of And XIV is estimates at T 7 Gyr. Thus, for these models, And XIV is predicted to be on its second or third orbit, and And XII on its fourth; i.e. neither satellite is on its first infall into the M31 system. However, from fig:psat , we conclude that these satellites are unlikely for all of the models that we have considered, so it is unwise to put too much faith into these periods.
For And XIV, multiple pericentric passages are possible and improved distance information is needed to draw further conclusions.

sources.summary: The paper finds And XIV has a balanced horizontal branch, is more metal-poor than expected from the mass-metallicity relation, and likely contains many old metal-poor stars.

quotes.quote: And XIV in particular is interesting because it hosts a more balanced (i.e., a more equal number of red HB and blue HB stars) HB martin17 in comparison to the other dSphs in the low-mass regime. From our spectroscopic abundance measurements, we find that it is more metal-poor than one would naively expect following the mass-metallicity relation (MZR, Section sec:MZR ). These spectroscopic measurements corroborate the evidence from photometric data that And XIV hosts a more substantial fraction of old, metal-poor stars in comparison to other dSphs of similar mass.

gmw_00006964.name: MS1008-12-427
gmw_00006964.overview: MS1008-12-427 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy at z ~ 7 that exhibits two distinct components with differing ultraviolet colors [arXiv:2011.08857, arXiv:2111.14882]. This system displays extreme Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2011.08857, arXiv:2111.14882], which may be driven by a merger, a rejuvenation event, or an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2011.08857].

sources.summary: The paper confirms RELICS-DP7 at z=7.0281, finding two components, extreme Lyman-alpha equivalent width, red whole-galaxy UV slope, spatially extended Lyman-alpha, and possible merger or rejuvenation.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a spectroscopically-confirmed strong Lyman- emitter at z=7.0281 0.0003 , observed as part of the Reionization Cluster Lensing Survey (RELICS). This galaxy, dubbed ``Dichromatic Primeval Galaxy" at z 7 (DP7), shows two distinct components. While fairly unremarkable in terms of its ultraviolet (UV) luminosity ( 0.3L^ _ UV , where L^ _ UV is the characteristic luminosity), DP7 has one of the highest observed Lyman- equivalent widths (EWs) among Lyman- emitters at z>6 ( >200 \ in the rest frame). The strong Lyman- emission generally suggests a young metal-poor, low-dust galaxy; however, we find that the UV slope of the galaxy as a whole is redder than typical star-forming galaxies at these redshifts, 1.13 0.84 , likely indicating, on average, a considerable amount of dust obscuration, or an older stellar population.

When we measure for the two components separately, however, we find evidence of differing UV colors, suggesting two separate stellar populations. Also, we find that Lyman- is spatially extended and likely larger than the galaxy size, hinting to the possible existence of a Lyman- halo. Rejuvenation or merging events could explain these results. Either scenario requires an extreme stellar population, possibly including a component of Population III stars, or an obscured Active Galactic Nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper highlights MS1008-12-427/DP7 as a z 7 lensed galaxy with extreme Ly emission, possible two-component structure, and high star formation rate surface density.

quotes.quote: The majority of galaxies in this sample do not have sufficiently deep spectral data to have observed Ly emission with the exception of MS1008-12-427. This is the Dichromatic Primeval Galaxy at z 7 (DP7) discovered by . With extreme Ly emission ( >200 \ equivalent width) compared to other LAEs at z 7 (e.g., pentericci2014,hoag2019b,Fuller2020 ) , it has the potential to be a Lyman continuum leaker, or to be residing in an ionised bubble nearby a Lyman continuum leaker.

describe the possibility of DP7 being host to two distinct components, one dusty and red, and the other highly star forming and blue, and this possible merger or rejuvination event could lead to a large outflow, producing the strong Ly emission. The very red SED and distinct UV slopes suggest that a majority of the UV flux comes from the blue component. We measure the combined effective radius of both components, as the sources are blended, but the possibility of two components suggests that the measured size of this source may be that of two distinct objects. Reducing the effective radius by half and attributing all of the UV flux to the bluer component results in double the star formation rate surface density. With _ SFR 11^ +55 _ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 (kpc) ^ 2 , the SFR surface density of DP7 then falls within our selection criterion.

gmw_00006965.name: cD galaxy
gmw_00006965.overview: The central region of the Abell 1703 cluster is dominated by a giant elliptical cD galaxy [arXiv:0802.4292]. This massive central galaxy acts as a gravitational lens, with the high-redshift source Abell 1703 zD6 located behind it at a redshift of approximately 7.045 [arXiv:1107.1261].

sources.summary: The paper treats Abell 1703's central cD galaxy as a dominant elliptical whose stellar mass and light profile must be modeled and subtracted for the cluster lens analysis.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The reference centre of our analysis is fixed at the cD centre: R.A.=13:15:05.276, Decl.=+51:49:02.85 (J 2000.0).

quotes.quote: Abell 1703 is rather circular from the general shape of its multiply imaged systems and is dominated by a giant elliptical cD galaxy in its centre. This cluster exhibits a remarkable bright ' central ring ' formed by 4 bright images at z_ spec =0.888 only 5 13 away from the cD centre. This unique feature offers a rare lensing constraint to probe the central mass distribution. The stellar contribution from the cD galaxy ( 1.25 10 ^ 12 M _ within 30 kpc) is accounted for in our parametric mass modelling, and the underlying smooth dark matter component distribution is described using a generalized nfw profile parametrized with a central logarithmic slope .

Abell 1703 exhibits a dominant central giant elliptical galaxy: its stellar contribution to the mass budget in the central part is to be taken properly into account. We worked out some of the cD galaxy properties from the broad band photometry. In particular, we are interested in computing its luminosities in the different filters in order to estimate its stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Abell 1703 zD6 as a lensed high-redshift source with a detected emission feature, independent nightly confirmation, and a Ly-alpha redshift near z=7.045.

quotes.quote: The two satisfactory detections refer to emission at 9045 2 \ for ERS 8496 in the LRIS mask and emission at 9780 \ 4 \ in the NIRSPEC exposure of the lensed source Abell 1703 zD6. Both objects are detected at 5 in our final exposures. In our 1D extraction of ERS 8496, the emission line has a FWHM of 9 1 , and displays an asymmetric profile with a steeply rising blue edge and slowly decaying red tail, characteristic features of Ly at high redshift. Because our spectral resolution is significantly lower (6.5 \ for NIRSPEC versus 4.1 \ for LRIS) in our spectrum of A1703 zD6, so we are unable to determine any line profile information.

The emission feature is seen independently in coadditions on two separate nights, indicating its reality. If both lines are Ly , the implied spectroscopic redshifts for ERS 8496 and Abell 1703 zD6 are z=6.441 0.002 and 7.045 0.004 , respectively, in excellent agreement with our photometric predictions of 6.52 and that of 7.0 derived by .
Under this assumption, taking the magnitude from the first filter in which the object is detected ( Y_ 098M for ERS 8496, and J_ 125 for 1703 zD6), we calculate EWs of 69 10 and 65 12 , respectively.

gmw_00006966.name: UGC 00568
gmw_00006966.overview: UGC 00568 is a low surface brightness galaxy that is barely visible in the far-ultraviolet band [arXiv:0803.3877]. Despite possessing a huge H I reservoir, the galaxy is extremely red and exhibits a spectral energy distribution similar to those of early-type galaxies [arXiv:0803.3877].

sources.summary: UGC 00568 is singled out as an unusually red LSB galaxy, barely visible in FUV, with an early-type-like SED despite a large H I reservoir.

quotes.quote: UGC 00568 is an exception: this galaxy is barely visible in the FUV image, and it was not possible to extract a reliable profile in this band. As a result, the FUV-NUV color corresponds only to the central 7 arcsec. The rest of the disk is probably also red since it is not detected in the FUV band.

This is the only object in our sample that shows a SED similar to those of early-type galaxies (Fig. figindividual ). Note that even if the magnitudes are integrated within a larger radius, the NUV-2MASS colors are still consistent with an early-type galaxy SED. This is our only case of extremely red LSB despite a huge HI reservoir.

gmw_00006967.name: Malin 1
gmw_00006967.overview: Malin 1 is classified as an early-type galaxy surrounded by a huge low surface brightness disk [arXiv:0803.3877]. Ultraviolet observations reveal a clear central region and diffuse outer-disk emission, which likely indicates recent star formation within the disk [arXiv:0803.3877].

sources.summary: The GALEX images show Malin 1 as an early-type galaxy with a huge LSB disk, visible central UV emission, and diffuse outer-disk regions from recent star formation.

quotes.quote: Recent studies suggested that Malin 1 is an early-type galaxy surrounded by a huge LSB disk sancisi07,barth07 . The UV images of Malin 1 show the central part of the galaxy very clearly. In the outer disk, we observe several diffuse emission regions, probably corresponding to relatively recent star formation within the LSB disk see also thilkerxuv .

gmw_00006968.name: UGC 3825
gmw_00006968.overview: UGC 3825 is an isolated, unbarred spiral galaxy with a symmetric grand-design structure [arXiv:1809.08048]. Its spiral arms exhibit a near-constant pattern speed consistent with a quasi-stationary density wave [arXiv:1809.08048].

sources.summary: The paper uses MaNGA stellar-population maps of UGC 3825 to measure spiral-arm offsets and finds a near-constant pattern speed consistent with a quasi-stationary density wave.

quotes.quote: Here, we use the detailed information on stellar populations of the grand-design spiral galaxy UGC 3825, extracted from spectral mapping, to measure the offset between young stars of a known age and the spiral arm in which they formed, allowing the first direct measure of _ P at a range of radii. The offset in this galaxy is found to be as expected for a pattern speed that varies little with radius, indicating consistency with a quasi-stationary density wave, and lending credence to this new method.

As a test case, we have selected the galaxy UGC 3825. This isolated system has a symmetric grand-design structure, which makes it a prime candidate for being the product of a global density wave of internal (rather than tidal) origin . According to the Galaxy Zoo citizen science project , it does not contain a bar, which might complicate the interpretation of its spiral structure, and it is at an ideal intermediate angle to the line of sight, allowing us both to identify its spiral structure and to measure the rotational motion of material via the Doppler shift.

The results of this analysis for UGC 3825, presented in Figure fig:Pattern_speed , are entirely consistent with the predictions of the density wave theory. At small radii, as expected from the qualitative analysis of offsets, matter is rotating faster than the derived pattern speed, but eventually the measured angular speed of material drops to where it is rotating at the same speed as the spiral pattern, a point known as the corotation resonance. The location of this resonance, at a radius of 6 kpc ( 0.6 R_ E ), is consistent with estimates for other galaxies using less direct techniques and with the predictions for a modal picture of quasi-stationary density waves .

gmw_00006969.name: Virgo UFD1
gmw_00006969.overview: Virgo UFD1 is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located in the intracluster field of the Virgo cluster core [arXiv:1410.2247]. It is a highly metal-poor system with no visible tidal features, suggesting it may be a fossil remnant of the universe's first galaxies [arXiv:1410.2247].

sources.summary: The paper reports Virgo UFD1 as an ultra-faint dwarf in Virgo with a narrow red giant branch, very low metallicity, and a possible first-galaxy fossil nature.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of an UFD in the intracluster field in the core of the Virgo cluster (Virgo UFD1), which is far from any massive galaxies. The color-magnitude diagram of the resolved stars in this galaxy shows a narrow red giant branch, similar to those of metal-poor globular clusters in the Milky Way. We estimate its distance by comparing the red giant branch with isochrones, and we obtain a value 16.4 0.4 Mpc. This shows that it is indeed a member of the Virgo cluster. From the color of the red giants we estimate its mean metallicity to be very low, [ Fe/H ] = -2.4 0.4 .

Its properties are similar to those of the Local Group analogs. No evidence of tidal features are found in this galaxy. Considering its narrow red giant branch with no asymptotic giant branch stars, low metallicity, and location, it may be a fossil remnant of the first galaxies.

gmw_00006970.name: GAMA 508421
gmw_00006970.overview: GAMA 508421 is a star-forming disk galaxy [arXiv:2005.04874]. It exhibits a high circular velocity of approximately 130 kilometers per second in its outer regions and a centralized peak in velocity dispersion [arXiv:2005.04874].

sources.summary: The paper revises GAMA 508421’s intrinsic velocity dispersion downward, attributing the earlier higher value to beam-smearing effects from its high outer circular velocity and central dispersion peak.

quotes.quote: Their remaining galaxy (GAMA 508421) was reported as _ gas = 87 44 . GAMA 508421 exhibits a high circular velocity in the outskirts of the SAMI FoV ( v 130 ) and a clear centralised peak in velocity dispersion that is typical of beam smearing affected galaxies. Our estimate for GAMA 508421 is = 22 . As such, we suspect that the reported velocity dispersion for GAMA 508421 is greater than it's intrinsic velocity dispersion. The discrepancy between Zhou2017 and our estimates, particularly with GAMA 508421, is most likely due to the different beam smearing corrections.

gmw_00006971.name: SDSS J075217.84+193542.2
gmw_00006971.overview: SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 is a blue quasar [arXiv:2202.11995] and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.11631] that exhibits soft X-ray variability [arXiv:2403.11319] and a 6.4-year optical quasi-periodic oscillation [arXiv:2202.11995]. These periodic variations suggest the galaxy hosts a central binary supermassive black hole system [arXiv:2403.11319, arXiv:2202.11995].

sources.summary: The paper tests SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 as a quasar with a binary black-hole nucleus, connecting Swift X-ray variability to a scaled secondary black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The BH duality of the quasar nucleus SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 (herein SDSS J0752) was recently proposed based on the observed strict periodicity of optical emission from the source. We tested this assumption using X-ray observations with Swift/XRT (2008--2010). We fitted the SDSS J075217 spectrum using a Comptonization model and discovered soft X-ray variability in the 0.3--10 keV energy range. We pursued a scenario in which two supermassive BHs at the center of SDSS J0752 form a pair; and the less massive (secondary) BH periodically crosses/punctures the disk around the more massive (primary) BH. We associate these periodic crossings with tidal disruptions of the disk and, as a consequence, with an increase in X-rays seen as a flare in SDSS J0752.

In addition, we discovered saturation of the photon index with the mass accretion rate in SDSS J0752, which allowed us to scale the secondary black hole in this object using the scaling method. We thus determine mass of 9 10 ^7 M _ for the secondary BH. We have shown that a scenario in which two supermassive black holes at the center of the quasar SDSS J0752 form an orbital pair, and the less massive secondary BH periodically crosses/pierces the disk around the more massive primary BH, can be applied to SDSS J0752.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 as a blue quasar with 6.4-year optical QPOs, arguing they indicate a central binary black hole system.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, a 6.4yr optical quasi-periodic oscillations (QPOs) is detected in the quasar SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 (= ) at a redshift 0.117, of which 13.6yr-long light curve from CSS and ASAS-SN directly described by a sinusoidal function with a periodicity 6.4yr. The 6.4yr QPOs can be further confirmed through the Generalized Lomb-Scargle periodogram with confidence level higher than 99.99 , and through the auto-correlation analysis results, and through the WWZ technique. The optical QPOs strongly indicate a central binary black hole (BBH) system in . The determined two broad Gaussian components in the broad H can lead to the BBH system with expected space separation about 0.02pc between the expected two central BHs with determined virial BH masses about 8.8 10^7 M_ and 1.04 10^9 M_ .

In this manuscript, a new BBH candidate is reported in the blue quasar SDSS J075217.84+193542.2 (= ) at a redshift 0.117 pa18 , due to detected optical QPOs through the long-term variabilities from the Catalina Sky Survey (CSS) dr09 and from the All-Sky Automated Survey for Supernovae (ASAS-SN) sp14, ks17 . The shown time duration of the is two times longer than the detected periodicity, indicating the optical QPOs in should be robust to some extent.

sources.summary: The paper lists J 0752 as a [NeV] 3427-emitting CL galaxy at z=0.12 with CLD 7.1 kpc, limited central continuity, and AGN classification through the [NeV] sample.

quotes.quote: The remaining four CL galaxies, J 0752 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ), J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ), J 1714 ( [ NeV ] 3347 ), and J 2051 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ) lack continuous CL emission within a 2. ^ 5 x 2. ^ 5 FoV.
We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 .

We show the [ NeV ] 3427 flux maps for J 0736 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 5.4 2.7 kpc), J 0752 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 7.1 2.7), J 2051 ( z = 0.11; spiral; CLD = 2.5 2.5 kpc), and J 2116 ( z = 0.08; spiral; CLD = 4.3 1.9 kpc) in Figure fevmaps . In each galaxy, [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 emission is predominantly concentrated within the galactic interior (within 2.^ 5 throughout the paper), suggesting that a central source governs CL production in these galaxies. We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.

gmw_00006972.name: NGC 5354
gmw_00006972.overview: NGC 5354 is a galaxy located approximately 70 arcseconds from its companion, NGC 5353 [arXiv:astro-ph/0406011]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with an unresolved radio core and symmetric, double-sided jet-like emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0401593].

sources.summary: The paper finds an unresolved VLA core in NGC 5354 and symmetric VLBA emission, interpreted as jet-like structure around an AGN core.

quotes.quote: The present new and published VLBA data show unresolved cores in NGC 524 (Fig. 1 a ), NGC 4552 (Nagar 2002) and NGC 5354 (Fig. 1 b, ). Some sources also show evidence of radio variability, as seems the case for NGC 4552 (see discussion in Filho, Barthel Ho 2000). Furthermore, all VLBA detected sample sources (including NGC 4552; Nagar 2002 and NGC 5866; Falcke 2000) show flat spectrum radio cores, although the inverted spectrum in NGC 524 is most probably an effect of resolution mismatch. The origin of the mas-scale radio emission is unlikely to be free-free emission. The detected radio cores show brightness temperatures several orders of magnitude above the thermal limit (10 ^4  K; Condon 1992).

Our 0 2 resolution VLA map of NGC 5354 shows an unresolved core, whereas our longer integration VLBA map of this source shows about 4 mJy in symmetric double-sided emission to the East and West (Fig. 1 ), suggesting the presence of jets cradling an AGN core.

sources.summary: The paper resolves NGC 5354 from NGC 5353 in ISO data, separately extracts its far-infrared fluxes, and treats the companion galaxies as individual FIR sources.

quotes.quote: The top panels in Figure fig:arrays show that the galaxies NGC 5353 and NGC 5354 are only about 70 apart, with the latter being in the north. This is sufficiently close for the two galaxies to be covered by the ISOPHOT C100 and C200 arrays, that have 3 3 and 2 2 pixels of size 46 and 92 each, respectively, and to allow the galaxies to be resolved on the C100 array. As shown on the left panels in Figure fig:arrays , NGC 5353 and NGC 5354 were respectively observed exactly with the center and corner pixels of the C100 array, ensuring that the galaxies would be clearly resolved from each other on this array. Those C100 pixels which are not centered on the two sources show some low-level flux (bottom-left histogram of Figure fig:arrays ), which stems from the wings of the point spread function, primarily of the central source. The same applies for the observations at 60 m. The right panels of Figure fig:arrays show that at 180 m NGC 5353 is centered on the 2 2 pixels of C200 array, so that its flux is distributed over all four pixels, and NGC 5354 is located on the corner pixel containing most of its flux.

gmw_00006973.name: NGC 4039
gmw_00006973.overview: NGC 4039 is the southern component of the interacting Antennae galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377, arXiv:1211.0767], hosting a mild nuclear starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377] and an off-center star formation peak [arXiv:2201.02270]. Its heavily disturbed disk is connected to a newly discovered southern molecular gas arm of tidal origin [arXiv:1211.0767, arXiv:astro-ph/0702341].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4039 as the southern Antennae nucleus, describes its dust, H emission, colors, and interprets its X-ray spectrum as hot ISM plus X-ray binaries.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of NGC 4039, the southern component of the Antennae, is much easier to recognize in the V and I images (see Fig. f:n4039 ). Our identified nuclear position (the position of the optical peak) in Table t:sample agrees to within about two arcseconds of the estimated nuclear positions from the radio continuum data zhang01 and near-infrared data meng01 . The nucleus is surrounded by dust patches and dust lanes. There is also a minor peak of H emission at the location of the nucleus, which could be due to continuum subtraction uncertainties. Much more intense H emission is seen in the arm connecting to the nucleus.

The V-I color of the nucleus of NGC 4039 is about 1.0, and the surrounding area has a color around 1.3, with values of 1.7 in a dusty region close to the nucleus. th Neither NGC 4038 nor NGC 4039 is classified as a Seyfert or LINER nucleus. These nuclei are instead hosts of mild starbursts e.g., daha85,ver86 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a newly discovered tidal molecular gas arm south of NGC 4039, measures its clumpy CO structure and high star-formation efficiency, and links it kinematically to NGC 4039.

quotes.quote: We report the properties of a newly discovered molecular gas arm of likely tidal origin at the south of NGC 4039 and the overlap region in the Antennae galaxies, with a resolution of 1 68 0 85, using the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array science verification CO(2--1) data. The arm extends 3.4 kpc (34 ) and is characterized by widths of 200 pc (2 ) and velocity widths of typically V 10--20 . About 10 clumps are strung out along this structure, most of them unresolved, with average surface densities of _ gas 10--100 M_ pc ^ 2 , and masses of (1--8) 10 ^6 M_ . These structures resemble the morphology of beads on a string, with an almost equidistant separation between the beads of about 350 pc, which may represent a characteristic separation scale for giant molecular associations. We find that the star formation efficiency at a resolution of 6 (600 pc) is in general a factor of 10 higher than in disk galaxies and other tidal arms and bridges.

That the molecular arm is a single entity linked to the spiral arm of NGC 4039 is proven by the smooth and continuous velocity change along its different components in the diagram shown in Figure fig3 . Due to the link in terms of distribution and kinematics with the spiral arm of NGC 4039, we believe that the most plausible explanation is that the molecular arm is of tidal origin, presumably caused by the recent passage of the northern galaxy, NGC 4038.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4039 has broader, non-Gaussian CO spectra, a larger velocity gradient, a CO bridge to the interaction region, and stronger disturbance than NGC 4038.

quotes.quote: The spectra attributed to NGC 4039 (nucleus centred at 1665 km s ^ 1 ) are mostly non-Gaussian and partly much broader than those of NGC 4038.
