The observed velocity gradient across the disk from 1640 (east) to 1800 (southwest) is larger than in NGC 4038. Several spectra show more than one emission component; a bridge of CO connects NGC 4039 to the IAR.

The disk of NGC 4039 therefore appears to us more edge-on than that of NGC 4038 and/or is more heavily disturbed by the interaction, the latter assumption being supported by the positional overlap with the IAR in the east. If part of the gradient is due to rotation, NGC 4039 also rotates counterclockwise. At the southwestern edge we see some indication of a spiral arm.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4039's southern nucleus with X-ray source 29, measuring high X-ray luminosity, a steep photon index, and an added thermal component associated with extended emission.

quotes.quote: The southern nucleus: NGC 4039 We identify the southern nucleus as our source 29 based on its radio position. It has an X-ray luminosity of 7.7 10^ 39 and a steep photon index of 3.3^ +0.5 _ 0.4 . The inclusion of a thermal component improves the fit and gives a relatively absorbed power-law ( 0.14 10^ 22 ) with a steep slope ( 2.14 ). The temperature of the thermal component is 0.77 keV. The existence of the additional thermal component is consistent with the fact that an extended component is associated with this source.

sources.summary: The paper excludes the compact central X-ray source in NGC 4039, discussing literature arguments against an AGN and favoring an X-ray binary or likely LMXB in the nuclear star cluster.

quotes.quote: In addition to these three galaxies we also decided to exclude the compact X-ray source in the center of NGC 4039, although its nature may be somewhat controversial. 2009ApJ...699.1982B ruled out the presence of an AGN in this galaxy, based on the lack of [ Ne V ] emission and the low [ O IV ] / [ Ne II ] ratio. Moreover, 2009ApJ...690..267D suggested that the X-ray spectrum is indicative of an X-ray binary in the very high state, rather than of an AGN. However, they pointed out that location of this source in the nuclear star cluster makes it a likely LMXB candidate, rather than an HMXB.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4039 as an Antennae component with high sSFR, exceptional galaxy-scale Kennicutt-Schmidt behavior, and an off-center SFR peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 4038 and NGC 4039, both of which are a part of the Antennae Galaxies, have sSFR higher than 1.0 10 ^ 9 yr ^ 1 .
The exceptional galaxy, NGC 4039, is the constituent galaxy of the Antennae Galaxies.
A peak of SFR in NGC 4038 (top) is at its centre, while a peak of NGC 4039 (bottom) is off the centre.

gmw_00006974.name: IGR J18457 + 0244
gmw_00006974.overview: IGR J18457 + 0244 is a highly absorbed source located at a redshift of approximately 0.07 [arXiv:1204.3645]. It is classified as a candidate Seyfert-2 active galactic nucleus, though a periodic signal could alternatively indicate a slowly rotating neutron star [arXiv:1204.3645].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IGR J18457 + 0244 as a highly absorbed candidate Seyfert-2 AGN at redshift 0.07, but notes that its periodic signal could instead imply a neutron-star source.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The X-ray position that we obtain for IGR J18457 + 0244 is R.A. =18^ h 45^ m 40 s . 30 and Dec. = +02^ 42^ 11 . 2 .

quotes.quote: IGR J18457 + 0244 is a highly-absorbed ( \ = 8 10^ 23 ) source in which the possible detection of an iron line suggests an active galactic nucleus (AGN) of type Sey-2 situated at z = 0.07(1) . A periodic signal at 4.4 ks could be a quasi-periodic oscillation which would make IGR J18457 + 0244 one of a handful of AGN in which such features have been claimed, but a slowly-rotating neutron star in an HMXB can not be ruled out.

Assuming that the rest energy of the iron K line is 6.41 keV, this would place IGR J18457 + 0244 at a redshift of z = 0.07(1) , suggesting an active galactic nucleus (AGN) most likely of the Seyfert-2 class given its large absorbing column. The corresponding (unabsorbed) X-ray luminosity in the 0.5--10-keV band is (9_ 2 ^ +3 ) 10^ 43 , assuming H_ = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and a flat cosmology. We point out that other than the high \ value (which can also be found among obscured X-ray pulsars), the main evidence supporting the AGN designation is the iron line energy, and this detection is marginal since only 2 spectral bins in pn show significant deviations ( 3 ) from the continuum (1 bin for each MOS detector). On the other hand, if the potential periodic modulation can be reproduced in other observations, and if the signal were found to be coherent, it would point instead to a neutron star spin period, and the AGN would no longer be a viable explanation.

gmw_00006975.name: P11-61A
gmw_00006975.overview: P11-61A is a galaxy classified as a Seyfert using classical single-fiber and resolved diagnostic diagrams [arXiv:1410.7781]. However, alternative analysis suggests its nuclear region is dominated by hot evolved stars rather than active galactic nucleus ionization [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper confirms P11-61A as Seyfert-like in classical resolved BPT diagnostics, while WHAN analysis suggests its nucleus is dominated by hot evolved stars rather than AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: P11-61A is the only galaxy in which we confirm the SDSS Seyfert classification. In three galaxies we detect extended LINER-like ionisation, traced by both diagnostic diagrams, extending out to 0.5-1 R_ e . Finally, three galaxies have a peculiar ionisation structure and we classify them as `Composite', since they present both LINER-like line ratios and SF-dominated regions, but the spatial distribution of the ionisation is more varied. We summarise the differences between the Sloan single fibre classification and the resolved P-MaNGA analysis in Table table2 . In the Appendix we show BPT diagrams and maps of the ionisation conditions as derived from the BPT diagrams for all the galaxies, together with line ratio maps ( [ NII ] /H and [ OIII ] 5007 ). Resolved Seyfert-like Ionisation in a Star Forming Disc In Fig. example_BPT we show the [ NII ] and [ SII ] BPT diagrams for galaxy P11-61A. Points in the BPT diagram correspond to individual Voronoi bins (and are colour-coded according to the demarcation lines shown). The open circle corresponds to the line ratios obtained from the Sloan 3'' diameter fibre (emission line fluxes from the MPA-JHU DR7 catalogue, based on Brinchmann2004, Kauffmann2003 The MPA-JHU catalogue is accessible at http://www.mpa-garching.mpg.de/SDSS/DR7/ ). We note that in both the [ NII ] and [ SII ] BPT diagrams the galaxy is classified as a Seyfert using the Sloan spectra.

We note that using this revised classification the nuclear regions of P9-127A, P11-61A, P4-127A and P4-127B are all classified as dominated by hot evolved stars, with no AGN signature. Interestingly the external regions of P9-61A, which are classified as LINER-like in the classical BPT diagram, present low EW(H ) and are consistent with our preferred interpretation of extraplanar DIG. Echoing the remark in we note that AGN might still be present in the nuclei of other galaxies (especially in P9-127A, which shows an increase in EW(H ) in the nuclear regions, and P11-61A, which is classified as AGN using the classical BPT diagram), but, according to this classification scheme, the AGN does not constitute the major contribution to the ionising photon flux.

gmw_00006976.name: P9-127A
gmw_00006976.overview: P9-127A, also known as NGC 2916, is a massive, unbarred spiral disk galaxy [arXiv:1410.7781, arXiv:1903.04818] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1903.04818, arXiv:1304.5788]. The galaxy exhibits a lopsided stellar component [arXiv:1903.04818, arXiv:1304.5788], a twisted ionized-gas disk [arXiv:1903.04818], and active star formation in its outer regions [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P9-127A as an extended central LINER-like system whose nuclear emission is reclassified as hot-star dominated, with gas-flow signatures in N/O versus O/H.

quotes.quote: Extended Central LINER-like Ionisation Three galaxies in our sample (P9-127A, P4-127A, P4-127B) show extended central LINER-like ionisation. Sloan single fibre fluxes (from the MPA-JHU catalogue) classify two of these galaxies as Seyfert-like, while P4-127A has an ambiguous classification. However, considering the errors associated with the emission line ratios (and the intrinsic porosity of the LINER-Seyfert demarcation line), the Sloan line ratios are also consistent with a LINER-like classification. We show the BPT-derived maps of these galaxies using P-MaNGA data in Fig. example_BPT_seyfert (top three galaxies, with the same colour-coding of regions as in Fig. example_BPT ). Both in P9-127A and P4-127B the LINER-like ionisation dominates within 0.5 \ R_e , while in the case of P4-127A, the LINER-like ionisation extends from the central regions out to galactocentric distances larger than 1.0 \ R_e . Interestingly, all three LINER-like galaxies are massive disc galaxies, ( log M_ /M_ > 10 , masses from MPA-JHU DR7 catalogue), and host active star formation in regions at large galactocentric radii.

We note that using this revised classification the nuclear regions of P9-127A, P11-61A, P4-127A and P4-127B are all classified as dominated by hot evolved stars, with no AGN signature. Interestingly the external regions of P9-61A, which are classified as LINER-like in the classical BPT diagram, present low EW(H ) and are consistent with our preferred interpretation of extraplanar DIG. Echoing the remark in we note that AGN might still be present in the nuclei of other galaxies (especially in P9-127A, which shows an increase in EW(H ) in the nuclear regions, and P11-61A, which is classified as AGN using the classical BPT diagram), but, according to this classification scheme, the AGN does not constitute the major contribution to the ionising photon flux.

Within our sample the only exception is P9-127A, where both the metallicity and _ SFR gradient are positive between 0.4 and 0.7 R_e (the largest radius sampled by the P-MaNGA observations). This is not completely surprising if we consider that for this galaxy we have limited radial sampling (in terms of R_e ), but better resolution, in terms of kpc, than the MaNGA survey data. Moreover we have checked that, taking advantage of the CALIFA IFS data, a negative metallicity gradient is observed in P9-127A at larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC2916 as the unbarred active twin, reporting a twisted ionized-gas disk, lopsided stellar component, patchy spiral-arm emission, and older central stellar populations.

quotes.quote: From these 15 AGN, we selected the two best matching in redshift, stellar mass, inclination and morphology, but differing in the presence of a bar, resulting NGC0214 (AGN-bar, hereafter) and NGC2916 (AGN-nbar, hereafter). These galaxies are low-luminosity AGN and hence will have low impact AGN feedback on the circumnuclear region, making them ideal targets to look for triggering imprints. We then selected the barred and unbarred galaxies among the remaining 389 objects located at the star-forming region (SF) in the BPT diagram best matching the galaxy properties listed above.

In the case of NGC2916 the difference between the inclinations estimated from the approaching and receding sides of the velocity field remains when we mask the inner 12 arcsec (6.7 3.4 ) suggesting a twisted disk. Therefore, we report an internal twist in the ionised gas disk of the unbarred AGN that is not evident in either of its twins. This internal twist in NGC2916 could be the imprint of the disk instability needed to promote the inflow of gas from large scales to the inner region and trigger the AGN.

NGC2916 (AGN-nbar) The stellar continuum map (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC2916_stars ) peaks at the nucleus and the H map (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC2916_ha ) shows weak emission in the central 3-4 kpc and stronger patchy emission tracing the spiral arms. Both the stellar and ionised gas velocity fields are dominated by rotation at large scales (to middle panels of Figs. fig:NGC2916_stars and fig:NGC2916_ha ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2916 to demonstrate spatially resolved stellar-population synthesis, describing its blue disk, intermediate mass, lopsidedness, satellite interaction, and low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: The Sb galaxy NGC 2916 (CALIFA 277) was chosen as a show-case. For the adopted distance of 56 Mpc, 1^ ( = 1 CALIFA spaxel) corresponds to 270 pc. With an band absolute magnitude of 21.17 , u- = 2.18 and g-=0.54 colors, and concentration index C = 2.16 , NGC 2916 can be characterized as a blue-disk galaxy with an intermediate mass ( 10^ 11 M ). Rudnick, Rix Kennicutt (2000) have shown that the galaxy show some degree of lopsidedness, indicating a weak interaction (possibly with its irregular satellite 5^ away, Guti errez, Azzaro Prada 2002) or a minor merger. Moorthy Holtzman (2006) have obtained long-slit spectra and measured emission lines and Lick indices along the position angle PA = -80^ .
Despite its active nucleus, the nuclear line fluxes are not particularly bright, implying a low luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2916/CALIFA 277 as the nearby spiral datacube for uncertainty simulations, finding robust radial stellar-population profiles and age/metallicity gradients.

quotes.quote: The products of this work flow were examplified using data for the nearby spiral NGC 2916 (CALIFA 277). Diagnostics such as 2D maps of physical properties (mean ages and metallicities, mass surface densities, star formation rates, etc.), 1D averages in the temporal and spatial dimensions, projections of the stellar light and mass growth onto radius-age diagrams, and cuts through the (x,y, age ) cubes were presented to illustrate the potential of the combination of IFS data with spectral synthesis as a tool to study galaxy evolution in time and space simultaneously.

The simulations are based on the data and \ fits of 1638 zone spectra of the Sb galaxy CALIFA 277 (NGC 2916). Uncertainties in mean age, metallicity, extincion, stellar mass and SFHs were evaluated both at the level individual zones (treating the datacube as a collection of unrelated spectra) and exploring the statistical power of IFS by means of radial profiles and other spatial averages.
For instance, uncertainties in the data and limitations of the synthesis have a negligible impact upon the negative age and metallicity gradients detected in CALIFA 277.

gmw_00006977.name: P4-127A
gmw_00006977.overview: P4-127A is a massive disc galaxy with active star formation occurring at large galactocentric radii [arXiv:1410.7781]. The galaxy exhibits extended central LINER-like ionization, though its nuclear region is classified as dominated by hot evolved stars rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper treats P4-127A as a massive disc galaxy with extended LINER-like ionization beyond one effective radius, later favoring hot evolved stars over AGN as the dominant ionizing source.

gmw_00006978.name: P4-127B
gmw_00006978.overview: P4-127B is a massive disk galaxy with a stellar mass exceeding 10 billion solar masses [arXiv:1410.7781]. The galaxy features extended central LINER-like ionization dominated by hot evolved stars, while active star formation occurs at larger galactocentric radii [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper describes P4-127B as a massive disc galaxy with central LINER-like ionization, outer star formation, hot-star-dominated nuclear emission, and gas-flow-like abundance deviations.

quotes.quote: Extended Central LINER-like Ionisation Three galaxies in our sample (P9-127A, P4-127A, P4-127B) show extended central LINER-like ionisation. Sloan single fibre fluxes (from the MPA-JHU catalogue) classify two of these galaxies as Seyfert-like, while P4-127A has an ambiguous classification. However, considering the errors associated with the emission line ratios (and the intrinsic porosity of the LINER-Seyfert demarcation line), the Sloan line ratios are also consistent with a LINER-like classification. We show the BPT-derived maps of these galaxies using P-MaNGA data in Fig. example_BPT_seyfert (top three galaxies, with the same colour-coding of regions as in Fig. example_BPT ). Both in P9-127A and P4-127B the LINER-like ionisation dominates within 0.5 \ R_e , while in the case of P4-127A, the LINER-like ionisation extends from the central regions out to galactocentric distances larger than 1.0 \ R_e . We note that using this revised classification the nuclear regions of P9-127A, P11-61A, P4-127A and P4-127B are all classified as dominated by hot evolved stars, with no AGN signature.

In particular, P9-127A, P9-61A, P11-61A and P4-127B show departures from the main DR7 contour in the horizontal (O/H) direction, at roughly constant N/O. These deviations seem to form a sharp sequence.

gmw_00006979.name: P9-61A
gmw_00006979.overview: P9-61A is a highly inclined star-forming disk galaxy [arXiv:1410.7781]. Its extraplanar regions exhibit LINER-like ionization, which is interpreted as diffuse ionized gas rather than shocked outflowing or inflowing gas [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper describes P9-61A as a highly inclined star-forming disc with extraplanar LINER-like emission, interpreted as diffuse ionized gas rather than shocked inflow or outflow.

quotes.quote: Extraplanar LINER-like Ionisation Galaxies P9-61A and P4-19B (see Fig. example_BPT_seyfert ) present peculiar ionisation conditions, as traced by the BPT diagrams, displaying a star formation dominated (highly inclined) disc and LINER-like ionisation in the extraplanar direction. We suggest these line ratios can be interpreted as signature of diffuse ionised gas (DIG, i.e. gas ionised by stellar radiation, likely from O and B stars in the disc), which dominates the ionised gas budget in extraplanar regions of low continuum emission. This observation shows that using MaNGA data for highly inclined/edge on galaxies we might be able to shed new light on the disc-halo interaction. We see no signature of kinematic distortion or increase in velocity dispersion in these regions in either P9-61A or P4-19B, at least within the relatively large errors associated with emission line fitting in these regions of low S/N. This argues against the identification of the LINER-like emission in the outer regions with shocked outflowing (or inflowing) gas.

Interestingly the external regions of P9-61A, which are classified as LINER-like in the classical BPT diagram, present low EW(H ) and are consistent with our preferred interpretation of extraplanar DIG.

gmw_00006980.name: P4-19B
gmw_00006980.overview: P4-19B is a highly inclined disk galaxy that exhibits active star formation within its disk [arXiv:1410.7781]. The galaxy displays extraplanar LINER-like emission, which is likely caused by diffuse ionized gas rather than kinematic shocks [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper groups P4-19B with P9-61A as a highly inclined star-forming disc showing extraplanar LINER-like emission consistent with diffuse ionized gas, not kinematic shocks.

quotes.quote: Extraplanar LINER-like Ionisation Galaxies P9-61A and P4-19B (see Fig. example_BPT_seyfert ) present peculiar ionisation conditions, as traced by the BPT diagrams, displaying a star formation dominated (highly inclined) disc and LINER-like ionisation in the extraplanar direction. We suggest these line ratios can be interpreted as signature of diffuse ionised gas (DIG, i.e. gas ionised by stellar radiation, likely from O and B stars in the disc), which dominates the ionised gas budget in extraplanar regions of low continuum emission. This observation shows that using MaNGA data for highly inclined/edge on galaxies we might be able to shed new light on the disc-halo interaction. We see no signature of kinematic distortion or increase in velocity dispersion in these regions in either P9-61A or P4-19B, at least within the relatively large errors associated with emission line fitting in these regions of low S/N. This argues against the identification of the LINER-like emission in the outer regions with shocked outflowing (or inflowing) gas.

gmw_00006981.name: P11-19C
gmw_00006981.overview: P11-19C is an emission-line galaxy that is currently experiencing a starburst [arXiv:1410.7781]. It exhibits nitrogen enhancement and a spatial distribution of gas consistent with a galactic fountain scenario [arXiv:1410.7781].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P11-19C as the sample galaxy with the highest SFR, likely experiencing a starburst, and showing N/O versus O/H behavior consistent with gas-flow or enrichment scenarios.

quotes.quote: It is worth commenting here on the galaxy with the highest SFR in our sample: P11-19C (light green in Fig. met_SFR , SFR 12 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 ). Most of the bins in this galaxy are classified as intermediate using the [ NII ] BPT, while in the [ SII ] BPT it lies safely inside the SF sequence. Its median EW(H ) is 10^2 (in fact these points are clearly visible in the [ NII ] BPT colour coded by EW(H ) in Fig. BPT_tot as the blue points just across the K03 line) corroborating our conjecture that this system is experiencing a starburst. We suggest that the reason why this galaxy lies so much above the SF sequence in the [ NII ] BPT is related to its nitrogen enhancement, as will be discussed in the next section.

Galaxies such as P9-19E and P11-19C show a different deviation from the DR7 contour with respect to the galaxies we mentioned above. Most of the spaxels in these two galaxies lie just above the DR7 contour and do not create a horizontal sequence in the N/O vs O/H plane. In both galaxies (but more clearly in P11-19C) we can also see that spaxels with the largest offset from the DR7 contour have the lowest D_n(4000) . The most deviating points are also the most distant from the centre (as shown in the bottom right panel of Fig. nitro_model ). This effect could be explained in the context of the galactic fountain scenario discussed above, i.e. by the mixing of a small amount of metal rich gas from central regions into the outer, younger (more gas rich) and metal poor regions of the disc.

gmw_00006982.name: host (galaxy) of FRB 20190714A
aliases_external: host galaxy / persistent radio source associated with FRB 20190714A
gmw_00006982.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 20190714A is located at a redshift of z=0.2365 [arXiv:2201.00069]. It is associated with diffuse radio continuum emission, which was detected using both MeerKAT and e-MERLIN observations [arXiv:2201.00069].

sources.summary: The paper reports MeerKAT and e-MERLIN persistent radio emission associated with the host galaxy of FRB 20190714A at z=0.2365, arguing the association is unlikely by chance.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the target pointing centre was R.A. = 12 ^h 15 ^m 55 ^s .12, Dec. = -13^ 01 15 7

quotes.quote: We obtain a radio upper limit of 15 Jy beam ^ 1 for persistent emission at the locations of both FRBs 20190711A and 20171019A, but detect diffuse radio emission with a peak brightness of 53 Jy beam ^ 1 associated with FRB 20190714A at z=0.2365 . This represents the first detection of the radio continuum emission potentially associated with the host (galaxy) of FRB 20190714A, and is only the third known FRB to have such an association. Given the possible association of a faint persistent source, FRB 20190714A may potentially be a repeating FRB whose age lies between that of FRB 20121102A and FRB 20180916A.

Using this spatial coincidence criterion, we identified a persistent 1283 MHz continuum source near FRB 20190714A, detected in both the 14 September 2019 and the 28 September 2019 epoch. The peak of the MeerKAT radio emission is offset by 2 ^ .1 from the peak of the i band magnitude of the optical galaxy identified in the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System (PanSTARRS, located at Haleakala Observatory) image (shown as contours in Figures fig:190714_e1 and fig:190714_e2 ). The MeerKAT radio source is offset by 1 68 from the localisation region of FRB 20190714 (cyan circle in Figures fig:190714_e1 and fig:190714_e2 ). e-MERLIN detection of compact emission towards FRB 20190714 Compact persistent emission was detected in the 1.51 GHz e-MERLIN image at R.A. = 12 ^h 15 ^m 55 ^s .116, Dec. = -13^ 01 14 48 at 86 Jy beam ^ 1 by e-MERLIN. The stochastic position uncertainty is (0.04, 0.15) arcsec and the uncertainty (due to the separation between phase-calibrator and target, and antenna position uncertainty) is (0.013, 0.056) arcsec, giving a total astrometric uncertainty of (0.04, 0.16) arcsec in R.A. and Dec., respectively. The offset from the FRB position is negligible in R.A. and 1.2 arcsec in Dec. The rms in this region (of full primary beam sensitivity) is 20 Jy beam ^ 1 , making this a 4.3 _ rms detection. It is 1.5 _ rms higher than that of the MeerKAT detection. Although the e-MERLIN flux scale nominal uncertainty is 5 , in these data it is possibly higher due to the low declination of the phase-reference source and to the strong RFI which were removed from the data but may have affected the linearity of the receiver response. The peak of the e-MERLIN radio emission is offset by 1 4 from the peak of the PanSTARRS i band emission in Figures fig:190714_e1 and fig:190714_e2 . The e-MERLIN radio source (shown by the cyan cross in Figures fig:190714_e1 and fig:190714_e2 ) is offset by 0 53 from the localised position of FRB 20190714. We estimate the probability of a chance alignment of a background persistent radio source and the host galaxy, following the procedure of EBWB18 . Instead of using the FRB localisation region, we use the area of the galaxy, which is taken as 2 ^ 2 ^ , twice the half light radius from Heintz . Given the source has a flux density of 90 Jy we estimate the chance alignment probability of 0.0008, which corresponds to 3.4 . The flux density threshold, assuming 3 , for an unresolved radio source is 15 Jy. If instead we consider the probability of detecting any radio source above our flux density threshold of 15 Jy, the probability of a chance alignment is, therefore, approximately 0.8 , making the statistical significance of our detection 2.6 . This represents the first detection of radio continuum emission associated with the host (galaxy) of FRB 20190714A (see Figure fig:190714_e1 and fig:190714_e2 ).

gmw_00006983.name: DLA J0212-0211
gmw_00006983.overview: DLA J0212-0211 is a luminous, metal-rich Lyman-break-like galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.096 [arXiv:1207.6088]. It exhibits an undisturbed morphology with a half-light radius of 1.2 kpc and shows no signs of galaxy interactions or mergers [arXiv:1207.6088].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DLA J0212-0211 as the z=3.096 counterpart of an intervening DLA, gives its position and impact parameter, and describes it as luminous, metal-rich, and LBG-like.

quotes.quote: we showed that the galaxy counterpart of the intervening DLA (object D3, z_ D3 =3.096 0.003 ) has an impact parameter of 7.9 kpc and an extinction-corrected R band magnitude of 24.41 0.02 mag (Fig. fig:fov_070721B , Table tab:candidates ). The co-ordinates are RA (J2000) =02^ h 12^ m 33 018 and DEC (J2000) =-02^ 11'40 99 with an uncertainty of 0 4 in each coordinate. We denote the object as DLA J0212-0211 in the following.

Chen2009a concludes that the DLA galaxy in the field of GRB 070721B is the most luminous DLA galaxy at z>2 . The morphology of the galaxy is undisturbed, based on the shape in the image, inset in Fig. fig:fov_070721B . We measure an ellipticity of 0.15 and a half-light radius of 1.2 kpc, obtained with SExtractor . The non-detection of a galaxy interaction or merger is not surprising. Overzier2010a argued that a detection is hampered at z 3 because of the reduced physical resolution and sensitivity in addition to the general difficulties of observing interacting galaxies.

DLA J0212-0211 is very metal rich, Fe / H > -0.21 . It does not differ from normal LBGs, with the exception of the large amount of H i in its halo.This supports a model in which DLAs can be gaseous halos of LBGs Fynbo1999a,Moller2002a,Fynbo2008a,Rafelski2011a .

gmw_00006984.name: PKS B0850-20
gmw_00006984.overview: PKS B0850-20 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of 1.337 [arXiv:1311.2989]. It has a derived radio spectral index of -0.77 and lacks any known high-energy X-ray or gamma-ray counterparts [arXiv:1311.2989].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS B0850-20 as a variable low-frequency radio galaxy counterpart with no known high-energy counterpart, redshift 1.337, and a measured radio spectral index.

quotes.quote: MWA J0852-2048 : Identified as radio galaxy PKS B0850-20. There is no known X-ray, ray counterpart. The radio galaxy is at a redshift z=1.337 Phil_Best . The spectral index derived from a best fit to the VLSS, MWA and NVSS data points is =-0.77 .

gmw_00006985.name: QSO B0919-142
gmw_00006985.overview: QSO B0919-142 is a variable quasar located at a redshift of 0.305 [arXiv:1311.2989]. It has a fitted low-frequency spectral index of -0.6 and lacks any known high-energy X-ray or gamma-ray counterpart [arXiv:1311.2989].

sources.summary: The paper identifies QSO B0919-142 as a variable quasar source, giving its low-frequency spectral index, redshift 0.305, fitted spectral index, and lack of known high-energy counterpart.

quotes.quote: MWA J0922-1428 : Identified as the quasar QSO B0919-142 with spectral index between 408 MHz and 1410 MHz of =-0.83 Quiniento . The Quasar is at a redshift z=0.305 1993MNRAS.263..999T . The spectral index derived from a best fit to the VLSS, MWA and NVSS data points is =-0.6 . There is no known X-ray or ray counterpart.

gmw_00006986.name: LEDA 2826147
gmw_00006986.overview: LEDA 2826147 is a radio galaxy that is consistent with the X-ray source 1RXS J100511.0-214500 [arXiv:1311.2989]. It exhibits a steep radio spectral index of -1.2 derived from VLSS, MWA, and NVSS data [arXiv:1311.2989].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 2826147 as a radio galaxy variable-source counterpart, notes consistency with an X-ray source, and derives a steep radio spectral index.

quotes.quote: MWA J1005-2148 : Identified as radio galaxy LEDA 2826147 LEDA . This source is also consistent with the X-ray source 1RXS J100511.0-214500. The spectral index derived from a best fit to the VLSS, MWA and NVSS data points is =-1.2 .

gmw_00006987.name: LEDA 2825560
gmw_00006987.overview: LEDA 2825560 is an active galactic nucleus associated with the radio source MWA J0837-1950 [arXiv:1311.2989]. The source exhibits a fairly flat radio spectral index and displays long-term variability that is consistent with intrinsic active galactic nucleus behavior or refractive scintillation [arXiv:1311.2989].

sources.summary: The paper associates LEDA 2825560 with a year-timescale variable AGN source, reporting its fractional variability, flat radio spectral index, and plausible intrinsic or scintillation-driven variability.

quotes.quote: The source MWA J0837-1950 had a fractional variability f=28.6 . This source is associated with an AGN (LEDA 2825560; LEDA_89 ) and the radio spectral index calculated from the VLSS, MWA and NVSS is fairly flat =-0.1 . The interpretation of the variability of this source is consistent with long term intrinsic AGN variability. It is also plausible that refractive scintillation has played a role in the flux density change.

gmw_00006988.name: COS-469110
gmw_00006988.overview: COS-469110 is a high-redshift, UV-bright galaxy located in the COSMOS field [arXiv:2010.03566, arXiv:2202.01219]. It has a redshift of approximately 6.65 [arXiv:2202.01219] and likely hosts an active galactic nucleus indicated by tentative nitrogen emission [arXiv:2010.03566].

sources.summary: The paper highlights COS-469110 as an exceptional z~7 emitter with tentative NV doublet emission, repeated detection evidence, measured NV flux, and a likely AGN signpost.

quotes.quote: As shown in Fig. fig:COS469110_NV , we tentatively identify the NV doublet in COS-469110 ( = 21.7) where the emission is located at the exact expected spatial position of the source. We regard both these features as tentative because each are detected at < 7 significance, specifically 6.4 and 5.2 for the 1238.8 and 1242.8 components, respectively. However, we note that if we split our data into two distinct stacks We divide our data such that the total weight of all exposures in each stack is roughly equal. , the NV 1238.8 feature is significantly detected in each stack (5.2 and 3.8 ), adding evidence that this feature is likely real. The NV 1238.8 component is clearly distinct from strong skylines and has a measured flux of (5.4 1.0) 10 ^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 which, using the Y band photometry for the continuum, corresponds to an EW of 8.4 3.0 .
Indeed, the tentative NV detection within COS-469110 (see Fig. fig:COS469110_NV ) signals that this source likely harbors an AGN.

sources.summary: REBELS-23 is a UV-bright z=6.645 COSMOS galaxy with moderate IRAC-inferred EW, detected Ly emission, lower-limit flux and escape fraction, and a skyline-affected profile.

quotes.quote: REBELS-23 REBELS-23 is a UV-bright ( = -21.6 ) galaxy situated in the wide-area COSMOS field at = 6.645 (Schouws et al. in prep) and was identified as COS-469100 in Endsley2021_OIII,Endsley2021_LyA . This galaxy is inferred to have a stellar mass of = 8.8 and its moderately blue IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 0.53 ^ +0.18 _ 0.19 ) suggests an EW = 830 , similar to the typical value of z 7 galaxies. The Binospec data reveal a 7.5 detection with a peak wavelength at 9299.3 corresponding to = 6.650 (Fig. fig:LyA_Detections ). From the portion of the spectrum that is unobscured by strong skylines, we measure a flux of (9.7 1.4) 10 ^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 indicating an EW of 13.5 3.8 using the VIRCam Y band photometry for the continuum flux density (0.27 0.07 Jy). The measured line flux implies =3.3^ +2.0 _ 1.3 for REBELS-23.

gmw_00006989.name: COS-940214
gmw_00006989.overview: COS-940214 is a massive, high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.748 [arXiv:2010.03566]. It is actively forming stars and is part of a compact trio of Lyman-alpha emitters undergoing a burst of star formation [arXiv:2010.03566].

sources.summary: The paper places COS-940214 in a compact z~7 trio of massive Ly-alpha emitters with high UV luminosity, active star formation, blue IRAC colors, and burst-like specific star formation rates.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopic results indicate that COS-940214 ( = 6.748), COS-1009842 ( = 6.761), and COS-995126 ( = 6.813) all lie within a spherical volume with radius R = 1.7 physical Mpc (Fig. fig:bubble ), consistent with the expected sizes of HII regions at z=7 e.g. Furlanetto2004,Mesinger2007,Lin2016 . These three sources have luminosities ranging from 0.8--2.3 L ^ _ UV and therefore are likely among the most massive galaxies present at z 7. All three are also inferred to be actively forming stars with SFRs of 7--28 yr ^ 1 using the SED fitting procedure described in sec:BEAGLE . Their very blue IRAC colours ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] < 0.6) further suggest large sSFRs ranging from approximately 10--130 Gyr ^ 1 (see Table tab:galaxy_properties ). Because the typical sSFR at z 7 is 5 Gyr ^ 1 ( Endsley2021 ), these three galaxies are likely undergoing a burst of star formation.

gmw_00006990.name: COS-995126
gmw_00006990.overview: COS-995126 is a massive Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.813 [arXiv:2010.03566]. It is actively forming stars with a high specific star formation rate, suggesting it is undergoing a burst of star formation [arXiv:2010.03566].

sources.summary: The paper places COS-995126 in a compact z~7 trio of massive Ly-alpha emitters with high UV luminosity, active star formation, blue IRAC colors, and burst-like specific star formation rates.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopic results indicate that COS-940214 ( = 6.748), COS-1009842 ( = 6.761), and COS-995126 ( = 6.813) all lie within a spherical volume with radius R = 1.7 physical Mpc (Fig. fig:bubble ), consistent with the expected sizes of HII regions at z=7 e.g. Furlanetto2004,Mesinger2007,Lin2016 . These three sources have luminosities ranging from 0.8--2.3 L ^ _ UV and therefore are likely among the most massive galaxies present at z 7. All three are also inferred to be actively forming stars with SFRs of 7--28 yr ^ 1 using the SED fitting procedure described in sec:BEAGLE . Their very blue IRAC colours ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] < 0.6) further suggest large sSFRs ranging from approximately 10--130 Gyr ^ 1 (see Table tab:galaxy_properties ). Because the typical sSFR at z 7 is 5 Gyr ^ 1 ( Endsley2021 ), these three galaxies are likely undergoing a burst of star formation.

gmw_00006991.name: COS-1205190
gmw_00006991.overview: COS-1205190 is a massive galaxy at a redshift of approximately 7 [arXiv:2010.03566]. It exhibits an extremely blue rest-UV slope, which suggests a very low dust content, low metallicity, and an intense radiation field [arXiv:2010.03566].

sources.summary: The paper describes COS-1205190 as a z~7 source with an intense radiation field, extremely blue UV slope, likely low dust and metallicity, and strong emission.

quotes.quote: We also find reason to believe that COS-1205190 may be powering an intense radiation field given its extremely blue rest-UV slope of = 3.44 1.03. Such a blue slope (the bluest in our sample; Table tab:shortTable ) is consistent with not only extremely low dust content, but also very low metallicity. This possible dearth of metals in COS-1205190 would naturally lead to relatively weak [ OIII ] emission While H EW does increase with decreasing metallicity, it only reaches 300 at 0.01 Z_ even in extremely rapidly star-forming systems (sSFR 100 Gyr ^ 1 ) according to the Gutkin2016 templates used in beagle . even if it recently experienced a burst of star formation activity (sSFR 30 Gyr ^ 1 ) as typically inferred for the strong emitters in our sample.
The strong emission (EW = 29 ) from our bluest source (COS-1205190) also motivates an investigation into how the observed EW is related to the rest-UV slope among massive z 7 galaxies.

gmw_00006992.name: F2MS1030+5806
gmw_00006992.overview: F2MS1030+5806 is a compact, reddened quasar [arXiv:1201.3922]. It hosts a small-scale radio jet measuring approximately 100 parsecs in linear size and has an estimated synchrotron age of under 2000 years [arXiv:1201.3922].

sources.summary: The paper describes F2MS1030+5806 as a compact reddened QSO with a roughly 100 pc radio jet, a low-frequency turnover, and a synchrotron age under 2000 years.

quotes.quote: Another 2MASS QSOs in Table 1, F2MS1030+5806, has been observed at milli-arcsec resolution at 5 GHz. The data are from the VLBA Imaging and Polarization Survey (VIPS) presented by . This source has a very compact radio morphology with a small scale (few milli-arcsec) radio jet with linear size at the redshift of the source of about 100 pc.

The radio spectrum of F2MS1030+5806 shows a turnover below about 5 GHz and therefore cannot be fit with the continuous injection model. However, the spectral index of this source at high frequencies is _ 8.4 - 22 GHz =0.52 0.01 , close to the canonical synchrotron slope and similar to the injection slopes estimated in Table tab_slopes for others 2MASS QSOs with spectra better described by the continuous injection model. It is thus likely that _ br >22 GHz for this source.
For the one source in the sample (F2MS1030+5806) that such observations are available, much younger ages are estimated, t_ syn <2000 yr, similar to GPS/CCS sources.

gmw_00006993.name: SDSS J141658.90-001442.5
gmw_00006993.overview: SDSS J141658.90-001442.5 is a faint optical galaxy located near radio and X-ray sources [arXiv:1707.08599]. It has been identified as a plausible host candidate for a high-frequency peaked BL Lac active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1707.08599].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141658.90-001442.5 as a faint Sloan galaxy and plausible HBL host candidate near radio and X-ray sources, but says later Swift data could not confirm its HBL nature.

quotes.quote: A further search in the ASDC optical catalogs found a faint galaxy, SDSS J141658.90-001442.5 (mv 23), at 9.6 arcsec from the FIRST source (14.8 arcsec from the RASS source). Assuming the radio/optical/X-ray emission comes from the same galaxy, we have produced the SED shown in Figure SED_giommicandidate . The high value of the ratio between the 1RXS J141658.0-001449 flux density in the 0.1-2.4 keV band and the FIRST radio source F_ value at 1.4 GHz (respectively, black and red points in Fig. SED_giommicandidate ) might hint to a non-thermal synchrotron emission peaking above 10^ 15 Hz, typical of a HBL AGN blazar.

Considering these types of e.m. sources as the most likely neutrino-emitters, the X-ray source 1RXS J141658.0-001449 (and the plausible host galaxy SDSS J141658.90-001442.5) appears as one of the candidate as origin of the ICECUBE-160731 event.

A new SWIFT ToO then has been submitted in order to characterize better this RASS-FSC source. Unfortunately, the ToO was performed about 6 months after the neutrino event, and the analysis of the XRT data from the almost 5 ks exposure did not reveal any significant X-ray emission at the 1RXS J141658.0 position, providing a 3 UL of 3.1 10^ 3 cts s^ 1 in the 0.3--10 keV band. We then cannot confirm at the moment our hypothesis about the HBL nature of this source that, anyhow, might have been detected during the ROSAT survey because in an intrinsic X-ray high-state.

gmw_00006994.name: UGC 2866
gmw_00006994.overview: UGC 2866 is a companion galaxy to UGC 2855 with an uncertain morphological classification [arXiv:astro-ph/9903409]. The galaxy features concentrated molecular gas in a bar-like structure and very bright, compact H-alpha emission that indicates an active nuclear starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/9903409].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 2866 as the smaller companion of UGC 2855, with uncertain classification, concentrated molecular gas, compact bright H emission, and evidence for a nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: The picture presented by UGC 2866, the smaller, distant companion of UGC 2855, is very different. The classification of this galaxy is not clear: The UGC catalogue (Nilson 1973) tentatively calls it an early spiral, while it is (mis)classified as an elliptical by Hau et al. \ 1995.

All the molecular gas of UGC 2866 seems to be concentrated in one feature of slightly more than 2 kpc length that has an oval, possibly bar-like appearance (Fig. 2866mom a). The emission peaks at = 03^ h \ 45^ m \ 7 7 and = 69^ 56' 36'' , unusually far ( 3'' ) offset from the radio continuum peak at = 03^ h \ 45^ m \ 7 2 and = 69^ 56' 34'' (Condon 1996).

The H emssion from UGC 2866 (Fig halpha , panel b, a part of the same CCD frame as the image of UGC 2866) is very bright and very compact: it extends over only 8'' or 700 pc. The intensity of the emission is more than 6 times higher than what is found toward the center of UGC 2855. The center position of H coincides with the CO peak in UGC 2866 to better than 1 '' , i.e. \ to within the measuring uncertainty. The strength and compactness of H in UGC 2866 and the FIR properties (see below) indicate that this galaxy is experiencing a nuclear starburst.

gmw_00006995.name: 2MIG 131
gmw_00006995.overview: The lenticular galaxy 2MIG 131 exhibits a weak negative radial gradient in its alpha-element to iron ratios [arXiv:2009.05548]. Its final episode of star formation likely occurred in its innermost region [arXiv:2009.05548].

sources.summary: The paper reports weak radial alpha-element trends in 2MIG 131 and notes that low-order fitting changes its inferred bulge compactness and component light fractions.

quotes.quote: 2MIG 131, CCC 43 and CCC 137) or immediately outside their core (2MIG 445), which may indicate that the final episode of star formation within these galaxies occurred in their innermost regions.
It can be seen that the [ /Fe ] ratios are relatively flat with radius across most galaxies, with 2MIG 131 and 2MIG 1546 showing a weak negative gradient with radius.

In 6 of the eight galaxies, the fits using these three different orders converged on similar fits, while in 2MIG 131 and 2MIG 1814 the fits using a polynomial of order 1 resulted in very different structural parameters In the case of 2MIG 131, the fits using a polynomial order 1 resulted in a more compact bulge with a lower S ersic index, and the light fractions for the spectra from each component varied by up to 40 .

gmw_00006996.name: 2MIG 445
gmw_00006996.overview: 2MIG 445, also known as IC 1989, is a lenticular galaxy featuring a regular stellar velocity field and a central elongated dispersion structure [arXiv:1912.08845]. It exhibits a ring of younger stellar populations [arXiv:2009.05548], a projected dwarf companion to the northwest [arXiv:1912.08845], and faint shell or tidal tail remnants from a merger [arXiv:2009.05548].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1989 as a lenticular galaxy with a regular stellar velocity field, central elongated dispersion structure, and a northwest kinematic discontinuity attributed to a projected dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: IC 1989 -- 2MIG 445 . The stellar velocity field is regular and reaches an observed peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 200 . The velocity dispersion ranges between 100 and 150 \ except in the central region, which is characterised by an elongated structure aligned along the kinematic major axis. This structure has a counter-part in the rotation map.

A discontinuity in the kinematic maps is observed at 10" on the North West side, along the major axis. It is most likely caused by the presence of a dwarf companion in projection along the line of sight. This region, approximately 5" wide, was removed in the derivation of , V/sigma , and V_ circ . The fit of ionised-gas emission lines also fails in this contaminated area.

sources.summary: The paper finds 2MIG 445 has younger ring-like stellar populations and residual shells or tidal tails, consistent with recent enriched star formation and a merger.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, 2MIG 445 and CCC 158, show significantly younger stellar populations than the rest of the sample.

2MIG 445 on the other hand shows a ring of younger stellar populations circling a core of intermediate-age stars with an outer radius of 12 \ ( 9.1 kpc), with intermediate ages throughout the rest of the galaxy.
Of particular interest is the residual white-light image for 2MIG 445, where the faint features appear to be remnants of shells or tidal tails following a merger.

gmw_00006997.name: 2MIG 1546
gmw_00006997.overview: The lenticular galaxy 2MIG 1546, also known as NGC 3546, exhibits regular stellar rotation, a central velocity-dispersion peak, and extended ionized gas with regular high-amplitude rotation [arXiv:1912.08845]. It also shows a weak negative alpha-element gradient with radius and a correlation between h_3 and v/sigma at larger radii that becomes an anti-correlation at lower radii [arXiv:2009.05548].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3546 has regular stellar rotation, a central velocity-dispersion peak that flattens outward, and extended ionized gas with regular high-amplitude rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3546 -- 2MIG 1546 . The stellar velocity field is regular and reaches an observed peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 350 . The stellar velocity dispersion has a central peak of 220 \ and then it decrease down to 100 \ at 10" from the centre and stay relatively flat. The ionised-gas component is extended towards the entire field of view and rotates regularly with a peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 500 .

sources.summary: The paper says 2MIG 1546 has a weak negative alpha-element gradient, fitting sensitivity in its bulge and disc parameters, and unusual kinematics.

quotes.quote: It can be seen that the [ /Fe ] ratios are relatively flat with radius across most galaxies, with 2MIG 131 and 2MIG 1546 showing a weak negative gradient with radius.

For example, on can see that the two fits to 2MIG 1546 result in up to a 10 difference in the light fractions and SEDs for the bulge and disc components in the blue end of the spectrum, while these differences are almost negligible in CCC 122.
At this point it is interesting to note the unusual relationship between h_3 and v/ in 2MIG 1546, which shows a correlation at larger radii and an anti-correlation at lower radii.

gmw_00006998.name: 2MIG 1814
gmw_00006998.overview: The lenticular galaxy 2MIG 1814, also known as PGC 045474, features a regular stellar rotation field and uniformly old stellar populations at all radii [arXiv:1912.08845, arXiv:2009.05548]. Its extended ionized gas displays regular disk-like rotation in the inner region, transitioning to an irregular, outflow-like field toward the southwest [arXiv:1912.08845].

sources.summary: The paper finds PGC 045474 has regular stellar rotation, a declining central velocity dispersion, and extended ionized gas whose central disk-like rotation becomes irregular and outflow-like southwest of the center.

quotes.quote: PGC 045474 -- 2MIG 1814 . The stellar velocity field is regular and reaches an observed peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 300 . The stellar velocity dispersion has a central peak of 250 \ and then it decrease down to 150 \ at 30" from the centre. The ionised-gas component is extended towards the entire field of view. It shows a disk-like regular rotation in the central 10" and then it shows an irregular, outflow-like field, with line-of-sight velocity of 200 \ with respect to the system centre in the South-West region.

sources.summary: The paper notes model-instability for 2MIG 1814, a very faint extended component in the flat fit, and uniformly old stellar populations.

quotes.quote: In 6 of the eight galaxies, the fits using these three different orders converged on similar fits, while in 2MIG 131 and 2MIG 1814 the fits using a polynomial of order 1 resulted in very different structural parameters In the case of 2MIG 131, the fits using a polynomial order 1 resulted in a more compact bulge with a lower S ersic index, and the light fractions for the spectra from each component varied by up to 40 .
On the other hand, the flat fit to 2MIG 1814 converged on a model with a very extended, very faint component which was 4-5 magnitudes fainter than the disc component in any other fit.

Similarly, the uniformly old stellar populations measured at all radii in 2MIG 1814 and CCC 122 in Figures fig:radial_stellar_pops_maps and fig:radial_stellar_pops_plots are also reflected in the ages of the individual components.

gmw_00006999.name: CCC 43
gmw_00006999.overview: CCC 43 is a lenticular galaxy characterized by a pseudobulge structure [arXiv:2009.05548]. The galaxy features a lens component with a significantly younger stellar population, indicating that this structure hosted its most recent star formation [arXiv:2009.05548].

sources.summary: The paper classifies CCC 43 as a pseudobulge system with a comparatively low-Sersic bulge and a young, metal-rich lens that hosted recent star formation.

quotes.quote: Within these components, the bulges were selected by eye as the more extended component of the two with a steeper surface brightness profile and the softer edge (i.e. less sharp drop in the flux in the outskirts), and in 6 of the eight galaxies this component was found to be the brightest structure at the core of the galaxy, with the exceptions being CCC 43 and CCC 158.
It can be seen that 6 of the galaxies have bulges with n>2.0 , classifying them as classical bulges while the other two, CCC 43 and CCC 158, are pseudobulges.

However, the lenses of 2MIG 445, CCC 43 and CCC 158 do show evidence of significantly younger stellar populations, indicating that these components hosted the most recent star formation within these galaxies.

gmw_00007000.name: CCC 122
gmw_00007000.overview: CCC 122, also known as NGC 4706, features a regular stellar velocity field [arXiv:1912.08845] and spiral arms in both its inner and outer regions [arXiv:2009.05548]. The galaxy's core hosts old, metal-rich stellar populations [arXiv:2009.05548], while ionized gas is detected in the innermost region with disk-like rotation [arXiv:1912.08845].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4706 as having regular stellar rotation, a rapidly declining central velocity dispersion, and ionized gas in the inner region with disk-like rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4706 -- CCC 122 . The stellar velocity field is regular and reaches an observed peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 400 . The stellar velocity dispersion has a central peak of 250 \ and then it rapidly decrease down to 50 \ at 30" from the centre. Ionised-gas is detected in the innermost 10" and shows a disk-like rotation of 400 (peak-to-peak).

sources.summary: The paper reports old, metal-rich central populations in CCC 122, prominent spiral arms, and a three-component-plus-PSF fit.

quotes.quote: Additionally, variations in the [ /Fe ] ratios are detected in the cores of several galaxies, with an increase in the inner 2 \ ( 0.5 kpc) of CCC 122 that coincides with the older, more metal-rich stellar populations, and a drop in the inner 2 \ ( 0.5 kpc) of CCC 137 where the significantly younger, more metal-rich populations are found.

It was found that all the galaxies in this sample achieved a better fit using at least three components, with an additional fourth component in the form of a PSF profile in the cases of 2MIG 1814, CCC 122 and CCC 137.
These spiral arms are particularly prominent in both the inner and outer regions of CCC 122.

gmw_00007001.name: CCC 137
gmw_00007001.overview: CCC 137 is a lenticular galaxy characterized by a lens structure and a disc [arXiv:2009.05548]. The core of the galaxy contains significantly younger, more metal-rich stellar populations [arXiv:2009.05548].

sources.summary: The paper describes CCC 137 as having younger, metal-rich core populations, extracted component spectra, and lens metallicity consistent with formation from disc material.

quotes.quote: Additionally, variations in the [ /Fe ] ratios are detected in the cores of several galaxies, with an increase in the inner 2 \ ( 0.5 kpc) of CCC 122 that coincides with the older, more metal-rich stellar populations, and a drop in the inner 2 \ ( 0.5 kpc) of CCC 137 where the significantly younger, more metal-rich populations are found.
An example of these spectra extracted for each component in CCC 137 is given in Fig. fig:spectra , showing the fluxes of each component relative to the total flux from the galaxy.

The results for the metallicities are more interesting, showing that the metallicities of the lenses are generally consistent with or higher than the discs, with the exception of CCC 137, which may reflect that the lenses have formed in situ from the disc material.

gmw_00007002.name: CCC 158
gmw_00007002.overview: CCC 158 is a lenticular galaxy that contains a young stellar population in its inner regions [arXiv:2009.05548] and exhibits regular stellar rotation [arXiv:1912.08845]. Its core shows evidence of an edge-on disc [arXiv:2009.05548] and hosts ionized gas with an irregular velocity field misaligned from the stars [arXiv:1912.08845].

sources.summary: The paper reports PGC 043466 has regular stellar rotation, a rapidly declining velocity dispersion, and central ionized gas with an irregular velocity field misaligned from the stars.

quotes.quote: PGC 043466 -- CCC 158 . The stellar velocity field is regular and reaches an observed peak-to-peak rotation amplitude of 200 . The stellar velocity dispersion has a central peak of 220 \ and then it rapidly decrease down to 50 \ at 30" from the centre. Ionised-gas is detected in the central 10" and shows a skewed and irregular velocity field that is 30 degrees misaligned with respect to the stellar velocity field. The degree of gas-stellar misalignment is poorly constrained because of the irregularity of the ionised gas velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper finds CCC 158 has young inner stellar populations, enriched recent star formation, and possible edge-on inner disc structure.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, 2MIG 445 and CCC 158, show significantly younger stellar populations than the rest of the sample.

The age plot for CCC 158 shows no strong gradient, but the map appears to show that the younger stellar populations lie along the major-axis in the inner regions of the galaxy, while the older stellar populations are seen in the outermost bins.
These images are given in Fig. fig:unsharp_mask , and in CCC 158 it is possible to see evidence of an edge-on disc in the core of the galaxy.

gmw_00007003.name: W0149+2350
gmw_00007003.overview: W0149+2350 is a hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured quasar powered by a highly active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1711.10615, arXiv:2011.09991]. The system is likely undergoing a gas-rich merger [arXiv:1711.10615] and exhibits luminous water emission driven by combined star formation and nuclear activity [arXiv:2011.09991].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W0149+2350 as a hyper-luminous dust-obscured QSO detected by ALMA in CO and continuum, with three CO components suggesting a gas-rich merger.

quotes.quote: We select three hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured QSOs (W0149+2350, W0220+0137 and W0410 0913) from a new population recently discovered in Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer ( WISE , wright2010 ) all-sky survey, by using a so-called `` W1W2 dropout" method wu2012,eisenhardt2012 . The criterion is to select objects which are prominent in the WISE 12 m ( W3 ) or 22 m ( W4 ) bands, and faint or undetected in the 3.4 m ( W1 ) and 4.6 m ( W2 ) bands. This selection is very effective for finding high luminosity dust-obscured galaxies with dominant hot dust emission mostly at redshift z 1-4 assef2015a . Using X-ray observations stern2014,piconcelli2015,ricci2017 and the detailed spectral energy distribution (SED) analysis fan2016b , clear evidence has been found that the selected mid-IR luminous objects are actually highly dust-obscured, possibly Compton-thick AGN, with a high accretion rate close to the Eddington limit wu2018 .

Three CO line components are detected together with a continuum detection at 4.8 level. The velocity separations of two adjacent line components are 380 and 560 km s ^ 1 , respectively. The angular resolution is 0.2 arcsec, which corresponds to 1.5 kpc at z 3.2 .
W0149+2350, showing three distinguishable line components, is possibly undergoing a gas-rich merger, though the scenario of having a molecular outflow can not be excluded.

sources.summary: The paper reports serendipitous ALMA H2O detection in W0149 + 2350, finding luminous compact emission with broad CO-like profiles and line behavior linked to star formation and AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Using ALMA Band-6 observations of two Hot DOGs, we have detected H _ 2 O( 2_ 02 1_ 11 ) in W0149 + 2350, and H _ 2 O( 3_ 12 3_ 03 ) and the multiplet OH ^+ ( 1_ 1 0_ 1 ) in W0410 0913. These detections were serendipitous, falling within the side-bands of Band-6 observations aimed to study CO(9 8) in these Hot DOGs. We find that both sources have luminous H _ 2 O emission with line luminosities of L_ H_2O(2_ 02 1_ 11 ) > 2.2 10^8 L_ and L_ H_2O(3_ 12 3_ 03 ) = 8.7 10^8 L_ for W0149 + 2350 and W0410 0913, respectively. The H _ 2 O line profiles are similar to those seen for the neighbouring CO(9 8) line, with linewidths of FWHM 800-1000 km s ^ 1 . However, the H _ 2 O emission seems to be more compact than the CO(9 8).

The bright H _2 O( 2_ 02 1_ 11 ), and H _2 O( 3_ 12 3_ 03 ) emission lines are likely due to the combined high star-formation levels and luminous AGN in these sources. The presence of OH ^+ in emission, and the agreement of the observed line ratios of the Hot DOGs with luminous AGN in the literature, would suggest that the AGN emission is dominating the radiative output of these galaxies.

gmw_00007004.name: IC 712
gmw_00007004.overview: IC 712 is the brightest cluster galaxy in the Abell 1314 cluster [arXiv:2205.01413, arXiv:1103.0450]. It features a compact wide-angle tailed radio structure [arXiv:1103.0450] and exhibits diffuse radio emission likely associated with historic active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2205.01413].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 712 as Abell 1314's brightest cluster galaxy, compact in total intensity and polarization, with a single Faraday-thin structure.

quotes.quote: However, they do detect irregularly-shaped diffuse radio emission likely to be associated with historic AGN activity from the IC 712.

IC 712 (Source 10; Table tab:a1314-sources ) is the brightest cluster galaxy in A1314 lin2004 and closest to the X-ray centre with a projected distance of 40 kpc, see Table tab:a1314-sources . The source appears compact in both Stokes I and polarization, with a small offset of the polarized emission from the peak total intensity, see Figure fig:ic712 . The Faraday depth spectrum of IC 712 shows a single Faraday thin structure, see Figure fig:ic712_fd , with a peak value of _ peak = 40.95 1.42 rad m ^ 2 .

Slight deviations from purely Faraday thin structure are also seen in the Faraday depth spectra for IC 711 and IC 712, see Figures fig:ic711_fd fig:ic712_fd ; however, we note that these sources are not detected at such high significance in polarisation as IC 708 and therefore the presence of additional structure is less well-defined.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 712 as the BCG of Abell 1314 main condensation, with a compact WAT radio structure and an unexplained nondetection in VLBA 5 GHz maps.

quotes.quote: Among the undetected sources (Sect. res ), we found 2 peculiar cases of BGCs, IC 712 and NGC 6047 for which the non detection in our previous VLBA data at 5 GHz was not expected and not at all understood. In particular: IC 712 is the BGC of the main condensation of a merging cluster Abell 1314.

IC 712 has a WAT structure of very small linear size ( 4.6 kpc) with a total flux density at 1.4 GHz of 26.3 mJy . In our VLBA maps 5 GHz, differently to IC 708 (one-sided at parsec scale), IC 712 is undetected at the 5 level ( < 0.45 mJy/ beam).

gmw_00007005.name: Hercules
gmw_00007005.overview: Hercules is a Milky Way satellite dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a distance of approximately 133 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0906.4017]. It hosts an old, metal-poor stellar population and shows evidence of being embedded in a larger stream of stars [arXiv:0906.4017].

sources.summary: The paper measures Hercules' distance, luminosity, elongated morphology, old metal-poor stellar population, and possible extended stream-like structure traced by stellar overdensities.

quotes.quote: We present imaging of the recently discovered Hercules Milky Way satellite and its surrounding regions to study its structure, star formation history and to thoroughly search for signs of disruption. We robustly determine the distance, luminosity, size and morphology of Hercules utilizing a bootstrap approach to characterize our uncertainties. We derive a distance to Hercules of 133 6 kpc via a comparison to empirical and theoretical isochrones.

Using the color magnitude fitting package StarFISH, we determine that Hercules is old ( >12 Gyr) and metal poor ( [ Fe/H ] 2.0 ), with a spread in metallicity, in agreement with previous spectroscopic work. We infer a total absolute magnitude of M_V=-5.3 0.4 . Our innovative search for external Hercules structure both in the plane of the sky and along the line of sight yields some evidence that Hercules is embedded in a larger stream of stars. A clear stellar extension is seen to the Northwest with several additional candidate stellar overdensities along the position angle of Hercules out to 35' ( 1.3 kpc). While the association of any of the individual stellar overdensities with Hercules is difficult to determine, we do show that the summed color magnitude diagram of all three is consistent with Hercules' stellar population.

gmw_00007006.name: COSMOS J095930+023427
gmw_00007006.overview: COSMOS J095930+023427 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of 0.892 [arXiv:1211.4439, arXiv:1505.00502]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens, producing four bright images of a distant background source [arXiv:1211.4439, arXiv:1505.00502] and displaying a strong [OII] emission line [arXiv:1211.4439].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes COSMOS J095930+023427 as an early-type galaxy-scale gravitational lens, measuring its Einstein radius, dark-matter fraction, and shear contribution from a foreground group.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-wavelength study of the gravitational lens COSMOS J095930+023427 ( z_l=0.892 ), together with the associated galaxy group located at z 0.7 along the line of sight and the lensed background galaxy. The source redshift is currently unknown, but estimated to be at z_s 2 . The analysis is based on the available public HST, Subaru, Chandra imaging data, and VLT spectroscopy. The lensing system is an early-type galaxy showing a strong [ OII ] emission line, and produces 4 bright images of the distant background source.

We have performed a multi-wavelength analysis of the gravitational lens COSMOS J095930+023427 ( z_l=0.892 ), based on the large set of public data from the COSMOS survey. The lensing system is an early-type galaxy with an Einstein radius of 0.79" . We perform a lensing analysis using both a SIE and a PIEMD: final results on the total mass, the dark matter (DM) fraction within the Einstein radius and the external shear due to a foreground galaxy group are robust with respect of the choice of the parametric model and the source redshift (yet unknown). We find that the DM fraction is f_ DM =0.75 at about 4 effective radii with a source at z_s=2 , similar to what recently found in local elliptical galaxies Deason12 .

sources.summary: The paper describes COSMOS J095930+023427 as an early-type strong lens with four bright images of a background source and reviews previous Lenstool analyses using SIE, SIS, and PIEMD components.

quotes.quote: COSMOS J095930+023427 The lens COSMOS J095930 was first described by Jackson Jackson2008 . COSMOS J095930 is an early-type galaxy with four bright images of a distant background source. It is located at z_ lens =0.892, and the background source is estimated at z_ source =2.00. While the exact z_ source is unknown, the value used by previous investigators is 2.00.

Models of this system were described by Faure using Lenstool Faure2011 . This model used a Singular Isothermal Ellipsoid (SIE) with external shear ( + ) and found an Einstein radius of 0.79" and _V =255 km s ^ 1 . More recently, an extensive multi-wavelength study of this system was reported by Cao and colleagues Cao2013 , also using Lenstool. This analysis used four different models, an SIE with two Singular Isothermal Spheres (SIS) as well as a Pseudo-Isothermal Elliptical Mass Distribution (PIEMD) model with two SIS, both with and without external shear Kassiola1993 .

sources.summary: The paper treats J095930.93+023427.7 as a convincing COSMOS strong lens, notes its ETG lens galaxy, redshift discrepancy, nearby lower-redshift cluster, and group lens-model contribution.

quotes.quote: Independently, Jackson (2008) inspected the complete set of galaxies in the COSMOS field, without discrimination. He has discovered two additional convincing strong lens candidates producing multiple images: J095930.93+023427.7 and J100140.12+020040.9. The lens galaxies both appear to be ETGs.

We notice that three systems show a strongly deviant photometric redshift (COSMOS 0254+1430: z _ phot =0.46 0.01 and z _ spec =0.417 0.001; J095930.93+023427.7: z _ phot =1.10 ^ +0.02 _ 0.00 and z _ spec =0.892 0.001; COSMOS 0018+3845: z _ phot =0.44 0.01 and z _ spec =0.9755 0.0003). In those three cases, the photometry of the lens galaxy obtained from ground-based data is contaminated by the close and bright images of the source, and the photometric redshift determination is consequently biased.

For J095930.93+023427.7, we have modeled the group in direction to the lens by a SIS which position is fixed to the position of the group in the X-ray catalog. Hence, the only parameter allowed to vary is the velocity dispersion of the profile.

gmw_00007007.name: XID178
gmw_00007007.overview: XID178 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of 1.253 [arXiv:1605.08631]. The galaxy hosts a spatially extended ionized gas outflow that reaches approximately 8.6 kiloparsecs, traced by a blue wing in its oxygen emission line profile [arXiv:1605.08631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID178 as a z=1.253 AGN host with definite [O III] outflow evidence, a blue wing, and spatially extended ionized outflow reaching about 8.6 kpc.

quotes.quote: XID178 and XID5627 are the candidates in our sample apart from XID2028 which was published in cresci15 which show definite evidence for the presence of outflow from the [ O iii ] 5007 profile. At a redshift of 1.253 and 1.337, both targets were observed using the J grating of SINFONI to sample the [ O iii ] lines at = 4959 and 5007 . The total exposure time on source was 3.5 hours for each object achieving a S/N ratio of 34 (XID178) and 11 (XID5627) on the integrated spectrum for the [ O iii ] 5007 line.

Figs. 178 and 5627 show the integrated spectrum, the flux maps corresponding to narrow and broad Gaussian components and the surface brightness profiles of XID178 and XID5627 respectively. In order to obtain the flux maps and therefore determine the spatial distribution of the outflow, we re-binned the reduced cube by clustering a 2 2 group of pixels into a single pixel for both the targets. This increases the S/N and allows for a better line fitting across the spaxels. As a result, the re-binning procedure increases the spatial scale of the pixel by a factor of 2 to 2.15 kpc and 2.16 kpc for XID178 and XID5627 respectively. The [ O iii ] 5007 profile in the integrated spectrum of XID178 in Fig. 178 , top panel shows a clear blue wing which traces outflowing gas moving towards us.
XID178 and XID5627 also show outflows which are spatially extended up to 8.6 kpc.

gmw_00007008.name: XID5627
gmw_00007008.overview: XID5627 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of 1.337 [arXiv:1605.08631]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal definite evidence of an outflow, with projected velocities ranging from 250 to 520 km/s [arXiv:1605.08631].

sources.summary: The paper treats XID5627 as a z=1.337 AGN host with definite [O III] outflow evidence, measured SINFONI spectra, and lower projected outflow velocities than XID2028 and XID54204.

quotes.quote: XID178 and XID5627 are the candidates in our sample apart from XID2028 which was published in cresci15 which show definite evidence for the presence of outflow from the [ O iii ] 5007 profile. At a redshift of 1.253 and 1.337, both targets were observed using the J grating of SINFONI to sample the [ O iii ] lines at = 4959 and 5007 . The total exposure time on source was 3.5 hours for each object achieving a S/N ratio of 34 (XID178) and 11 (XID5627) on the integrated spectrum for the [ O iii ] 5007 line.

The velocity values cover the range 490 - 730 and 250 - 520 km/s for XID178 and XID5627 respectively. Clearly, these ranges of velocities will imply a range in the outflow properties calculated in Sec. sec_outflow_properties , as discussed in Sec. sec_discussion .

The one dimensional spectra of three of the objects from our sample (XID2028, XID5627 and XID54204 a.k.a XCOS2028, XCOS5627 and COS178 respectively) have also been presented in as part of the KMOS AGN survey at High Redshift (KASHz). Except for XID54204, our one dimensional results (line wavelengths, widths and luminosity) are compatible with those reported by .

gmw_00007009.name: XID5330
gmw_00007009.overview: XID5330 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of 2.169 [arXiv:1605.08631]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy detect both [O III] and H-alpha emission lines [arXiv:1605.08631].

sources.summary: The paper observes XID5330, a z=2.169 AGN target, with short H+K SINFONI exposure and detects [O III] and H lines, but lacks enough S/N for wing analysis.

quotes.quote: XID5330 has the shortest integration time among the targets of our program. The spectral resolution is lower compared to the rest of the sample since the observations were taken in the SINFONI H+K band. At a redshift of 2.169, the [ O iii ] line falls in the H band at a wavelength of 1.593 m and we also detect H line at 2.088 m in the K band.

In 50 minutes, we were able to reach a S/N of 5 for [ O iii ] 5007 and 8.3 for H in the integrated spectrum. The integrated H band spectrum around the [ O iii ] 4959,5007 lines is shown in Fig. 5330_h , top left panel. We have a low S/N ratio for the [ O iii ] 5007 profile and hence only one Gaussian has been used for line fitting.

gmw_00007010.name: XID54204
gmw_00007010.overview: XID54204 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.356 [arXiv:1605.08631]. The galaxy exhibits a faint but broad [O III] emission line profile with a blue-shifted wing, indicating a gas outflow directed toward the observer [arXiv:1605.08631].

sources.summary: The paper reports faint but broad [O III] in XID54204, identifying a blue wing, high outflow velocities, and unresolved spatial information due to low S/N.

quotes.quote: XID54204 has a very faint [ O iii ] 5007 signal despite an integration time of 3.5 hours on the target. The [ O iii ] 5007 line is detected at a wavelength of 1.1796 m consistent with the object being at z = 1.356. A S/N of 4.4 could be reached in the integrated spectrum of the [ O iii ] 5007 emission line. Although faint, the [ O iii ] 5007 profile in the integrated spectrum is quite broad, so a single Gaussian is not able to provide a good fit of the line profile. Therefore we added a second component and the resulting integrated spectrum is given in Fig. 54204_flux , left panel.

The integrated spectrum indicates the presence of a blue wing in the [ O iii ] 5007 profile, hence the outflow direction is towards the observer. The velocity offset between the narrow and the (blue-shifted) broad Gaussian component is 360 km/s. Fig. 54204_flux , right panel shows the continuum subtracted [ O iii ] 5007 line core map, confirming [ O iii ] emission from this object.

gmw_00007011.name: NGC 4839
gmw_00007011.overview: NGC 4839 is a cD galaxy [arXiv:2301.05269] that features a cD envelope [arXiv:0906.0287, arXiv:0808.1521] and a central kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0906.0287, arXiv:0808.1521]. It serves as the central galaxy of an infalling group merging with the Coma cluster [arXiv:1811.07944, arXiv:2401.14730], hosting a wide-angle-tailed radio source [arXiv:2207.06624] and a stripped tail of cool gas [arXiv:1811.07944].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4839 as the central galaxy of an infalling group whose cool gas tail, hot sheath, and post-apocenter motion trace a merger with Coma.

quotes.quote: One of the most prominent is the group of galaxies associated with the elliptical galaxy NGC 4839. It lies in the cluster outskirts ( 1 Mpc in projection) south-west of the cluster center. The X-ray image (see Fig.  fig:all ) exhibits an edge-like structure at the head of the group and an elongated tail of ram pressure stripped gas toward the south-west, i.e. opposite to the direction to the Coma center.

We study a merger of the NGC 4839 group with the Coma cluster using X-ray observations from the XMM-Newton and Chandra telescopes. X-ray data show two prominent features: (i) a long ( 600 kpc in projection) and bent tail of cool gas trailing (towards south-west) the optical center of NGC 4839, and ii) a `sheath' region of enhanced X-ray surface brightness enveloping the group, which is due to hotter gas. While at first glance the X-ray images suggest that we are witnessing the first infall of NGC 4839 into the Coma cluster core, we argue that a post-merger scenario provides a better explanation of the observed features and illustrate this with a series of numerical simulations. In this scenario, the tail is formed when the group, initially moving to the south-west, reverses its radial velocity after crossing the apocenter , the ram pressure ceases and the ram-pressure-displaced gas falls back toward the center of the group and overshoots it. Shortly after the apocenter passage, the optical galaxy, dark matter and gaseous core move in a north-east direction, while the displaced gas continues moving to the south-west.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 4839 to diffuse radio emission, stripes near the relic, a merger-driven shock scenario with Coma, and possible turbulence and seed-electron injection into the bridge.

quotes.quote: Its emission is connected on both sides to two head-tail radio galaxies, namely NGC 4839 to the North-East, and NGC 4789 to the South-West of the relic. From NGC4839, we detect diffuse emission that blends into the bridge and relic emission Bonafede21 . The surface brightness of the relic is not uniform, but composed of patchy and filamentary sub-structures (see Fig. fig:zoomRelic ). Stripes of radio emission depart from the relic and are directed towards the bridge and NGC 4839. A similar stripe is detected also near the tail of NGC 4839, directed towards the relic.

According to this scenario, both the relic and the W shock would be caused by the merger of NGC 4839 with the Coma cluster. During its first passage NGC4839 would have driven a first shock that should now be located at the position marked by the radio relic.

NGC4839 is now at its second passage towards the cluster centre Lyskova19,Churazov21 . Its motion might have injected additional turbulence in the ICM and a considerable amount of seed electrons, that originate to the radio bridge Bonafede21 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4839 as the cD galaxy of an infalling Coma group whose compact, truncated globular-cluster system indicates substantial stripping during a previous core passage and supports a second-infall scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 4839 is the brightest galaxy (cD) of the NGC 4839 group at R 1 Mpc in the south-west of the Coma cluster, which is known to be falling into Coma. However, it has been controversial whether it is in the first phase of infall or in the second phase of infall after passing the Coma center. We present a wide field study of globular clusters (GCs) in NGC 4839 and its environment based on Hyper Suprime-Cam gr images in the Subaru archive.

Interestingly the spatial distribution of the GCs in NGC 4839 is significantly more compact than that of the GCs in NGC 4816. In addition, the radial number density profile of the GCs in NGC 4839 shows an abrupt drop at R_ N4839 80 kpc, while that of the GCs in NGC 4816 shows a continuous slow decline even in the outer region at 80<R_ N4816 <500 kpc. The effective radius of the NGC 4839 GC system is about three times smaller than that of the NGC 4816 GC system. This striking difference can be explained if NGC 4839 lost a significant fraction of the GCs in its outskirt when it passed through Coma.
