quotes.quote: We consider one dwarf spheroidal (CenA-dE1) and two dwarf elliptical (SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066) galaxies that are dominated by an old population. The most recent periods of star formation are estimated to have taken place between 2 and 5 Gyr ago for SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066, and approximately 9 Gyr ago for CenA-dE1.

There are in total 13 AGB candidates for CenA-dE1, 41 for SGC1319.1-4216 and 176 for ESO269-066. For CenA-dE1, we can see that the four brightest candidates are detached from the rest of the red dots, so we perform a visual inspection of the images and conclude that they are barely resolved background galaxies.
From the combined optical and NIR CMD analysis of the previous Section, we are left with 9 luminous AGB candidates for CenA-dE1, 41 for SGC1319.1-4216 and 176 for ESO269-066.

gmw_00007102.name: SGC1319.1-4216
gmw_00007102.overview: SGC1319.1-4216 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Centaurus group [arXiv:2001.02241] that is dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1103.3706]. The galaxy exhibits oblate rotation [arXiv:2001.02241], hosts a nucleated star cluster [arXiv:2001.02241], and experienced its most recent star formation period between 2 and 5 Gyr ago [arXiv:1103.3706].

sources.summary: The paper analyses KK197 as a nucleated dwarf elliptical, confirming its NSC and two globular clusters, detecting oblate rotation, and measuring a metal-poor NSC.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a NSC in the dwarf galaxy KKs58 and investigate its properties together with those of another NSC in KK197. Both NSCs are hosted by dwarf elliptical galaxies of the Centaurus group. Combining ESO VLT MUSE data with photometry from VLT FORS2, CTIO Blanco DECam, and HST ACS, as well as high-resolution spectroscopy from VLT UVES, we analyse the photometric, kinematic and stellar population properties of the NSCs and their host galaxies. We confirm membership of the NSCs based on their radial velocities and location close to the galaxy centres. We also confirm the membership of two globular clusters (GCs) and detect oblate rotation in the main body of KK197.

Based on high signal-to-noise spectra taken with MUSE of the NSCs of both KKs58 and KK197 we measure low metallicities, [ Fe/H ] = 1.75 0.06 dex and [ Fe/H ] = 1.84 0.05 dex , and stellar masses of 7.3 10^5 M_ and 1.0 10^6 M_ , respectively. Both NSCs are more metal-poor than their hosts that have metallicities of 1.35 0.23 dex (KKs58) and 0.84 0.12 dex (KK197). This can be interpreted as NSC formation via the in-spiral of GCs.

sources.summary: The paper treats SGC1319.1-4216 as an early-type Centaurus A dwarf dominated by old stars, with luminous AGB candidates indicating intermediate-age star formation roughly 2-5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We consider one dwarf spheroidal (CenA-dE1) and two dwarf elliptical (SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066) galaxies that are dominated by an old population. The most recent periods of star formation are estimated to have taken place between 2 and 5 Gyr ago for SGC1319.1-4216 and ESO269-066, and approximately 9 Gyr ago for CenA-dE1.

The number of stars from the NIR photometry in each galaxy is: 347 (CenA-dE1), 1505 (SGC1319.1-4216) and 3428 (ESO269-066). We have tied our photometry to the 2MASS photometric system carpenter01 http://www.ipac.caltech.edu/2mass/releases/allsky/doc/sec6 4b.html/. by matching all the point sources from 2MASS observed in our fields with our J band and K band detections (the adopted photometric system is the Vega system).

Given the number of stars in the low completeness region, we estimate that there could additionally be at least 10 undetected AGB stars for SGC1319.1-4216 and 40 for ESO269-066. There are in total 13 AGB candidates for CenA-dE1, 41 for SGC1319.1-4216 and 176 for ESO269-066.

gmw_00007103.name: ALMACAL--1
gmw_00007103.overview: ALMACAL--1 is a bright submillimeter starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.442 [arXiv:1607.06464]. It exhibits compact dust emission, warm far-infrared properties, and high star formation rate surface densities, with molecular line emissions suggesting the influence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1607.06464].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMACAL--1 as a z=3.442 bright SMG with compact dust emission, high SFR surface density, warm FIR properties, and possible AGN influence.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA ultra--high--spatial resolution ( 20 mas or 150 pc ) observations of dust continuum at 920 m and 1.2 mm in a pair of submm galaxies (SMGs) at z = 3.442 , ALMACAL--1 (A--1: S_ 870 m = 6.5 0.2 mJy ) and ALMACAL--2 (A--2: S_ 870 m = 4.4 0.2 mJy ). These are the brightest and most luminous SMGs discovered so far in ALMACAL, a wide and deep (sub)mm survey, which is being carried out in ALMA calibrator fields and currently contains observations at sub-arcsec resolution down to an .m.s. of 15 Jy beam^ 1 in more than 250 calibrators fields. The spectroscopic redshifts of A--1 and A--2 have been confirmed via serendipitous detection of up to nine emission lines, in three different ALMA bands.

However, it is important to point out here that A--1 is warmer (see section_FIR_SEDs and Table table_properties_J1058_SMGs ), has relatively bright ^ 12 CO(13--12), ^ 12 CO(14--13) and H_2O (4_ 22 4_ 13 ) lines, and its CO SLED seems to plateau at J = 10-13 , suggesting an influential AGN in A--1 and less so in A--2, for which the upper limits in the high- J CO lines suggest a less excited CO SLED.
The high SFR and the compact size of all the star-forming clumps in A--1 and A--2 indicate extremely high SFR surface densities of up to _ SFR 6,000 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 .

gmw_00007104.name: ALMACAL--2
gmw_00007104.overview: ALMACAL--2 is a luminous submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.442 [arXiv:1607.06464]. It features a compact star-forming dust region with an extremely high star formation rate surface density [arXiv:1607.06464].

sources.summary: The paper describes ALMACAL--2 as a z=3.442 luminous SMG with confirmed emission-line redshift, compact star-forming dust emission, and a cooler, less excited SED than A--1.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA ultra--high--spatial resolution ( 20 mas or 150 pc ) observations of dust continuum at 920 m and 1.2 mm in a pair of submm galaxies (SMGs) at z = 3.442 , ALMACAL--1 (A--1: S_ 870 m = 6.5 0.2 mJy ) and ALMACAL--2 (A--2: S_ 870 m = 4.4 0.2 mJy ). These are the brightest and most luminous SMGs discovered so far in ALMACAL, a wide and deep (sub)mm survey, which is being carried out in ALMA calibrator fields and currently contains observations at sub-arcsec resolution down to an .m.s. of 15 Jy beam^ 1 in more than 250 calibrators fields. The spectroscopic redshifts of A--1 and A--2 have been confirmed via serendipitous detection of up to nine emission lines, in three different ALMA bands.

Only one star-forming clump (A--2A) is detected in A--2 with the .m.s. of our data. We have measured the primary-beam corrected flux density at 920 m and beam--deconvolved size of each component with the task imfit within casa . The derived values are quoted in Table properties_SF_clumps , where we only include the three clumps which are detected at > 10 , since sizes cannot be reliably measured at lower signal to noise. We see that components A--1A and A--2A dominate the dust emission in A--1 and A--2, respectively, and appear very compact, with FWHM sizes of 300 pc .
The high SFR and the compact size of all the star-forming clumps in A--1 and A--2 indicate extremely high SFR surface densities of up to _ SFR 6,000 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 .

gmw_00007105.name: NGC0214
gmw_00007105.overview: NGC0214 is an isolated, barred spiral galaxy that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1903.04818]. Its central region is dominated by intermediate-age and old stellar populations, while younger stars and patchy hydrogen-alpha emission increase further outward [arXiv:1903.04818].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC0214 as an isolated barred low-luminosity AGN, finding bar-related inner velocity-field structure and central stellar populations dominated by intermediate-age and old stars.

quotes.quote: From these 15 AGN, we selected the two best matching in redshift, stellar mass, inclination and morphology, but differing in the presence of a bar, resulting NGC0214 (AGN-bar, hereafter) and NGC2916 (AGN-nbar, hereafter). These galaxies are low-luminosity AGN and hence will have low impact AGN feedback on the circumnuclear region, making them ideal targets to look for triggering imprints. We then selected the barred and unbarred galaxies among the remaining 389 objects located at the star-forming region (SF) in the BPT diagram best matching the galaxy properties listed above.

NGC0214 (AGN-bar) The stellar continuum map (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC0214_stars ) reflects the elongation of the bar in the inner region. The H map (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC0214_ha ) shows a patchy distribution with weak emission in the central 12 arcsec (3 kpc) of the galaxy and stronger emission in the spiral arms. The global photometric and stellar/gaseous kinematic PA are in agreement.

The central region of NGC0214 is dominated by the light of intermediate age (56 ) and old (33 ) stellar populations (see Table tab:table_maps_profiles_light_weighted and Fig. fig:maps_profiles_SFH_Tomas ), while the contribution of the young component increases outwards (11 in the central kpc and 43 between 1 and 5 kpc).

gmw_00007106.name: NGC2253
gmw_00007106.overview: NGC2253 is a barred spiral galaxy classified as a star-forming system [arXiv:1903.04818]. Its structure features a stellar bar elongation, a faint circumnuclear ring of ionized gas, and a morpho-kinematic position angle misalignment of approximately 10 degrees [arXiv:1903.04818].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2253 as the barred star-forming twin, describing its bar, faint circumnuclear ring, kinematic misalignment, rotation curves, and young population-free ring.

quotes.quote: The selected objects were NGC2253 (SF-bar, hereafter) and NGC0001 (SF-nbar, hereafter). Finally, we confirmed the classification of these four galaxies using the alternative diagnostic diagram [ OIII ] / [ OII ] versus [ NII ] /H proposed by 2010MNRAS.403.1036C . Figure fig:BPT_diagram_mc shows the location of the four selected objects in the two diagnostic diagrams used to assess their nuclear type.

NGC2253 (SF-bar) The stellar continuum map clearly shows the bar elongation (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC2253_stars ). The H map reveals a faint circumnuclear ring at 1-2 kpc from the nucleus (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC2253_ha ). NGC2253 shows a morpho-kinematic PA misalignment of around 10 degrees.

The stellar rotation curves (total, approaching and receding) fall below the ionised gas ones, suggesting that a pressure supported contribution to the stellar kinematics is not negligible in NGC2253 (Fig. fig:NGC2253_velocity_curves ).

gmw_00007107.name: NGC0001
gmw_00007107.overview: NGC0001 is an unbarred star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:1903.04818]. Its stellar continuum peaks at the center, while H-alpha emission is concentrated in the spiral arms, and its light-weighted metallicity is highest in the nucleus [arXiv:1903.04818].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC0001 as the unbarred star-forming twin, finding good sided-kinematic agreement, pressure-supported stellar motions, flat ionized-gas rotation, and a centrally peaked metallicity profile.

quotes.quote: The selected objects were NGC2253 (SF-bar, hereafter) and NGC0001 (SF-nbar, hereafter). Finally, we confirmed the classification of these four galaxies using the alternative diagnostic diagram [ OIII ] / [ OII ] versus [ NII ] /H proposed by 2010MNRAS.403.1036C . Figure fig:BPT_diagram_mc shows the location of the four selected objects in the two diagnostic diagrams used to assess their nuclear type.

NGC0001 (SF-nbar) The stellar continuum map (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC0001_stars ) peaks at the center of the galaxy, while the H map shows emission in the spiral arms (top left panel of Fig. fig:NGC0001_ha ). The kinematic parameters derived from the approaching and receding sides are in good agreement within the estimated uncertainties (see Table tab:big_table and tab:big_table_mask ). The stellar rotation curves (from total, receding and approaching sides) fall below the ionised gas rotation curves and the stellar velocity dispersion is higher than the gaseous velocity dispersion.

Beyond 15 arcsec, only the rotation curve of the ionised gas can be obtained showing a flat behaviour (see Fig. fig:NGC0001_velocity_curves ). The velocity residual maps show patchy structures for both the stellar and ionised gas kinematics (last column of Fig. fig:NGC0001_ha ). The radial behavior of the light-weighted age (see Fig. fig:age_Tomas_arcs ) in NGC0001 is quite flat, while the light weighted metallicity (see Fig. fig:metallicity_Tomas_arcs ) shows high values in the nucleus of the galaxy, then decreases until the R _ e and then remains flat.

gmw_00007108.name: SDSSJ1000+5730
gmw_00007108.overview: SDSSJ1000+5730 is a faint, extended object identified as a candidate dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612173]. It has a surface brightness near 31 magnitudes per square arcsecond, which is lower than other known dwarf spheroidals discovered from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey [arXiv:astro-ph/0612173].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ1000+5730 as a faint, extended Milky Way satellite candidate whose surface brightness is below known SDSS dSphs, while its Hess diagram motivates follow-up.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ1000+5730 SDSSJ1329+2841 : SDSSJ1000+5730 and SDSSJ1329+2841 are faint, extended objects with half light radii similar to that of most of dSphs. They have surface brightness near _ V 31 mag/arcsec^ 2 , which are lower than all known dSphs discovered from SDSS. Their type cannot be determined only by SDSS due to their faintness, but they look like dwarf galaxies. In this paper we report five interesting overdensities detected in the SDSS database. They show features of globular clusters or dSphs in their CMDs.

The half-light radius and absolute magnitude of SDSSJ1329+2841 suggest a likely dSph. Although surface magnitude measurements suggest that SDSSJ1000+5730 is fainter than all known dSphs, its Hess diagram implies that it is an interesting object that needs follow-up observations.

gmw_00007109.name: SDSSJ1329+2841
gmw_00007109.overview: SDSSJ1329+2841 is a faint, extended dwarf galaxy candidate with a very low surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0612173]. Its half-light radius and absolute magnitude suggest it is likely a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612173].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ1329+2841 as a faint extended object with dSph-like half-light radius, very low surface brightness, CMD features, and size-luminosity evidence suggesting a likely dSph.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ1000+5730 SDSSJ1329+2841 : SDSSJ1000+5730 and SDSSJ1329+2841 are faint, extended objects with half light radii similar to that of most of dSphs. They have surface brightness near _ V 31 mag/arcsec^ 2 , which are lower than all known dSphs discovered from SDSS. Their type cannot be determined only by SDSS due to their faintness, but they look like dwarf galaxies. In this paper we report five interesting overdensities detected in the SDSS database. They show features of globular clusters or dSphs in their CMDs.

The half-light radius and absolute magnitude of SDSSJ1329+2841 suggest a likely dSph. Although surface magnitude measurements suggest that SDSSJ1000+5730 is fainter than all known dSphs, its Hess diagram implies that it is an interesting object that needs follow-up observations.

gmw_00007110.name: FIRST 0747 + 2739
gmw_00007110.overview: FIRST 0747 + 2739 is a radio-quiet, optically luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=4.11 [arXiv:astro-ph/0404543]. Its spectrum displays an overabundance of C IV absorption systems, which are likely intrinsic to the quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0404543].

sources.summary: The paper describes FIRST 0747 + 2739 as a radio-quiet, optically luminous z=4.11 quasar with many intrinsic C IV absorption systems but no notable X-ray weakness.

quotes.quote: FIRST 0747 + 2739 Although the z=4.11 quasar FIRST 0747 + 2739 is radio-quiet (a faint detection in the FIRST survey gives a radio-loudness of R 2 ), it is one of the most optically luminous quasars at z>4 with M_ B =-29.2 (compare with Figure 1 of Vignali et al. \ 2003a). Richards et al. \ (2002) found an overabundance of C iv absorption systems in the spectrum of FIRST 0747 + 2739, with at least 14 independent absorption systems spanning a redshift range of z 1 longward of the Ly forest. They concluded that the absorption is most likely intrinsic to the quasar, resulting from multiple high-velocity absorption systems at the spatial, density, or temporal edge of a BAL region.

FIRST 0747 + 2739 may be a quasar in the transition region between BAL and normal quasars. From the \ observation of FIRST 0747 + 2739, we measure an observed-frame 0.5--2 keV flux of 1.02 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , from which we derive a rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity of 2.3 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 and an optical-to- \ spectral index of =-1.86 .

gmw_00007111.name: A2218-Flanking
gmw_00007111.overview: A2218-Flanking is a lensed compact dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:1702.06540]. It is a young, star-forming galaxy with a half-light radius of 2.4 kiloparsecs and a firm detection of Lyman continuum emission [arXiv:1702.06540].

sources.summary: A2218-Flanking is presented as a lensed compact dwarf at z=2.518 with low mass, compact size, detected LyC emission, and a high inferred escape fraction.

quotes.quote: We present the HST WFC3/F275W UV imaging observations of A2218-Flanking, a lensed compact dwarf galaxy at redshift z 2.5 . The stellar mass of A2218-Flanking is =9.14 +0.07 0.04 and SFR is 12.5 +3.8 7.4 M_ yr ^ 1 after correcting the magnification. This galaxy has a young galaxy age of 127 Myr and a compact galaxy size of r_ 1/2 =2.4 kpc.

The HST UV imaging observations cover the rest-frame Lyman continuum (LyC) emission ( 800 ) from A2218-Flanking. We firmly detect ( 14 ) the LyC emission in A2218-Flanking in the F275W image. Together with the HST F606W images, we find that the absolute escape fraction of LyC is f_ abs,esc >28-57 based on the flux density ratio between 1700 and 800 ( f_ 1700 /f_ 800 ).

gmw_00007112.name: ESO341-IG004
gmw_00007112.overview: ESO341-IG004 is a galaxy in an advanced stage of merging, featuring an old stellar bulge and a southwestern loop [arXiv:2308.16152]. The system displays plumes and tidal tails, along with bright knots of recent star formation on its eastern side [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: ESO341-IG004 is described as an advanced merger with plumes, tidal tails, a southwestern loop, eastern star-forming knots, and no available MUSE follow-up.

quotes.quote: ESO341-IG004 ESO341-IG004 is in an advanced stage of merging, with plumes and tidal tails extending towards the nucleus's east and west. These tidal features could be due to the gas stripping of another galaxy that has merged with ESO341-IG004. The galaxy shows a loop on the southwestern side in the NIR map. The old stellar bulge is also prominent in the NIR image. The galaxy also shows plumes having bright knots of recent star formation on the eastern side in the FUV map (Fig. fig:eso341 ).

gmw_00007113.name: IC5110
gmw_00007113.overview: IC5110 is an advanced merger system featuring two central bulges in their final stage of merging [arXiv:2308.16152]. The galaxy exhibits star formation along its arms and contains resonant inner and outer rings likely formed by interaction-induced torques [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: IC5110 is treated as an advanced merger whose central bulges are nearly merging, with recent star formation along arms and interaction-linked resonant rings.

quotes.quote: IC5110 IC5110 represents an advanced merger system, where the bulges of the two galaxies are in the final stage of merging. The J-band images show two bulges in the centre. The third bright point in the centre is a foreground star. IC5110 shows star formation along the arms, evident from the FUV and H images (Fig. fig:IC5110 ). The galaxy has a mean FUV and H _ SFR of 0.007 and 0.061 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) respectively.
Since IC5110 shows an inner ring and outer rings in FUV and H , the gas may have been subjected to positive torques due to the interaction, which has led to resonant rings formation in IC5110.

gmw_00007114.name: IC5250
gmw_00007114.overview: IC5250 is an advanced merger system composed of interacting spiral and S0 galaxies [arXiv:2308.16152]. The system is gas-poor, exhibits little to no active star formation, and features tidal tails that are bright in the infrared [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: IC5250 is described as an advanced, gas-poor merger of S and S0 components, with infrared-bright tidal tails and little present star formation.

quotes.quote: IC5250 IC5250 is an advanced merger system composed of two interacting galaxies, IC5250A and IC5250B. IC5250A and IC5250B are S and S0 types respectively Donzelli2000AJ....120..189D . The FUV image shows bright emission in the central region, associated with the hot, low mass, evolved population of stars. The extent of the system is much larger in infrared. The system also shows tidal tails, which are bright in infrared (Fig. fig:IC5250 ).
IC5250 is gas-poor, and there is no star formation.

gmw_00007115.name: ESO235-IG023
gmw_00007115.overview: ESO235-IG023 is an interacting system composed of two galaxies, ESO235-IG023NED01 and ESO235-IG023NED02, which share a common envelope [arXiv:2308.16152]. The system features a bar visible in the near-infrared and a resonant ring near the bar's corotation [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: ESO235-IG023 is discussed as a two-galaxy system sharing an envelope, with a bar and resonant ring; MUSE data were unavailable for the interacting system.

quotes.quote: ESO235-IG023 ESO235-IG023 system is composed of two galaxies, ESO235-IG023NED01 and ESO235-IG023NED02. These galaxies share the same envelope. The southern galaxy ESO235-IG023NED02 shows a prominent bulge in the infrared map, which is faint in the FUV map. The minor galaxy ESO235-IG023NED02 is faint in the FUV map (Fig. fig:eso235 ). ESO235-IG023 shows a bar in the NIR and a ring near the corotation of the bar in UVIT.

gmw_00007116.name: NGC646NED01
gmw_00007116.overview: NGC646NED01 is an interacting galaxy that features a short bar and two spiral arms [arXiv:2308.16152]. Its western arm contains bright star-forming knots, while its distorted eastern arm appears to be transferring gas toward its companion galaxy [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: NGC646NED01 is the western component of the NGC646 interaction, with two arms, star-forming knots, a distorted diffuse arm, a bar, and gas transfer toward NGC646NED02.

quotes.quote: The western component, NGC646NED01, shows two arms. The western arm of NGC646NED01 shows bright star-forming knots in FUV and H . These star-forming regions are also bright in the NIR image (Fig. fig:NGC646 ). The eastern arm of NGC646NED01 is distorted and appears smooth and diffuse. NGC646NED01 also has a short bar deVaucouleurs1991rc3..book.....D .

The H velocity of the eastern arm of NGC646NED01 differs from its stellar velocity, suggesting that gas is moving towards NGC646NED02 from NGC646NED01. This shows that the NGC646NED02 is accreting gas from the eastern arm of NG646NED01.

gmw_00007117.name: NGC646NED02
gmw_00007117.overview: NGC646NED02 is a satellite galaxy attached to the eastern arm of NGC646NED01 [arXiv:2308.16152]. The galaxy exhibits a recent episode of star formation, counter-rotating stellar and ionized gas disks, and gas accretion from its larger companion [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: NGC646NED02 is described as a satellite attached to NGC646NED01, with recent star formation, counter-rotating disks, distinct metallicity, and gas accretion from NGC646NED01.

quotes.quote: A satellite galaxy, NGC646NED02, is attached to the eastern arm of NGC646NED01. The system shows a recent episode of star formation. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC646NED01 is 0.015 and 0.451 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 )respectively. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC646NED02 is 0.022 and 0.260 respectively (Fig. fig:NGC646 ).

The stellar and ionized gas velocity maps of NGC646NED02 show counter-rotating disks. Such counter rotation has been linked with external gas accretion Corsini2014ASPC..486...51C . In these systems, ionized, molecular and atomic gas rotate with similar amplitude to the stellar body but in the opposite direction. The counter-rotation is also evident from the velocity and metallicity maps. The metallicity of the eastern arm of NGC646NED01 differs from that of NGC646NED02 (Fig. fig:NGC646 ). The H velocity of the eastern arm of NGC646NED01 differs from its stellar velocity, suggesting that gas is moving towards NGC646NED02 from NGC646NED01.

gmw_00007118.name: NGC7733
gmw_00007118.overview: NGC7733 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2106.12441, arXiv:2308.16152] that hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:2106.12441]. It is interacting with the galaxies NGC7734 and NGC7733N [arXiv:2308.16152], showing recent star formation in its arms [arXiv:2106.12441, arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7733 as an interacting barred spiral with a Seyfert nucleus, ongoing arm star formation, an ENLR, and a connection to the newly identified NGC 7733N companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 7733 is a barred spiral that shows knots in the arms and hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus 2016Tempel . NGC 7734 is a barred spiral with peculiar arms 1991deVaucouleurs, 1989Paturel ). The atomic gas content of the galaxy pair is 4.2 10^ 9 M and the molecular content of NGC 7733 and NGC 7734 is 15.4 10^ 8 M and 1.073 10^ 10 M respectively 1997Horellou . 2001Jahan-Miri identified the star-forming knots and their ages in the individual galaxies and found that NGC 7733 has a much younger stellar population than NGC 7734.

The BPT diagram of NGC 7733 also shows the ongoing star formation in the arms and Seyfert-like emission along the minor axis of the bar. Fig.  fig:Ha_OIII_NII shows the color composite image of H (blue), [ O iii ] (green) and [ N ii ] (red) emissions, respectively. The bright H emission, in blue along the arms of NGC 7733, confirms the ongoing recent star formation in the arms of the galaxy. The [ O iii ] emission is extended towards the northeast, which confirms that it is due to emission from the extended narrow-line region (ENLR) along the minor axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC7733 is discussed as a barred spiral in an interacting pair, separated from NGC7734 by 48.3 kpc, with rings, tidal-arm knots, and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC7733-34 NGC7733-34 is a pair of interacting galaxies that show star formation knots in their inner disks, rings and tidal arms. Both NGC7733 and NGC7734 are spiral barred galaxies 1991deVaucouleurs . NGC7733 and NGC7734 are separated by a distance of 48.3 kpc. NGC7733 is also interacting with a minor galaxy, NGC7733N Yadav2021A&A...651L...9Y . NGC7733 and NGC7734 show a recent burst of star formation.

gmw_00007119.name: NGC7734
gmw_00007119.overview: NGC 7734 is a barred spiral galaxy with peculiar arms [arXiv:2106.12441] and a central LINER nucleus [arXiv:2106.12441]. It is interacting with the nearby galaxy NGC 7733 [arXiv:2106.12441, arXiv:2308.16152], showing rings, tidal-arm star formation knots, and a recent burst of star formation [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7734 as part of the interacting NGC 7733--34 pair, a barred spiral with peculiar arms, molecular gas, older stellar population, and central LINER/Seyfert activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 7733--34 is a group of interacting galaxies at a distance of 154 Mpc 1987Arp . NGC7734 is at a distance of 48.3 kpc and has a velocity of 406 km s ^ 1 with respect to NGC7733 1991deVaucouleurs . NGC 7733 is a barred spiral that shows knots in the arms and hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus 2016Tempel . NGC 7734 is a barred spiral with peculiar arms 1991deVaucouleurs, 1989Paturel ).
We find that the paired galaxies NGC 7733 and NGC 7734 host Liner and Seyfert type nuclei, respectively.

sources.summary: NGC7734 is treated as a barred spiral in the NGC7733-34 pair, with rings, tidal-arm star formation knots, recent star formation, and measured FUV/H-alpha SFR.

quotes.quote: NGC7733-34 NGC7733-34 is a pair of interacting galaxies that show star formation knots in their inner disks, rings and tidal arms. Both NGC7733 and NGC7734 are spiral barred galaxies 1991deVaucouleurs . NGC7733 and NGC7734 are separated by a distance of 48.3 kpc. NGC7733 is also interacting with a minor galaxy, NGC7733N Yadav2021A&A...651L...9Y . NGC7733 and NGC7734 show a recent burst of star formation. NGC7733N appears to be an early-type galaxy interacting with NGC7733 and NGC7734. We did not find any SFCs from NGC7733N. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC7733 is 0.008 and 0.194 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 )respectively. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC7734 is 0.014 and 0.200 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 )respectively (Fig. fig:NGC7733 ).

gmw_00007120.name: NGC1356
gmw_00007120.overview: NGC1356 is an intermediate barred galaxy that is tidally interacting with the southwestern galaxy IC 1947 [arXiv:2308.16152]. This interaction has distorted NGC1356's spiral arms, which exhibit active star formation, enhanced metallicity, and a nuclear ring [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: NGC1356 is described as an intermediate barred galaxy interacting with IC1947, with distorted arms, a nuclear ring, active star formation, and arm metallicity enhancement.

quotes.quote: NGC1356 NGC1356 is an intermediate barred galaxy with prominent star formation along its spiral arms. The arms of the galaxy appear distorted due to the tidal interaction (Fig. fig:NGC1356 ). It is interacting with the galaxy IC 1947, which is present in the southwestern region. IC 1947 also shows ongoing star formation in the disk (Fig. fig:IC1947 ). The torques due to the bar can drive the gas radially, inward or outward from corotation, and lead to nuclear, inner and outer rings. Nuclear rings are present near the nuclear region of the galaxy at the inner Lindblad resonance, while the inner rings encircle the bar and are elongated along the bar axis Buta1999Ap&SS.269...79B . NGC1356 also has a nuclear ring, and IC1947 has an inner ring hosting several SFCs. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC1356 is 0.058 and 0.131 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) respectively.
It is also clear from the MUSE images that NGC1356 and IC1947 currently have active episodes of star formation. NGC1356 and IC1947 show higher metallicity along the arms.

gmw_00007121.name: IC1947
gmw_00007121.overview: IC1947 is an interacting companion to the galaxy NGC1356 [arXiv:2308.16152]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus confirmed by WHAN diagram analysis [arXiv:2308.16152], ongoing disk star formation [arXiv:2308.16152], and an inner ring containing several star-forming complexes [arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: IC1947 is presented as NGC1356's interacting companion, with disk star formation, an inner ring with SFCs, arm metallicity enhancement, and WHAN-confirmed AGN activity.

quotes.quote: It is interacting with the galaxy IC 1947, which is present in the southwestern region. IC 1947 also shows ongoing star formation in the disk (Fig. fig:IC1947 ). The torques due to the bar can drive the gas radially, inward or outward from corotation, and lead to nuclear, inner and outer rings. Nuclear rings are present near the nuclear region of the galaxy at the inner Lindblad resonance, while the inner rings encircle the bar and are elongated along the bar axis Buta1999Ap&SS.269...79B . NGC1356 also has a nuclear ring, and IC1947 has an inner ring hosting several SFCs. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of NGC1356 is 0.058 and 0.131 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) respectively. The mean FUV and H _ SFR of IC1947 is 0.082 and 0.073 (M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) respectively (Fig. fig:NGC1356 and Fig. fig:IC1947 ).
It is also clear from the MUSE images that NGC1356 and IC1947 currently have active episodes of star formation. NGC1356 and IC1947 show higher metallicity along the arms.

AGN in IC1947 Some of the galaxies in our sample show LINER emission from their central region in the BPT diagram. This is the case for IC5110, NGC1356 and IC1947. The LINER emission can also arise due to the hot low mass evolved population of stars. These evolved populations of stars can ionize the gas and produce a line ratio similar to that of LINER. We find that the emission from the central region of only IC1947 lies in the strong AGN region of the WHAN diagram (Fig. fig:IC1947 bottom panel), which confirms that IC1947 harbours an accreting black hole.

gmw_00007122.name: 3CR 27
gmw_00007122.overview: 3CR 27 is a classical Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.184 [arXiv:1806.11125]. Optically, it is classified as an elliptical High Excitation Radio Galaxy situated in a region of high optical obscuration [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 27 as a low-redshift FR II HERG in a heavily obscured field, with a probable X-ray nucleus between radio lobes and no detected extended IGM emission.

quotes.quote: 3CR 27 is a classical FR II radio galaxy at z =0.184. From an optical perspective it is an elliptical High Excitation Radio Galaxy (HERG), as confirmed by the spectrum used to update its optical identification after the last revision of the 3CR catalog hiltner91 . It is located in a region of high optical obscuration at low Galactic latitude (i.e., b =5 ^ .5). With the current radio map at 1.4 GHz, it was not possible to locate the radio core. However, its X-ray nucleus was probably detected with a total of 63 counts in a region of radius 2 \ centered on the celestial coordinates reported in Table tab:main hiltner91 and located between the two lobes of the radio galaxy (see Figure fig:3CR27 ).

gmw_00007123.name: 3CR 36
gmw_00007123.overview: 3CR 36 is a compact radio galaxy classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II high-excitation radio galaxy at a redshift of 1.301 [arXiv:1806.11125]. It features a dominant knotty jet extending to the north and a compact optical host galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3CR 36 as a compact high-redshift FR II HERG with a northern knotty jet, compact optical host, and enough nuclear X-ray photons for spectral analysis.

quotes.quote: 3CR 36 is classified as an FR II HERG at z =1.301 hewitt91,jackson97 . In the 8.4 GHz map, its angular extension is 9 , corresponding to a projected size of less than 80 kpc, with a dominant knotty jet in the north. In the HST optical image, 3CR 36 appears as a compact galaxy, and only a central nuclear component is detected mccarthy97 . We detected the nucleus also in our \ snapshot survey (see Figure fig:3CR36 ) and the total 335 X-ray photons observed in the nuclear region allowed us to perform a spectral analysis, which yielded a spectral index _X =0.7 (see Table tab:spec ).

gmw_00007124.name: 3CR 69
gmw_00007124.overview: 3CR 69 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.458 [arXiv:1806.11125]. Optically classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy, its 8.4 GHz radio image reveals two extended lobes and a fainter nucleus detected in X-rays [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3CR 69 as an obscured-field FR II HERG whose 8.4 GHz image shows two lobes and a fainter nucleus detected in X-rays.

quotes.quote: 3CR 69 is an FR II radio galaxy, optically classified as HERG, at z =0.458 hiltner91 . Since the source lies in a heavily obscured region at Galactic latitude b =-0 ^ .9, its optical identification was made only after the last revision of the 3CR catalog. The radio image at 8.4 GHz shows two extended lobes and a fainter nucleus that was the only X-ray detection (see Figure fig:3CR69 ). The spectral analysis of the nucleus yielded a spectral index _X =0.4, as reported in Table tab:spec , and the pileup fraction estimate is 5 .

gmw_00007125.name: 3CR 124
gmw_00007125.overview: 3CR 124 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.083 [arXiv:1806.11125]. Optically classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy, it features a compact double-lobe structure alongside a misaligned, more extended radio emission that may represent a relic lobe [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper details 3CR 124 as an FR II HERG with compact double lobes, a misaligned relic-lobe candidate, aligned optical emission, and diffuse X-ray/lobe-associated emission.

quotes.quote: 3CR 124 is an FR II radio galaxy at z =1.083 hewitt91 . Optically, it is classified as a HERG jackson97 . The 8.4 GHz image shows a small scale compact double-lobe structure, with a fainter central core and angular extension of 2 .5 , corresponding to about 20 kpc. However, 3CR 124 also shows additional, more extended, asymmetric radio emission misaligned by 45^ measured in the west direction from the line connecting the other two, separated by 6 \ (see Figure fig:3CR124 ), suggestive of a relic lobe mccarthy89,dunlop93 .

gmw_00007126.name: 3CR 173
gmw_00007126.overview: 3CR 173 is a compact steep-spectrum radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.035 [arXiv:1806.11125]. Optically classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy, it features a small double radio structure where the southwest core is the sole source of X-ray emission [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3CR 173 as a compact steep-spectrum HERG with a small double radio structure and a southwest core that is the only X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: 3CR 173 is a CSS at z =1.035, optically classified as a HERG jackson97 . A double structure is visible in the radio image at 4.9 GHz, with the two peaks of radio surface brightness separated by only 2 , correspondent to 15 kpc at the source redshift (see Figure fig:3CR173 ). The core is the dominant south-west component, characterised by a flattening of the integrated radio spectrum near 38 MHz tyul00 and it is the only X-ray detection in this source.

gmw_00007127.name: 3CR 194
gmw_00007127.overview: 3CR 194 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125] classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125]. Its optical host is elongated and composed of two components, while its asymmetric radio lobes display an irregular quadrupolar shape [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 194 as an FR II HERG with asymmetric lobes, quadrupolar radio structure, elongated two-component optical host, and high rotation measures near the NW hotspot.

quotes.quote: 3CR 194 , at z =1.184 strom90 , was optically classified as a HERG jackson97 and it is an FR II radio galaxy with a double hotspot in the NW lobe (see Figure fig:3CR194 ). A high-sensitivity VLA image at 8.4 GHz showed that the two radio lobes are asymmetrically located with respect to the radio core and the north-western lobe does not extend along the radio source axis, but is almost perpendicular to it fernini14 . Weak radio emission in the EW direction was also observed at 5 GHz, giving an irregular quadrupolar shape and suggesting a movement of the source in the ambient medium in a westward direction strom90 . The optical galaxy in HST images is elongated North-South and composed of two components separated by 2 djorgovski88,mccarthy97 .

gmw_00007128.name: 3CR 208.1
gmw_00007128.overview: 3CR 208.1 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.02 [arXiv:1806.11125]. Its optical image reveals a compact nucleus, while its radio structure features a curved jet in a northwestern lobe and an X-ray-detected radio core [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3CR 208.1 as a quasar whose flux is modestly gravitationally amplified, with a compact optical nucleus, curved radio lobe, and X-ray-detected radio core.

quotes.quote: 3CR 208.1 is a quasar (QSO). The optical counterpart was first identified with a N-type galaxy at z =1.02 spinrad85 . Gravitational amplification due to a foreground source 3 SE at z = 0.159 was found to brighten the QSO by several tenths of a magnitude and to increase its radio flux by a factor of 1.5 lefevre90 . The optical image of 3CR 208.1 has a compact nucleus and a secondary component 0.6 \ NE mccarthy97 while the radio image at 14.9 GHz shows a lobe 5 \ NW with a curved jet. The only feature detected by \ is the radio core (see Figure fig:3CR208.1 ).

gmw_00007129.name: 3CR 220.2
gmw_00007129.overview: 3CR 220.2 is a compact quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.157 [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125] within a group of large spiral galaxies [arXiv:1605.03196]. The system features two radio lobes separated by 60 kpc [arXiv:1806.11125], with a hotspot detected in the southwestern lobe [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: 3C 220.2 is a compact QSO in a group of large spiral galaxies, with nearby IR sources and radio lobes including a southwestern hot spot.

quotes.quote: 3C 220.2 (z=1.157) 3C 220.2 appears in our IR and optical images as a compact source with a few immediate neighbors, located within a group of large spiral galaxies. In the IR there are 5 sources to the south and one to the northwest, all within 6 . There is little to no signal from these sources in our optical image. Radio data show signal centered on the QSO, as well as two lobes to the northeast and southwest. There is evidence of a hot spot in the southwestern lobe.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3CR 220.2 as a quasar with radio lobes and hotspot X-ray emission, plus a bright nuclear X-ray source affected mildly by pileup.

quotes.quote: 3CR 220.2 is a QSO at z =1.157 spinrad85 . In the radio image at 4.9 GHz, both lobes, separated by 60 kpc, show a hotspot in the outer region. The peak of the radio surface brightness in the southern hotspot is coincident with a feature visible in the HST optical image, but also with the peak of the X-ray emission detected and designated as h s 5 in Table tab:jets . Two X-ray photons are detected in the radio NE lobe/hotspot region. However, given the presence of X-ray photons at similar angular separation from the radio core with no radio counterparts, we cannot exclude that those in the northern lobe/hotspot are fluctuations of the local background.

gmw_00007130.name: 3CR 222
gmw_00007130.overview: 3CR 222 is the most distant Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy in the 3CR catalog [arXiv:1806.11125]. It is classified as an ultra-steep spectrum, compact double radio source with a weak X-ray nucleus and a faint-quartet optical identification [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 222 as the most distant known 3CR FR I radio galaxy, an ultra-steep compact double source with a faint-quartet optical identification and weak X-ray nucleus.

quotes.quote: 3CR 222 is known to be the most distant FR I radio galaxy of the 3CR catalog to date, having z =1.339 heckman94 , although there is no published spectrum in the literature. It was classified as an ultra steep spectrum radio source, since it has a radio spectral index _R =1.52 in the frequency range from 10 MHz to 1 GHz roland82 . 3CR 222 was described as a compact double radio source, dominated by the nuclear emission at 1.4 GHz and with steep-spectrum emission coming from a region separated by 1 .2 to the south law-green95, strom90 . The optical identification is the W member of an apparent faint quartet djorgovski88 . The X-ray nucleus is detected at 3 level of significance with only three photons observed by \ in a circular region of radius 2 \ (see Figure fig:3CR222 ).

gmw_00007131.name: 3CR 238
gmw_00007131.overview: 3CR 238 is a high-redshift Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125]. It features a compact nucleus with faint optical extensions, and its northern radio knot is also detected in X-rays [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3CR 238 as a high-redshift FR II HERG possibly affected by foreground lensing, with HST extensions and an X-ray-detected northern radio knot/hotspot.

quotes.quote: 3CR 238 is a HERG with a classical FR II morphology at z =1.405 jackson97 . It could be affected by gravitational lensing by the Abell cluster A949 with an estimated redshift z =0.142 along its line of sight lefevre88bis . However, the core of this galaxy cluster is outside the field of view of the \ observation. The optical observation made with the HST shows a compact nucleus and faint extensions in the NS direction mccarthy97 . In the 8.4 GHz image, the southern jet shows two elongated knots separated by 1 \ and 2 \ from the nucleus while the northern knot has a wider structure that is also detected in the X-ray (see Table tab:jets and Figure fig:hotspots ).

gmw_00007132.name: 3CR 255
gmw_00007132.overview: 3CR 255 is a high-redshift radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 1.355 [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125] that is optically classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125]. It is the largest member of a compact group within a likely cluster [arXiv:1605.03196], showing optical emission that suggests active star formation [arXiv:1605.03196].

sources.summary: 3C 255 appears as the largest object in a compact group, likely in a cluster, with optical emission suggesting active star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 255 (z=1.355) Our IR image shows a collection of extended sources in the vicinity of 3C 255, which appears as the largest object in the central compact group. There are two objects to the southeast (at about 1.3 and 2 from the target), one to the west at 2.5 , and two elongated features to the northwest. Spectroscopic data from g90 suggests that all of the objects in the central compact group are members of a cluster. All of these objects, except for one of the elongated galaxies, appear in the optical image as well, suggesting that active star formation is ongoing. Radio maps indicate a relatively weak signal at the center of the host galaxy, as well as a brighter, compact radio source, possibly a hot spot, roughly 1 to the southeast of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 255 as a high-redshift HERG radio galaxy with two radio components, an optical host, possible nearby group/cluster environment, and weak X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: 3CR 255 is a z =1.355 radio galaxy with two radio components separated by 10 kpc giraud90 . It is optically classified as a HERG jackson97 . The 8.4 GHz map indicates a weak signal at the centre of the host galaxy, as well as a brighter, compact radio source to the southeast (see Figure fig:3CR255 ). The northern radio component is located at the position of the optical galaxy, however more data are necessary to verify if it is compact and if it has a flat radio spectrum, as expected for the core of radio galaxies.

gmw_00007133.name: 3CR 297
gmw_00007133.overview: 3CR 297 is a quasar at a redshift of z = 1.406 [arXiv:1806.11125] that exhibits a complex morphology suggestive of ongoing merger activity [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125]. Its optical structure resolves into two distinct components [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125], while its radio emission is elongated and curved with a knotty northern jet [arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: 3C 297 is described as a complex merger candidate, with a resolved core, diffuse emission, an arc north of the core, and an elongated southern source.

quotes.quote: 3C 297 (z=1.406) This target appears in our data with a complex morphology suggestive of ongoing merger activity marco15 . In the IR image, 3C 297 appears as a compact but resolved core surrounded by an area with a radius of more than 2 of more diffuse emission. Directly north of the core by 2 , there is an arc-shaped area of emission. Approximately 1 south of the target there is also a highly elongated source. In the optical image, the core of 3C 297 is resolved into two distinct areas of emission, with the eastern area much more extended than the western region.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3CR 297 as a quasar with peculiar curved radio morphology, possible merger activity, extended jet-related X-ray emission, and diffuse environmental X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 3CR 297 is a quasar with a peculiar radio morphology, lying at z =1.406 spinrad85 . The emission observed in the 8.4 GHz and 4.85 GHz maps, computed in sec:Radobs , is elongated and curved with a knotty enhancement at the end of the northern jet (see Figure fig:3CR297combo ). In both radio images the emission to the south spreads over a much wider area, with a stronger concentration of signal centered on a double elongated source approximately 8 kpc (i.e., 1 ) in projected distance from the host galaxy. The optical image shows two components separated by 0 .3 mccarthy97 . Recent HST IR and optical images suggest the presence of ongoing merger activity in the area hilbert16 .

gmw_00007134.name: 3CR 300.1
gmw_00007134.overview: 3CR 300.1 is a high-redshift Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125] with an irregular morphology likely resulting from a recent merger [arXiv:1605.03196]. The galaxy features a diffuse stellar envelope [arXiv:1605.03196] containing localized regions of active star formation [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125].

sources.summary: 3C 300.1 is an extended galaxy with a bright nucleus, diffuse stellar envelope, localized star-forming region, and irregular merger-like morphology.

quotes.quote: 3C 300.1 (z=1.159) This galaxy appears within our IR image as an extended object containing a bright nucleus within an area of diffuse stellar emission. Also present within the extended envelope is a localized region of increased brightness to the southwest of the galaxy center. This is the only region of the galaxy other than the core that also appears within our optical image, suggesting an area of active star formation. The overall morphology is irregular most likely as a result of a recent merger event. Numerous extended sources are visible in the IR image around the central galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3CR 300.1 as a classical FR II HERG with weak central core, irregular star-forming optical host, possible overdensity, and an X-ray point source nearby.

quotes.quote: 3CR 300.1 is a classical FR II HERG at z =1.159 djorgovski88 , with two lobes separated by 10 \ (i.e., 80 kpc at the source redshift) and a central core fainter than the lobes in the 14.9 GHz map jackson97 . The radio core is not detected in the 8.4 GHz radio image. In HST IR images, the galaxy appears as an extended irregular source with diffuse emission that suggests the presence of active star formation regions hilbert16 . Kotyla et al. (2016) reported a possible presence of a source over density surrounding 3CR 300.1 and a possible cluster/group detection using ``red sequence'' method that does not appear clearly evident to us, however we checked for the presence of diffuse X-ray emission on tens of kpc scale in our \ snapshot observation.

gmw_00007135.name: 3CR 305.1
gmw_00007135.overview: 3CR 305.1 is a low-excitation radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 1.132 [arXiv:1806.11125]. It exhibits a complex morphology suggestive of an ongoing merger [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125], with nuclear optical emission that indicates widespread star formation [arXiv:1605.03196].

sources.summary: 3C 305.1 shows complex merger-like structure, a bright extended center, nearby elongated and compact sources, and nuclear optical emission suggesting widespread star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 305.1 (z=1.132) This galaxy clearly appears in our IR image with a complex morphology suggestive of ongoing merger activity, and includes an extended, bright central area surrounded by more diffuse signal. Approximately 1.5 north of the brightest emission, there is a highly elongated galaxy running almost directly east-west. In addition, there is a distinct, compact source located 2 southwest of the center of the target. In the optical image, the region in the center of the galaxy appears with an irregular shape spanning the same area covered by the brightest area in the IR image, suggesting wide spread star formation in the nucleus. SED-fitting of Herschel data suggests a star formation rate of 220^ +40 _ 20 M_ per year for 3C 305.1. podigachowski The elongated object to the north also displays significant optical signal.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3CR 305.1 as a CSS LERG with merger-like optical morphology, aligned radio/optical structure, jet knots, no clear radio nucleus, and X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: 3CR 305.1 at z =1.132 spinrad85 , is catalogued as a CSS odea98 and optically it is the only Low Excitation Radio Galaxy (LERG) in our sample. The optical galaxy has a complex morphology suggestive of on-going merger activity hilbert16 : the optical emission can be seen over more than 2 \ with a curved S-shaped structure in the inner 1 \ radius and a close agreement between optical and radio orientation mccarthy97 . Radio images at 8.4 GHz show the northern jet with more elongated emission and a southern circular lobe. In the 15 GHz radio map the northern jet shows three knots: the southernmost has a radio spectral index of 0.6, and it is highly polarised (60 ), therefore unlikely to be the core vanbreugel92 .

gmw_00007136.name: NGC454W
gmw_00007136.overview: NGC454W is a blue irregular galaxy that forms the western component of the interacting NGC454 system [arXiv:1201.2169]. It shows no optical evidence of an active galactic nucleus and is characterized by a population of young star clusters [arXiv:1201.2169].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC454W as the blue irregular western component of the interacting NGC454 system, associated with young star clusters and lacking optical AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: Optical studies ( Arp87,Johansson88,Stiavelli98 ) of the interacting system NGC454 (see Figure fig:imaging , right panel) describe it as a pair of emission line galaxies consisting of a red elliptical galaxy (eastern component, hereafter NGC454E) and a blue irregular galaxy (western component, hereafter NGC454W), at redshift z=0.0122. The distorted morphology of both these galaxies, together with the spectroscopic and photometric evidence of a young stellar population, is a clear sign of the interacting nature of this system. Furthermore, three very blue knots (discussed in section other_src ), probably Strongren spheres surrounding clusters of very hot newly formed stars, are located (and likely related) to the south of NGC454W. HST observations of the system, performed with the Wide Field Planetary Camera 2, confirmed that NGC454 is in the early stages of interaction Stiavelli98 . The above authors stated also that an important fraction of gas has drifted to the center of the eastern component, but it has yet not produced any significant visible star formation activity; a population of young star clusters has formed around the western component. The optical spectrum of NGC454E is consistent with that of a Seyfert 2 galaxy (although none of the high excitation lines, e.g. HeII lines, can be seen) while no optical evidence of an AGN is present in the spectrum of NGC454W which is fully consistent with that of a star-forming galaxy Johansson88 .

gmw_00007137.name: J2253 + 1116
gmw_00007137.overview: J2253 + 1116 is a low-mass galaxy that exhibits relatively fast outflows [arXiv:2112.08045]. These outflows may be driven by excitation from an active galactic nucleus or fast radiative shocks [arXiv:2112.08045].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J2253 + 1116 as a low-mass galaxy with unusually fast outflows, a high outflow-to-escape velocity ratio, and possible AGN or shock excitation.

quotes.quote: J2253 + 1116 has relatively fast outflows with v_ max 200 km s^ 1 in both H and [ Oiii ] lines.

In particular, J2253 + 1116 has v_ max /v_ esc 3 , which is significantly larger than unity. Given that the H line in J2253 + 1116 has relatively weak broad components, deeper observations are needed to reveal the fate of outflowing gas in this galaxy.
The other two objects J2253 + 1116 and J1401 0040 are offset from the star-forming region, indicating possible excitation from active galactic nuclei (AGN) or fast radiative shock.

gmw_00007138.name: DES J0029-3814
gmw_00007138.overview: DES J0029-3814 is a strong gravitational lensing system that has been modeled in the infrared F160W band [arXiv:2209.03094]. The system exhibits a high external shear magnitude of 0.2, which is likely caused by an overdense environment located to its north [arXiv:2209.03094].

sources.summary: DES J0029-3814 was modeled in F160W only; the paper describes its lens light, PSF, mass-light offset, and high external shear likely tied to an overdense environment.

quotes.quote: DES J0029-3814 This lensing system was modeled solely in the IR F160W band. In the remaining bands sufficient arc light from the host galaxy could not be identified by eye . We used two S e rsic profiles and a central point source component for the primary lens light. The PSF was estimated by selecting two stars in the field. The best-fit parameters in Table tab:results_param show that the centers of the mass and light distribution are offset by 0.04 \ in x direction and by 0.02 \ in y direction. We obtain a high shear magnitude of 0.2 with a low position angle (14 ^ ), which is possibly due to the overdense environment to the North of the lensing system.

gmw_00007139.name: DES J0214-2105
gmw_00007139.overview: DES J0214-2105 is a quadruply lensed quasar system discovered within the Dark Energy Survey footprint [arXiv:1901.04414]. Multi-epoch observations of the system show evidence of flux variability, which may be caused by microlensing or intrinsic quasar variability [arXiv:1901.04414].

sources.summary: The paper confirms WG0214-2105 as a quadruply lensed quasar, measures source and deflector properties, models the lens, and finds two-epoch photometric evidence for variability.

quotes.quote: Here we present the spectroscopic confirmation of two new quadruplets WG0214-2105 and WG2100-4452 discovered by within the Dark Energy Survey (DES) public footprints. We have conducted spectroscopic follow-up of these systems with the Southern African Large Telescope as part of a program that aims at confirming the largest possible number of optically selected strong gravitational lensing systems in the Equatorial and Southern Hemisphere. For both systems, we present the spectra for the sources and deflectors that allowed us to estimate the source redshifts and unambiguously confirm their lensing nature.

One of the quadruplets, WG0214-2105, was also observed by Pan-STARRS, allowing us to estimate the apparent brightness of each quasar image at two different epochs, and thus to find evidence for flux variability. This result could suggest a microlensing event for the faintest components, although intrinsic variability cannot be excluded with only two epochs. Finally, we present simple lens models for both quadruplets, obtaining Einstein radii, SIE velocity dispersions, ellipticities, and position angles of the lens systems, as well as time delay predictions assuming a concordance cosmological model.

sources.summary: DES J0214-2105 was modeled across three bands, but underfitted quasar amplitudes led the paper to present a F160W single-band lens-mass model with aligned mass and light.

quotes.quote: DES J0214-2105 We modeled this system in all three considered bands (F160W, F475X, and F814W) and noticed a strong underfitting of the quasar amplitudes in the F475X band after step 5(a) (see Table tab:Modelingtable_multi ). This can be due to the faint arc light and an imperfect PSF model resulting in higher residuals in the multiple quasar image regions. The underfitting is not directly obvious from the residuals because the missing quasar amplitude is compensated by high source intensity values in a single pixel. To avoid negative impact of this band on the lens mass model, we present the F160W single-band model. The PSF was approximated with one star from the field in the F160W band, and 2 stars in each of the F475X and F814 bands.

gmw_00007140.name: DES J0420-4037
gmw_00007140.overview: DES J0420-4037 is a strong gravitational lensing system containing a lensed quasar [arXiv:2209.03094]. Modeling of the system reveals a small offset of less than 0.01 arcseconds between its mass and light distributions [arXiv:2209.03094].

sources.summary: DES J0420-4037 was modeled in three bands, with the F160W result favored; the paper reports small mass-light offsets and possible counterimages of a second lensed background source.

quotes.quote: DES J0420-4037 This lensing system was modeled in all three bands. As in the case of J0214 2105, the quasar amplitudes in the F475X band are strongly underfitted. We present the F160W single-band model results. We approximated the PSF in the F160W band by choosing two stars in the field; for the F475X and F814 bands, we used one star. Mass and light show a small offset of <0.01 \ in the x direction and 0.01 \ in the y directions.

gmw_00007141.name: PS J1606-2333
gmw_00007141.overview: PS J1606-2333 is a strongly lensed system containing a central lens galaxy and four resolved quasar images [arXiv:1803.07601]. The primary lens light has been modeled with two Seersic profiles, a central point source, and a nearby satellite component [arXiv:2209.03094].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1606 2333 as a resolved quad lens with HST imaging, a lens galaxy, shear in the mass model, and possible astrometric perturbation.

quotes.quote: J1606 2333 This system was identified as a double. Though the seeing for the WHT spectrum was poor, quasar emission lines are visible across the broad trace. The two-component decomposition is made the same distance as that between the detections, and a narrow uniform aperture is used for the spectral extraction. An archival, shallow Chandra image shows extended emission at the positions of the two detections and also near image C, as labelled in Figure fig:psf_subtraction .

Recent HST imaging (proposal 15320, PI: Treu) clearly resolves four quasar images and a lens galaxy. The mass model suggests a shear of 0.15 and an image position chi-squared of 10. The majority of this is attributed to a poorly fitting position of image D. The best-fit models consistently place D 0.02-0.04 \ more northern than the measured value.

sources.summary: The paper gives PS J1606 a population-mean halo-mass prior and derives a photometric deflector-redshift PDF because it lacks a measured spectroscopic redshift.

quotes.quote: We use this procedure to construct a prior for the halo mass of each quad, with the exceptions of B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405. The stellar velocity dispersions implied by the Einstein radii of these systems is significantly lower than the stellar velocity dispersion in the sample of quads used to calibrate the halo-to-stellar mass ratio in Lagattuta++10 , and as such the estimate of the halo mass using the above procedure may not be accurate for these systems. For B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405, we therefore assume the population mean of 10^ 13.3 0.3 inferred by Lagattuta++10 .

The quads PS J1606 and WGD J0405 do not have measured spectroscopic redshifts, so we use photometry from Shajib++18 to obtain photometric redshift estimates. The photometry from Shajib++18 comes in three bands: F160W, F814W, and F475X with magnitude uncertainties of 0.1-0.3 dex. We use the software package eazy Brammer++08 , and restrict the templates to only consider the SEDs for early-type galaxies, which are 90 of galaxies acting as strong lenses. We verify this procedure is accurate by applying it to other deflectors in sample analyzed by Shajib++18 that have measured spectroscopic redshifts, and then proceed to derive PDFs for deflector redshifts in the systems PS J1606 and WGD J0405. The results are shown in Figure fig:photozs . The top row shows four quads from the sample analyzed in Shajib++18 with measured spectroscopic redshifts, and the bottom row shows the pdfs output by eazy for the systems PS J1606 and WGD J0405.

sources.summary: PS J1606-2333 was modeled in F160W with a nearby satellite component; the paper reports significant arc-light complications but close mass-light alignment and a low power-law slope.

quotes.quote: PS J1606-2333 This lensing system was modeled in the F160W band with two S e rsic profiles and a central point source component for the primary lens light. Although we initially included the other bands because they show significant arc light, the source reconstruction shows no visible source (above the noise level) in these bands. We exclude these bands and optimize only the single-band model. For this lensing system, the PSF was stacked from 5 neighboring stars. There is a bright light clump in the South below image B which is embedded in a faint, extended structure.

gmw_00007142.name: DES J2100-4452
gmw_00007142.overview: DES J2100-4452 is a quadruply lensed quasar system [arXiv:1901.04414]. The lens galaxy is crossed from north to south by a dust lane [arXiv:2209.03094], and its stellar velocity dispersion has been spectroscopically measured [arXiv:1901.04414].

sources.summary: The paper confirms WG2100-4452 as a quadruply lensed quasar, measures the brighter deflector’s velocity dispersion, and uses blended SALT spectra plus lens modeling to support its lensing nature.

quotes.quote: Here we present the spectroscopic confirmation of two new quadruplets WG0214-2105 and WG2100-4452 discovered by within the Dark Energy Survey (DES) public footprints. We have conducted spectroscopic follow-up of these systems with the Southern African Large Telescope as part of a program that aims at confirming the largest possible number of optically selected strong gravitational lensing systems in the Equatorial and Southern Hemisphere. For both systems, we present the spectra for the sources and deflectors that allowed us to estimate the source redshifts and unambiguously confirm their lensing nature. For the brighter deflector (WG2100-4452), we measure the stellar velocity dispersion from the spectrum. We also obtain photometry for both lenses, directly from DES multi-band images, isolating the lens galaxies from the quasar images.
For WG2100-4452 we obtained only one slit position with PA = 40 degrees and 3.0 , observed under thin cloud and very high high lunar brightness (94 , corresponding to a moon distance of 30 degrees).

However, having clear evidence for emission lines from A+B, given the very specific geometrical configuration and the colors, and finally, given that this system can be perfectly fit with a simple lens model (as we will show in Sec sec:models ), we are fully confident about its lensing nature.

sources.summary: DES J2100-4452 is modeled only in F160W; the paper notes weak arc light in other bands, a dust lane across the lens galaxy, and mass-light offsets.

quotes.quote: DES J2100-4452 The final model of this system includes only the F160W band. The F475X and F814W bands show small hints of arc light, but including these bands in the model lead to no visible source reconstruction. The PSF was created by using five stars. There seems to be a dust lane crossing the lens galaxy from North to South whose influence on the model needs to be investigated in future work. The dust lane can be seen best in the bluest band (in our case the F475X band) and given its rest frame wavelength of 1.3 microns in the F160W band the effect on the model should be minimal.

gmw_00007143.name: SDSS J082451.43+211743.3
gmw_00007143.overview: SDSS J082451.43+211743.3 is a faint, small dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 65.9 Mpc [arXiv:2405.00113]. The galaxy is characterized by a metal-poor environment and a spectrum dominated by strong emission lines [arXiv:2405.00113].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J082451.43+211743.3 as SN 2023ufx’s faint, small, metal-poor dwarf host, deriving its redshift, distance, emission-line metallicity, and integrated photometric properties.

quotes.quote: SDSS J082451.43+211743.3 is a faint ( g = 20.86 0.04  mag) extended blue source with an absolute g band luminosity of M_g = -13.23 0.16  mag. The galaxy is small, with a g band Kron radius kron1980 of just R_g = 3 6 corresponding to a projected intrinsic radius of 1.1  kpc. exploded 2 7 ( 0.8  kpc) from the nucleus.

Fitting the emission-line spectrum of the host-galaxy with , , , , and produces a refined redshift of z=0.0152 0.0001 . At this redshift, the host-galaxy is located at d 65.9 4.4  Mpc from the dynamical velocity-field estimate of shaya2022 , corresponding to a distance modulus of 34.09 0.15  mag using cosmological parameters H_0 = 70  km/s/Mpc and _m = 0.3 . The host galaxy spectrum is dominated by these strong emission lines.

The faintness of the host ( M_ g, 13  mag) places it outside the bounds of the calibration samples for these relations, but extrapolating to such faint dwarfs (as done by anderson2018 for the host of the metal-poor SN II 2015bs) produces a similar global metallicity estimate of _ 10 (Z_ host / Z_ ) = -1.3 0.3  dex. We derive integrated host-galaxy properties by fitting the host-galaxy photometry from the Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX; martin2005, morrissey2007 , SDSS york2000 , the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer WISE; wright2010 using Fast fast with free parameters for the total stellar mass, stellar age, internal extinction, star-formation rate (SFR), and the star-formation history modeled as a delayed exponential.

gmw_00007144.name: SDSS J110057.70-005304.5
gmw_00007144.overview: SDSS J110057.70-005304.5 is a changing-look quasar located at a redshift of z=0.378 [arXiv:1805.06921]. Over a twenty-year period, its ultraviolet continuum and broad hydrogen emission lines faded and subsequently recovered [arXiv:1805.06921].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J110057.70-005304.5 as a changing-look quasar whose UV continuum and broad lines faded and recovered, best explained by inner accretion-disk changes.

quotes.quote: Here we report on four epochs of spectroscopy of SDSS J110057.70-005304.5, a quasar at a redshift of z=0.378 whose UV continuum and broad hydrogen emission lines have faded, and then returned over the past 20 years. The change in this quasar was initially identified in the infrared, and an archival spectrum from 2010 shows an intermediate phase of the transition during which the flux below rest-frame 3400 \ has decreased by close to an order of magnitude. This combination is unique compared to previously published examples of changing-look quasars, and is best explained by dramatic changes in the innermost regions of the accretion disk. The optical continuum has been rising since mid-2016, leading to a prediction of a rise in hydrogen emission line flux in the next year. Increases in the infrared flux are beginning to follow, delayed by a 3 year observed timescale.

gmw_00007145.name: J2317+0005
gmw_00007145.overview: J2317+0005 is a UV cutoff quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.321 [arXiv:1805.06921]. Over a timescale of just a few weeks, the active galactic nucleus underwent a cycle of UV emission collapse, dimming, and subsequent recovery [arXiv:1805.06921].

sources.summary: J2317+0005 is described as a UV cutoff quasar with a weeks-long cycle of UV collapse, dimming, and recovery, making it an archetype for accretion-disk models.

quotes.quote: Those authors report the first discovery of a UV cutoff quasar, SDSS J231742.60 +000535.1 (hereafter J2317+0005; redshift z = 0.321 ), observed spectroscopically by SDSS three times, on UT 2000 September 29, UT 2001 September 25, and UT 2001 October 18. In the case of J2317+0005, a cycle of UV emission collapse, quasar dimming, and recovery was observed over the course of just a few weeks. For J1100-0053, the cycle is far longer; however, the combination of optical and infrared light curves, as well as observing J1100-0053 at four separate spectral stages is currently unique. As such, J1100-0053 and J2317+0005 are now two archetypal objects that any accretion disk model must predict and explain e.g., Lawrence2018 .

gmw_00007146.name: MCG -01-06-070
gmw_00007146.overview: MCG -01-06-070 is a barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.016 [arXiv:1904.10034]. It has a star formation rate of 0.649 solar masses per year and hosted the supernova SN 2016hnk [arXiv:1904.10034].

sources.summary: The paper uses integral-field spectroscopy of MCG -01-06-070, the SB host of SN 2016hnk, to measure its stellar history, star formation, metallicity, dust, and local supernova environment.

quotes.quote: The object exploded in the type SB galaxy MCG -01-06-070 of the Morphological Catalogue of Galaxies 1962TrSht..32....1V , 11.5 arcsec East of the center, which at the redshift of the galaxy z=0.016 corresponds to 3.71 kpc (see inner panel of Fig. fig:finder ). The main parameters of the supernova and host galaxy are listed in Table table:sn_parameters .

The resulting integrated spectrum of MCG -01-06-070 is presented in Figure fig:envglobal with the best STARLIGHT SSP fit. The recovered star-formation history is also shown in the inner plot. The stellar mass of the galaxy is 4.84 10 ^ 10 , and the average stellar age of the galaxy is 690 Myr, although we were able to recover three main peaks of formation: a very old component at around 6 Gyr, an intermediate burst at around 1 Gyr, and a young component of a few Myr. The best fit also provides an oversolar stellar metallicity of Z=0.03 and a non-significant optical stellar extinction of A _V =0.02 0.06 mag.

For MCG -01-06-070 we found an H EW of 7.04 0.18 , a SFR of 0.649 0.234 yr ^ 1 , and an oxygen abundance of 8.64 0.11 dex. The big colored dot represent the position of MCG -01-06-070 parameters in such distributions, and the vertical and horizontal strips show the actual parameter value of this galaxy and the rank within PISCO.

gmw_00007147.name: FCC 083
gmw_00007147.overview: FCC 083 is a massive early-type galaxy located in the low-density region of its cluster [arXiv:1906.08187]. Its globular cluster system exhibits rotation along the same axis as the host galaxy [arXiv:2003.13705] and contains several large spatial overdensities [arXiv:2201.06704].

sources.summary: The paper uses FCC 083 as a worked example: its globular cluster system rotates with the galaxy, has constrained kinematic modeling, separable red and blue populations, and compared metallicities.

quotes.quote: As one example, Fig. fig:vel_maps_example shows the stellar LOS velocity map of FCC 083, and we overplotted the LOS velocities of the GCs with circles. Because used a slightly different setup to extract the LOS velocity, for example with a different masking of sky regions and a different wavelength range, we corrected any possible systematic offsets in velocity between the map and the GCs by separately fitting the central pixel of each galaxy with the same pPXF setup that was used for the GCs. We found no offset > 20 km s ^ 1 . The GC system of FCC 083 clearly shows rotation along the same axis as the galaxy and we found similar behaviours in other galaxies such as FCC 147, FCC 184, FCC 190, FCC 153, FCC 167, FCC 193, and FCC 219 as can be clearly seen in App. app:all_the_plots . In these figures, we show the LOS velocities of the GCs in comparison to the stellar light, both on the LOS velocity maps and as a profile along the main kinematic axis.

For FCC 083, FCC 161, FCC 147, FCC 167, and FCC 276, we can compare the red and blue GCs separately. Except for FCC 147, the red GCs show higher rotation amplitudes and follow the stellar rotation more closely (see also Pota2013 ). In general, the rotation amplitudes of the blue GCs are smaller. In FCC 161, FCC 083, and FCC 276, the red GCs further have lower velocity dispersions than the blue population.

Metallicities of globular clusters Figure fig:metal_example compares the radial distribution of the spectroscopic metallicities of the GCs of FCC 083 to that of the host galaxy, as an example (see also the right panels in Fig. fig:maps2 fig:maps15 for the remaining galaxies).

sources.summary: FCC 83 is described as a bright, massive early-type intermediate infaller with regular stellar rotation, declining velocity dispersion, and central line-strength peaks.

quotes.quote: FCC 83 (NGC 1351) -- Fig. fig:FCC083map FCC 83 is located on the western side of the cluster at a distance R_ proj =1.7^ from the cluster centre. It is among the brightest and more massive ETGs in the low-density region of the cluster and is an intermediate infaller. It shows a regular stellar velocity map with a maximum rotation velocity of 100 km s ^ 1 . The stellar velocity dispersion peaks in the centre ( 150 km s ^ 1 ) and decreases outwards ( 60 km s ^ 1 at R 50 arcsec). Except for the central regions ( R 5 arcsec), PA_ kin is consistent with PA_ phot .

sources.summary: The paper reports several GC structures in NGC1351, including elongated I1 and I2 features plus smaller circular overdensities with distinct red- and blue-GC visibility.

quotes.quote: The globular clusters system of NGC1351 features several large spatial structures (Figure fig:ngc1351 ). The very statistically significant overdensities I1 and I2 display similarly elongated morphology: I1 lies along an almost tangential direction to the D _ 25 elliptical isophote of the host galaxy at the S end of the major axis, unlike I2 that originates in the center and radially extends to the W outskirts of the observed field. Both structures are also visible in the blue GCs residual map, while I1 is not detected in the red residual map. Four additional density structures (I3 and the smaller i1, i2 and i3) have roughly circular shapes and are located at different galactocentric distances in the E and W sides of the galaxy. i1, i2 and i3 are observed in the blue GCs map, while I3 is only weakly detected in the red GCs residual map.

gmw_00007148.name: blob 1
gmw_00007148.overview: Blob 1 is a 700 kpc remnant radio galaxy characterized by a low-surface-brightness, amorphous morphology [arXiv:1508.07239, arXiv:2111.04776]. Its host galaxy features a faint core [arXiv:1508.07239, arXiv:2111.04776] and is currently interacting with a companion galaxy located 15 kpc away [arXiv:1508.07239].

sources.summary: The paper presents blob1 as a 700 kpc low-surface-brightness remnant radio galaxy whose inactive engine, weak core, spectral curvature, and nearby companion suggest a long fading phase.

quotes.quote: Here we present one 700 kpc remnant radio galaxy at coordinates J2000.0 RA 18h 28m 20.4s Dec +49d 14m 43s (we call this blob1) that was discovered as part of this search. blob1 was first identified on purely morphological arguments based on its unusually large angular extension with amorphous shape, low surface-brightness, and lack of compact features. We investigate the physics of blob1 in comparison with already known active and remnant radio galaxies with the aim to reconstruct the evolution history of the radio source from its birth to its death.

The source has a relatively smooth, diffuse, amorphous appearance together with a very weak central compact core that is associated with the host galaxy located at z=0.051.
