From our ageing and morphological analysis it is clear that the nuclear engine is currently switched off or, at most, active at a very low power state. We find that the source has remained visible in the remnant phase for about 60 Myr, significantly longer than its active phase of 15 Myr, despite being located outside a cluster. The host galaxy is currently interacting with another galaxy located at a projected separation of 15 kpc and a radial velocity offset of 300 \ km \ s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes blob 1 as a large LOFAR-discovered remnant radio galaxy with a sharp high-frequency break, long remnant phase, faint core, and non-USS low-frequency spectrum.

quotes.quote: The first object is the so-called blob 1 , presented in . This object was discovered with LOFAR as a large (700 kpc), amorphous, and low surface-brightness structure. The remnant nature was first suggested based on the morphology and then confirmed by the sharp frequency break observed at high frequencies (>1400 MHz). With multi-frequency observations, we traced the radio spectral energy distribution (SED) and found not only an extreme spectral index above 1400 MHz (typical of remnant sources), but also a very large spectral index curvature (SPC) between frequencies below and above 1400 MHz (see ).

In the case of blob 1, a relatively short active phase (15 Myr) was followed by a significantly longer time ( 60 Myr) spent in the remnant phase. This source is a remnant located in a low-density environment, i.e., outside a galaxy cluster, unlike where most of the remnant sources were considered to reside . Despite having confirmed (based on their spectrum) that its large scale lobes are not fuelled anymore with fresh particles, we detect in blob 1 a faint core , suggesting that either the black-hole emission never really stops and continues at low levels, or the nucleus has restarted its activity very recently.

gmw_00007149.name: B2 1321+31
gmw_00007149.overview: B2 1321+31 is a low-power, lobed radio galaxy [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:2111.04776] that features a bent central dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256] and a symmetric two-sided radio jet [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256]. It exhibits boxy outer isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256] and tentative central and outer-lobe H I absorption [arXiv:1003.2835].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5127 as a bright Zwicky-cluster galaxy with a bent central dust lane, compact nuclear emission, symmetric two-sided radio jet, and boxy isophotes.

quotes.quote: NGC 5127 NGC 5127 is the second brightest galaxy in Zwicky cluster 1319.6+3135. It has a bended dust lane (size 940 pc/ 3.0'' ) across the center which forks in two towards the northeast. There is a compact nuclear emission peak surrounded by low-surface-brightness emission associated with the dust lanes. NGC 5127 has a symmetric two-sided VLA radio jet (Xu 1999a). Our isophotal analysis agrees well with an analysis by De Juan (1994).
They report the same PA twist and find that the isophotes become increasingly boxy beyond 10'' .

sources.summary: The appendix reports tentative central and outer-lobe H I absorption in B2 1321+31, with no optical galaxy at the outer feature and a typical extended radio morphology.

quotes.quote: B2 1321+31: Two tentative (3 ) absorption features are detected against the radio continuum of B2 1321+31; one against the central radio continuum slightly redshifted with respect to the optical systemic velocity as determined by woo06 and another against the bright radio continuum at the tip of the north-western lobe, roughly 100 kpc from the nucleus (see Fig fig:absorption1321_extended ). The tentative \ feature against this outer lobe is spatially unresolved, since the estimated \ column density of the absorbing gas ( N_ HI 3.6 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ) would have been sufficiently high for detecting part of this \ structure also in emission outside the radio continuum, which is not the case. There is no galaxy visible near the location of this outer absorption in optical SDSS images. The tentative absorption against the outer lobe of B2 1321+31 resembles \ absorption features detected against the outer edge of the powerful radio sources 3C 234 pih01 and 3C 433 mor04 and could represent a region where the radio plasma interacts with ambient inter-galactic medium. The extended radio source has a typical \ morphology.

sources.summary: The paper discusses B2 1321+31 as a lobed low-power FRI whose spectral flattening and weak VLBI core may indicate an aged low-luminosity FR-II structure.

quotes.quote: Our results for B2 1321+31 show that detailed spectral index distribution can be now routinely derived for all of the radio galaxies in the area covered by LOFAR and Apertif. 2 The flattening seen in the region at the end of the jet for a (lobed) FR-I of low-power object, such as B2 1321+31, could perhaps be connected to the population of low-luminosity radio galaxies with FR-II morphology, highlighted by as described above. When adding the spectral index information, we could speculate that B2 1321+31 may represent an example of such low-luminosity FR-II that has already aged and is found in an advanced stage of evolution. In B2 1321+31, the fading of the FR-II structure has resulted in a lower luminosity, but also in fading of the hot spots, which now can only be recognised in the spectral properties at low frequencies. Further support to this interpretation is the very weak VLBI core (peak flux 3.7 mJy; ) which suggests a core prominence lower than what typically observed in FR-I.

gmw_00007150.name: NGC 23
gmw_00007150.overview: NGC 23 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622] that contains bright, young H II regions [arXiv:1006.2219]. Its nuclear region is dominated by evolved stellar populations [arXiv:1006.2219], while its velocity field exhibits non-circular motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper extends NGC 23 rotation measurements, finds significant non-circular motions and broadened circumnuclear H profiles, and leaves the presence of a larger bar unresolved.

quotes.quote: The rotation curve of the galaxy (Fig. ngc23 d) possesses a local maximum first noted by Afanasiev et al. (1988a). We have prolonged this rotation curve to the radius twice as large as that reached by Afanasiev et al. (1988a). Since at >30 there is only one bright ``spiral arm'' that contributes to the rotation velocity measurements, the local minimum of the rotation curve at 40 is a possible artifact related to non-circular motion within this arm.

The problem of possible bar presence in NGC 23 remains unsolved. According to RC3, the galaxy is classified as SB, and de Jong (1996), by applying a 2D decomposition method to its brightness map, finds a bar with a length of 29 32 aligned along PA=154 . But the velocity field obtained by us does not demonstrate any noticeable turn of the kinematic major axis (Figs. ngc23 and ngc23 d), and there is also no sign of any isovelocity twisting when passing from the outer to the inner region of the galaxy (Afanasiev et al. 1991b).

sources.summary: The paper extracts H II-region spectra in NGC 23, finds evolved light may dominate its nucleus, and reports low-EW spaxel line ratios not explained by H II models.

quotes.quote: Although the H nebular emission line is much weaker than H and H , it can also be observed in emission in bright/young H ii regions (see for instance the HII-1 region in NGC 23, Figures 4 and 5).
On the other hand, in the nuclear regions of NGC 23 and IC 860, and NGC 7771, the evolved stellar populations may be the main contributors to the optical light (see also Table 6).

On the other hand, galaxies like NGC 23, NGC 7591 and NGC 7771 show a significant numbers of spaxels with EW(H )_ em <20 whose line ratios (not corrected for stellar absorption) cannot be explained by the Dopita et al. (2006) H ii region models.

gmw_00007151.name: NGC 5936
gmw_00007151.overview: NGC 5936 is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1006.2219]. The majority of the spaxels in its central region are located in the H II region of the diagnostic diagram, indicating emission from H II regions [arXiv:1006.2219].

sources.summary: The paper places most NGC 5936 spaxels in the H II-region diagram and treats its NICMOS diffuse-emission fraction as an upper limit.

quotes.quote: For instance the majority of the spaxels of MCG +12-02-001 and NGC 5936 are located in the H ii region of the diagram, while other cases such as UGC 1845 most spaxels are in the composite region.

As can be seen from Figure 3, the [ S ii ] 6717,6731/H vs. [ N ii ] 6584/H diagrams of the central regions of some galaxies (e.g., MCG+12-02-001 and NGC 5936) could be mostly explained as emission coming from H ii regions, although it has to be noted that there are no spaxels with EW(H )_ em <5 in the central regions of these galaxies.

In the case of NGC 5936 we attribute the discrepancy to the fact that, except for the very nuclear regions, the other H ii regions in the FoV of the NICMOS images appear quite diffuse (see Alonso-Herrero et al. 2006, and Paper I), and thus the DIG fraction estimated from the NICMOS Pa images is clearly just an upper limit.

gmw_00007152.name: HIPPIES-YsDrop01
gmw_00007152.overview: HIPPIES-YsDrop01 is a bright galaxy candidate at a redshift of approximately 7.4 [arXiv:1010.2261]. The galaxy is resolved in the near-infrared, shows no time variability, and has an estimated star formation rate of 23.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:1010.2261].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HIPPIES-YsDrop01 as one of two very bright z>7 Y-dropout galaxy candidates, resolved and non-variable, with high UV luminosity and an inferred star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: The remaining two are good candidate galaxies at z 7.4 . None of them show time variability. Both are resolved (FWHM 0 3 - 0 4 ) in J_ 125 and H_ 160 bands and hence are not likely brown dwarfs.

Even assuming that their redshifts are at the lower end of the selection window ( z 7.4 ), we obtained their absolute magnitudes of M=-21.6 0.16 and 21.2 0.15  mag for YsDrop01 and YsDrop02, respectively, which are at least 2 brighter than any current estimates of the M^* value at z 7 or 8. Earlier surveys over contiguous fields have resulted in a number of z band dropouts (candidate galaxies at z 7 ) of comparable brightness (e.g., Ouchi et al. 2009; Hickey et al. 2010; Castellano et al. 2010) or even brighter (Capak et al. 2009), and our current work indicates that such high luminosity galaxies could exist at z 7.4 . Using the formalism of Madau et al. (1998), the rest-frame UV luminosities of our candidates correspond to star formation rates (SFRs) of 23.7^ +3.5 _ 3.1 and 16.4^ +2.4 _ 2.1   M_  yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00007153.name: HIPPIES-YsDrop02
gmw_00007153.overview: HIPPIES-YsDrop02 is a resolved, non-variable Y-dropout galaxy candidate at a redshift of approximately 7.4 [arXiv:1010.2261]. It is characterized by a very blue color and a high luminosity that corresponds to an estimated star formation rate of 16.4 solar masses per year [arXiv:1010.2261].

sources.summary: The paper treats HIPPIES-YsDrop02 as a resolved, non-variable z>7 Y-dropout galaxy candidate, emphasizing its very blue color, high luminosity, and inferred star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: The remaining two are good candidate galaxies at z 7.4 . None of them show time variability. Both are resolved (FWHM 0 3 - 0 4 ) in J_ 125 and H_ 160 bands and hence are not likely brown dwarfs. Finally, they are not likely red galaxies at z 1 -3 whose 4000 break could mimic Lyman break, as their J_ 125 H_ 160 colors are much bluer than those of such interloppers. This is particularly true for YsDrop02, which has a very blue color of J_ 125 H_ 160 0.2  mag.

Even assuming that their redshifts are at the lower end of the selection window ( z 7.4 ), we obtained their absolute magnitudes of M=-21.6 0.16 and 21.2 0.15  mag for YsDrop01 and YsDrop02, respectively, which are at least 2 brighter than any current estimates of the M^* value at z 7 or 8. Earlier surveys over contiguous fields have resulted in a number of z band dropouts (candidate galaxies at z 7 ) of comparable brightness (e.g., Ouchi et al. 2009; Hickey et al. 2010; Castellano et al. 2010) or even brighter (Capak et al. 2009), and our current work indicates that such high luminosity galaxies could exist at z 7.4 . Using the formalism of Madau et al. (1998), the rest-frame UV luminosities of our candidates correspond to star formation rates (SFRs) of 23.7^ +3.5 _ 3.1 and 16.4^ +2.4 _ 2.1   M_  yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00007154.name: PGC 37625 (Mrk 1309)
gmw_00007154.overview: PGC 37625, also known as Mrk 1309, is the host galaxy of the broad-line type Ic supernova SN 2014ad [arXiv:1801.07046]. The galaxy has an estimated radial velocity of 1904 km/s and exhibits a sub-solar metallicity environment at the supernova region [arXiv:1801.07046].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 37625 (Mrk 1309) as SN 2014ad's host galaxy, estimates its distance and host reddening, and finds sub-solar metallicity at the supernova region.

quotes.quote: We present optical and ultraviolet photometry, and low resolution optical spectroscopy of the broad-line type Ic supernova SN 2014ad in the galaxy PGC 37625 (Mrk 1309), covering the evolution of the supernova during 5 to +87 d with respect to the date of maximum in B band.

The distance to the host galaxy Mrk 1309 of SN 2014ad is estimated using the radial velocity of the host, corrected for the local group infall onto the Virgo cluster V _ Virgo = 1904 32 km s ^ 1 moul00 .

The average value of 12 + (O/H) = 8.4 0.2, corresponds to 0.5 Z . This shows that the metallicity at the supernova region is sub-solar. modj08 have shown that GRBs are found in metal poor environment as compared to the broad-line type Ic SNe without GRBs.

gmw_00007155.name: J1850+70
gmw_00007155.overview: J1850+70 is an early-type, quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of 0.0894 [arXiv:2302.14782]. It has a high stellar mass and exhibits a very low star formation rate [arXiv:2302.14782].

sources.summary: J1850+70 is identified as the FRB 20220509G host, an early-type, quiescent galaxy at redshift 0.0894 with low star formation and high stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Following this procedure, we find that FRB 20220509G is associated to a host galaxy at (R.A. J2000, decl. J2000) = 18h50m41.92s, +70d14m33.95s with 1 false association probability. This galaxy is cataloged as 2MASX J18504127+7014359 (J1850+70 hereafter) in the NASA Extragalactic Database 1991ASSL..171...89H .

The strong [ Ca II ] , H and [ Mg II ] absorption features with [ O II ] emission are evident in the spectrum of J1850+70 thus indicating that it is an early-type galaxy (Figure fig:hosts_ppxf_fits ). The spectroscopic redshift of J1850+70 is also measured using pPXF , where the best fit with a reduced- ^2 of 1.0166 (N 1000) indicates a redshift of 0.0894 0.0001 . The Milky Way galactic dust extinction corrected [ O II ] line flux is (8.74 1.39) 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , which corresponds to an SFR of 0.04 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 .

Further, a high stellar mass, M _ ( M _ ) = 11.13_ 0.02 ^ +0.02 and a consistent with zero SFR averaged over the last 100 Myr, SFR = 0.08_ 0.04 ^ +0.06 M _ /yr implies that this is a quiescent galaxy.

gmw_00007156.name: J1848+73
gmw_00007156.overview: J1848+73 is a star-forming spiral galaxy located at a redshift of 0.1139 [arXiv:2302.14782]. It hosts the fast radio burst FRB 20220914A and contains a mixture of young and old stellar populations [arXiv:2302.14782].

sources.summary: J1848+73 is identified as the FRB 20220914A host, a star-forming spiral-like galaxy at redshift 0.1139 with mixed-age stellar populations and significant recent star formation.

quotes.quote: FRB 20220914A is associated to a host galaxy at (R.A. J2000, decl. J2000) = 18h48m13.96s, +73d20m10.70s (J1848+73 hereafter) with 3 false association probability. The analysis with P(U)=0.5 reveals a 6 and 8 false association probability for the likely hosts of FRB 20220509G and FRB 20220914A, respectively. We obtained the optical spectrum of both the host galaxies with the Low-Resolution Imaging Spectrometer on the Keck I telescope Keck-I/LRIS 1995PASP..107..375O .

The spectrum of J1848+73 exhibits strong emission lines and absorption features thus indicating a composition of young and old stellar populations in this galaxy (Figure fig:hosts_ppxf_fits ). We measure the spectroscopic redshift of the host galaxies using the Penalized PiXel-Fitting software pPXF ; 2017MNRAS.466..798C, 2022arXiv220814974C by jointly fitting the stellar continuum and nebular emission using the MILES stellar library 2006MNRAS.371..703S . The best pPXF fit to the spectrum has a reduced- ^2 of 0.9076 (number of degrees of freedom, N 1000) and reveals a redshift of 0.1139 0.0001 .

The star formation history of the host galaxy of FRB 20220914A indicates a variety of stellar population ages, which is consistent with our inference from the spectrum, as discussed in sec:observations . The high dust attenuation, A_V = 1.64_ 0.23 ^ +0.22 and stellar mass, M _ ( M _ ) = 9.99_ 0.09 ^ +0.09 with significant ongoing star formation averaged over the last 100 Myr, SFR = 1.45_ 0.61 ^ +1.05 M _ /yr indicates that it is a Milky Way-like star-forming spiral galaxy.

gmw_00007157.name: SNL-1
gmw_00007157.overview: SNL-1 is an early-type galaxy confirmed as a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1509.01253]. Although its lensing mass-to-light ratio favors a Milky Way-like initial mass function, its sodium absorption indices are characteristic of a very steep initial mass function slope [arXiv:1509.01253].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SNL-1 as a strong-lensing SNELLS galaxy whose lensing mass favors a Milky-Way-like IMF, while its sodium absorption indices resemble steep-IMF high-dispersion galaxies.

quotes.quote: Among the SNELLS galaxies are two which were confirmed as strong lensing systems (SNL-1 and SNL-2) by Smith et al. (2015). The lensing analysis shows that these two galaxies have mass-to-light rations similar to what is expected for a MW-like IMF. Specifically, the mass-excess factors, relative to the Kroupa IMF, are = 1.20 0.13 and = 0.94 0.17; hence bottom-heavy models are strongly disfavoured in these cases.

Figure fig:ixgrid also shows the sodium indices for all of the individual SNELLS galaxies, highlighting the lenses SNL-1 and SNL-2. There is a wide spread in \ among the sample, spanning the full range of model IMFs, at [ Na/H ] = 0.3--0.5. As expected from the similarity of their spectra to the SNELLS composite, SNL-1 and SNL-2 have line strengths consistent with the properties of the stack, yielding [ Na/H ] +0.4 and x = 3.0--3.5. Thus despite having mass-to-light ratios consistent with a MW-like IMF, these galaxies have sodium indices characteristic of very steep IMF slopes, at least if interpreted using otherwise ``vanilla'' versions of the CvD models (old, enhanced, other abundances solar).

gmw_00007158.name: SNL-2
gmw_00007158.overview: SNL-2 is an early-type galaxy that has been confirmed as a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1509.01253]. Although its lensing mass-to-light ratio is consistent with a Milky Way-like initial mass function, its sodium line strengths suggest a very steep stellar mass distribution [arXiv:1509.01253].

sources.summary: The paper treats SNL-2 as a strong-lensing SNELLS galaxy with MW-like lensing mass constraints but sodium features that, under simple models, imply a steep IMF.

quotes.quote: Among the SNELLS galaxies are two which were confirmed as strong lensing systems (SNL-1 and SNL-2) by Smith et al. (2015). The lensing analysis shows that these two galaxies have mass-to-light rations similar to what is expected for a MW-like IMF. Specifically, the mass-excess factors, relative to the Kroupa IMF, are = 1.20 0.13 and = 0.94 0.17; hence bottom-heavy models are strongly disfavoured in these cases.

Figure fig:ixgrid also shows the sodium indices for all of the individual SNELLS galaxies, highlighting the lenses SNL-1 and SNL-2. There is a wide spread in \ among the sample, spanning the full range of model IMFs, at [ Na/H ] = 0.3--0.5. As expected from the similarity of their spectra to the SNELLS composite, SNL-1 and SNL-2 have line strengths consistent with the properties of the stack, yielding [ Na/H ] +0.4 and x = 3.0--3.5. Thus despite having mass-to-light ratios consistent with a MW-like IMF, these galaxies have sodium indices characteristic of very steep IMF slopes, at least if interpreted using otherwise ``vanilla'' versions of the CvD models (old, enhanced, other abundances solar).

gmw_00007159.name: J1124+4325
gmw_00007159.overview: J1124+4325 is an X-shaped radio galaxy with a redshift of 2.245, making it the farthest known galaxy of this type [arXiv:2011.03839]. Spectroscopically, the object is classified as a quasar [arXiv:2011.03839].

sources.summary: The catalog paper highlights J1124+4325 as the farthest known X-shaped radio galaxy, with the highest XRG redshift in the sample and quasar classification.

quotes.quote: J1124+4325 and J1319+0502 are the farthest known XRG and ZRG, respectively.
J1124+4325 showed the highest redshift with z = 2.245 Ri09 . Spectroscopically, J1124+4325 is classified as a quasar Xu14 .

gmw_00007160.name: J1319+0502
gmw_00007160.overview: J1319+0502 is a Z-shaped radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.285 [arXiv:2011.03839]. It is the farthest known galaxy of this type and features a primary jet with a linear size of 1.41 megaparsecs [arXiv:2011.03839].

sources.summary: The catalog paper highlights J1319+0502 as the farthest known Z-shaped radio galaxy and reports it as the highest-redshift ZRG with a 1.41 Mpc primary jet linear size.

quotes.quote: J1124+4325 and J1319+0502 are the farthest known XRG and ZRG, respectively.
Amongst the sources with known redshift, the source with the highest primary jet linear size is J1319+0502 (1.41 Mpc).
Amongst the ZRGs, J1319+0502 has the highest redshift at z = 1.285 Ri09 . J1124+4325 and J1319+0502 are the farthest known XRG and ZRG, respectively.

gmw_00007161.name: UDG 13
gmw_00007161.overview: In a sample of 11 new ultra-diffuse galaxies in Hydra I, UDG 13 is one of three galaxies that are poorly fitted by a single Sérsic function and require a second component [arXiv:2206.07385]. Despite this poor fit, UDG 13 is classified as a ultra-diffuse galaxy because its effective radius derived from the growth curve is larger than 1.5 kpc [arXiv:2206.07385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 13 as one of three new Hydra I UDGs poorly fitted by a single Sersic profile, but still safely classified as a UDG from its growth-curve radius.

quotes.quote: In the sample of 11 new UDGs in Hydra I, three of them (UDG 13, UDG 15 and UDG 23) are poorly fitted with a single Sérsic function, and a second component would be required. This is not done here, since we aim at consistently compare the structural properties of all UDGs in Hydra I with those of UDGs in other clusters of galaxies, which are obtained by adopting a single Sérsic function. Nevertheless, for the three UDGs listed above, the R_e^ 50 derived by the growth curve is larger than 1.5 kpc, and it is consistent with the R_e estimated by the best fit. Therefore, they can be safely identified as UDGs. It is worth nothing that these UDGs might host a diffuse and faint halo in the galaxy outskirts.

gmw_00007162.name: UDG 15
gmw_00007162.overview: Out of eleven new ultra-diffuse galaxies (UDGs) in the Hydra I cluster, UDG 15 is one of three galaxies that are poorly fitted by a single Sersic function and require a second component [arXiv:2206.07385]. Despite requiring this second component, UDG 15 still meets the UDG size criterion as its effective radius derived from the growth curve is larger than 1.5 kpc [arXiv:2206.07385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 15 as one of three new Hydra I UDGs needing more than a single Sersic component, while still meeting the UDG size criterion from growth-curve measurements.

quotes.quote: In the sample of 11 new UDGs in Hydra I, three of them (UDG 13, UDG 15 and UDG 23) are poorly fitted with a single Sérsic function, and a second component would be required. This is not done here, since we aim at consistently compare the structural properties of all UDGs in Hydra I with those of UDGs in other clusters of galaxies, which are obtained by adopting a single Sérsic function. Nevertheless, for the three UDGs listed above, the R_e^ 50 derived by the growth curve is larger than 1.5 kpc, and it is consistent with the R_e estimated by the best fit. Therefore, they can be safely identified as UDGs. It is worth nothing that these UDGs might host a diffuse and faint halo in the galaxy outskirts.

gmw_00007163.name: UDG 23
gmw_00007163.overview: UDG 23 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Hydra I cluster [arXiv:2206.07385]. Although it is poorly fitted by a single Sersic function, its growth-curve and best-fit effective radii confirm its classification as an ultra-diffuse galaxy that may host a faint, diffuse halo [arXiv:2206.07385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 23 as one of three new Hydra I UDGs whose single-Sersic fits are poor, but whose growth-curve and best-fit effective radii support UDG classification.

quotes.quote: In the sample of 11 new UDGs in Hydra I, three of them (UDG 13, UDG 15 and UDG 23) are poorly fitted with a single Sérsic function, and a second component would be required. This is not done here, since we aim at consistently compare the structural properties of all UDGs in Hydra I with those of UDGs in other clusters of galaxies, which are obtained by adopting a single Sérsic function. Nevertheless, for the three UDGs listed above, the R_e^ 50 derived by the growth curve is larger than 1.5 kpc, and it is consistent with the R_e estimated by the best fit. Therefore, they can be safely identified as UDGs. It is worth nothing that these UDGs might host a diffuse and faint halo in the galaxy outskirts.

gmw_00007164.name: IC 5181
gmw_00007164.overview: IC 5181 is a morphologically undisturbed lenticular galaxy featuring a classical bulge composed of old, metal-rich stars [arXiv:1309.5812]. The galaxy hosts a decoupled component of ionized gas in orthogonal rotation [arXiv:1309.5812], as well as a spiral gaseous disk likely influenced by a non-axisymmetric central potential [arXiv:1610.01174].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 5181 as a morphologically undisturbed S0 galaxy whose orthogonally rotating ionized gas likely came from external accretion.

quotes.quote: We study the nearby S0 galaxy IC 5181 to address the origin of the ionized-gas component orbiting the galaxy on polar orbits. We measure the surface brightness distribution of the stars and ionized gas of IC 5181 from broad and narrow-band imaging. The structural parameters of the galaxy are obtained with a photometric decomposition assuming a S ersic and exponential profile for the bulge and disk, respectively. We measure the ionized-gas and stellar kinematics and the line strengths of the Lick indices of the stellar component along both the major and minor axis.

IC 5181 is a morphologically undisturbed S0 galaxy with a classical bulge made by old stars with super solar metallicity and overabundance. Stellar age and metallicity decrease in the disk region. The galaxy hosts a geometrically and kinematically decoupled component of ionized gas. It is elongated along the galaxy minor axis and in orthogonal rotation with respect to the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 5181 has mismatched kinematic position angles, a spiral gaseous disk, and a radially varying stellar-rotation PA probably linked to a non-axisymmetric central potential.

quotes.quote: In IC 5181, the difference is 29 ^o . We showed in paper III that the gaseous disc of IC 5181 has a spiral structure, which may be the result of a non-axisymmetric potential in the central region of this galaxy (see e.g., 2006MNRAS.366.1151S ). Although we did not see any clear sign of deviation from an axisymmetric rotation in the radial velocity map, the tomogram associated with the stellar rotation of IC 5181 has more weight close to the nucleus of this galaxy. Thus, there is a small variation of the PA with radius of the stellar structure in rotation that may also be related to a non-axisymmetric potential in the circumnuclear region of IC 5181.

gmw_00007165.name: SDSS J0956+5100
gmw_00007165.overview: SDSS J0956+5100 is an early-type lens galaxy that exhibits strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1405.3666]. It hosts a luminous companion galaxy located at a projected distance of approximately 5 kpc from the host center [arXiv:1405.3666].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0956+5100 with a luminous satellite, finds no additional substructure, and characterizes the satellite as likely associated with the lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: For the case of SDSS J0956+5100, since the luminous satellite was explicitly included as part of the smooth model, this implies that no substructure other than the luminous satellite was found. By repeating the analysis of this lens without the inclusion of the luminous satellite, we find that this satellite is also detected via the gravitational imaging technique in both I and V bands at the 37 and 20 level, respectively. Including our previous detection in SDSS J0946+1006 Vegetti10 , we detected in total, two satellites in 11 gravitational lens systems. In both cases the satellites have a projected distance of about 5 kpc from the centre of the host galaxy. Although we do not know its redshift, the colours of this luminous clump are consistent with those of the lens galaxy, indicating that it is most likely a satellite.

gmw_00007166.name: PKS 0402-362
gmw_00007166.overview: PKS 0402-362 is a bright flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 1.4228 [arXiv:2303.03039, arXiv:2308.13966]. It is a consistent gamma-ray emitter that has exhibited multiple bright activity epochs and asymmetric flare profiles [arXiv:2303.03039].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0402-362 as a bright FSRQ blazar at z=1.4228, identifying major Fermi-LAT activity epochs, steep gamma-ray spectra, BLR-scale emission, and one-zone SED behavior.

quotes.quote: We study the long-term behavior of the bright gamma-ray blazar PKS 0402-362. We collected approximately 13 years of Fermi-LAT data between Aug 2008 to Jan 2021 and identified three bright ray activity epochs. The second was found to be the brightest epoch ever seen in this source. We observed most of the ray flare peaks to be asymmetric in profile suggesting a slow cooling time of particles or the varying Doppler factor as the main cause of these flares. The ray spectrum is fitted with PL and LP spectral models, and in both cases, the spectral index is very steep. The ray spectrum does not extend beyond 10 GeV energy suggesting the emission is produced within the BLR.

Blazar, PKS 0402-362 is an FSRQ type source located at redshift, z = 1.4228 Jones2009Oct with RA = 60.975, Dec = -36.087. It has been continuously monitored by Fermi-LAT since the beginning of the operation and has been a consistent gamma-ray emitter. In the 4FGL DR3 catalog, it has been recognized as 4FGL J0403.9-3605.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the FSRQ blazar PKS 0402-362, using long-term Fermi-LAT and multiwavelength data to study flaring, spectral hardening, correlations, and SED models.

quotes.quote: In this work, our study focuses on addressing some of these inquiries through a comprehensive analysis of the temporal and spectral characteristics of the blazar PKS 0402-362 (4FGL J0403.9-3605). This source is an FSRQ type source located at redshift, z = 1.4228 Jones2009 with RA = 60.975, Dec = -36.087 and it has been continuously monitored by Fermi-LAT since the beginning of the operation. Gamma-ray We analyzed the Fermi-LAT https://fermi.gsfc.nasa.gov/ssc/data/analysis/software/ data for the gamma-ray observation. We downloaded the Pass8R2 data from August 2008 to January 2021 (approximately 12.5 years) from the blazar, PKS 0402-362.

We utilized `GAMERA', an open-source code, to model the multi-wavelength SEDs of the blazar PKS 0402-362. This code is publicly available on GitHub https://github.com/libgamera/GAMERA . This code solves the time-dependent transport equation; it estimates the propagated particle spectrum N(E,t) for an input injected particle spectrum. It further uses the propagated spectrum to calculate the Synchrotron and Inverse-Compton (IC) emissions.

gmw_00007167.name: El Anzuelo
gmw_00007167.overview: El Anzuelo is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of 2.29 [arXiv:2402.18636]. It is quintuply imaged due to strong gravitational lensing by three member galaxies of a massive foreground cluster [arXiv:2402.18636].

sources.summary: The paper models El Anzuelo as a quintuply imaged dusty star-forming lensed galaxy, using JWST data to constrain foreground cluster members and source structure.

quotes.quote: Probing the structure of cluster members with multi-band extended arcs in JWST data JWST lens modeling of El Anzuelo A. Galan tum , mpa Corresponding author ( mailto:aymeric.galan@gmail.com aymeric.galan@gmail.com ).

We present the detailed analysis of , a prominent quintuply imaged dusty star forming galaxy ( z_ s =2.29 ), mainly lensed by three members of the massive galaxy cluster ACT-CL J0102 4915, also known as ( z_ d =0.87 ). We leverage JWST/NIRCam data containing previously unseen lensing features using a Bayesian, multi-wavelength, differentiable and GPU-accelerated modeling framework that combines (lens modeling) and (field model and inference) software packages.

We find that the two main deflectors (L1 and L2) have logarithmic mass density slopes steeper than isothermal, with _ L1 = 2.23 0.05 and _ L2 = 2.21 0.04 . We argue that such steep density profiles can arise due to tidally truncated mass distributions, which we probe thanks to the cluster lensing boost and the strong asymmetry of the lensing configuration. Moreover, our three-dimensional source model captures most of the surface brightness of the lensed galaxy, revealing a clump of at most 400 parsecs at the source redshift, visible at wavelengths _ rest 0.6 m.

gmw_00007168.name: IRAS 10494 + 4424
gmw_00007168.overview: IRAS 10494 + 4424 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2109.06880, arXiv:0906.2797] at a redshift of z=0.092 [arXiv:0906.2797]. It features two north-northwest spikes of likely tidal origin [arXiv:0906.2797], shows no signs of a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0906.2797], and exhibits an anomalously high Br-alpha to Br-beta line ratio [arXiv:2109.06880].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10494 + 4424 as one of three ULIRGs with an anomalously high Br/Br line ratio, above case B and not explainable by dust extinction.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three galaxies for which the spectra are shown in Figure fig:anospe , a change in the wavelength range does not affect the line fluxes by more than 5 . We conclude that the three galaxies (shown as blue circles in Figure fig:brflux ), IRAS 10494 + 4424, IRAS 10565 + 2448, and Mrk 273, show Br /Br line ratios of 0.96 0.12, 0.883 0.085, and 1.086 0.053, respectively, all significantly higher than the case B ratio (0.565). These line-ratio anomalies are not explainable with case B theory and dust extinction, which could reduce but not increase the Br /Br line ratio. We show the spectra around the Br and Br lines for these three galaxies in Figure fig:anobr .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10494 + 4424 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10494 + 4424. This z=0.092 ULIRG presents two spikes to the north-northwest which vks02 suggest tidal origin. In their SPITZER spectroscopic study, ima07 find no signature of a buried AGN in this system, which is consistent with the SED study by farrah03 . The upper limit of the AGN contribution to the total IR flux estimated by farrah03 is less than 13 .

gmw_00007169.name: L6
gmw_00007169.overview: L6 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.8786 [arXiv:2312.02336]. It is gravitationally lensed by a massive galaxy cluster, appearing as a giant arc with three bright, separated images [arXiv:2312.02336].

sources.summary: The paper treats L6 as a strongly lensed z=4.8786 LAE with three bright images, HST and Spitzer photometry, and foreground-source contamination considered separately.

quotes.quote: L6 contains three bright, separated images. A bright galaxy is seen projected onto L6, which we label S and indicate with a set of cyan bars. This galaxy is not gravitationally lensed nor part of L6, but is instead a foreground z=3.66 galaxy (identified by Swinbank2007 ) that serendipitously appears to be part of the arc.

L6 was first identified in the Red-Sequence Cluster Survey RCS; Gladders2003 as a highly extended ``giant arc'' around a massive galaxy cluster, RCS1 J0224-0002 (see fig:view ). It was found to have a redshift of z=4.8786 based on strong \ emission observed with the FORS2 spectrograph on the VLT Gladders2002 . L6 has been the subject of several detailed studies using ground-based optical and NIR spectroscopy Swinbank2007,Smit2017,Witstok2021 .

gmw_00007170.overview: The lenticular galaxy 5422 features a single, old thick stellar disc component that is between 8 and 11 billion years old [arXiv:1604.07624]. It also hosts a slightly inclined, non-starforming gaseous disc that exhibits a warp and weak emission lines [arXiv:1604.07624].

sources.summary: The paper finds 5422 has a single old thick disc, a warped non-starforming gaseous disc, insignificant age and metallicity gradients, and a formation history consistent with early turbulent thick-disc formation.

quotes.quote: 5422 5422 has a single disc component as evident from its vertical density profile analysis. The two-dimensional decomposition by the S4G project reveals a bulge and one thick disc with the scale-length and scale-heights 24.3 and 6.7 arcsec Salo2015 . The disc warp semi-amplitude reaches 110 pc at =3 kpc. 5422 also possesses a large scale non-starforming gaseous disc slightly inclined at 5 deg to the main plane manifested by weak emission lines.

5422 is the only galaxy were we see an old thick disc 8 11 Gyr with slightly subsolar 0.2 dex metallicity and a moderate enhancement ( [ Fe/H ] \ 0.15 ). The age and metallicity gradients in 5422 are not statistically significant.
The rapid turbulent thick disc formation by Bournaud2009 looks plausible only for 5422.

gmw_00007171.name: PGC 032873
gmw_00007171.overview: PGC 032873 is an isolated, compact massive relic galaxy [arXiv:1701.05197, arXiv:1711.09983] that has evolved passively over the past 13 billion years [arXiv:1711.09983]. It features an old stellar population [arXiv:1701.05197, arXiv:2402.00103] and is notably faint in X-ray emissions [arXiv:1711.09983].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PGC 032873 as a local massive relic galaxy with compact red-nugget-like structure, old stellar populations, high velocity dispersion, and relic properties intermediate between NGC 1277 and Mrk 1216.

quotes.quote: We confirm two new local massive relic galaxies, i.e. untouched survivors of the early universe massive population: Mrk 1216 and PGC 032873. Both show early and peaked formation events within very short timescales ( < 1 Gyr) and thus old mean mass-weighted ages ( 13 Gyr). Their star formation histories remain virtually unchanged out to several effective radii, even when considering the steeper IMF values inferred out to 3 effective radii.

PGC 032873 is a field galaxy firstly reported in vandenBosch2012 and later employed in AFM15 for the SMBH-massive relic study using SDSS data. However, there is no detailed dynamical study for it yet, having only an upper limit for its SMBH vandenBosch2015 .
PG032873 seems to be always in between, sometimes resembling more to NGC 1277, sometimes more to Mrk 1216.

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 032873 as an isolated compact relic galaxy observed by Chandra, X-ray fainter than MRK 1216, passively evolved, and possibly affected by stronger AGN outbursts.

quotes.quote: The most isolated of the currently confirmed low redshift massive, compact, relic galaxies are MRK 1216 ( z=0.021328 , D=97 Mpc) and PGC 032873 ( z=0.024921 , D=108 Mpc), with their closest neighbours at distance 1 Mpc ferre2017,yildirim2017 . However, while PGC 032873 is X-ray faint and its 22.7 ks archival Chandra observation only provides 200 counts, which only allow for a temperature measurement see Section analysis and buote2017 , MRK 1216 is X-ray bright and allows to perform a detailed study of the X-ray emitting atmosphere of the galaxy.
MRK 1216 has been observed by Chandra for 12.9 ks in June 2015 and PGC 032873 for 22.7 ks in March 2015 using the Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS) chip 6.

The X-ray luminosity of the other isolated, massive, compact, relic galaxy PGC 032873, which has a similar stellar mass to MRK 1216 ferre2017 , is about an order of magnitude lower, L_ X =(5.6 0.5) 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . Given that over the past 13 billion years both galaxies appear to have evolved passively and in isolation, the difference in their current X-ray luminosity could be traced back to a difference in the vigour of the AGN outbursts in these systems. MRK 1216 and possibly PGC 032873 are outliers above the black hole mass--bulge mass relation at z=0 .

sources.summary: The paper uses PGC 032873 as an observed compact massive relic benchmark, comparing its old stellar age and kinematics with MOND collapse models.

quotes.quote: Our analysis focuses on the collapse dynamics of isolated non-rotating post-Big-Bang gas clouds, revealing kinematic and density profiles comparable to observed compact massive relic galaxies like NGC 1277, Mrk 1216, and PGC 032873.

This is similar to compact massive relics ( 2014ApJ...780L..20T, 2017MNRAS.467.1929F, 2024MNRAS.527.8793S ) where NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 are found to have formed their stars around 10 Gyr ago.

Here we study the kinematical properties of one of the models from 2022MNRAS.516.1081E of comparable mass with the observed compact massive relic galaxies NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 2014ApJ...780L..20T,2017MNRAS.467.1929F listed in Table tab:values and find the model to be very similar to these observed compact massive relic galaxies. These compact massive relics are found to have their average stellar ages similar to ETGs of comparable mass in a high-density environment as found by 2005ApJ...621..673T , where a galaxy with M_ * = 10^ 11 M_ would have formed 10 Gyr ago.

gmw_00007172.name: SW161117.30+541759.2
gmw_00007172.overview: SW161117.30+541759.2 is a high-redshift dust-obscured quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1110.2493]. Its optical spectral energy distribution is dominated by starlight from a stellar population estimated to be 100 million years old [arXiv:1110.2493].

sources.summary: The paper describes SW161117.30+541759.2 as a high-redshift dust-obscured quasar host whose optical SED is starlight dominated, with limited far-infrared emission and marginally resolved morphology.

quotes.quote: However, the spectral energy distribution (SED) of SW161117.30+541759.2 suggests it is dominated by starlight in the rest-frame optical.

In the far-infrared SW161117.30+541759.2 lacks detection in either Spitzer or SPIRE data longward of 24 m, so we placed a limit on the far-infrared luminosity by fitting the maximal warm plus cold dust combination, constrained such that the warm component fit smoothly onto the cold (Sajina et al. \ 2006).

SW161117.30+541759.2 is well fit by a 100Myr old stellar population, with a modest amount of reddening. XFLS171419.97+602724.8 seems to require a superposition of an old (1Gyr) and young (100Myr) stellar population to account for a significant Balmer break while retaining a blue UV continuum. Host galaxy morphologies A major observational advantage to studying heavily obscured quasars is the ability to image the host free of the bright nuclear light. The host of SW161117.30+541759.2 is only marginally resolved in ground-based seeing, but the host of XFLS171419.97+602724.8 is clearly resolved in an H band IRTF image in 0.7-arcsec seeing (Figure 5).

gmw_00007173.name: XFLS171419.97+602724.8
gmw_00007173.overview: XFLS171419.97+602724.8 is a type-2 obscured quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1110.2493]. The galaxy features a resolved H-band morphology [arXiv:1110.2493], a mixture of young and old stellar populations [arXiv:1110.2493], and high-temperature dust likely heated by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1110.2493].

sources.summary: The paper treats XFLS171419.97+602724.8 as a type-2 obscured quasar host with high dust temperature, likely AGN-heated dust, mixed stellar populations, and resolved H-band morphology.

quotes.quote: As only XFLS171419.97+602724.8 has a rest-frame optical spectrum (Figure 2), we are only sure of this one as being a type-2 quasar by our definition.

XFLS171419.97+602724.8 required a relatively high ``cold'' dust temperature ( 135 K) to fit the Spitzer 70 m and 160 m detections and the SPIRE 250 m limit. The high dust temperature, combined with the lack of PAH detection in the IRS spectrum of XFLS171419.97+602724.8, make it unlikely that the dust is heated by normal star formation in this object.

SW161117.30+541759.2 is well fit by a 100Myr old stellar population, with a modest amount of reddening. XFLS171419.97+602724.8 seems to require a superposition of an old (1Gyr) and young (100Myr) stellar population to account for a significant Balmer break while retaining a blue UV continuum. Host galaxy morphologies A major observational advantage to studying heavily obscured quasars is the ability to image the host free of the bright nuclear light. The host of SW161117.30+541759.2 is only marginally resolved in ground-based seeing, but the host of XFLS171419.97+602724.8 is clearly resolved in an H band IRTF image in 0.7-arcsec seeing (Figure 5).

gmw_00007174.overview: 0849+27 is a peculiar broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.08533] with a highly extended radio structure spanning 382 kpc [arXiv:1507.08533, arXiv:1304.3021]. High-resolution observations reveal multiple components, including a flat-spectrum core identified as component B [arXiv:1304.3021].

sources.summary: The paper highlights 0849+27 as a peculiar, highly extended BAL QSO with EVLA/VLBA radio structure, multiple components, and evidence for different radio-emitting phases.

quotes.quote: During our polarimetry campaign, we could observe the peculiar BAL QSO 0849+27 with the EVLA at 4.86 GHz. The map of the resolved structure of this source at 1.4 GHz, obtained from the FIRST survey, was already presented in a previous work from our group ( Bruni ). This resulted to be the most extended BAL QSO of our sample (44 arcsec, 382 kpc, between components A and C).

We could obtain a map at 4.8 GHz from our subsequent EVLA observation in 2011, taking advantage of the improved performance of this instrument, and also a high resolution (pc-scale) map fo the core component from our VLBA program (see Bruni2 ). Results are presented in Fig. 0849 . Four out of five components detected in the FIRST data are visible in our EVLA map (A, B, C, E), while the flux density of component D seems to drop below the 3- significance level.

sources.summary: The paper says 0849+27 was FIRST-resolved into a 382 kpc three-component radio source; VLBA maps target its central component and identify component B as the flat-spectrum core.

quotes.quote: 0849+27 This source has already been resolved in the FIRST survey (VLA at 1.4 GHz, see Bruni ) showing three components with a projected linear size of 382 kpc, and a position angle for the structure of 45^ . These VLBA observations were designed to study the central component, which was unresolved on the arcsecond scale. The maps show two components at 5 GHz (A and B) separated by 3.5 mas (30 pc), while only B is visible at 8.4 GHz, with a spectral index of 0.12 0.20 (flat) that can be considered as the source core. This component is resolved at both frequencies in p.a. 30^ .

gmw_00007175.overview: The object 0756+37 is classified as a dusty broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.08533]. It exhibits a 250 GHz flux density that is clearly distinguishable from its expected synchrotron-emission tail [arXiv:1507.08533].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0756+37 as the only clear dusty BAL QSO in the sample, revising its VLA flux and finding 250 GHz excess above the synchrotron tail.

quotes.quote: For three sources (0756+37, 0816+48, 1335+02) we provide here a revised flux density for the VLA measurements presented in : for source 0816+48, during previous data reduction the vicinity of a strong source led to an incorrect phase referencing, resulting in an overestimated flux density measurement. While for sources 0756+37 and 1335+02, our flux-extraction algorithm missed the source, displaced of a few arcsec from the map centre, due to atmospheric effects. The revised values are given in Tab. errata .

Only one source (0756+37) shows a flux density at 250 GHz clearly distinguishable from the expected synchrotron-emission tail: this is the only genuine example of dusty BAL QSO found among these objects.

gmw_00007176.name: GOODS-S 43683
gmw_00007176.overview: GOODS-S 43683 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.89 [arXiv:2206.05293]. It forms a merger pair with the nearby galaxy GOODS-S 43114, with their centroids separated by only 18 kpc [arXiv:2206.05293].

sources.summary: The paper treats GOODS-S 43683 as a z=1.89 merger companion, using interaction classifications and photometric redshift assumptions to model it alongside GOODS-S 43114.

quotes.quote: GOODS-S 43114 and GOODS-S 43683 have been identified as a merger pair in van10 and the CANDELS morphology catalog kar15 . The latter classifies mergers and other galaxy features based on the visual inspection. For GOODS-S 43114 (GOODS-S 43683), 100 (66 ) of people classified it as having ``any interaction'' while 66 (100 ) claimed that tidal arms exist. GOODS-S 43114 is a class 4 merger, while GOODS-S 43683 is a class 3 merger, both of which are high confidence merger classifications.

For the SED fitting, we fix GOODS-S 43114 and GOODS-S 43683 redshifts to the MOSFIRE spectroscopic redshift obtained for GOODS-S 43114. Given that these galaxies are in a merger with the centroids being only 18 kpc apart, this redshift assumption for GOODS-S 43683 seems reasonable. van10 validate our assumption, reporting z = 1.898 0.003 for GOODS-S 43683 (they report z = 1.902 0.002 for GOODS-S 43114).

gmw_00007177.name: EDwC1
gmw_00007177.overview: EDwC1 is a newly identified candidate nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2405.13499]. It is located at the end of a spiral arm of NGC 6744, where high stellar density previously prevented its detection [arXiv:2405.13499].

sources.summary: The paper reports EDwC1 as a newly identified candidate nucleated dwarf spheroidal near NGC 6744, giving its coordinates, luminosity, size, surface brightness, and likely missed detection in a crowded arm.

quotes.quote: The new candidate galaxy is designated as EDwC1 ( Dwarf galaxy Candidate, number 1), and located at = 19;09;08.33 , = 63;41;07.9 (J2000). EDwC1 is a nucleated dwarf elliptical with an absolute band magnitude of 12.2 , assuming a distance of 8.8 Mpc, with an effective radius of 0.4 kpc and a surface brightness within one effective radius of 24.1 , placing it clearly within the scaling relations of the classical dwarf regime. It is located at the end of a spiral arm and therefore has avoided past detection, most likely because of the high stellar density in this region. However, the combination of low surface brightness sensitivity and high spatial resolution of allows us to easily detect such galaxies, even in crowded regions.

gmw_00007178.name: galaxy no. 81
gmw_00007178.overview: Galaxy no. 81 is spectroscopically classified as an a-type or e(a)-type active galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0311497]. It coincides with a point-like X-ray peak that is consistent with an active galactic nucleus and exhibits low-level mid-infrared emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0311497].

sources.summary: The paper classifies galaxy no. 81 as an a/e(a)-type active galaxy, links its X-ray peak and weak emission lines to a dust-obscured AGN, and estimates faint mid-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: Galaxy no. 81 shows the strongest H EW, and it is therefore spectroscopically classified as 'a'. However, it also shows evidence of mild [ OII ] emission, EW 3 . It would have been classified 'e(a)' in the scheme of Poggianti et al. ( ). The galaxy coincides with one of the X--ray peaks, identified at the smallest wavelet scale. The X--ray emission could therefore be point--like, and consistent with emission from an AGN.

Although galaxy no. 81 was not detected at 15 m with ISOCAM by Barvainis et al. ( ), one can see in Fig. 4 of Barvainis et al. ( ) low--level isocontours of mid--IR emission centered on this galaxy. Given that their rms noise level is 0.11 mJy, and their faintest detected source has a 15 m flux density of 0.53 mJy, the 15 m flux density of galaxy 81 should be 0.4 -0.5 mJy.

gmw_00007179.name: galaxy no. 42
gmw_00007179.overview: Galaxy no. 42 is a cluster-member galaxy classified as an e(a) type [arXiv:astro-ph/0311497]. Its spectral energy distribution is best fit by a model of a young early-type spiral with ongoing star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0311497].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy no. 42 as a cluster-member e(a) galaxy whose optical-mid-infrared SED and infrared luminosity indicate dusty ongoing star formation rather than a post-starburst state.

quotes.quote: Only galaxy no. 42 is a cluster member. The other two are foreground galaxies. Galaxy no. 42 is an a--type galaxy, with an H EW 8 . Its spectrum shows evidence of a mild [ OII ] emission, EW 3 . It would have been classified 'e(a)' in the scheme of Poggianti et al. ( ).

We find that the observed SED of galaxy no. 42 is best--fitted by a model of a young (3 Gyr--old) early--type spiral, with significant ongoing star formation (see Fig. figbiv ). The best--fit model SED is used to compute the galaxy total IR luminosity, 4 10^ 10 h^ 2 L_ . The galaxy star formation rate, SFR, is derived from the total IR luminosity using the relation of Kennicutt ( ).

gmw_00007180.name: 2MASX J02301709+2836050
gmw_00007180.overview: 2MASX J02301709+2836050 is a nearby spiral galaxy characterized by blue spiral arms and a redder, bright nuclear core [arXiv:2309.03011]. Although it lacks a bright active galactic nucleus, its line diagnostics are consistent with a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2309.03011].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J02301709+2836050 as the nearby spiral host of the recurrent X-ray source, finding no bright AGN but line diagnostics consistent with a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: The position derived from XRT for is 0.2 away from the photometric center of the nearby spiral galaxy 2MASX J02301709+2836050 located at 165 Mpcs. The galaxy shows prominent blue spiral arms and a redder bright nuclear core (see the top right panel in EDF fig:host ). The host galaxy does not appear in large AGN catalogs . There is no previous X-ray detection at the position of this galaxy: the X-ray upper limit server returns only upper limits, from Slew Survey, ROSAT all-sky survey (RASS) and previous observations by /XRT (see exact values in section sec:constraint_begin ).

The galaxy shows no excess in the infrared bands that could indicate the presence of a hot dust emitting `torus': the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE, ) infrared W1 - W2 color 0.1 does not pass standard AGN selection criteria , instead is consistent with pure stellar population emission. The NeoWISE light curves from 2014 to 2021 show no significant variability ( < 1 ). Our recently obtained optical spectra show no signs of broad emission lines. Together, these multi-wavelength properties exclude the existence of a bright AGN (e.g., Seyfert-I or Quasar-like) in 's host.

gmw_00007181.name: AM 1300-233
gmw_00007181.overview: AM 1300-233, also known as ESO 507-G070, is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1803.11225] and a post-merger system [arXiv:2207.08929]. The galaxy hosts a Seyfert-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.08929] and contains two distinct star-forming regions with different ultrarelativistic electron populations [arXiv:2207.08929].

sources.summary: AM 1300-233 is one of the detected ALMA CO/CN galaxies; the paper treats it as a LIRG or merger remnant with compact emission and relatively weak CN.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

AM 2055-425 has been classified as an AGN/starburst composite, while AM 2246-490 and AM 1300-233 are LIRGs with no sign of an AGN or an obscured AGN in their near-infrared spectra imanishi2010,iwasura2011 . Although the CN emission for these galaxies is quite weak, CN emission does appear at all the strong CO peaks in the image cubes, and so the S/N-matched CO/CN ratio measurements are likely fairly robust. However, AM 2055-425 and AM 1300-233 have the lowest CO signal-to-noise ratios in the sample (as measured in the CO peak emission channel).

sources.summary: The paper classifies ESO 507-G070 as a Seyfert nucleus in a post-merger system and models its broad radio SED with two star-forming components and a high-frequency turnover.

quotes.quote: ESO 507-G070 : classified as a Seyfert-type AGN nucleus Condon96 in a post-merger system Stierwalt13, Pasparialis2021 , its radio SED covering 2.3 decades in frequency (70 MHz-14.9 GHz) is best-fitted with a two-component model representing two distinct star-forming regions with different ultrarelativistic electron populations and high frequency turnover. The synchrotron spectral indices for the two populations, and _2 , are 0.77 and 1.50, respectively. The TF is 9 . The SFR _ IR , L_ dust , AGN fraction are 22.40 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 10.93 L_ , 2.0 , respectively. Its UV- IR SED has previously been modeled with CIAGLE by Pasparialis2021 who reported comparable L_ dust (=10 ^ 11.1 L_ ), and SFR _ IR (45.1 M_ yr ^ 1 ), and lower AGN fraction (0 ) as compared to our results.

gmw_00007182.name: AM 2246-490
gmw_00007182.overview: AM 2246-490 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1803.11225] and a compact late-stage merger remnant [arXiv:1803.11225, arXiv:1509.08468]. Its nucleus exhibits composite or LINER behavior [arXiv:1509.08468], while a broad blueshifted sodium absorption feature indicates the presence of a galactic outflow [arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: AM 2246-490 is a LIRG in the ALMA CO/CN sample with weak but robust CN detections and the smallest CO/CN ratio in the sample.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

AM 2246-490 is distinguished by having the smallest CO/CN line ratio ( 7 1 ) in our entire sample, implying the CN emission is bright relative to CO. stierwalt2014 find a relatively low ratio of PAH luminosity to infrared luminosity ( L_ PAH /L IR ), which they say is consistent with its classification as a merger remnant in ueda2014 . diaz-santos2013 report a low ratio of [ CII ] to far-infrared luminosity, which is consistent with its relatively large infrared luminosity. They also find that AM 2246-490 lies at the warm colour end of their sample of LIRGs.

sources.summary: The paper describes this compact late-stage merger as LINER/composite in its nucleus, post-starburst in blue light, with tidal-arm emission and possible outflow-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: IRAS F22467-4906 (ESO 239-IG002) The nuclear regions of this compact late-stage merger have very high / and / line ratios, with moderate / ratios indicating composite/LINER behavior. The nucleus also shows a post-starburst spectrum in the blue, with older stellar features in the tidal debris to the southeast of the nucleus. There is some detection of low-level region like emission in the tidal arm to the SE as well, likely similar to the star formation in IRAS F10038-3338, likely related to the shock-induced star formation in NGC 7252 described by Chien10 . The Chandra data show a point-like source detected in the hard band, while the soft X-rays extend to the N and S and the overall hardness of the X-ray data are not consistent with an AGN Iwasawa11 . There is a very broad blue shifted Na D absorption feature indicating an outflow which may be driving shocks in the is galaxy.

gmw_00007183.name: DOG 1
gmw_00007183.overview: DOG 1 is a bright, dust-obscured galaxy that is resolved as a compact, disk-like system [arXiv:0807.1115]. Its structure is best fit by an exponential disk with a secondary central component consistent with an exponential bulge [arXiv:0807.1115, arXiv:0904.1231].

sources.summary: DOG 1 is a bright dust-obscured galaxy imaged with Keck AO; it is resolved as a compact disk-like system with possible weak secondary central structure.

quotes.quote: Due to poor weather, we were only able to image three DOGs (denoted DOG 1, DOG 2, and DOG 3 for simplicity). In order to ensure successful observations of a preliminary sample despite limited observing time, the three DOGs selected were among the brightest Ks band sources in the sample ( Ks <19 ).

DOG 1 is best fit by an exponential disk (actual best fit Sersic is n=1.5 ) with an effective radius of 0.11 . DOG 2 is best fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile (actual best fit Sersic is n=3.5 ) with effective radius 0.09 . While both DOGs 1 and 2 are resolved they have very small effective radii.

DOG 1 is the only object of the three suggestive of a secondary central component. We used GALFIT to simultaneously fit DOG 1 for both a disk and a bulge. The fit for the central component is actually disk-like with a Sersic index of n=0.3 , similar to the truncated central disk in NGC 2787 Peng02 .

sources.summary: The paper finds significant residual structure in DOG 1 and says a second component, interpreted as an exponential bulge rather than a merging companion, best explains the fit.

quotes.quote: DOG 1 also shows significant structure in the GALFIT residual image (Figure 5). This is best accounted for by adding a second component to the fit. This second component is consistent with an exponential bulge, rather than a merging companion.

gmw_00007184.name: DOG 2
gmw_00007184.overview: DOG 2 is a bright dust-obscured galaxy with a power-law spectral energy distribution [arXiv:0807.1115]. The galaxy is resolved but compact, featuring a de Vaucouleurs-like profile and a possible strong emission line in the Ks band [arXiv:0807.1115].

sources.summary: DOG 2 is a bright dust-obscured galaxy with a power-law SED, compact resolved morphology, and a de Vaucouleurs-like profile; its Ks-band point may include strong line emission.

quotes.quote: Due to poor weather, we were only able to image three DOGs (denoted DOG 1, DOG 2, and DOG 3 for simplicity). In order to ensure successful observations of a preliminary sample despite limited observing time, the three DOGs selected were among the brightest Ks band sources in the sample ( Ks <19 ).

DOG 1 is best fit by an exponential disk (actual best fit Sersic is n=1.5 ) with an effective radius of 0.11 . DOG 2 is best fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile (actual best fit Sersic is n=3.5 ) with effective radius 0.09 . While both DOGs 1 and 2 are resolved they have very small effective radii.

While DOG 2 appears to have a power law SED, the Ks band data point is offset from the rest of the power law. This data point is based on the FLAMEX Ks band image, and is measured to within 10 . In addition, we checked the FLAMEX zeropoint with 2MASS images of the field and found good agreement. It is possible that a strong emission line is contributing to the deviation of the Ks band data point from the power law SED.

gmw_00007185.name: DOG 3
gmw_00007185.overview: DOG 3 is a dust-obscured galaxy that is best fit as a point-like source [arXiv:0807.1115, arXiv:0904.1231]. Its point-like morphology suggests the presence of a strong central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0807.1115], while a bump in its spectral energy distribution indicates there is also strong star formation [arXiv:0807.1115].

sources.summary: DOG 3 is a bright dust-obscured galaxy best fit as point-like; the paper suggests possible obscured AGN plus star formation from its morphology and SED bump.

quotes.quote: DOG 3 is best fit by a point source of magnitude Ks 18.6 . The structure seen in the image of DOG 3 appears to be primarily the result of structure in the AO PSF rather than ``true'' structure in the galaxy. This PSF structure is demonstrated in the GALFIT model of DOG 3 (middle column) which is effectively a scaled version of the observed PSF (in this example, we used images of the tip-tilt star for DOG 2 as the PSF).

DOG 3 does show point-like morphology suggesting that it too may contain a strong central AGN. Surprisingly, DOG 3 shows the least power-law like SED. The ``bump'' in the SED of DOG 3 may be indicating that there is also strong star formation in this galaxy, and while the morphology is point like, the C and A measures for DOG 3 suggest that it is not a pure point source galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats DOG 3 as point-source dominated: its single-Sersic fit is PSF-sized, added components are difficult to measure, and a two-component model gives an unphysical size.

quotes.quote: For DOGs 3 and 11, the single Sersic fits were already consistent with point sources. Recall, the single component Sersic fits to DOGs 3 and 11 had effective radii of 0.02 and 0.03 respectively, i.e. the size of PSFs. Therefore, any additional components were difficult to measure. When GALFIT fit for an additional component in the DOG 3 model, it measured a nonsensical size of 90 arcsec for it (see Table tab:measure ); usually this indicates that GALFIT has confused sky with object. Therefore the single component, point source dominated fit is a better approximation for the morphology of DOG 3 than the two component model.

gmw_00007186.name: UGC 9215
gmw_00007186.overview: UGC 9215 is a galaxy with local metallicity measurements derived from strong line ratios [arXiv:1910.04596]. Its outer disk and center exhibit unusually excited carbon monoxide line ratios, indicating dense gas conditions typical of active star-forming and starburst regions [arXiv:1910.04596].

sources.summary: UGC 9215 has local metallicity measurements, ALFALFA H I data, and unusually excited CO line ratios, with the O1 region and center compared to starburst-like conditions.

quotes.quote: For NGC 5496, UGC 9215 and UGC 9299 metallicities were taken from deVis2017CE , who used the SDSS and GAMA spectra and strong line ratios, and are quoted in the Pettini2004 O3N2 calibration.

The errors on the high R_ 21 >1 ratios in NGC 5496 O1,O2 and UGC 9299 are large enough that they are still consistent with a `normal' R_ 21 =0.7-1.0 value, however UGC 9215 O1 appears to be significantly excited with R_ 21 =2.11 0.58 .

The regions with the highest R_ 21 and R_ 31 are those in the outer disk of UGC 9215 and in UGC 9299. That the R_ 31 ratios are also high (and less prone to uncertainty due to the closer match of the beam areas), supports the earlier conclusion of more excited, dense gas typical of star forming regions. The centre of UGC 9215 has an intermediate value of R_ 31 0.53 while for UGC 9215 O1 and UGC 9299 the ratio is R_ 31 > 0.70 , which is more typical of star-bursts, LIRGs and actively star forming regions where R_ 31 0.5-0.8 Yao2003,Narayanan2005,Mao2010,PPP2012,Bauermeister2013 .

gmw_00007187.name: UGC 9299
gmw_00007187.overview: UGC 9299 is a galaxy classified as a BADGRS with measured metallicity and neutral hydrogen properties [arXiv:1910.04596]. Its carbon monoxide ratios indicate the presence of excited, dense gas typical of active star-forming regions [arXiv:1910.04596].

sources.summary: UGC 9299 is discussed as a BADGRS with measured metallicity and H I properties; its CO ratios indicate excited dense gas typical of active star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: For NGC 5496, UGC 9215 and UGC 9299 metallicities were taken from deVis2017CE , who used the SDSS and GAMA spectra and strong line ratios, and are quoted in the Pettini2004 O3N2 calibration.

The errors on the high R_ 21 >1 ratios in NGC 5496 O1,O2 and UGC 9299 are large enough that they are still consistent with a `normal' R_ 21 =0.7-1.0 value, however UGC 9215 O1 appears to be significantly excited with R_ 21 =2.11 0.58 .

The regions with the highest R_ 21 and R_ 31 are those in the outer disk of UGC 9215 and in UGC 9299. That the R_ 31 ratios are also high (and less prone to uncertainty due to the closer match of the beam areas), supports the earlier conclusion of more excited, dense gas typical of star forming regions. The centre of UGC 9215 has an intermediate value of R_ 31 0.53 while for UGC 9215 O1 and UGC 9299 the ratio is R_ 31 > 0.70 , which is more typical of star-bursts, LIRGs and actively star forming regions where R_ 31 0.5-0.8 Yao2003,Narayanan2005,Mao2010,PPP2012,Bauermeister2013 .

gmw_00007188.name: DELVE 2
gmw_00007188.overview: DELVE 2 is an ultra-faint stellar system located near the Magellanic Clouds [arXiv:2009.08550]. It consists of an old, metal-poor population of stars, though its classification as either a dwarf galaxy or a star cluster remains unresolved [arXiv:2009.08550].

sources.summary: The paper reports an ultra-faint old, metal-poor stellar system near the Magellanic Clouds whose size and luminosity leave its dwarf-galaxy versus star-cluster classification unresolved.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a new ultra-faint stellar system found near the Magellanic Clouds in the DECam Local Volume Exploration (DELVE) Survey. This new system, , is located at a heliocentric distance of D_ = 71 4 , which places it at a 3D physical separation of 12 from the center of Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC) and 28 from the center of the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC). is identified as a resolved overdensity of old ( > 13.3 ) and metal-poor ( = -2.0_ 0.5 ^ +0.2 dex) stars with a projected half-light radius of r_ 1/2 = 21^ +4 _ 3 and an absolute magnitude of M_V = -2.1^ +0.4 _ 0.5 .

Alternately, if 's properties prove to be most consistent with the population of recently discovered star clusters, the system should continue to be referred to as DELVE 2, following the convention that faint star clusters are named after the survey they are discovered in. Follow-up studies are needed to make a conclusion about the classification of , and its true nature remains unclear at this time.

sources.summary: The paper treats DELVE 2 as a tiny possible UFD near the SMC and concludes that its two nearby RRL are most likely SMC members.

quotes.quote: DELVE 2 is located at only 6 9 from the SMC. Although the two RRL stars found in our search are within 1 from the UFD, the galaxy is tiny and they are indeed >35 R_h from the center of DELVE 2. In addition, the magnitudes and proper motions of the two RRL completely overlap with those of the SMC (Fig. fig:SMC ). Thus, it is clear in this case that those stars are most likely members of the SMC rather than of DELVE 2, in agreement with the conclusions by Cerny2021a .

gmw_00007189.name: ADF22A1
gmw_00007189.overview: ADF22A1 is a member of the SSA22 protocluster at a redshift of z = 3.09 [arXiv:2305.06400, arXiv:2303.16227]. It is a star-forming galaxy that hosts an X-ray-detected active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.06400, arXiv:2303.16227], which represents an obscured, early phase of supermassive black hole growth [arXiv:2305.06400].

sources.summary: The paper includes ADF22A1 as an X-ray-detected protoquasar candidate in SSA22, consistent with obscured SMBH growth in a star-forming galaxy and possible later massive-galaxy evolution.

quotes.quote: We analyze the physical properties of 8 X-ray selected active galactic nuclei (AGN) and one candidate protoquasar system (ADF22A1) in the z = 3.09 SSA22 protocluster by fitting their X-ray-to-IR spectral energy distributions (SEDs) using our SED fitting code, https://www.github.com/rafaeleufrasio/lightning .

The remaining SSA22 systems, including ADF22A1, are consistent with obscured supermassive black hole (SMBH) growth in star forming galaxies. We estimate the SMBH accretion rates and masses, and compare the properties and SFH of the 9 protocluster AGN systems with X-ray detected AGN candidates in the Chandra Deep Fields (CDF), finding that the distributions of SMBH growth rates, star formation rates, SMBH masses, and stellar masses for the protocluster AGN are consistent with field AGN.

The proto-quasar system ADF22A1, identified as an AzTEC 1.1 mm source in tamura2010 , is also X-ray detected. We include ADF22A1 in our sample as it possibly represents an early phase of AGN growth in the protocluster, and may (given its projected mass and location in the protocluster) evolve into one of the largest galaxies in the protocluster by z=0 .

sources.summary: ID23 is an SSA 22 protocluster member with an X-ray luminous AGN; high-resolution observations reveal edge-on rotating-disk features and extended S-shaped major-axis PVDs.

quotes.quote: ID23 - 25 These galaxies are members of the SSA 22 protocluster Steidel_1998 that was extensively studied with ALMA Umehata_2015, Umehata_2017, Umehata_2018 . Lehmer_2009 identified X-ray luminous AGNs in ID 23 and 25 using observations from the Chandra Space telescope. Extended Lyman- emission from multiple filaments between galaxies within SSA 22 are identified using MUSE observations Umehata_2019 and are thought to be responsible for the accretion of gas within the protocluster and the growth of galaxies and their supermassive black holes. htbp The high-angular resolution observations employed here allow us to clearly see the typical features of edge-on rotating disks in ID 23, 24, and 25. The three galaxies have very extended S-shape major-axis PVDs (Fig. fig:pv4 ).

gmw_00007190.overview: The galaxy 062 is a late-type galaxy that displays a cometary optical morphology with a tail oriented to the southeast [arXiv:1801.03601]. While its velocity field shows regular rotation across the brightest part of the optical disk, it exhibits a warped tail and outer-disk perturbations [arXiv:1801.03601].

sources.summary: The paper describes 062 as a cometary late-type galaxy with optical and HI tails, warped tail kinematics, and outer-disk HI perturbation likely linked to the optical tail.

quotes.quote: 062 This galaxy displays a cometary optical morphology with its tail oriented to the SE. As the left panel of Figure 97062 shows, the highest column density \ (1.7 10 ^ 21 atoms cm ^ 2 ) is projected at the optical centre with a lower column density \ counterpart to the optical tail, extending to the star projected at the end of the optical tail. black See also section offvla_ages . The \ velocity field contours in the right panel of the figure show regular rotation across black the brightest part of the optical disk, but signs of a warp along the tail. 062 shows clear signs of perturbation of the \ in the outer disk most probably caused by the same mechanism that produced the optical tail.

gmw_00007191.overview: The gas-rich Sc galaxy 068 exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen morphology and a warped disk [arXiv:1801.03601]. It features an eastern extension reaching toward a smaller companion galaxy, suggesting ongoing tidal or ram-pressure interactions [arXiv:1801.03601].

sources.summary: The paper describes 068 as a gas-rich Sc galaxy with asymmetric HI, a column-density offset, an eastern extension toward a companion, and possible ram-pressure or tidal disturbance.

quotes.quote: 068 This gas rich ( \ deficiency -0.3, Table table2 ) Sc galaxy shows several \ morphological and kinematic ongoing interaction signatures: i) in projection the \ 3 contour in Figure 97068 (left panel) extends 58 arcsec (24 kpc) black north of the optical center (perpendicular to the major axis), more than twice 26 arcsec (11 kpc) it extends in the opposite direction. This confirms the earlier reports of black an asymmetric \ morphology in and Paper I; ii) \ column densities rise much more rapidly black south of the optical centre than to the black north of it; iii) An eastern \ extension reaches the projected position of a smaller Irr cluster galaxy, SDSS J114229.18+200713.7; iv) In the \ velocity field (Figure 97068 , right panel) there is a systematic change in the black of the angle \ isovelocity lines, relative to the major optical axis indicating stronger warping of the \ disk at its eastern edge compared to the western edge; v) FUV and NUV (GALEX) images show 068 to have an extended UV disk (XUV disk) with a similar extent to the \ disk, except in the eastern \ extension. The column density maximum ( black 2.6 10 ^ 21 atoms cm ^ 2 ) is offset 18.6 2.9 arcsec (7.7 kpc) W of the optical centre.

gmw_00007192.overview: The late-type galaxy 072 exhibits a truncated neutral hydrogen disk that is roughly the size of its optical disk [arXiv:1801.03601]. Its gas morphology and kinematics are perturbed to the north, while infrared images show possible tidal signatures near the western edge of the disk [arXiv:1801.03601].

sources.summary: The paper says 072 has a truncated HI disk, offset HI maximum, perturbed northern gas morphology and kinematics, and possible tidal signatures in infrared images.

quotes.quote: 072 The integrated \ map for 072 (Figure 97072 ) shows the \ disk is truncated to approximately the radius of the optical disk consistent with its \ deficiency of 0.5 (Table table2 ), although it has an \ excess of -0.20 (Paper III). Its \ column density maximum in the VLA C--array map ( black 8.2 10 ^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 ) is offset 8.6 2.9 arcsec (3.6 kpc) SE of the optical centre. An overall rotation pattern is seen in the velocity field in the right hand panel of the figure, but there is also evidence of perturbed \ kinematics and morphology black north of the \ column density maximum.
