gmw_00007242.overview: SDSS J150243.09+111557.3 is a quasar system at redshift z = 0.39 [arXiv:1406.6365] that is currently in an early merger stage [arXiv:1501.00325]. The system displays visible tidal-interaction signatures [arXiv:2011.09961] and contains a kiloparsec-scale dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1406.6365, arXiv:1501.00325].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1502+1115 as a z=0.39 quasar system containing kiloparsec-scale dual AGN and a newly resolved 138-pc pair of flat-spectrum accreting black-hole cores.

quotes.quote: SDSS J150243.09+111557.3 (J1502+1115 hereafter) was identified as a quasar at redshift z = 0.39 with double-peaked [ OIII ] emission lines, which can be a signature of dual active galactic nuclei (AGN) . Adaptive optics assisted near-infrared ( K band) images reveal two components separated by 1.4 arcseconds (7.4 kpc) and are referred to as J1502P and J1502S . Integral-field spectroscopy determined that the double-peaked [ OIII ] emission in the SDSS spectrum is explained by a 657 km s ^ 1 velocity offset between J1502S and J1502P, which are dust-obscured and unobscured AGN respectively. These two components were also observed as steep-spectrum radio sources ( < -0.8 , S_ ^ ) with the Jansky Very Large Array (JVLA) at 1.4, 5, and 8 GHz. The combination of the above results supported the discovery of a kiloparsec-scale dual AGN system .

We performed 1.7 GHz and 5 GHz Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations of J1502+1115 with the European VLBI Network (EVN), revealing two flat-spectrum ( 0.1 0.1 ) radio components within the spatially-unresolved near-infrared component J1502S. Flat radio spectra identify optically-thick radio emission which is characteristic of the core of relativistic jets generated by an accreting black hole . The two components (labelled J1502SE and J1502SW in Fig. 1a) are each marginally resolved and have an angular separation of 26 milli-arcseconds which corresponds to a projected spatial separation of 138 parsec at z=0.39 .

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS1502+1115 as a disturbed compact radio binary AGN with weak tidal features, the largest nuclear separation, and complex OIII-continuum cores.

quotes.quote: The three objects with double-nucleated IR structure (SDSS0952+2552, SDSS1151+4711, SDSS1502+1115) show clear merger features.

SDSS1502+1115 on the other hand, while clearly disturbed and showing several distinct knots of emission in is compact and shows only weak tidal features. Note that this source shows the largest separation between the nuclei, indicating an early merger stage.

SDSS1502+1115 shows the most complex kinematic structure (Fig. F:twod_1502 ). This source is a confirmed radio binary AGN fu_kiloparsec-scale_2011 . There are two very distinct cores of OIII and continuum emission associated with the two AGN. Additionally, there are other extended OIII components.

sources.summary: The review describes SDSSJ1502+1115 as a galaxy pair with two bright radio cores, one of which may contain either two SMBHs or double hot spots.

quotes.quote: The galaxy pair SDSSJ1502+1115 (Sect. 3.1) is remarkable for its overall radio structure. It consists of two bright radio cores at 7.4 kpc separation (Fu et al. 2011b). One of the two is further resolved into two knots of about equal brightness at 140 pc projected separation. These have been interpreted as representing either two separate SMBHs, or else double hot spots around a single SMBH (Deane et al. 2014, Wrobel et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper says J150243.10+111557.3 has asymmetric Gaussian spectral profiles, ambiguous low-offset decomposition behavior, and visible tidal-interaction signatures in imaging.

quotes.quote: For the remaining 3 sources (J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3), we notice asymmetric Gaussian profiles in the spectral shapes that can be fitted with two Gaussian profiles.

If the velocity offset is 4 69 \ (4th panel in Fig. 1), we can still visually identify double-peaked lines for 5 out of 12 sources (J112659.54+294442.8, J114642.47+511029.6, J115822.58+323102.2, J142507.32+323137.4, J171544.05+600835.7; FWHM of them are narrower than that of the average value and their peak fluxes are comparable), but it is difficult to tell if the spectral shape is composed of a single or double Gaussian lines for 4 of them (J102325.57+324348.4, J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3).
Signs of tidal interactions can be seen in J092455.24+051052.0, J110851.04+065901.4, and J150243.10+111557.3.

gmw_00007243.name: UGCA 130
gmw_00007243.overview: UGCA 130, also designated as MRK 5, exhibits a disturbed velocity field that indicates an ongoing interaction [arXiv:2008.04528]. The galaxy's outer region shows mild warping, and its envelope features a diffuse northern extension with possible nearby isolated clouds [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper says UGCA 130/MRK 5 has warped outer H I contours, a disturbed velocity field, and diffuse northern extension suggesting ongoing interaction and possible nearby clouds.

quotes.quote: UGCA 130 The galaxy is also designated as MRK 5. The low-resolution image shows mild warping of the contours in the outer region. The velocity field appears disturbed, indicative of ongoing interaction as also speculated in Lopez2010A&A...521A..63L . The envelope shows diffuse extension towards north with a possibility of isolated clouds in the vicinity.

gmw_00007244.name: MRK 22
gmw_00007244.overview: MRK 22 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy containing young star-forming regions with Wolf-Rayet emission line features [arXiv:1710.02645]. The system exhibits a diffuse tidal tail-like structure, an irregular velocity field, and double nuclei, indicating an ongoing interaction or merger [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 22 as a blue compact dwarf with WR features, young star-forming regions, radio deficiency, low metallicity, disturbed gas, and likely merger-triggered evolution.

quotes.quote: The optical spectroscopic and radio interferometric 21 cm-line observations of the blue compact dwarf galaxy Mrk 22 are presented. The Wolf-Rayet (WR) emission line features corresponding to high ionization lines of He II 4686 and C IV 5808 from young massive stars are detected. The ages of two prominent star forming regions in the galaxy are estimated as 10 Myr and 4 Myr. The galaxy has non-thermal radio deficiency, which also indicates a young star-burst and lack of supernovae events from the current star formation activities, consistent with the detection of WR emission lines features.

A significant N/O enrichment is seen in the fainter star forming region. The gas-phase metallicities [ 12 + log(O/H) ] for the bright and faint regions are estimated as 7.98 0.07 and 7.46 0.09 respectively. The galaxy has a large diffuse envelop. The images reveal disturbed gas kinematics and clouds outside the optical extent of the galaxy, indicating recent tidal interaction or merger in the system.

sources.summary: The paper interprets MRK 22 as interacting or merging, citing a southward tidal-tail-like structure, irregular velocity field, double nuclei, metallicity differences, and multiple profile peaks.

quotes.quote: MRK 22 The image shows an ongoing interaction with a diffuse tidal tail-like structure extending towards south. The velocity field is highly irregular. A bright region is also seen towards the north. It is very likely that MRK 22 is interacting with another galaxy or cloud in the vicinity.

Alternatively, it could be a merger system as double nuclei is indicated Mazzarella1991AJ....101.2034M . Paswan2018MNRAS.473.4566P recently found a significant metallicity difference between the two nuclei, which also suggests that MRK 22 could be a merging system of two galaxies. The global profile shows multiple peaks.

gmw_00007245.name: IC 2524
gmw_00007245.overview: IC 2524 exhibits a lopsided, cometary neutral hydrogen morphology that may represent a tidal tail [arXiv:2008.04528]. This structure includes a large region of low-column-density gas extending to the southeast and an emission peak offset from the galaxy's center, possibly indicating gas accretion or an interaction with an unidentified faint galaxy [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper reports low-column-density gas extending southeast of IC 2524, lopsided cometary H I morphology, a possible tidal tail, and an offset H I peak.

quotes.quote: IC 2524 The galaxy shows a large region of low column density gas extending towards the south-east of the galaxy. The high resolution image reveals lopsidedness with a cometary shape, which can also be interpreted as a tidal tail. The velocity field shows smooth gradient.

The galaxy could be accreting gas or interacting with some other faint galaxy, not identified in the optical images. The global profile shows lopsidedness. The peak of the emission has an offset from the galaxy centre.

gmw_00007246.name: KUG 1013+381
gmw_00007246.overview: KUG 1013+381 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2305.16786] that exhibits a cometary, tadpole-like morphology [arXiv:2305.16786] and a disturbed velocity field [arXiv:2008.04528]. It shows signs of interaction with an isolated cloud or faint galaxy [arXiv:2008.04528], alongside intense star formation and hot dust [arXiv:2305.16786].

sources.summary: The paper maps W1016+3754 as a tadpole-like blue compact dwarf with hot dust, active star formation, low metallicity, and H I morphology suggesting gas infall.

quotes.quote: W1016+3754 W1016+3754 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with cometary tadpole-like morphology in optical images (Fig. fig1 ) at redshift 0.0039 which corresponds to a luminosity distance D_ L 17 Mpc. An edge-on disk galaxy, UGC 5540, is at same redshift, z=0.0039 , and angular distance of 8 towards the south. Red WISE MIR colours ( W1 [ 3.4 m ] W2 [ 4.6 m ] = 0.55 mag, W2 [ 4.6 m ] W3 [ 12 m ] = 3.94 mag) and bright 22 m emission (W4 [ 22 m ] = 6.7 mag) 2013yCat.2328....0C , in W1016+3754 indicate the presence of hot dust heated by intense star formation. Stellar mass of (M_ / M _ ) 7.2 is estimated using the correlation between optical ugriz colours and mass-to-light ratio from .

Using the method of and , star formation rate (SFR) of 0.04 M _ yr ^ 1 is estimated from Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX ; bianchi2017ApJS..230...24B Far UV luminosity corrected for dust extinction with 22 m values from WISE (Chandola et al. submitted). This gives a specific star formation rate 10^ 8.6 yr ^ 1 for this galaxy. The H image shows presence of two star formation regions 2016MNRAS.462...92J . The equivalent width (EQW) of H line for this system is 96.7 2012MNRAS.427.1229I , implying starburst age of 2 Myr 1986A&A...156..111C, 1996ApJS..107..661S .

sources.summary: The paper describes KUG 1013+381 as interacting with a cloud or faint galaxy, with a disturbed velocity field and no obvious disk rotation; UGC 5540 is also detected nearby.

quotes.quote: KUG 1013+381 It also shows signs of interactions with an isolated cloud or optically unidentified faint galaxy towards the east of the galaxy. The velocity field appears disturbed with no obvious sign of rotation in a disk. emissions from the spiral galaxy UGC 5540 at 8' angular distance towards south of the galaxy KUG 1013+381 was also detected. The images of UGC 5540 are not presented here.

gmw_00007247.name: CGCG 038-051
gmw_00007247.overview: CGCG 038-051 is located in a group-like environment with nine nearby galaxies, which are mostly giant spirals alongside two dwarf neighbors [arXiv:1810.12768]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed, lopsided velocity field and a cometary optical morphology with multiple nuclei, suggesting a recent tidal interaction or merger [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 038-051 as lying in a group-like environment with nine nearby galaxies, mostly giant spirals with two dwarf neighbours in a narrow velocity range.

quotes.quote: CGCG 038-051 In the defined volume, a total of 9 galaxies are listed. It represents an average galaxy density of 19 Mpc ^ 3 . The neighbour galaxies are found to be in a narrow velocity range of 986-1070 km s ^ 1 . Except two dwarf galaxies (LSBC L1-137 and LSBC L1-137A), all other galaxies are giant systems which have well-developed bright spiral arms and disk. These galaxies together appear to be residing in a group-like environment.

sources.summary: The paper finds CGCG 038-051 disturbed and lopsided, with central bright regions, and says tidal interaction or a merger is consistent with its multiple-nucleus cometary optical morphology.

quotes.quote: CGCG 038-051 The velocity field appears disturbed and lopsided. Two bright regions are seen near the center. It is possible that the galaxy has recently encountered tidal interaction or alternatively it is a merger system, which appears more consistent with the optical morphology showing multiple nuclei and cometary shape.

gmw_00007248.name: IC 2828
gmw_00007248.overview: IC 2828 is a galaxy that exhibits a warped morphology [arXiv:2008.04528]. The system displays anomalous velocity emissions and a highly irregular, disturbed velocity field, indicating that it is very likely a merging system [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper says IC 2828 has anomalous velocity emission, a highly irregular and disturbed velocity field, warped morphology, and is very likely a merging system.

quotes.quote: IC 2828 The morphology of this galaxy shows anomalous velocity emissions and as a result the velocity field appears highly irregular and disturbed. The morphology also indicates a warp. It is very likely a merging system as indicated by the velocity field.

gmw_00007249.name: UM 439
gmw_00007249.overview: The galaxy UM 439 features low-column-density gas extensions projecting toward both the northeast and southwest [arXiv:2008.04528]. This tail-bridge morphology suggests that the galaxy is likely undergoing a tidal interaction with an unidentified companion [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper describes UM 439 as having low-column-density gas extensions on opposite sides, a tail-bridge morphology, and likely tidal interaction with an unidentified galaxy.

quotes.quote: UM 439 The map indicates some low column density gas extending towards north-east. An extension is also seen in the opposite side (south-west). This morphology is indicative of tail-bridge structure. It is likely that this galaxy is undergoing a tidal interaction with some unidentified galaxy, as also predicted by Taylor1995ApJS...99..427T and van1998AJ....116.1186V .

gmw_00007250.name: I SZ 59
gmw_00007250.overview: I SZ 59 is a galaxy characterized by a highly irregular and disturbed velocity field [arXiv:2008.04528]. Its emission map is lopsided with a peak offset from the galaxy center, and it likely experienced a recent tidal interaction or gas accretion [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper reports I SZ 59 has an irregular disturbed velocity field, lopsided emission offset from the galaxy center, anomalous-velocity clouds, and likely recent interaction or gas accretion.

quotes.quote: I SZ 59 The velocity field is highly irregular and disturbed. The map also appear disturbed and lopsided with peak of the emission having an offset from the the galaxy centre. Some clouds with anomalous velocity are detected in channel maps. It is very likely that this galaxy has recently experienced some tidal interaction or is accreting gas.

gmw_00007251.name: SBS 1222+614
gmw_00007251.overview: SBS 1222+614 is part of a small group of galaxies that includes four nearby blue dwarf neighbors [arXiv:1810.12768]. The galaxy is closely associated with UGC 7534, UGC 7544, and MCG +10-18-044, the latter of which shows nearby neutral hydrogen emission [arXiv:1810.12768, arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 1222+614 as part of a small group with four nearby galaxies, mostly blue dwarf neighbours, including close associations with MCG +10-18-044, UGC 7534, and UGC 7544.

quotes.quote: SBS 1222+614 Within the defined volume, a total of 4 galaxies are listed, implying an average galaxy density of 10 Mpc ^ 3 . All these neighbour galaxies have velocities in a very narrow range of 709-722 km s ^ 1 , except for SDSS J12195.11+613110.0 which has velocity of 516 km s ^ 1 . Interestingly, all the neighbour galaxies are dwarf galaxies which are blue in optical band as seen in their SDSS colour composite images. The galaxies MCG +10-18-044 and UGC 7534 are closely associated with SBS 1222+614 and UGC 7544 as revealed in the work of blue Jaiswal Omar (private communication) and 2002A&A...389...29S,2002A&A...389...42S . SBS 1222+614 together with its neighbour galaxies indicates a small group of galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports no H I detection for SBS 1222+614 within its sensitivity, gives an H I mass upper limit, and notes nearby emission from MCG +10-18-044.

quotes.quote: SBS 1222+614 The emission from SBS 1222+614 is not detected within the sensitivity of the observations. The mass upper limit to the galaxy is 3 10^6 M_ , assuming a minimum line-width of 20 and 3 detection. However, emission is detected from a nearby galaxy MCG +10-18-044 at an angular distance of 5'.5 southward of SBS 1222+614.

gmw_00007252.name: MCG +10-18-044
gmw_00007252.overview: MCG +10-18-044 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular galaxy with weak H I emission [arXiv:2008.04528]. The galaxy features an irregular velocity field and clear tidal tails, indicating that it is likely undergoing tidal interactions [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG +10-18-044 as a low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular with weak H I emission, irregular velocity field, and clear tidal tails indicating regional interactions.

quotes.quote: MCG +10-18-044 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with low optical surface brightness Huchtmeier2000A&AS..141..469H . A weak emission is detected from MCG +10-18-044, giving SFR = 0.007 0.003 M_ yr ^ 1 . emission is also detected from a region at an angular distance of 4'.5 southward of MCG +10-18-044. The single-dish integral flux of MCG +10-18-044 from the 100-m radio telescope at Effelsberg is 6.2 Jy Huchtmeier2000A&AS..141..469H . Our estimate is in good agreement with this value after considering the flux contribution from all the emission regions.

The velocity field of MCG +10-18-044 is irregular. The images clearly indicate the presence of tidal tails in MCG +10-18-044. It is very likely that the galaxies in this region are undergoing tidal interactions.

gmw_00007253.name: UGC 9273
gmw_00007253.overview: UGC 9273 is an interacting galaxy with an asymmetric H I disk that is offset from its optical disk [arXiv:2008.04528]. It likely interacts with two northern gas clouds, displaying a highly irregular velocity field and two distinct central regions [arXiv:2008.04528].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 9273 as interacting with northern clouds, with an asymmetric H I disk, central regions, irregular velocity field, and diffuse high-resolution emission.

quotes.quote: UGC 9273 Two clouds having no obvious optical counterparts are detected towards the north and north-east of the galaxy. It is very likely that the galaxy is interacting with these clouds, which may be associated with a faint dwarf galaxy. The disk is asymmetric with respect to the stellar disk with an offset from the optical disk.
Two regions are seen near the center. The velocity field is very irregular and disturbed. The high resolution emissions are found to be associated with the diffused emission regions.

gmw_00007254.name: KK84
gmw_00007254.overview: KK84 is a relatively massive dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a distance of 9.7 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0505624]. It hosts a rich system of globular cluster candidates, with a luminosity function that peaks near a magnitude of -7.3 [arXiv:astro-ph/0505624].

sources.summary: The paper treats KK84 as the richest dwarf-spheroidal globular-cluster-candidate host in the sample, noting its distance limits and a candidate luminosity-function peak near M_V=7.3.

quotes.quote: With the current dataset we cannot rule out the presence of GCCs fainter than M_ V 6.5 and with core radii 10 pc in the dSph galaxy KK84, which is the most distant host galaxy in our sample, situated at 9.7 Mpc.

For KK84 and UGC 3755, which host the richest GCC systems in their morphology class, we investigate their GCC luminosity distributions individually. The GCC luminosity function of the dSph galaxy KK84 peaks near M_ V 7.3 mag. For the dIrr galaxy UGC 3755 we find peaks near M_ V 7.0 mag for both the blue and red sub-population. Both galaxies are relatively massive compared to the rest of the sample and show typical turnover magnitudes.

gmw_00007255.name: KK221
gmw_00007255.overview: KK221 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0505624] that is a satellite of Centaurus A [arXiv:2301.07113]. It contains multiple central globular cluster candidates that are offset from the galaxy's center, potentially due to tidal forces from NGC 5128 [arXiv:astro-ph/0505624].

sources.summary: The paper treats KK221 as a robust non-member of the Centaurus A plane because its orbit stays far from the CASP and its initial position is nearly orthogonal to the plane.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, KK221 is a satellite that only manages to orbit within 3 of the CASP. In addition, no transverse velocities result in orbital pole alignments of within 45^ in the least-constrained TV map, and KK221 can be unequivocally rejected as a CASP member. This is a direct consequence of its initial position being nearly orthogonal to the CASP, and can be inferred without referring to our TV constraints.

However, this correcting effect is minimized for heavily misaligned satellites such as KK221, with initial positions nearly orthogonal from the Centaurus A plane -- the triaxial potential appears symmetric when orbiting about one of its defining axes.

sources.summary: The paper highlights KK221 as a dwarf spheroidal with many central globular-cluster candidates, all within a surface-brightness isophote but offset from the galaxy center, possibly due to NGC5128 tides.

quotes.quote: Six dSph galaxies contain GCCs located near their centers. KK84 and KK221 have the largest number of GCCs among our dSph sample (see Tab. tab:prop and Figure ps:image ,d). Overplotted on Figure 1d is the isophote of constant surface brightness _B 26.5 mag/arcsec ^2 . All globular cluster candidates in KK221 are located within the boundaries of this isophote, but the location of the brightest cluster and the whole globular cluster system seems to be shifted from the central position in the galaxy. We can only speculate whether KK221 has an elongated orbit and experienced strong tidal forces from NGC5128, which moves its globular cluster system from a centered position.

gmw_00007256.name: CX 40
gmw_00007256.overview: CX 40 is an active galactic nucleus that has been spectroscopically verified through its optical emission lines [arXiv:1207.5915]. The source is also detected in both the NVSS and the Parkes-MIT-NRAO radio surveys [arXiv:1207.5915].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically verifies CX 40 as an AGN, measuring optical emission lines, a redshift indicator, Seyfert 2-like line widths, and an X-ray luminosity typical of low-luminosity Seyferts.

quotes.quote: CX 40=CXO J174404.3-260925, a new spectroscopically verified AGN This source is detected with 35 X-ray counts and a radio flux density of 7.3 0.5 mJy in NVSS. The ratio of radio flux density to X-ray flux is thus about 10^ 12.5 Hz ^ 1 . The source is also detected in the Parkes-MIT-NRAO (PMN) 4.85 GHz radio survey's tropical region (Griffith et al. 1994) with a flux density of 62 11 mJy, and a positional offset of about 62'', within the positional uncertainties at this signal-to-noise and at this frequency from Parkes.

The optical spectrum for this source shows a series of strong lines and blends of strong lines. The redshift for this object is determined primarily from the [ O I ] line at 6300 (rest frame), the only unblended line in the spectrum. The uncertainty of this redshift is difficult to estimate, since the only unblended line we detect at high signal to noise is the [ O I ] line which peaks at 6577 in the observed frame -- rather close to the wavelength of H in the rest frame, which is potential problematic given the nearby foreground star. Regardless, the uncertainty in the redshift should not affect the conclusion that the source is an active galactic nucleus.

gmw_00007257.name: CX 49
gmw_00007257.overview: CX 49 is a candidate active galactic nucleus associated with a bright infrared counterpart [arXiv:1207.5915]. The source exhibits optical variability of approximately 0.5 magnitudes and displays possible extended radio emission [arXiv:1207.5915].

sources.summary: The paper treats CX 49 as a candidate AGN based on NVSS/Chandra detection, optical variability, a likely PMN counterpart, possible extended radio emission, and a bright infrared counterpart.

quotes.quote: CX 49 = CXO J173146.8-300309, a candidate AGN This source is detected at a flux density of 90 2.8 mJy in the NVSS data and with 30 counts by Chandra. Its ratio of radio flux density to X-ray flux is thus about 10^ 11.4 Hz ^ 1 . The source is detected in the VVV data with an offset of 0.21'', at H=14.22 0.03 and K_s=13.45 0.02 .

This object also shows about 0.5 magnitudes of variability in the ' variability survey (see figure 4), with a typical value of 20.6 0.5 . A source near this position is also detected in the zenith region of the PMN survey (Wright et al. 1996), with a 4.85 GHz flux density of 123 14 mJy, and a positional offset of 98'', and is flagged as a likely extended source (although there is no evidence for extension from the NVSS catalog). Given that there is no NVSS source nearer to the PMN source than the one we have associated with CX 49, we regard the match as likely reliable. If the source is extended on the spatial scales of the 4.2' beam size of the Parkes data, then likely the VLA resolved out some of the flux from the source in the NVSS data set, and a spectral index cannot be estimated from these data, but otherwise, the source has a flat to slightly inverted spectrum. The possible extended radio emission and bright infrared counterpart both suggest that the source is an active galactic nucleus, but the identification is insecure at this point.

gmw_00007258.name: CX 52
gmw_00007258.overview: CX 52 is a compact background quasar located behind the Galactic bulge [arXiv:1709.02464]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus with a radio morphology consisting of a compact core and extended emission [arXiv:1207.5915].

sources.summary: The paper uses J1744-312 as a compact background quasar behind the Galactic bulge, noting its small apparent size, negligible scintillation, millimeter flux densities, and placement away from prominent molecular complexes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The coordinates for the quasar used for the observations were (J2000): 17^ h 44^ m 23.5782^ s , (J2000): 31 16'36''.287 .

quotes.quote: We have chosen the quasar J1744-312 as the background source to look for the absorption components in the Galactic center (GC) region. J1744-312 is at (l, b) = (-2 ^ .13, -1 ^ .00) i.e., behind the Galactic bulge Throughout this work, we refer as the Galactic center region to the central kpc of the Galaxy, which is shown in Fig. overview and which is related to the bulge of the Galaxy . Fig. overview shows the position of the quasar in the GC region. This quasar is compact, since it is at best marginally resolved with a 4.5x1.1 mas beam at 24 GHz B1741-312 in Charlot_et_al_2010 , and it is expected that the quasar is even more compact at mm-wavelengths.

In addition interstellar scintillation, which can result in a broadening of the source size, is negligible in our case since the quasar is far from the CMZ and scintillation becomes less visible at mm-wavelengths. The quasar has a flux density of 0.571 Jy at 103 GHz and 0.563 Jy at 91.5 GHz in the ALMA-Band 3 calibrator source catalog (see the ALMA Calibrator Source Catalogue https://almascience.eso.org/sc/ ); it is therefore an adequate source for absorption measurements of gas in the Galactic bulge. Unlike the absorption detected by Geballe_Oka_2010 , this quasar is not behind one of the prominent molecular complexes. This may serve to avoid confusion with absorption by denser gas components.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CX 52 as a likely AGN/extragalactic object with strong radio and X-ray detections, hard X-ray color, absent nearby infrared stars, and compact-core plus extended radio morphology.

quotes.quote: CX 52 = CXO J174423.5-311636, a likely AGN This source is detected at a flux density of 425 15 mJy in NVSS, and with 29 counts by Chandra, giving a ratio of radio flux density to X-ray flux of about 10^ 10.7 Hz ^ 1 . There are no optical or infrared stars near this source, indicating that it is likely in a region of high extinction -- the nearest sources in the VVV catalog are more than 3'' away, and all have very red colors; not surprisingly, the source is not detected in our optical variability survey. The source's X-ray hardness ratio is 0.83 0.26 , making it likely one of the hardest sources in the GBS, and while, with the relatively small number of counts, this designation cannot be secure, this finding supports the idea that the source is in a region of large foreground extinction.

Roy et al. (2005), using imaging data from the VLA and the Australia Telescope Compact Array with higher angular resolution than the NVSS data identify this source as a compact core plus extended emission, and hence an extragalactic object. A likely match is also found in the Nobeyama 10 GHz survey (Handa et al. 1987) -- source number 4 in that catalog is about 2' from CX 52, and appears pointlike in that survey with a flux density of 0.46 0.2 Jy.

gmw_00007259.name: CX 293
gmw_00007259.overview: CX 293 is classified as a likely active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1207.5915]. Radio imaging of the source reveals a triple morphology, and it is detected in both X-ray and 330 MHz surveys [arXiv:1207.5915].

sources.summary: The paper classifies CX 293 as a likely AGN because it has NVSS, Chandra, and 330 MHz detections, triple radio morphology, and a foreground stellar spectrum likely due to chance superposition.

quotes.quote: CX 293 = CXO J174000.6-274816, a likely AGN This source is detected at a flux density of 170 6 mJy in NVSS, and with 9 X-ray counts, yielding L_R/L_X of 10^ 10.7 Hz ^ 1 . It is also detected in the 330 MHz survey of Nord et al. (2004), with a flux density of 404 mJy. The source is identified in the radio imaging of Roy et al (2004) as a triple source, and hence is likely to be an active galactic nucleus.

It has a VVV infrared counterpart 1.0'' away, with J=14.80 0.02 , H=13.75 0.02 and K_s=13.33 0.03 . An optical spectrum was taken of this VVV source and showed a purely stellar spectrum with no emission lines, indicating that it is likely a chance superposition (see figure cx293 ). A 10 flux drop is seen around 6564 , so it is clear that the source is a Galactic foreground object rather than, e.g. a background blazar, but most of the strong features in the spectrum correspond to atmospheric features that have not been removed fully. Additionally, the astrometry from our optical variability survey indicates that the source of which the spectrum was taken was probably not the real match.

gmw_00007260.name: CX 937
gmw_00007260.overview: CX 937 is a background active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1207.5915]. High-resolution radio imaging resolves the source into a double radio morphology, and it is associated with a bright infrared counterpart [arXiv:1207.5915].

sources.summary: The paper describes CX 937 as a likely background AGN, citing its radio/X-ray ratio, resolved double radio morphology, PMN and 330 MHz identifications, and an infrared counterpart.

quotes.quote: CX 937 = CXO J175359.2-281720, a likely AGN This source is detected with a flux density of 52 2 mJy and with 3 X-ray counts, yielding a L_R/L_X of 10^ 10.1 Hz ^ 1 . Roy et al. (2005) identify this source as a background active galactic nucleus because it is resolved into a double source with the high resolution radio imaging data. The source is identified in the PMN survey (Wright et al. 1996) as a 104 12 mJy source at 4.85 GHz.

It is also identified at 186 mJy in the 330 MHz data set of Nord et al. (2004), with the spectral index implied from comparing the 330 MHz data with the NVSS data consistent with the expectations for a typical background active galactic nucleus. A bright infrared counterpart is seen 1.1'' away, with J=13.68 0.01 , H=13.39 0.02 and K_s=13.32 0.03 from VVV, and with J=13.82 0.1 from 2MASS, with the 2MASS photometry at H and K_s flagged as unreliable.

gmw_00007261.overview: The source 1514 + 197 is a blazar that exhibits distinct radio variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0509030]. Its rapid variations at 2 GHz are dominated by interstellar scintillation, while its slower 8 GHz variations are driven by intrinsic synchrotron bursts [arXiv:astro-ph/0509030].

sources.summary: The paper uses blazar 1514 + 197 to motivate its scintillation analysis, describing rapid 2 GHz variations, slower intrinsic 8 GHz variations, and ISS-dominated short-timescale behavior.

quotes.quote: Methodology We motivate our analysis by considering the blazar 1514 + 197 Throughout the paper we refer to sources by their 1950 coordinates . Figure f:flux1514 shows its light curve from 1988.3 to 1995.3. Immediately evident is that the 2 GHz variations are much more rapid (5--50 days) than the 8 GHz variations, which encompass only three main peaks in 7 yrs.

These slow variations also appear at 2 GHz, but with a reduced amplitude and significant delay compared to those at 8 GHz, as expected for intrinsic synchrotron bursts. In contrast, the fast variations are relatively strong at 2 GHz and much weaker at 8 GHz, which is consistent with the expected decrease in ISS amplitude with increasing frequency.

gmw_00007262.name: LAE7745
gmw_00007262.overview: LAE7745 is a typical star-forming Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.1 [arXiv:1402.5227]. Near-infrared observations of the galaxy have detected a single emission line with a flux of 13.7 +/− 10^-17 erg/s/cm^2, while the second expected line remains undetected [arXiv:1402.5227].

sources.summary: The paper reports LAE7745 as a typical star-forming z 3.1 LAE with a credible near-infrared line detection, measured line flux, central wavelength, and no detected second line.

quotes.quote: The other new detection, henceforth LAE7745, appears to be a typical star-forming LAE, and will be discussed in more detail below. Only one object, LAE7745, shows a detection in the reduced 2-D (Figure fig:twod ). The detection corresponds to the expected spatial-direction location of the LAE based on its distance from the bright continuum object that shared the slit.

Following this procedure, LAE7745 has an \ line flux of 13.7 1.8 10^ 17 . The other characteristics of the best-fit Gaussian for LAE7745 are a central wavelength of 20636.7 1.3 , FWHM = 19.7 0.12 , and a constant term consistent with zero (0.9 1.3 10 ^ 18 ). We do not detect the second \ line at rest-frame 4960 \ in LAE7745.

gmw_00007263.name: LAE27878
gmw_00007263.overview: LAE27878 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1402.5227]. It has a previously detected emission line flux of 7.0 ± 0.3 × 10^-17 erg/s/cm^2 and an associated velocity offset of 125 ± 17.3 km/s [arXiv:1402.5227].

sources.summary: The paper says LAE27878 has a previous line detection and velocity offset, but its model prediction underestimates the observed line flux despite assigning it the smallest predicted flux among detections.

quotes.quote: This is in addition to the our two previous detections reported in Mc11, where line fluxes of 7.0 0.3 10^ 17 and 35.5 1.2 10^ 17 \ were reported for LAE27878 and LAE40844, respectively. The measurement reported here is in addition to the velocity offsets of 125 17.3 and 342 18.3 \ we previously reported for LAE27878 and LAE40844, respectively, making the new measurement the smallest velocity offset we have seen.

The model prediction for LAE27878 provides the worst agreement. The model prediction is only 0.7 ^ +1.9 _ 0.7 10 ^ 17 , while the observed line flux in this object is 7 0.3 10 ^ 17 . In this object, the bobserved \ line contributes 100 per cent of the flux in the \ filter, where the model only predicts a contribution of 10 per cent. The agreement is not good, but it is worth noting that LAE27878 has the smallest \ line flux of the three line fluxes we have measured to date, and the model correspondingly assigns the smallest predicted line flux of the three to this object as well.

gmw_00007264.name: LAE40844
gmw_00007264.overview: LAE40844 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1402.5227]. Observations of the galaxy have detected a Lyman-alpha emission line flux of 35.5 x 10^-17 erg/s/cm^2, which has been used to model its stellar population [arXiv:1402.5227].

sources.summary: The paper uses LAE40844 as a previously detected LAE and SED-fitting example, linking its observed line flux, velocity offset, and allowed stellar-population model to ionizing-photon production.

quotes.quote: The previously observed LAEs in Mc11 were LAE40844, LAE27878, and LAE14310. LAE40844 and LAE27878 yielded detections of the \ line. This is in addition to the our two previous detections reported in Mc11, where line fluxes of 7.0 0.3 10^ 17 and 35.5 1.2 10^ 17 \ were reported for LAE27878 and LAE40844, respectively.

An example of this calculation, for LAE40844, is shown in Figure fig:ionize . Figure fig:ionize shows the strength of the \ line (solid curve) as function of stellar populations of increasing age for constant mass, metallicity, and . This particular figure is constructed using the best allowed-fit model for LAE40844, where metallicity is 0.2 , is 0.001 Gyrs, and mass is 2.9 10 ^9 .

gmw_00007265.overview: 1213496 is a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy with a low neutral hydrogen mass [arXiv:1912.01062]. It exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen morphology and a disturbed velocity field in its outer disk, which may indicate a gravitational interaction with the neighboring galaxy 1180660 [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1213496 as a nearby dwarf irregular with low HI mass, high specific star formation, disturbed HI morphology, and possible interaction with 1180660.

quotes.quote: 1213496 1180660 -- The two nearest galaxies in our sample are dwarf irregulars (Figures A1 A2). They are close spatially with a separation of around 70 kpc, as shown in Figure 6a, and have a velocity difference of 5 km s ^ 1 . Both 1213496 and 1180660 lie within 300 kpc of filamentary structure. These galaxies have HI masses on the order of 10 ^ 7 M _ and have the highest SSFR of our sample. Both galaxies have HI offset from the optical major axis as well as asymmetric HI morphology.

For 1213496, the northern part of the velocity field in the outer disk seems to shift more towards polar. For 1180660, the velocity field looks even more disturbed with the contours to the west nearly perpendicular to the HI major axis, more like a polar ring of counter-rotating gas. Given their proximity, the disturbances in the outer disks may be an indication that these two galaxies are gravitationally interacting. Ionized gas is not detected in 1213496 and is limited in extent in 1180660, consistent with their low star formation rates.

gmw_00007266.overview: 1180660 is a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy with a low star formation rate and limited ionized gas [arXiv:1912.01062]. It exhibits highly disturbed neutral hydrogen kinematics, possibly resembling a polar ring of counter-rotating gas, which may indicate a gravitational interaction with its neighbor 1213496 [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1180660 as a nearby dwarf irregular whose HI kinematics are strongly disturbed, possibly resembling counter-rotating polar gas during interaction with 1213496.

quotes.quote: 1213496 1180660 -- The two nearest galaxies in our sample are dwarf irregulars (Figures A1 A2). They are close spatially with a separation of around 70 kpc, as shown in Figure 6a, and have a velocity difference of 5 km s ^ 1 . Both 1213496 and 1180660 lie within 300 kpc of filamentary structure. These galaxies have HI masses on the order of 10 ^ 7 M _ and have the highest SSFR of our sample. Both galaxies have HI offset from the optical major axis as well as asymmetric HI morphology.

For 1213496, the northern part of the velocity field in the outer disk seems to shift more towards polar. For 1180660, the velocity field looks even more disturbed with the contours to the west nearly perpendicular to the HI major axis, more like a polar ring of counter-rotating gas. Given their proximity, the disturbances in the outer disks may be an indication that these two galaxies are gravitationally interacting. Ionized gas is not detected in 1213496 and is limited in extent in 1180660, consistent with their low star formation rates.

gmw_00007267.overview: The galaxy 1419315 exhibits a high star formation rate and contains decoupled ionized and neutral gas at large radii [arXiv:1912.01062]. It may be gravitationally interacting with its neighbor 1221696, which features a tidal tail extending toward 1419315 [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1419315 as a distant sample galaxy with asymmetric HI, high star formation, possible interaction with 1221696, and decoupled ionized and neutral gas at large radii.

quotes.quote: 1419315 1221696 -- The two farthest galaxies in our sample are close spatially with a separation of around 170 kpc, and have a velocity difference of 94 km s ^ 1 . 1221696 (Figure A10 Figure 6b) has a tail that extends in the direction of 1419315 (Figure A9). This feature along with their proximity may indicate that they are gravitationally interacting. Both 1419315 and 1221696 are located 5.6 Mpc from filamentary structure. These galaxies have HI and stellar mass on the order of 10 ^ 9 M _ and have SFRs that are some of the highest in our sample.

However, in 1419315, the ionized gas rotation seems to flatten/decline at a lower velocity than the HI towards the receding edge of the galaxy. The HI and H are decoupled at large radii, which is a unique phenomenon in this sample.

gmw_00007268.overview: 1221696 is a distant irregular galaxy that exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen morphology and elevated sulfur-line ratios across its disk [arXiv:1912.01062]. It has a high star formation rate and features a tail extending toward the galaxy 1419315, suggesting a gravitational interaction [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1221696 as a distant irregular galaxy with a tail toward 1419315, asymmetric HI morphology, high star formation, and elevated sulfur-line ratios.

quotes.quote: 1419315 1221696 -- The two farthest galaxies in our sample are close spatially with a separation of around 170 kpc, and have a velocity difference of 94 km s ^ 1 . 1221696 (Figure A10 Figure 6b) has a tail that extends in the direction of 1419315 (Figure A9). This feature along with their proximity may indicate that they are gravitationally interacting. Both 1419315 and 1221696 are located 5.6 Mpc from filamentary structure. These galaxies have HI and stellar mass on the order of 10 ^ 9 M _ and have SFRs that are some of the highest in our sample.

In galaxy 1221696, we again see little variation in the line ratios across the measured region of the disk. The [ SII ] /H ratio is, however, higher at all points across the measured area of the disk.

gmw_00007269.overview: The galaxy 1204837 features a dominant central bulge [arXiv:1912.01062] and exhibits very weak nitrogen emission [arXiv:1912.01062]. It possesses an asymmetric, extended neutral hydrogen structure [arXiv:1912.01062], with both neutral and ionized gas missing from its center [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1204837 as a galaxy near a neighbor, with extended asymmetric HI, good HI-H kinematic agreement, a dominant bulge, and weak nitrogen emission.

quotes.quote: 1204837 1227948 -- 1204837 (Figure A4) and 1227948 (Figure A5) are two galaxies that are within 371 kpc of their nearest neighbor. Both galaxies have extended HI with asymmetric morphology and are located 3.5 Mpc from filamentary structure. Both have HI offset from the optical major axis, with irregular galaxy 1227948 having a PA offset of 68 ^ . The ionized gas rotation fits well with the HI PV diagram of 1204837, with both neutral and ionized gas missing from the center of the galaxy.
1204837 has the most dominant bulge in our sample, which may account for the lack of neutral and ionized gas in the center of the PV diagrams. 1204837 has very weak [ NII ] 6583 emission.

gmw_00007270.overview: 1227948 is an irregular galaxy located 3.5 Mpc from a filamentary structure [arXiv:1912.01062]. It features extended, asymmetric neutral hydrogen gas with a large position-angle offset of 68 degrees relative to its optical major axis [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1227948 as an irregular galaxy with extended asymmetric HI, a large position-angle offset, and ionized gas aligned with the HI contours despite scatter.

quotes.quote: 1204837 1227948 -- 1204837 (Figure A4) and 1227948 (Figure A5) are two galaxies that are within 371 kpc of their nearest neighbor. Both galaxies have extended HI with asymmetric morphology and are located 3.5 Mpc from filamentary structure. Both have HI offset from the optical major axis, with irregular galaxy 1227948 having a PA offset of 68 ^ . The ionized gas rotation fits well with the HI PV diagram of 1204837, with both neutral and ionized gas missing from the center of the galaxy. 1204837 has the most dominant bulge in our sample, which may account for the lack of neutral and ionized gas in the center of the PV diagrams. 1204837 has very weak [ NII ] 6583 emission. In 1227948 the ionized gas lays along with the system velocity for both position angles, agreeing well with the HI contours (albeit with some scatter since the galaxy is irregular and one of the smallest in the sample).

gmw_00007271.overview: 1432731 is a face-on spiral galaxy located 1.2 Mpc from its nearest neighbor [arXiv:1912.01062]. It features a highly asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution that extends over six times past its optical diameter, with possible counter-rotation indicated by low-level emission on the receding side [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper highlights 1432731 as a face-on spiral with the sample’s largest HI mass, very extended asymmetric HI, possible counter-rotation, and radial line-ratio changes.

quotes.quote: 1432731 -- 1432731 (Figure A6) is a spiral galaxy and is the most face-on galaxy of our sample, inclined at 14 ^ . It is at a distance of 1.2 Mpc from its nearest neighbor. This galaxy has the largest HI mass in the sample at 1.1 10^ 10 M _ and resides 9.6 Mpc from a filament spine. 1432731 has an HI diameter that is 71 kpc across, extending over six times past the optical diameter. The HI distribution and morphology are very asymmetric and the PV diagram shows lower velocity low-level emission on the receding side which could indicate counter-rotation.

The ionized gas overall consistently traces the HI contours. There is a region of ionized gas emission on the receding edge, likely corresponding to a bright HII region in one of the faint extended spiral arms visible in the HST image of the galaxy. In 1432731, both [ NII ] /H and [ SII ] /H are similar values in the central region of the disk, but [ NII ] emission diminishes while [ SII ] emission increases at increasing radii.

gmw_00007272.overview: 1197518 is an isolated irregular galaxy with an optical morphology that suggests a warped outer disk [arXiv:1912.01062]. It features a low star formation rate, highly irregular ionized gas kinematics, and an asymmetric, extended neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1197518 as an isolated irregular and bright HI detection with extended asymmetric HI, low star formation, highly irregular ionized gas, and possible warped morphology.

quotes.quote: 1197518 -- 1197518 (Figure A3) is an irregular galaxy and one of the more isolated galaxies in our sample, at a distance of 1.5 Mpc from its nearest neighbor and 3.7 Mpc from a filament spine. It is the brightest HI detection so far in the full survey and has extended HI with very asymmetric morphology. The HI mass is several times its stellar mass and the SFR is low at 0.05 M _ yr ^ 1 . The ionized gas kinematics for this galaxy are the most irregular in the sample. Weak [ NII ] 6583 is present along with H .

The steep rise and fall of the ionized gas in the central region may be indicative of a strong bar, though the galaxy seems to be viewed almost edge-on, making this conclusion difficult to confirm. Sofue1999 identified that, while barred galaxies exhibit similar general properties compared to unbarred galaxies, they have larger velocity amplitude variation in the innermost disk. This large velocity variation arises from the barred potential, and simulations of PV diagrams for edge-on barred galaxies show many tens of km s ^ 1 fluctuations, superposed on the usual flat rotation curve Athanassoula1999 . Considering this is a highly inclined galaxy, the PV diagram can also be interpreted as to have a steeply rising rotation curve at the approaching side, and a more slowly rising rotation curve at the receding side, not inconsistent with the H . The optical morphology of the outer disk in the HST image of the galaxy seems suggestive of a warp. The HI contours have a mild curvature, but the ionized gas is more dramatically perturbed.

gmw_00007273.overview: The galaxy 1437568 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy located several megaparsecs away from any filamentary structure [arXiv:1912.01062]. It is characterized by a high stellar mass, an elevated star formation rate, and an extended, asymmetric neutral hydrogen morphology [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 1437568 as an isolated barred spiral with high stellar mass and SFR, extended asymmetric HI, mostly normal ionized gas, and flat line ratios.

quotes.quote: 1437568 969633 -- Two barred spirals are the most isolated galaxies in our sample, at a distance of 1.7 Mpc from their nearest neighbor. Both 1437568 (Figure A7) and 969633 (Figure A8) are located several Mpc from filamentary structure, at 7.1 Mpc and 6.0 Mpc respectively. These galaxies are large in stellar mass and have higher SFRs. Both galaxies have extended HI and 1437568 has asymmetric HI morphology.

The ionized gas in 1437568 is mostly unremarkable, following the HI contours in slope and velocity center. In galaxy 1437568, the line ratios remain relatively flat across the measured portion of the disk, albeit with more scatter than other galaxies in the sample. Though it is a barred spiral, we do not see a significant enhancement of [ NII ] /H (see 969633 notes).

gmw_00007274.overview: The galaxy 969633 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy located several megaparsecs away from any filamentary structure [arXiv:1912.01062]. It is a star-forming galaxy with extended neutral hydrogen and ionized gas emission that reaches across most of its stellar radius [arXiv:1912.01062].

sources.summary: The paper describes 969633 as an isolated barred spiral whose ionized gas extends far across the disk and whose broad central lines and enhanced nitrogen arise from the bar.

quotes.quote: 1437568 969633 -- Two barred spirals are the most isolated galaxies in our sample, at a distance of 1.7 Mpc from their nearest neighbor. Both 1437568 (Figure A7) and 969633 (Figure A8) are located several Mpc from filamentary structure, at 7.1 Mpc and 6.0 Mpc respectively. These galaxies are large in stellar mass and have higher SFRs. Both galaxies have extended HI and 1437568 has asymmetric HI morphology. The ionized gas emission in 969633 extends to 8" along PA = 60 ^ , corresponding to a distance of 19.9 kpc, or 80 of the stellar radius (R _ * = 24.44 kpc).

The galaxy is not, however, an AGN. Line ratios measured using H , [ NII ] , and [ SII ] from the SALT spectrum, as well as SDSS spectrum values for [ OIII ] 5007 / H , place this galaxy firmly within the star-forming region of a BPT diagram Baldwin1981 . We thus attribute the broad line widths in the central region to the kinematic influence of the bar (see Section 3.3).

gmw_00007275.name: NGC 3006
gmw_00007275.overview: NGC 3006 is a nearby group member galaxy with an extended, asymmetric neutral hydrogen disk that exhibits a U-shaped morphology [arXiv:2002.10076]. Despite past interactions that likely caused this asymmetry, the galaxy preserves its rotation, with its gas and stellar masses sharing the same sense of rotation [arXiv:2002.10076].

sources.summary: NGC 3006 is described as a nearby group member with extended asymmetric H I, a blue low-surface-brightness extension, and rotation preserved despite possible interaction with the dwarf pair.

quotes.quote: HI distribution in NGC 3006 is extended significantly beyond the optical size of the galaxy. The HI disk of NGC 3006 is almost U-shaped and extends towards , though no HI bridge was detected between the NGC 3006 and .

The velocity field of NGC 3006 seems to be preserving its rotation while showing an extended U-shaped morphology, an asymmetry possibly caused by some past interactions. The gas kinematic centre seems to be coincident with the optical centre. The HI spectrum of NGC 3006 show a regular rotating feature as we can see the classic double-horn profile and it's centre perfectly matches with the optical velocity of the galaxy centre, Figure hispec blue line. SDSS spectroscopy exists for two pointings in NGC 3006 -one at the centre and another to its west, which have been marked by the black crosses. We find that the gradient in optical velocity of NGC 3006 matches with that of the HI velocity, suggesting that the gas and stellar masses share same sense of rotation.

gmw_00007276.name: MRK 1388
gmw_00007276.overview: MRK 1388 is an active galactic nucleus with an ambiguous classification, tentatively categorized as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2010.12595] or a Type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.12500]. The galaxy displays evidence of an outflow [arXiv:2010.12595], and its central engine is intercepted by the bulk of its torus [arXiv:2306.12500].

sources.summary: The paper keeps MRK 1388 classified as an NLS1 galaxy while emphasizing ambiguous broad components, likely outflow evidence, no broad NIR H I line, and compact NLR scale.

quotes.quote: The classification of MRK 1388 is ambiguous. It displays a broad component in the Balmer lines with FWHM of 1040 km s ^ 1 . This would tentatively classify it as a narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLS1). However, a similar broad component is also detected (in blue-shift) in the \ lines (see Fig. fig: mrk1388 ), which suggests the presence of an outflow rather than a genuine BLR. Previously, classified MRK 1388 as a NLS1 because of the strong featureless continuum observed in that source although the optical spectrum does not display evidence of broad components in the permitted lines.

On our NIR spectrum of MRK 1388, a similar broad component as the one detected in \ is also found in [ S iii ] (see Table tab:mrkinfra_tabelao ). It suggests that this object, indeed, displays evidence of an outflow. Notice that no broad NIR H i line was found.

sources.summary: The paper finds MRK 1388 has optical depth near comparison peaks, a skirtor line of sight intercepted by the torus, and a Type II AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: The edge-on optical depth at 9.7 microns, _ 9.7 m : The values of 5, 5 and 7 found for III ZW 77, MRK 1388 and 2MASX J113+16, respectively, coincide with the peaks of the distribution in gonzalez/2019 .

It is possible to notice, by looking at Figure fig:polartodos , that Mrk 1388 is the only object fit with skirtor where the line of sight to the central engine is intercepted by the bulk of the torus.
The results obtained in this work suggest that Mrk 1388 is a Type II AGN.

gmw_00007277.name: NGC 508
gmw_00007277.overview: NGC 508 is an early-type galaxy located at a projected distance of 30 kpc from the nearby group NGC 507 [arXiv:2201.04591]. It exhibits small X-ray emission tails extending for 6 kpc, which may support a potential interaction between the two galaxies [arXiv:2201.04591].

sources.summary: The paper considers nearby NGC 508 as a possible but unlikely trigger of NGC 507 sloshing, noting its magnitude difference and small X-ray tails.

quotes.quote: The nearby (30-kpc projected distance) galaxy NGC 508 could for example play a role in this. NGC 508 and NGC 507 show a significant magnitude difference equal to m =1.42, and so it is unlikely that their interaction is responsible of initiating the sloshing motions of NGC 507. However, report that NGC 508 shows small tails in its X-ray emission extending for 6 kpc. These features are very rarely detected in the coronae of early-type galaxies sun2007 , and might support the idea of an interaction with the nearby group NGC 507.

gmw_00007278.name: SDSS J005527.46--002148.7
gmw_00007278.overview: SDSS J005527.46--002148.7 is a compact star-forming Lyman Break Analogue located 805 Mpc away [arXiv:1909.10574]. The galaxy features a strong central point source surrounded by diffuse emission, a northeast high-surface-brightness region, and peculiar extended emission that suggests an outflow [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The paper describes SHOC 042 as a distant Lyman Break Analogue with high metallicity, strong star formation, weak nebular emission, and detected X-ray output.

quotes.quote: SHOC 042 spectoscopically characterized in the SDSS galaxies with Oxygen abundances Catalog, SHOC; kniazevStrongEmissionLine2004 is a compact star-forming galaxy at z=0.167 classified as an LBA on the basis of high FUV luminosity and surface brightness comparable to LBGs at high redshift heckmanPropertiesUltravioletluminousGalaxies2005,hoopesDiversePropertiesMost2007,overzierHubbleSpaceTelescope2008 . It is the most distant galaxy in our sample at 805 Mpc, hosts relatively high metallicity gas at 12+ O/H =8.21 , and harbors the largest star formation rate in our sample: SFR ( H )= 5.9 M_ / yr (Section subsec:basicprop ). Nebular is detected in this system with MMT/BC (Figure fig:mmtheii ), with /H =0.0046 0.0021 .

In the ACIS-S image of this source (ObsID: 16019, PI: Kaaret), we detect a source 0.2 (0.7 comoving kpc) away from the center of the optical spectroscopic target (within our adopted 99 confidence spatial offset tolerance of 1.4 : Section sec:chandra ), with a derived luminosity in the 0.5--8 keV band of 6.6 1.6 41 erg/s (Table tab:chandra ). This flux measurement is in agreement (within 1.5 ) with that of brorbyEnhancedXrayEmission2016 using the same dataset Since brorbyEnhancedXrayEmission2016 extract flux in a large predefined ellipse rather than using srcflux , small offsets such as this are expected. . This corresponds to an X-ray production efficiency of 1.1 0.3 41 erg/s per M_ /year of star formation.

sources.summary: The object is described as a compact point source with surrounding diffuse emission, a northeast high-surface-brightness region, and peculiar extended emission interpreted as an outflow.

quotes.quote: SDSS J005527.46--002148.7 . This object consists of a strong point source surrounded by diffuse emission that can be seen in , \ and . The diffuse emission has a high surface brightness region extending to the NE with respect to the nucleus, which suggests either the infall of a small diffuse galaxy or an additional off-center star forming region. The continuum-subtracted \ emission is peculiar with an `arm' of emission extending to about 1 \ South of the nucleus. The morphology of the \ is very different from the \ morphology, and at its outer extremity, the \ is not associated with any UV emission. We suspect that the extended \ is due to an outflow over the larger region probed by the \ continuum.

sources.summary: The paper finds J005527 has slightly higher electron density, enhanced nitrogen relative to the other LBAs, and the strongest detected Wolf-Rayet signatures.

quotes.quote: While J005527 shows signs of slightly higher electron density, we are still well inside the low density regime for each of our targets.

Again, the red circle marks the location of J005527, which shows signs of enhanced nitrogen relative to the other LBAs, even though it still remains well within the parameter space occupied by H 2 regions.
J005527 displays the strongest Wolf-Rayet signatures, specifically N 3 4640 \ and a broad bump from 4600--4700 , in addition to the C 4 and He 2 emission lines.

gmw_00007279.name: SDSS J032845.99+011150.8
gmw_00007279.overview: SDSS J032845.99+011150.8 is a ultraviolet-luminous galaxy that appears to be a merger of two low-surface-brightness galaxies [arXiv:0709.3304]. This system features symmetric tidal tails extending to the northeast and northwest, alongside a nuclear starburst containing five isolated point sources [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The HST images suggest this UVLG is a merger of low-surface-brightness galaxies, with symmetric tidal tails and a nuclear starburst containing several point sources.

quotes.quote: SDSS J032845.99+011150.8 . The \ image suggests a merger of two low surface brightness galaxies, as evidenced by the tidal tails extending symmetrically from the center to the Northwest and Northeast. The starburst dominates the nuclear region, in which five isolated point sources can be seen. The easternmost knot dominates in .

gmw_00007280.name: SDSS J040208.86--050642.0
gmw_00007280.overview: SDSS J040208.86--050642.0 is a starburst galaxy that appears to be a merger of two diffuse systems [arXiv:0709.3304]. Its center is dominated by three point sources, with several fainter compact regions located further out [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The object is described as a likely merger of two diffuse systems, with bent faint-continuum contours, three central point sources, and additional fainter compact regions.

quotes.quote: SDSS J040208.86--050642.0 . The \ image suggests a merger of two diffuse systems as the contours of faint \ continuum emission show a sharp bend in position angle. The image center is dominated by three point sources, and several fainter ones lie further out on either side of the center. One of these faint, compact regions (about 1 \ South of the nucleus) is more extended in .

gmw_00007281.name: SDSS J102613.97+484458.9
gmw_00007281.overview: SDSS J102613.97+484458.9 is a starburst galaxy with a ring-shaped morphology resembling a "drop-through" ring galaxy [arXiv:0709.3304]. It features a faint southern companion, possible eastern tidal debris, and star formation concentrated along its eastern rim [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The galaxy has a ring-shaped starburst morphology, bright unresolved components, a faint southern companion, possible eastern tidal debris, and resemblance to a drop-through ring galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J102613.97+484458.9 . This galaxy has a ring-shaped morphology with most of the starburst occurring along the Eastern rim. There is also an isolated knot to the Southwest. The star-forming region contains several bright, unresolved components and diffuse emission. A very faint diffuse companion can be seen in , about 2 \ to the South, and very faint extended emission, possibly tidal debris, is seen directly to the East along the entire extent of the galaxy. The overall morphology is very similar to that of the ``drop-through'' ring galaxy NGC922 studied by wong06 .

gmw_00007282.name: SDSS J135355.90+664800.5
gmw_00007282.overview: SDSS J135355.90+664800.5 is a highly irregular ultraviolet-luminous galaxy characterized by numerous star-forming knots and diffuse emission [arXiv:0709.3304]. The system is interacting or merging with a redder, warped, edge-on or filamentary galaxy to its east in a likely counter-rotating merger [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The paper describes this UVLG as highly irregular, knotty, and probably interacting or merging with a nearby redder edge-on or filamentary galaxy in a counter-rotating merger.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135355.90+664800.5 . This UVLG is highly irregular in all bands, showing many star-forming knots and diffuse emission. The object is interacting or merging with a warped, edge-on or filamentary galaxy located just to the East, which is much redder and only just detected in . A longslit spectrum along the major axis of the system shows that the two objects are likely to be a counter-rotating merger (Overzier et al., in prep.).

gmw_00007283.name: SDSS J214500.25+011157.6
gmw_00007283.overview: SDSS J214500.25+011157.6 is a galaxy featuring a compact, elongated far-ultraviolet starburst region with three distinct knots [arXiv:0709.3304]. Its optical morphology differs from its ultraviolet appearance, showing a possible faint spiral structure and a companion object that appears to have passed through the star-forming nucleus [arXiv:0709.3304].

sources.summary: The object has a compact elongated FUV starburst with several knots, different optical morphology, possible faint spiral structure, and a companion apparently crossing the nucleus with tidal debris.

quotes.quote: SDSS J214500.25+011157.6 . This is the only object in our sample for which the ACS image provides a direct match at the wavelength of the GALEX FUV image. The F150LP image shows a very compact, but elongated starburst region in which we can discern three different knots. Fainter FUV emission comes from a slightly more extended region of 1 .

The optical morphology in \ is strikingly different. The image is still dominated by the emission surrounding the starburst region, and a faint spiral structure may be present. A companion object appears to have gone straight through the star forming nucleus as evidenced by its tadpole-like morphology with a faint tail pointing towards the main galaxy and a trail of tidal debris that can be traced back to a location that is about equidistant from the nucleus on the opposite side of the main galaxy.

gmw_00007284.name: VCC 2019
gmw_00007284.overview: VCC 2019 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster that is dominated by an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2308.16768, arXiv:2207.06977]. It hosts a central nuclear star cluster containing a younger, more metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:2308.16768, arXiv:2207.06977], though it lacks a clear presence of ionized gas [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: The paper groups VCC 2019 with galaxies showing young central populations, linking young NSC stars and, for some systems, ionised gas to ongoing or recent in-situ nuclear growth.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with young central populations FCC 119 and FCC 148 are the only two sample galaxies that show the presence of ionised gas in their NSC as traced by the presence of H , H , [ NII ] , [ SII ] and [ OIII ] lines in the MUSE spectra (see also the emission line maps in Iodice2019 ). Our analysis uncovered young stellar populations ( < 3 Gyr) in both NSCs, while their host stellar bodies are older. The same was found for VCC 2019 although without the clear presence of ionised gas.

This ongoing or recent star formation is a clear sign of in-situ star formation building up the NSCs. However, FCC 119 also shows an older, more metal-poor component in its NSC and is on average more metal-poor than the host galaxy. This drop in metallicity could indicate additional accretion of metal-poor GCs from the galaxy outskirts to the centre.

sources.summary: VCC2019 hosts a distinct young, metal-rich nuclear stellar cluster, but its broader old, metal-poor, enhanced population supports rapid early star formation before Virgo accretion.

quotes.quote: VCC0990 and VCC2019: Both dEs host a central NSC that shows distinct stellar population properties from the rest of their host galaxies e.g., 2011Paudel,2021Fahrion . As evident from the maps of these two dEs, their central NSC is, on average, younger, more metal-rich, and less enhanced than the rest of the galaxy. The central NSC aside, these two dEs are dominated by old stellar populations, and exhibit similar radial variations of [ M/H ] and [ /Fe ] , with a metal-poor and enhanced population in the outer regions.

Below we compare the results of this work with those of B22: Maps of light-weighted stellar population properties for three dEs (namely VCC0794, VCC1896 and VCC2019) presented in Section Results_1 indicate the presence of old, metal-poor and enhanced stars all over these galaxy. Hand in hand with their SFH profiles, shown in Fig. SFH profiles , our results support B22's interpretations that the high [ /Fe ] and low [ M/H ] in these three dEs can be due to their rapid star formation and its early cessation which has taken place, long before their accretion onto Virgo and possibly due to to the effect of the parent group.

sources.summary: VCC 2019 may host a warped or distorted inclined disc, changing isophotes, a central metallicity peak, a velocity-dispersion drop, a metallicity gradient, and faint emission lines.

quotes.quote: VCC 2019 = IC 3735 (Fig figvcc2019 ). A ``possible inclined disc, maybe warped or distorted'' is reported in this galaxy by . The isophotes demonstrate the change of ellipticity and positional angle Ferrarese+06 . We see a central peak in the metallicity distribution and the corresponding drop in the velocity dispersion profile, while the quality of the age measurements is not sufficient to clearly say whether the nucleus harbours younger stellar population than the outer parts of the galaxy. A noticeable metallicity gradient is observed.

sources.summary: The paper finds VCC2019 among the oldest sample dwarfs, with a young nuclear star cluster affecting integrated spectra but a pre-processed, quenched main body.

quotes.quote: The youngest stellar populations, with an average age = 2.10 Gyr and a mean [ M/H ] = 0.61 dex, are detected in VCC0170, while the oldest stellar populations are observed in VCC1896 and VCC2019.

The largest discrepancy concerns the age of VCC2019, for which STARLIGHT estimates a value of 6.5 Gyr (compared to 10.5 Gyr from our index fitting method). In Appendix app:3 we show that the presence of a young nuclear star cluster in the core of VCC2019 leverages the contribution of younger SSPs in the STARLIGHT final best fit for this dE. The central nuclear star cluster hosts a distinct, younger stellar population compared to the rest of VCC2019 and noticeably contributes to its integrated light see 2010Paudel,2021Fahrion .
It thus appears that VCC2019 was accreted onto Virgo with its main body already pre-processed and quenched also in 2021Fahrion .

gmw_00007285.name: VCC 990
gmw_00007285.overview: VCC 990 is a flattened nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2207.06977, arXiv:0812.3272] that contains an inclined disc [arXiv:0812.3272] and faint ionized gas [arXiv:0812.3272]. Its central nuclear star cluster is younger and more metal-rich than the rest of the galaxy [arXiv:2104.06412, arXiv:2308.16768], which is dominated by an older, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2308.16768].

sources.summary: The paper finds VCC0990 older than prior work, with double star-formation-history peaks linked partly to its nuclear star cluster and continued post-accretion star formation.

quotes.quote: However, we estimate an older age for VCC0990 (7.77 ^ +3.59 _ 1.59 Gyr) than what was determined by (4.0 1.2 Gyr). Nevertheless, this discrepancy is less than 2 and not significant.

Two dEs of our sample, namely VCC0990 and VCC2019, show double peaks in their stellar light and mass fraction profiles. According to our results in Appendix app:3 , the peak at younger ages (i.e., at 2 Gyr for VCC0990 and 4 Gyr for VCC2019) is mostly due to their nuclear star cluster, while the second peak at older ages appears to be related to star formation in their main body.
According to Fig. NSC_correction1 the star formation activity of VCC0990 continued even after its accretion onto the Virgo cluster.

sources.summary: VCC 990 is a flattened nucleated galaxy with an inclined disc, strong metallicity gradient, central velocity-dispersion minimum, radial-velocity peculiarity, and faint ionized-gas emission.

quotes.quote: VCC 990 = IC 3369 (Fig figvcc0990 ). report the detection of an inclined disc in this flattened nucleated galaxy. Inside 1 r_ e we see a quite strong metallicity gradient from about 0.3 dex to 0.8 dex while the age remains nearly constant at a level of 5 Gyr. The velocity dispersion distribution exhibits a local minimum in the centre associated with a peculiarity on the radial velocity profile. We detected faint emission lines (H , [ OIII ] ) in the fitting residuals suggesting the presence of ionised gas in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says VCC 990 belongs to a set with similar galaxy and NSC properties, where young, metal-rich NSCs indicate dominant in-situ nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: FCC 301 and VCC 990 have similar galaxy ( M_ gal 10^ 9 M_ ) and NSC masses ( M_ NSC 7 10^6 M_ ), and their stellar population properties are also similar to that of FCC 255 which is slightly more massive. In these three galaxies, the NSCs are younger and more metal-rich than their hosts, their SFHs show a single peak at age 3 Gyr. The young ages and high metallicities indicate NSC formation dominated by in-situ star formation either in recently accreted young massive star clusters or directly in the NSC.

sources.summary: VCC0990 hosts a central nuclear stellar cluster that is younger, more metal-rich, and less enhanced than the galaxy, while the rest is dominated by old, metal-poor outskirts.

quotes.quote: VCC0990 and VCC2019: Both dEs host a central NSC that shows distinct stellar population properties from the rest of their host galaxies e.g., 2011Paudel,2021Fahrion . As evident from the maps of these two dEs, their central NSC is, on average, younger, more metal-rich, and less enhanced than the rest of the galaxy. The central NSC aside, these two dEs are dominated by old stellar populations, and exhibit similar radial variations of [ M/H ] and [ /Fe ] , with a metal-poor and enhanced population in the outer regions.

gmw_00007286.name: FCC 190
gmw_00007286.overview: FCC 190 is a barred lenticular galaxy located near the center of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1906.08187]. It features an ancient stellar population [arXiv:2104.06412], a red inner region [arXiv:1906.08187], and a nuclear star cluster that shows evidence of multiple star formation episodes [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: FCC 190 is described as a barred lenticular ancient infaller near the Fornax center, with a prominent inner red region, bar-related PA mismatch, and bar-enhanced dispersion.

quotes.quote: FCC 190 (NGC 1380B) -- Fig. fig:FCC190map FCC 190 is one of the four barred lenticular galaxies close to the cluster centre. It is located at R_ proj =0.4^ on the north-western side of the cluster. It is one of the ancient infallers of the north-south clump within the high-density region of the cluster. This galaxy has a prominent inner and red ( g-i 1.1 mag) with respect to the bluer outskirts. PA_ kin and PA_ phot differ by 90^ in the region of the bar ( R 40 arcsec).
The maximum rotation velocity in the stellar disc is v_ max 100 km s ^ 1 . The stellar velocity dispersion is larger along the bar ( 80 km s ^ 1 ) and decreases outwards ( 50 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper places FCC 190 among intermediate-mass galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs, interpreting their stellar populations as evidence for repeated central star formation or young-cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs FCC 190, FCC 310, FCC 277, and FCC 249 have similar stellar masses ( 3 - 5 10^9 M_ ). Their NSCs are more metal-rich than the hosts and any GCs. The NSCs and galaxies are characterised by old stellar populations ( > 10 Gyr) and FCC 190 and FCC 277 show a minor young, metal-rich population in its NSC. The high metallicities of the NSCs as well as the extended SFHs in FCC 190, FCC 310, and FCC 277 are in agreement with formation from multiple episodes of in-situ star formation or the repeated accretion of young star clusters.

gmw_00007287.name: FCC 310
gmw_00007287.overview: FCC 310 is a barred lenticular galaxy situated on the eastern side of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1906.08187]. The galaxy features a peanut-shaped bar [arXiv:1906.08187] and hosts a metal-rich nuclear star cluster characterized by an old stellar population [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: FCC 310 is described as the only eastern barred lenticular, with a peanut-shaped bar, rotating stellar disc, bulge/bar dispersion structure, blue nucleus, and bar-enhanced line strengths.

quotes.quote: FCC 310 (NGC 1460) -- Fig. fig:FCC310map FCC 310 is the only barred lenticular galaxy on the eastern side of the cluster. It is located at a distance R_ proj =2.0^ which corresponds to about the virial radius of the Fornax cluster. It is an intermediate infaller in the south-eastern low-density region of the cluster. The prominent peanut-shaped bar is evident in the reconstructed galaxy image and gives rise to the difference of 40^ between PA_ kin and PA_ phot . The underlying stellar disc has a maximum rotation velocity v_ max 100 km s ^ 1 at R 40 arcsec.

The stellar velocity dispersion is relatively large in the bulge and bar ( 70 km s ^ 1 ) and decreases outside ( 40 km s ^ 1 at R 40 arcsec). As also observed in FCC 277 and FCC 301, which are the two other ETGs on the eastern side of the cluster, FCC 310 has a blue nucleus which causes a dip in the velocity dispersion ( 50 km s ^ 1 at R 2 arcsec). The bar structure clearly stands out in the line-strength maps of Mg b ( 3 dex) and Fe5015 ( 5 dex).

sources.summary: The paper places FCC 310 among intermediate-mass galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs, interpreting their stellar populations as evidence for repeated central star formation or young-cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs FCC 190, FCC 310, FCC 277, and FCC 249 have similar stellar masses ( 3 - 5 10^9 M_ ). Their NSCs are more metal-rich than the hosts and any GCs. The NSCs and galaxies are characterised by old stellar populations ( > 10 Gyr) and FCC 190 and FCC 277 show a minor young, metal-rich population in its NSC. The high metallicities of the NSCs as well as the extended SFHs in FCC 190, FCC 310, and FCC 277 are in agreement with formation from multiple episodes of in-situ star formation or the repeated accretion of young star clusters.

gmw_00007288.name: FCC 188
gmw_00007288.overview: FCC 188 is a galaxy that features a nuclear star cluster with a central drop in metallicity compared to its host galaxy [arXiv:2104.06412]. This nucleus contains a secondary population of younger, more metal-rich stars, suggesting that both star cluster accretion and in-situ star formation have occurred [arXiv:2104.06412].
