sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 188 as having a central metallicity drop and mixed NSC populations, requiring both metal-poor cluster infall and younger metal-rich in-situ or cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with evidence of both NSC formation scenarios The radial metallicity profiles of FCC 188, FCC 202, FCC 222, and FCC 245 all clearly show a drop in metallicity in the galaxy centres and their NSCs are on average more metal-poor than the host galaxies. This drop in metallicity is indicating formation from the infall of metal-poor GCs, but the SFHs of these galaxies all show a secondary component with a higher metallicity and significantly younger age (also seen in FCC 119). This metal-rich, young population can be explained by additional in-situ star formation or the accretion of a young star cluster. The NSCs of these galaxies are therefore examples of NSCs were both formation channels are acting together.

gmw_00007289.name: FCC 202
gmw_00007289.overview: FCC 202, also known as NGC 1396, is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1608.07042]. It features a central nuclear star cluster with a drop in metallicity [arXiv:2104.06412], an extended star formation history [arXiv:1608.07042], and a heliocentric velocity of approximately 815 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0401218].

sources.summary: The paper presents MUSE stellar-population analysis of the Fornax dwarf elliptical NGC 1396, finding age, metallicity, abundance gradients, IMF constraints, and an extended enrichment history.

quotes.quote: Deep observations of the dwarf elliptical (dE) galaxy NGC 1396 (M _V = -16.60 , Mass 4 10^8 M _ ), located in the Fornax cluster, have been performed with the VLT/ MUSE spectrograph in the wavelength region from 4750-9350 . In this paper we present a stellar population analysis studying chemical abundances, the star formation history (SFH) and the stellar initial mass function (IMF) as a function of galacto-centric distance. Different, independent ways to analyse the stellar populations result in a luminosity-weighted age of 6 Gyr and a metallicity [ Fe/H ] 0.4 , similar to other dEs of similar mass. We find unusually overabundant values of [ Ca/Fe ] +0.1 , and under-abundant Sodium, with [ Na/Fe ] values around 0.1 , while [ Mg/Fe ] is overabundant at all radii, increasing from +0.1 in the centre to +0.2 dex. We notice a significant metallicity and age gradient within this dwarf galaxy.

To constrain the stellar IMF of NGC 1396, we find that the IMF of NGC 1396 is consistent with either a Kroupa-like or a top-heavy distribution, while a bottom-heavy IMF is firmly ruled out. An analysis of the abundance ratios, and a comparison with galaxies in the Local Group, shows that the chemical enrichment history of NGC 1396 is similar to the Galactic disc, with an extended star formation history. This would be the case if the galaxy originated from a LMC-sized dwarf galaxy progenitor, which would lose its gas while falling into the Fornax cluster.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1396 as the velocity-correlation template, describing its spectrum, mask placement, and measured heliocentric velocity compared with earlier literature values.

quotes.quote: As template, we used a high S/N-spectrum (S/N about 40) of NGC 1396, a small galaxy with low intrinsic velocity dispersion and a spectrum similar to that of a cluster. This object is on mask 82, which accordingly served as the reference mask. Zero-point differences with the other masks had been accounted for by using the position of the sky O i line. We determined NGC 1396's velocity by measuring lines and obtained 815 8 km/sec as the heliocentric velocity. This value is considerably lower than most older values from the literature: da Costa et al. ( ) found 856 37 km/sec and in the RC3 catalogue 894 29 km/sec is given (de Vaucouleurs et al. ). However, Drinkwater et al. ( ) quote 808 8 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 202 as having a central metallicity drop and mixed NSC populations, requiring both metal-poor cluster infall and younger metal-rich in-situ or cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with evidence of both NSC formation scenarios The radial metallicity profiles of FCC 188, FCC 202, FCC 222, and FCC 245 all clearly show a drop in metallicity in the galaxy centres and their NSCs are on average more metal-poor than the host galaxies. This drop in metallicity is indicating formation from the infall of metal-poor GCs, but the SFHs of these galaxies all show a secondary component with a higher metallicity and significantly younger age (also seen in FCC 119). This metal-rich, young population can be explained by additional in-situ star formation or the accretion of a young star cluster. The NSCs of these galaxies are therefore examples of NSCs were both formation channels are acting together.

gmw_00007290.name: FCC 222
gmw_00007290.overview: FCC 222 is a dwarf galaxy that hosts two central point sources, consisting of a nuclear star cluster and a fainter projected bright globular cluster [arXiv:2005.01532]. The nuclear star cluster exhibits a central drop in metallicity, indicating a mixed formation history involving both metal-poor cluster infall and younger, metal-rich star formation [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: The paper finds two central point sources in FCC 222, models them separately, and argues from kinematics that the fainter source is probably a projected bright globular cluster.

quotes.quote: One interesting finding from this step was that FCC 222 was found to have a pair of point sources at the very centre. In this case, both objects were modelled independently, as shown in Fig. fig:galfit_fit_FCC222 , and will be discussed further in Section sec:membership .

The velocity of the fainter point source has been plotted in Fig. fig:velocities as the hollow circle, and it can be seen that while the velocity of this object is still within the velocity dispersion range of the host galaxy, the velocity difference relative to the host galaxy is significantly larger than that of the brighter source, the NSC. Therefore, while this object may still be an infalling GC that has been detected close to its maximum separation from the NSC on sky, the more likely explanation is that it is simply a bright GC within FCC 222 which happens to be projected along the same line of sight as the NSC.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 222 as having a central metallicity drop and mixed NSC populations, requiring both metal-poor cluster infall and younger metal-rich in-situ or cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with evidence of both NSC formation scenarios The radial metallicity profiles of FCC 188, FCC 202, FCC 222, and FCC 245 all clearly show a drop in metallicity in the galaxy centres and their NSCs are on average more metal-poor than the host galaxies. This drop in metallicity is indicating formation from the infall of metal-poor GCs, but the SFHs of these galaxies all show a secondary component with a higher metallicity and significantly younger age (also seen in FCC 119). This metal-rich, young population can be explained by additional in-situ star formation or the accretion of a young star cluster. The NSCs of these galaxies are therefore examples of NSCs were both formation channels are acting together.

gmw_00007291.name: FCC 182
gmw_00007291.overview: FCC 182 is a small early-type galaxy located west of NGC 1399 [arXiv:1906.08187]. It features a central distinct component with redder colors [arXiv:1906.08187] and a nuclear star cluster likely formed through both globular cluster infall and in-situ star formation [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: FCC 182 is described as a small ancient infaller whose stellar velocity map indicates an early-type system with a central distinct component and redder inner structure.

quotes.quote: FCC 182 -- Fig. fig:FCC182map FCC 182 is a small galaxy at R_ proj =0.3^ west of FCC 213 (NGC 1399). It is one of the ancient infallers in the north-south galaxy clump. Although it was classified as a barred galaxy by Ferguson1989 , the stellar velocity map is consistent with that of an ETG hosting a distinct component in the central R 10 arcsec with a maximum rotation velocity v_ max 15 km s ^ 1 . The stellar velocity dispersion is small ( 40 km s ^ 1 ) and is almost constant at all radii. The line-strength maps show that the inner distinct component has larger values of Mg b and Fe5015 (by 1 ) than the outskirts, while H does not show significant variation inside the galaxy ( 2 ).
FDS imaging shows that this structure is characterised by redder colours than its surrounding regions Iodice2018 .

sources.summary: The paper calls FCC 182 especially intriguing: its NSC is a central metallicity dip, but its complex SFH and present GC population require both GC infall and prolonged in-situ formation.

quotes.quote: FCC 182 is an especially intriguing case. The NSC has roughly the same metallicity as the galaxy at 2 , but the radial metallicity profile of FCC 182 reveals that the NSC still constitutes a dip in the metallicity profile. This suggests formation from in-spiral of one or two metal-poor GCs to the centre which could also explain the low NSC mass ( M_ NSC 10^6 M_ ).

In contrast, the GC population of FCC 182 today does not exhibit GCs that reach the metallicity of the NSC and the extended NSC SFH would rather indicate formation from prolonged in-situ star formation or the accretion of multiple star clusters of different ages. However, also the SFH shows a minor old, metal-poor component in addition to higher metallicities and younger ages. To fully explain the radial metallicity profile and the complex SFH, a likely contribution from both NSC formation channels is required.

gmw_00007292.name: 7C 1748+6731
gmw_00007292.overview: 7C 1748+6731 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a double optical structure [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314]. This optical structure is almost perfectly aligned with the galaxy's radio axis, which may be caused by a dust disk aligned with the radio jet [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314].

sources.summary: The paper describes 7C 1748+6731 as a strongly aligned radio galaxy with a double optical structure, analyzed using a midpoint center and possibly explained by an aligned dust disk.

quotes.quote: 7C 1748+6731 This is the most well-aligned object in either sample due to its double structure which is aligned almost perfectly with the radio axis.
For 7C 1748+6731, we have used the halfway point between the two symmetric optical components (and in this case, also used an exclusion radius of only 0 05 for the flux analysis).
In at least one case (7C 1748+6731) the observed alignment may be due to a dust disk whose axis aligns with the radio jet axis.

gmw_00007293.name: 7C 1807+6831
gmw_00007293.overview: The radio galaxy 7C 1807+6831 features a peculiar double component located to its southwest [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314]. This aligned component exhibits a red stellar population consistent with the redshift of the host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314].

sources.summary: The paper reports 7C 1807+6831 has a red aligned component southwest of the host, with stellar spectra near the host redshift and morphology linked to radio-source alignment.

quotes.quote: 7C 1807+6831 The peculiar double component `a' seen to the SW of the host galaxy has the spectrum of a red stellar population consistent with the host redshift. The aligned component has a tentative H emission line at z=0.595 , redshifted by 2800 kms ^ 1 relative to the host galaxy. This was checked by cross-correlating the spectra of the host and extended emission (after subtraction of emission lines) with a template spectrum obtained from a high signal-to-noise observation of a low redshift member of the 7C-III sample. This cross-correlation technique yields a redshift of the aligned component of 1300 700 kms ^ 1 with respect to the host, broadly consistent within the errors.

Two of the objects in the 7C sample, 7C1807+6831 and 7C1826+6510 have aligned components with red stellar populations. Furthermore, the morphology of these components is peculiar, with both having fainter aligned material strung out along the line joining them to the radio source host galaxy.

gmw_00007294.name: 7C 1815+6815
gmw_00007294.overview: 7C 1815+6815 is a radio galaxy with a large angular size [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314]. The galaxy exhibits a weak emission line and is likely situated within a group or cluster of galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314].

sources.summary: The paper treats 7C 1815+6815 as a large angular-size radio source with two possible identifications, adopting the brighter H-band object with a weak emission line.

quotes.quote: 7C 1815+6815 In Lacy et al. \ (1993) two possible identifications for this large angular size source were proposed. We have adopted `' as the identification as it has a weak emission line and is brighter in the H band (Lacy et al. \ 1998b). The alternative identification, `a' has a similar stellar spectrum to `' and is probably in the same group or cluster.

gmw_00007295.name: SDSS J004240.65+141529.6
gmw_00007295.overview: SDSS J004240.65+141529.6 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3.70 [arXiv:1305.4357]. It exhibits rapid broad emission line variability, showing a 26 percent decrease in line flux over 14 rest-frame days with minimal change in its continuum flux [arXiv:1305.4357].

sources.summary: The paper highlights this z=3.70 quasar as a fast BEL variability example, with a 26 percent line-flux decrease over 14 rest-frame days and little continuum change.

quotes.quote: SDSS J004240.65+141529.6 The spectra of SDSS J004240.65+141529.6 ( z 3.70 ) are shown in Figure SpAl057 , 14 days apart in the quasar rest frame. The redshift of the \ BEL is somewhat larger than indicated by the SDSS pipeline redshift, but the redshift of the \ BEL is a close match. The measured \ BEL flux seen in the second LSN-epoch spectrum is 26 fainter than in the first HSN-epoch spectrum, whereas the nearby continuum flux decreases only 6 . The relatively fast change in \ BEL flux in this quasar might be considered surprising given its high luminosity. For example, if the luminosity of this quasar is compared to the luminosity of AGN NGC 5548, we find: (L_ quasar /L_ AGN )^ 0.5 13.6 .

gmw_00007296.name: SDSS J132750.44+001156.8
gmw_00007296.overview: SDSS J132750.44+001156.8 is a high-redshift quasar at a redshift of z=2.53 [arXiv:1305.4357]. Over an 84-day rest-frame period, the galaxy exhibited a bluer continuum spectral slope and a 34 percent increase in its broad emission line flux [arXiv:1305.4357].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=2.53 quasar as showing increased Lyman-alpha and continuum flux, broad BELs, shifted line peaks, and no significant C IV flux change.

quotes.quote: SDSS J132750.44+001156.8 SDSS J132750.44+001156.8 (Figure SpAl013 , z=2.53 ) shows increases in the \ BEL flux and nearby continuum flux of 34 and 12 per cent during a period of 84 days in the quasar rest frame. The BELs are very broad, such that the wings of the \ and \ BELs are not included in the 6200 km s ^ 1 interval that we measure. Relative to the SDSS pipeline redshift, the redshift of the peak of the \ BEL appears higher and the redshift of the peak of the \ BEL appears lower. A significant flux change in the \ BEL is not observed. The continuum spectral slope at the brighter HSN-epoch is bluer than observed at the LSN-epoch.

gmw_00007297.name: SDSS J095106.32+541149.8
gmw_00007297.overview: SDSS J095106.32+541149.8 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=2.69 [arXiv:1305.4357]. Over a rest-frame period of 610 days, the object exhibited flux increases of 25 percent in its Lyman-alpha broad emission line and 20 percent in its C IV broad emission line, while the nearby continuum flux increased by only 1 percent [arXiv:1305.4357].

sources.summary: The paper presents this z=2.69 quasar as having significant Lyman-alpha and C IV BEL flux changes over 610 rest-frame days, with little nearby continuum change.

quotes.quote: SDSS J095106.32+541149.8 The spectra of SDSS J095106.32+541149.8 (Figure SpAl167 , z=2.69 ) were taken 610 days apart in the quasar rest frame. Significant flux changes in both the \ and \ BELs are present, whereas no significant change in the nearby continuum flux is observed. Relative to the initial LSN-spectrum the \ BEL flux increased 25 , the nearby continuum flux increased 1 , and the \ BEL flux increased 20 . The redshift of the \ BEL is consistent with the SDSS redshift, but the redshift of the \ BEL is somewhat lower. The \ BEL may show a larger flux change in the line core than in the wing.

gmw_00007298.name: SDSS J120802.64+630328.9
gmw_00007298.overview: SDSS J120802.64+630328.9 is an active galactic nucleus and high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=2.57 [arXiv:1305.4357]. Over a period of 613 rest-frame days, the object exhibited a change in its nearby continuum flux, while its broad emission lines showed no major flux variations [arXiv:1305.4357].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=2.57 quasar as having a significant continuum change but no significant Lyman-alpha or C IV BEL changes over 613 rest-frame days.

quotes.quote: SDSS J120802.64+630328.9 The spectra of SDSS J120802.64+630328.9 (Figure SpAl188 , z=2.57 ) were taken 613 days apart in the quasar rest frame. No significant flux changes in both the \ and \ BELs are observed, whereas a significant change in the nearby continuum flux is present. Relative to the initial HSN-spectrum the \ BEL flux increased 2 , the nearby continuum flux increased 23 , and the \ BEL flux decreased 7 . The redshift of the \ BEL is consistent with the SDSS redshift, but the redshift of the \ and \ BEL is somewhat lower.

gmw_00007299.name: NGC 5930
gmw_00007299.overview: NGC 5930 is a companion galaxy that is in an active interaction with NGC 5929 [arXiv:1311.6142]. This gravitational interaction has disturbed the gas velocity field of its companion and triggered a transfer of gas toward the central region of NGC 5929 [arXiv:1311.6142].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5930 as the companion whose interaction with NGC 5929 likely disturbed the gas velocity field and triggered gas transfer toward NGC 5929.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929 seems to be in interaction with its companion galaxy NGC 5930.

As the gas velocity field rotates in the opposite direction to that of the stars, our interpretation is that this gas was captured in the interaction with NGC 5930 and is now rotating around the nucleus of NGC 5929 but still showing distortions from a ``regular" rotation field due to interaction with the companion.

We have fitted a rotation model to the one component gas and found a steep rotation curve, and residuals as high as 100 , evidencing deviations from simple rotation, which we attribute to the effect of the companion galaxy NGC 5930. A possible scenario for the complex gas velocity field we have observed is the following: the interaction with NGC 5930 has triggered the transfer of gas to the central region of NGC 5929.

gmw_00007300.name: Leo I
gmw_00007300.overview: Leo I is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1406.6704]. It contains a population of variable stars, including RR Lyrae stars and intermediate-age central-helium-burning Cepheids [arXiv:1406.6704].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes variable stars in the Leo I dwarf spheroidal, deriving RR Lyrae and Cepheid properties, distance constraints, and comparisons to Galactic halo populations.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The photocenter of Leo I has been estimated to lie at ( , ) = (152.1125 , 12.30833333) (= 10 08 27 00 +12 18 30 0) according to mateo98 ; we ourselves estimate the center of \ to lie at 10 08 27 13 +12 18 22 2 [ (x,y) = (+160.0,-282.8) ] , with an uncertainty of order 1 \ in each coordinate.

quotes.quote: In this paper we analyse in detail the properties of variable stars that we have identified in the Leo I dwarf spheroidal galaxy. Part of these data have already been presented in fiorentino12d , where we focused our attention on understanding the intermediate-age central-helium-burning stellar population (Anomalous and/or short-period Cepheids, hereinafter AC/spCs). Here we present the full infrastructure underlying that discussion: identifications and equatorial positions for the complete set of variable star candidates together with their periods and light curve parameters. In rr we describe in detail the RRLs of Leo I.

Next, in dSph and GCfield we discuss the variable stars in Leo I within the larger context of Milky Way formation and evolution discussed above. In particular we will compare the period distributions and period-amplitude relations of the RRLs in (a) our data for Leo I and literature data for other classical dSph galaxies with literature data for (b) the Galactic halo field, () the Galactic halo GCs, and (d) the new Galactic halo UFDs.

gmw_00007301.name: BCG of MACS J1447.4+0827
gmw_00007301.overview: The BCG of MACS J1447.4+0827 is an elliptical brightest cluster galaxy that hosts a central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.04815]. This galaxy is surrounded by optically bright, filamentary structures that extend up to 25 kiloparsecs in size [arXiv:2006.04815].

sources.summary: The paper describes the brightest cluster galaxy in MACS J1447.4+0827 as filament-rich, elliptical, and hosting an X-ray detected central AGN coincident with HST emission.

quotes.quote: These filters contain the redshifted H emission line (H 6562) and highlight the filamentary structure of the BCG.
The BCG is surrounded by optically bright filaments up to 25 kpc in size. We can denote two prominent filaments: one oriented in the west-south direction and the other in the west-north direction.

Radiative properties of the central AGN Using the Chandra 3 -- 8 keV image, we found evidence of a central point source whose position coincides with the brightest pixels of the HST image (RA = 14^ h 47^ m 26.022^ s , DEC = +8^ 28 25.26 ) in the BCG: this emission is characteristic of an AGN.

gmw_00007302.name: HCG 86A
gmw_00007302.overview: HCG 86A is the brightest member galaxy of its group [arXiv:2105.06970]. Photometric analysis reveals that the galaxy has an integrated stellar population age of 12.8 Gyr and features an extended outer exponential component [arXiv:2105.06970].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCG 86A as the brightest group member, maps its surface-brightness and color profiles, and finds an extended outer exponential component.

quotes.quote: According to Proctor_2004 , the two brightest members of the group, HCG 86A and HCG 86B, are 12.8 Gyr and 9 Gyr old, respectively, based on ages and metallicities of unresolved (integrated light) stellar populations, measured from Lick system analysis.

The first fit was performed on the brightest group member, HCG 86A, since it dominates the light distribution. The resulting azimuthally average surface brightness and color profiles for HCG 86A are shown in Fig. fig:prof , where R corresponds to the semi-major axis (sma) in arcmin.

The 1D fit of HCG 86A shows an extended exponential component in the outskirts, at R 51 arcsec ( 20.4 kpc), with a central surface brightness of _0=26.30 mag/arcsec ^2 and a scale length r_h=132 arcsec ( 53 kpc) in the g band. Based on the 1D-fitting results, the 2D model for HCG 86A has been made for the brightest regions of the galaxy, i.e. for R R_ tr =51 arcsec, and then subtracted from the image in each band.

gmw_00007303.name: HCG 86B
gmw_00007303.overview: HCG 86B is a bright member of the HCG 86 galaxy group [arXiv:2105.06970]. The galaxy has an estimated age of 9 Gyr and exhibits a bar-like internal structure [arXiv:2105.06970].

sources.summary: The paper treats HCG 86B as a bright group member, part of a possible second kinematic structure, with a bar-like feature in the smoothed image.

quotes.quote: According to Proctor_2004 , the two brightest members of the group, HCG 86A and HCG 86B, are 12.8 Gyr and 9 Gyr old, respectively, based on ages and metallicities of unresolved (integrated light) stellar populations, measured from Lick system analysis.

They found that HCG 86 has the most members and is the most complex group of their catalogue, since it consists of two different kinematic structures, centred on the BGG HCG 86A and on HCG 86B . Based on their analysis, the authors proposed two possible scenarios for group formation: it could be a bimodal structure in a merging process, or it is a single group, not relaxed yet, where HCG 86B is an extension of HCG 86A in the velocity space.

The small smoothing box allow us to reveal the internal structure of the group members: HCG 86A has a disk in the central region, a bar-like feature appears to be present in both HCG 86B and HCG 86C, and the disk in HCG 86D appears warped in the outskirts.

gmw_00007304.name: HCG 86C
gmw_00007304.overview: HCG 86C is a lenticular galaxy located to the southeast of its group's center [arXiv:2105.06970]. It is connected to the group center by a faint, diffuse stellar bridge and may be the last member to join the group [arXiv:2105.06970].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 86C as an S0 galaxy southeast of the group center, connected by a faint bridge and possibly the last member joining HCG 86.

quotes.quote: In addition, in the Southern part of the group, we detect a faint stellar bridge ( _g 27.8 - 29.1 mag/arcsec ^2 ) connecting HCG 86C with the group center of about 1.2 arcmin long and 1 arcmin wide, and two faint streams ( 1.2 arcmin long) protruding from the SE regions of HCG 86A (these are marked with red arrows in Fig. fig:HCG86_dss ).

As a final step, on the resulting image where all the three galaxies in the core of the group have been modelled and subtracted, we have performed the analysis of HCG 86C, the S0 galaxy located to the SE of the group centre (see Fig. fig:col_comp ).

The faint and diffuse bridge connecting HCG 86C to the rest of the group is the only evident tidal feature in the group, which would reconcile with the late assembly scenario proposed by Diaz-gimenez2021 , where HCG 86C might be the last member joining the group.

gmw_00007305.name: HCG 86D
gmw_00007305.overview: HCG 86D is a galaxy located in the close core of its group [arXiv:2105.06970]. It features a warped outer disk and shares a common envelope with the neighboring galaxy HCG 86A [arXiv:2105.06970].

sources.summary: The paper includes HCG 86D among the close core members, noting a warped outer disk, a shared envelope with HCG 86A, and modeled surface-brightness profiles.

quotes.quote: The small smoothing box allow us to reveal the internal structure of the group members: HCG 86A has a disk in the central region, a bar-like feature appears to be present in both HCG 86B and HCG 86C, and the disk in HCG 86D appears warped in the outskirts.
In Fig. fig:limitrad we show the fitted isophote intensity as a function of the semi-major axis (sma) centered on HCG 86A (HCG 86B and HCG 86D share the same envelope as HCG 86A) and HCG 86C.

Using the residual image, the other two galaxies in the core of the group, HCG 86B and HCG 86D, have been modelled in turn, i.e. the azimuthally-averaged surface brightness profiles are obtained from the isophotal analysis and then fitted.

gmw_00007306.name: NGC 2329
gmw_00007306.overview: NGC 2329 is a galaxy located within the Abell 569 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256]. It hosts a wide-angle tailed radio source and features an exceptionally blue nucleus containing a small, inclined central dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2329 as an Abell 569 galaxy with an inclined central dust disk, blue nucleus, exceptional color, and wide-angle tailed radio source.

quotes.quote: NGC 2329 NGC 2329 is part of Abell cluster 569. The galaxy has a small inclined central dust disk (diameter 740 pc/ 2.0'' ). The V I image shows a few brighter pixels over the disk ending in a lighter spot at the edge of disk at PA 318 . There also seems to be a small dust protuberance to the disk in the opposite direction. The nuclear emission is slightly resolved and shows a small extension roughly in the direction of the dust protuberance.

The nucleus is clearly bluer than its surroundings. The galaxy has an exceptional blue V I ( 1.20 ) color compared to the rest of the sample. NGC 2329 hosts a wide-angle tailed radio source (Feretti 1985; Xu 1999a).

gmw_00007307.name: NGC 2892
gmw_00007307.overview: NGC 2892 is an isolated galaxy with a nearby apparent elliptical companion [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256]. It features a blue nuclear point source, a central emission peak, and a regular double-sided radio jet, with no detected dust [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2892 as an isolated galaxy with a nearby apparent companion, no detected dust, central emission, blue nucleus, and regular double-sided radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 2892 NGC 2892 is an isolated galaxy apart from a nearby apparent elliptical companion at 10'' (4.4 kpc assuming same distance) which is not identified in NED. No dust is detected in NGC 2892. The emission image shows a central peak with low-level emission around it out to 0.3'' (130 pc). The V I image shows a blue nuclear point source. NGC 2892 has a very regular double-sided radio jet (CB88; Xu 1999a).

gmw_00007308.name: NGC 4335
gmw_00007308.overview: NGC 4335 is an isolated field galaxy that hosts a two-sided radio jet and nuclear emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256]. It features a settling dust disk with arc-shaped dust structures, which are likely accreted material that aligns closer to the major axis at smaller radii [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256, arXiv:astro-ph/0502075].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4335 as a field galaxy with arc-shaped dust features, nested central dust disks, nuclear emission, tentative CO absorption, and two-sided radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 4335 NGC 4335 is a field galaxy without nearby companions. Arc shaped dust features (with a total diameter of 4 kpc/ 13.5'' ) surround the nucleus and appear to be the visible half of a settling dust disk. The outer parts consist of `arms' of dust while the inner parts are formed by a small central dust disk (diameter 240 pc/ 0.8'' ) embedded in a larger dust disk (diameter 750 pc/ 2.5'' ) with similar axial ratio and PA. There is nuclear \ emission and low-level emission is associated with the extended dust features throughout the galaxy. Knapp Rupen (1996) report a tentative detection of CO in absorption.
NGC 4335 hosts a two-sided radio jet (CB88; Xu 1999a).

sources.summary: NGC 4335 is used as evidence for settling dust: its larger-scale arc-like dust attaches to the disk and shows closer major-axis alignment at smaller radii.

quotes.quote: Some disks have in addition more irregular dust at larger scales (e.g., NGC 315, NGC 4335 and NGC 4261). In particular the case of NGC 4335 is interesting as there the larger scale dust is attached to the disk and has an arc-like morphology very reminiscent of a settling disk (e.g., Verdoes Kleijn ).

Similarly, one expects large dust distributions to display closer alignment with the major axis at smaller radii because of the decreased dynamical time scale. NGC 4335 displays this behaviour, but it is not seen in general. It is reasonable to assume that the current dust distribution was accreted by the galaxy before or around the time of the on-set of the AGN.

gmw_00007309.name: NGC 5141
gmw_00007309.overview: NGC 5141 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1504.02772] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1504.02772] and a low-power Fanaroff-Riley type I radio galaxy [arXiv:2202.05857, arXiv:1504.02772]. It features a central dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256, arXiv:1504.02772], two-sided radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256, arXiv:1504.02772], a single X-ray cavity [arXiv:2202.05857], and H I emission clouds extending toward its companion galaxy, NGC 5142 [arXiv:1003.2835].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1322+36 as a radio source with H I emission clouds and absorption probably tracing one large low-surface-brightness structure near companion NGC 5142.

quotes.quote: B2 1322+36 (NGC 5141): This system shows two clouds of \ emission in the direction of the nearby companion galaxy NGC 5142. As can be seen from Fig. fig:HIsample , \ absorption detected against the radio continuum is also slightly extended in this direction. The column density of the absorbing \ gas is very similar to the peak column density seen in emission. This suggests that the emission and the absorption are possibly part of the same large-scale \ structure, which has a too low surface brightness to be detected in emission at other locations.

The systemic velocity based on the stellar kinematics see noe03 corresponds to the peak in the \ absorption profile. We detect no optical counterpart at the location of the \ emission in our deep optical image. B2 1322+36 shows no obvious features in our deep optical image, but the bulge dominated companion system NGC 5142 shows indications of a minor and very faint warped disc. The radio source has a total linear extent of 19 kpc and a typical \ morphology.

sources.summary: The paper reports a Chandra-detected radio-filled X-ray cavity in NGC 5141, linking its low-power FRI jet to heating that can balance ISM cooling.

quotes.quote: We present the first Chandra detection of a single X-ray cavity within the interstellar medium of the small Fanaroff-Riley type I (FRI) radio galaxy NGC 5141. The X-ray surface brightness depression, located 4 kpc away from the galaxy center, is projected on the northern radio lobe, which is completely contained within the galaxy. The thermal gas surrounding the cavity, which extends to 20 kpc, has a bolometric X-ray luminosity (0.1 - 100 keV) of L _X 2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and a temperature of kT 0.8 keV. We calculated the total energy (E _ cav = 4PV 10^ 55 erg) required to inflate the cavity and its age ( t_ cav 9 Myrs), assuming that it is filled with relativistic particles and rises buoyantly. The inferred total cavity power is as low as P _ cav =E_ cav /t_ cav 6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , which is the lowest one among the radio-filled systems.

We report the discovery of an X-ray cavity within the ISM of NGC 5141. The X-ray cavity is located in the northeast region of the galaxy within a few kiloparsecs from the central AGN, and it partially corresponds to the northern radio lobe. NGC 5141 is classified in the radio band as a small low-power FRI whose emission is contained within the optical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5141 as a group galaxy with companion NGC 5142, central wedge-shaped dust lane, central emission, two-sided radio jets, and altered inner isophotes.

quotes.quote: NGC 5141 NGC 5141 is part of a group of six galaxies (Ramella 1989) and has a companion galaxy, NGC 5142, at 2.3' (47 kpc). It has a central slightly wedge-shaped dust lane with a size of 800 pc ( 2.3'' ). The \ emission peaks at the center and low-level emission is detected in the dust lane. NGC 5141 has two-sided radio jets with broad lobes (Xu 1999a). Our isophotal analysis is consistent with a ground-based study by Gonz a lez-Serrano (1993).

In contrast with our results, however, they report a sharper decrease in ellipticity inward of 3'' . This difference might be caused by their larger seeing and/or a distortion of the inner isophotes by the central dust distribution.

sources.summary: J1324+3622 is presented as a weak-lined red-spectrum Seyfert 2 in an S0 galaxy with a nuclear dust lane and VLA-resolved FR I twin jets.

quotes.quote: J1324+3622 This is a Seyfert 2 galaxy with weak emission lines and a red spectrum. It is hosted by a S0 galaxy with a strong nuclear dust lane. Its radio morphology is that of a FR I radio galaxy with twin jets resolved on VLA scales .

gmw_00007310.name: MG 0751+2716
gmw_00007310.overview: MG 0751+2716 is a gravitationally lensed source quasar located at a redshift of 3.200 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594]. Its lensing system consists of four radio images and a partial ring, which are produced by a lens galaxy situated within a small group [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594].

sources.summary: MG 0751+2716 is described as a four-image radio lens whose lens galaxy is a satellite in a small group, with a complex environment requiring external shear.

quotes.quote: MG 0751+2716 (hereafter MG0751), discovered as a part of the MIT--Greenbank--VLA search for gravitational lenses, is a radio lens with four images and a partial ring Leharmg0751-93 . Optical imaging of the system by Leharmg0751-97 identified an R = 21.3 galaxy (G3) located 0.2 northeast of the brightest radio spot as the likely lens galaxy.

G3 is a satellite of a much brighter R=19.1 galaxy (G1) located 6 away. T&Kmg0751-99 determined the redshifts of the galaxies to be z_ G1 =0.3501 0.0003 and z_ G3 =0.3502 0.0003 . They also found a nearby emission line galaxy to have redshift 0.3505 0.0003 , indicating that the lens galaxy lies in a small group with at least three members. Lens models by suggest that MG0751 requires more external shear that can be accounted for by the observed galaxies, which is consistent with the hypothesis that the lens environment is complex. The redshift of the source quasar is z_s = 3.200 0.001 T&Kmg0751-99 .
